Yénd njam kwéném fene méngot, fémo yau nu waram tane kafkaf umbarghét, wénde amafom yengam fétre kwaramete a tawaine yánjo séfe. 45 Fémo yau wésneta, wénde yéné amafamro, kétándmayo yénd nélawan, yau fá nékmbo tane kafkaf séneghan ta umyoghét. 46 Fémo yau yéfai oil tane sénkoyan ta umyoghét, wénde amafom mer wimafé wékwér yasuryéng tane kafkafan. 47 Yénémamén nde yéndon némaufrotan fe, yéné amafoene tárfár yaufi té yawalindangén, ménamén minde uwéghafé téfandau. Wénde emon njam ewawalinde némbne kuyafongé némbnar uwéghafé wéi ndenáyo."
bt: "Then he turned toward the woman and said to Saimon, 'Did you see this woman? When I came to your house, you did not give me any water for washing my feet, but the woman wet me with tears and wiped them with her hair. 45 You did not kiss me, but only this woman, from the timeI entered, has not stopped kissing my feet. 46 You did not put oil on my head for welcoming me, but she has poured special smelling liquid on my feet. 47 Therefore I tell you, this woman's many sins have been washed, because she has shown big love. But one who has been washed little loves little same way.

nama: Féyo Yesum yémndai amaf, "Fene yaufi té némawalindangén."
bt: Then Yesu said to her, "Your sins have been washed."

nama: Ámb wan téfnár si nufngoyénd yéfene kénjúnro, "Efe yéné yém emo yaufi tawainangé?"
bt: The other guests began to say among themselves, "Who is this who washes sins?"

nama: Yesum yawangoi amafoe, "Fene ndimbal mwighayam té némanjar, nguwano mere a némor."
bt: Yesu said to the woman, "Your big mind(faith) has saved you, go well and live."

nama: Yéné soramé, Yesu karendau tesenamé a méngotuamé, mer si téngwaitotau Ngánján limánéghmén. 12ro tamorwén yémafé, 2 ámb amaf tamorwén efe mer eyátoi yaufi mbérmbérafé a enjnefé, Meri Makdalin ende tambén 7 yaufi mbérmbér néntoryénd. 3 Erodene méngo wanjengégh ár Kusa, yáne amaf Joana, Susanaf a amaf tárfár. Yéné amafém tawáutánwét yéfenjo rokare.
bt: After this, Yesu traveled from one town and village to another, [he] preached the good talk about the ruling of God. The 12 were with him, 2 some women who had been healed from evil spirits and diseases, Meri Magdalin from whom 7 evil spirits have come out. 3 Erod's household mananger Kusa, his wife Joana, Susana and many other women. These women helped them with their things.

nama: Njamandio tárfár ár káumendawét Yesuefaf fénatafénaté tesenta, yémon rengerenge si tawaitotau:
bt: While a large crowd was gathering to Yesu from different towns, he preached parable:

nama: "Ndau téfnár nuwanoi yánjo wén mbwe taranéghét. Njam yémon téngtarnau, ámb nuyáyénd téndomé, yéné tatumndawét kafkafe, a menam tánetawét. 6 Ámb nuyáyénd wímafé mbandan, njam kanotawét, kér emormén ménamén nuofnarmén. 7 Ámb mbwe nuyáyénd wétwét sifayan wétwétam njangénjangén eyátoyénd anoghan. 8 Ámb mbwe nuyáyénd mer mbandot. Kanotawét a nafoyénd kwéki 100 mindefifi tárfár ndené kés eitaménd." Fá njam norayai yéné, yémon emndai, "Fá efe njam ténganafé em áyárghét, fá so káyárangi."
bt: "A farmer went out to sow his seed. When he scattered the seed, some fell along the road, it was trampled on, and the birds ate it. 6 Some seed fell on the land with stones, when it came up, it died because there is no water(moisture). 7 Other seed fell among the thorns place which it grew up with thorns and choked it. 8 Other seed fell on good land. It grew and produced a crop 100 times more than was sorn. When he talked this, he said, "He who has ears to hear, he will hear."

nama: Yáne waitogh árém yémotayénd yéné rengerenge fifi némé yém. 10 Yémon emndai, "Ngánjánam yuyafote féfe fronde némé yau tauyafotau Ngánján limánéghmén, wénde ámb árét yéndon norayan rengerenge sie, ménát, "'Ghékfe so yéfandat, wénde yau so tinjongi, so náyáretat, wénde yau so tayamngongi.'
bt: His following men asked him what is meaning of this parable. 10 He said, "God is showing to you what he did not show before about ruling of God, but I am speaking to other people with parables, so that, The eye will see, but they may not find, they will hear, but they may not understand.

nama: Rengerengeyoene fifi siyém: Mbwe siyém Buk si. 12 Áyárégh ár efe em endmé fá em, féyo Sáifér kénwanongé yéfene tikéfta si orot, ménát yémofem yau so efalo tiyátongi a kawanjarngi. 13 Yéné wímafé mbandan fá em emofem si tanangi áuwéghafé njam yémofem náyáretat, wénde fá yénéf em sérémbofnar. Yéfene efalo yiyátogh siyém tekofnar, wénde ufogh efghon fá kuyangi. 14 Árké mbwe nuyáyénd wétwét sifayan, fá esérmbéne efe náyáretat, wénde fá méghmégh em tárfár rokarmén ménát so em wemár yéfene ghérsayan, a yéfene ndimbal mwighé yau kénamndangi muyafé. 15 Wénde árké mbwe mer mbandot nuyáyénd fá esérmbéne efe em mer a efalo ár tikfan, efe náyáretat si, yarawenat wafroghyamot yékmetat a ámb mbwe nafotat. Rengerenge si kwayaghamén
bt: The meaning of parable is this: The seed is the talk of God. 12 Those along the road are ones who hear, and then devil comes and steals [the talk] from their hearts, so that they may not truly approve(believe) and not be saved. 13 Those on the land with stone are ones who receive the talk with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. Their big mind(faith) is not long, but in the time of testing they fall away. 14 Which seeds fell among the thorns place, they stand for those who hear, but they expect/think/worry about many things to become rich in their life and their big mind(faith) do not grow strong. 15 But which seeds on good land stands for those with a good and true heart, who hear the talk, they endure and put it practice and produce other seeds." Parable of a light

nama: "Árom yau lambefé korongé tétnongé sin kénjún o tékmangé yáf mbanmbanan. Wénde yémon so téfayongé kitarékitarayan. 17 Warambnoghe némé yém so tinjongi, a némé ténoghe yém sáfét so kakmangi. 18 Kor fété ndernáye fém náyárete. Efe ár rokarafé em yéfe minde fifi so tawaramangé, wénde endefene yau ewam, a nemnat ndené tane féyotaro em so tawakawangé." Yesue yátmé a yánngnéf
bt: "People don't put lamp in the pot or put under the basket. But he will put on the table. 17 What is hidden will be found, and what is secret will be put out the sight. 18 Pay attention to how you listen. Whoever have something will be given more, but whoever does not have, and they think that mine are enough will lose." Yesu' mother and his younger brothers

nama: Yesue yátmé a yanngnéf enmormén yá fandaghét, wénde ménamén ár tárfár tamorwén, fá yau kaka tamorwén yáfaf. 20 Ámb árom yaufroi yá, "Fene amé a fenngnéf kétándmé ewakai safén, nawanjetat fe fandaghét."
bt: Yesu's his mother and his younger brothers came to see him, but because there are many people, they can't come to close to him. 20 Someone told him, "Your mother and your younger brothers are standing outside, waiting to see you."

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, "Tane amé a nénngnéf fá em efe náyáretat Ngánjánene si a wafroghyaman ekmetat." Yesum yékmboi ndimbal mbormbor
bt: He answered, "My mother and younger brothers are those who hear God's talk and put it action." Yesu calms big wind(storm)

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, "Kuwanongém sawot kéférmangém witmé karéf." Féyo fá nuflitaménd boutan nuwanoyénd. 23 Njamandio fá karayawét, Yesu nakmai. Ndimbal mbormboram eutfoi sawan, féyo bout táfandio olitoghétro témorwén, fá tamorwén ndimbal kértayan.
bt: One day Yesu said to his following men, "Let's go to theh lake and pass over to the other side." So they got into the bout and went. 23 As they started, Yesu fell asleep. Big wind blew in the lake, so bout almost was sink, they were in the big problem.

nama: Waitogh ár naflayénd yanoyénd fá, nutnayénd, "Yuré, Yuré, yénd yéném olitoghétro!"
bt: The following men went and woke him, shouted, "Lord, Lord, we're going to drown!"

nama: Yémo emndai yánjo waitogh ár, "Ánde yém féfene ndimbal mwighé?" Waitogh ár férfér a sélngwél emormén, fáro yémndayénd, "Efe fá yém? Njam fá korayangé mbormbor a sofam tiyárangi fá!" Yesum mer yiyátoi yaufi mbérmbérafé ár
bt: He said to his following men, "Where is your big mind(faith)?" The following men came to fear and amazement, they said themselves, "Who is he? When he speak to wind and waves, [they] obey him!" Yesu healed man with evil spirit

nama: Fá nufaryénd Gerasenes yuwot, árké yém witmé karéf kétamé Galili yu. 27 Yesum njam kafkaf eyai, yaufi mbérmbérafé árom yéfángoi. Teke yéné ár yau sáláme tawongérwén a yau témorangérwén méngon, wénde mbéwé sifayan. 28 Njam Yesu yinjoi, fá nutnai a nuyai yáne kafkafot, nutnam tukén, "Fém némé ménde ném tafaf, Yesu, Minde Tukén Ngánjánene ambum? Yéndon némamnendan, yau tékrátan kwékém!" 29 Ménamén Yesum yémndai yaufi mbérmbér étorghét ároe tambén. Túmén yaufi mbérmbéram tusérmbotawét a árém yumbetaménd ngángayan a kafkafan wambégh mbanmbanan témorwén, yémon etiaryéng sein a yaufi mbérmbéram yalimai fanfne yuwot.
bt: They arrived at the region of Gerasenes, which is across from the Galili region. 27 When Yesu stepped out, a man with evil spirit met him. For the long time this man had not worn clothes and didn't stay in the house, but in the grave place. 28 When he saw Yesu, he shouted and fell at his feet, shouted loudly, "What do you want with me, Yesu, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don't curse me!" 29 For Yesu said evil spirit to go out of the man. Many times evil spirits attacked him and people bound with chains on his hand and legs and was under the guard, [but]he broke the chain and evil spirit drove him into the clear places.

nama: Yesum yémotai, "Fene yétkwén efe yém?"
bt: Yesu asked him, "Who(What) is your name?"

nama: A yémofem yamnendayénd fá férghe, "Yénd yau so yéngém kétán kémboghofnar yékwét."
bt: And they begged him repeatedly not to order that, 'We will not go to into the never ending hold.'

nama: Tárfár mérén kimb taménjnawét mband tunakéf téndon. Yaufi mbérmbéram yamnendayénd Yesu ménát élaughét yéfefaf féyo wafté ewaramai. 33 Njam yaufi mbérmbér nétoryénd árta, fá nélawayénd kimbafefaf, a néngengoyénd ngewonawét sawot a kolitotawét.
bt: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the mountainside. Evil spirits begged Yesu to let them go into them, and he gave them power(permission). 33 When evil spirits came out of the man, they went to the pigs, and they rushed down steep bank to the lake and was drowned.

nama: Njam kimb wanjengégh téfnáram yinjoyénd némé yam kufrotau, fá nétonaménd a nafroyénd yéné tesenan a witmé karéf yuta. 35 A nuwanoyénd fandaghét némé kufrotau. Fá njam engmorménd kétán Yesuefaf, yémofem yinjoyénd ár ende tambén yaufi mbérmbér ewifnam témorangérwén Yesuene kafkaffaf. A umaneghkaf témorwén a mer mwighafé. Wénde fá férfér tamorwén. 36 Yémofem efe yinjoyénd ewafroyénd árét ndernáye yaufi mbérmbér wasérmboghkaf ár mer namndai. 37 Kétamé Gerasenes méinyotio árém Yesu yamnendayénd sái tafrangongai, ménamén fá férféram ewifoi. Féyo fá boutan nuflayénd álighét.
bt: When the men looking for the pigs saw what had happened, they ran off and told this in the town and contryside. 35 And they came to see what had happened. When they came to Yesu, they found the man from whom evil spirits had gone out, staying at Yesu's feet. And he dressed and was with the straight mind. But they were afraid. 36 Those who had seen it told to the people how evil spirit possessed man had been became good(healed). 37 All the people from the Gerasanes begged Yesu to leave them, because they were full of fear. So they got into the bout to leave.

nama: Yéné ár kétándmé ende tambén yaufi mbérmbér tawifnau, yémon yamnendai Yesu yémafé álighét, wénde Yesum yéngwatembai ndené siafé, 39 "Ngangota méngotuot a tawaufro nderé ndimbal Ngánjánam kémawafrote feyot." Féyo ár nuwanoi, a kafrotau tesenamé nderé ndimbal Yesum yawafrotam yáyot. Kér mérés a enjnefé amaf
bt: That man from whom the evil spirits gone out, he begged Yesu to go with him, but Yesu sent him with talk that, 39 "Return to the village and tell how much God has done for you." So the man went away and told through the city how much Yesu had done for him. A dead girl and a sick woman

nama: Yesu njam néngangotam, ár mérnam yumyoyénd, ménamén yémofem méghméghafé tanjetawét. 41 Ár yétkwén Jairus, wáwefnogh méngo ndimbal ár yénmormén a nénuyai Yesuene kafkafot, yamnendai álighét yánjo méngot 42 ménamén yáne féfét mérés, 12 yunjéf krotro témorwén.
bt: When Yesu went back, a crowd welcomed, because they were waiting for him with expecting. 41 The man name Jairus, a meeting house head man came and fell at Yesu's feet, begged him to go to his house 42 because his only daughter, [a girl] 12 years old was dying.

nama: A amaf kété témorwén efe férék kertau 12 yunjéf, wénde emon yau yéfrendai. 44 Fá sémta yénmormén a yiyánjai Yesuene sáláme fétfétro férék táf nékmboi. 45 Yesu namotai, "Emo wiyánja?" Njam fá méinyotio nawanyotayénd, Fité norayai, "Yuré, ár mérnam némarendangi a néngsotat fém."
bt: And a woman was there who had been bleeding for 12 years, but no one heal her. 44 She came him from backside and touched the edge of Yesu's clothes that time her bleeding stopped. 45 Yesu asked, "Who touched me?" When they all denied it, Fitè said, "Lord, the crowd are surrounding and pressing you."

nama: Wénde Yesu norayai, "Ámb árom té wiyánja. Yénd mat náyáton wafté té néngférém ta tambén."
bt: But Yesu said, "Someone has touched me. I know [my]power has gone out from me."

nama: Njam amaf yénmormén fandaghofnar a mat siofnar, férférafé ténmorwén nénuyai yáne kafkafot. Méinyotio árfene sáfréré, yémon yémndai némamén yémon yiyánjai a ndernáye fá táfandio frengégham yanai. 48 Ndenaye Yesum yémdayéng, "Mérés, fenjo ndimbal mwighayam mer niyáto. Kuwano mer so ném."
bt: When the woman came without watching man and without notice, she had a fear and knelt down at his feet. In front of all the people, she told why she had touched him and how she had been healed at once. 48 Then Yesu said to her, "Daughter, your big mind(faith) has healed you. Go in peace."

nama: Njamandio Yesu mé korayau, ámb ár yénmormén Jairusene méngota, efe témormén áumengégh méngo ndimbal ár. Yémon yémndai, "Fene mérés kér tém. Yau yawérsota watamegh ár nde nayu." 50 Yesu njam náyárai yéné, fá yémndai Jairus, "Yau férfér ném. Efalo tiyáto a so mer káyátongé." 51 Njam fá nufaryéng Jairusene méngot, yémon yau efe yumyoi yémafé álighét fáro Fité, Jon, Jems a ambumoe yárefé a yátmé. 52 Njamandio méinyotio ár kendawét a kutnawét ghérghérsisé tamorwén yáfaf. Yesum emndayai, "Yau nendati, yau kér tém wénde kémégh yékém." 53 Yémofem turtawét ménamén fá mat tamorwén ndené kér tém. 54 Wénde yerayéng ngángayan a yémndai, "Tane ambum, kano!" 55 Yáne mbérmbér nénangoi, táfandio nénokayoi. Féyo Yesum emndai njamke taraménd nénet. 56 Yárefaf sélngwél emormén, wénde Yesum emndayéng yau nafrotati némé kufrote.
bt: While Yesu was still speaking, someone came from house of Jairus, who was meeting house head man. He said, "Your daughter has died. Don't bother the teaching man anymore." 50 When Yesu heard this, he said to Jairus, "Don't be afraid. Believe and she will be good." 51 When he arrived at the Jairus' house, he didn't permit anyone to go with him except Fitè, Jon and Jems and her father and her mother. 52 While all the people were crying and shouted with sadness for her(moured for her). Yesu said, "Don't cry, she is not dead but asleep." 53 They laughed at him because she was dead. 54 But he took her by the hand and said, "My child, get up!" 55 Her spirit returned, immediately she stood up. Then Yesu told them to give the food. 56 Her parents were surprised, but he ordered them not to report [anyone] what had happened.

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum eumendai yáne 12 waitogh ár mérne a ewarametam wafté a muyé ménát yaufi mbérmbér étorghét a enjneta mer yátoghét. 2 A fá ewáfáretai Ngánjánene limánéghmén si waitoghét a enjne téfnár frengéghét 3 Yémon emndai, "Némé yau álighan tawanangi-yau wétu, yau yáf, yau njamke, yau mani, yau ámb awalogh sáláme. 4 Árké méngo njam kélawangi, kétémiya emor kétánotio yéné tesen téfrangongi. 5 Árém yau njam taumyongi, kafkaf ngwérméng tasénangi táf ténfrangongi yéfene tesen, awéfarogh so yém yéfeyot njam Ngánjánene nékw kufarngé ménamén yáne si yékenayénd." 6 Féyo fá náfrendaménd a tayotau méinyotio méngotuamé, Ngánjánene mer si táitotawét a ár tangéfrendawét méinyotio sifayan.
bt: One day Yesu called his 12 following men together and he gave them power and authority to cast out evil spirits and to heal diseases. 2 And he sent them to preach about ruling of God and to heal the sick. 3 He told them, "Don't take anything in journey- no staff, no bag, no food, no money, no extra clothes. 4 Which house you enter, stay in there until you leave that town. 5 If people do not welcome you, shake the dust from your feet, must leave their town, [as] a it will be warning to them as God's punishement comes because they rejected his talk. 6 So they left and went from the village to the village, proclaiming God's good talk and healing people in every places.

nama: Erod limánégh ár náyárai méinyotio némé kufrotawét. A fá kéwérsotawét ménamén ámb korayawét, "Yéné siyém Jon kér tambén nénano," 8 ámb kemnawét, "Elaija nufar," a ámb kemnawét nde ámb tándáwár Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár nénango ghérsat. 9 Wénde Erod norayai, "Yéndon emndayén Jonene sénko néfghét. Efe yéné yém yénd tárfár si náyáretan yánemén?" A fá ménde témorwén sái tinjongai Yesu. Yesum emanjnam 5,000 ár
bt: Erod the ruler heard [about] all what was happening. And he worried because some were saying, 'This is Jon who had been raised from the dead,' 8 other people thought, 'Elaija appeared,' and other people thought that one of the God's people who make know(prophets) of long ago had come back to life. 9 But Erod said, "I had Jon's head cut off. Who is this I hear many talks about him?" And he wanted to see Yesu. Yesu fed 5,000 people

nama: Njam Yesuene muyafé ár nénangoyénd, yémofem nafroyénd Yesuefaf némé tawafrotawét. Yémon engwasroi a fá néngwanoi enaghét tesen yétkwén Betsaidat. 11 Wénde tárfár ár mat náyátoyénd yénémamén a yaitoyénd fá. Yémon eumyoi a emndayeng Ngánjánene limánéghmén a efrendayéng fá efe frengégh ménde tamorwén.
bt: When the men with Yesu's authority(apostles) returned, they told to Yesu what they had done. He took them and they went to rest to the town called Betsaida. 11 But many people knew about it and followed him. He welcomed them and spoke to them about the ruling of God and healed them who needed healing.

nama: Seitefifi 12 waitogh ár enmormén Yesuefaf a yémndayénd, "Tawafarna ár mérén, fá so néngangotat kaka méngotuot a téndo yuwot ménát njamke a kémégh sifayat áronjaghét, ménamén yénd yéném yauyau sifayan."
bt: Late in the afternoon 12 following men came to him and said, "Send the crowd, they will go back to the close village and countryside in order to find food and sleeping place, because we came in remote place here.

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, "Fémofem némndafi tawaramangi nénet yéfeyot."
bt: He answered to them, "You(plural) give them something to eat."

nama: Yéné ár tamorwén 5,000nit. Wénde emndai yánjo waitogh ár, "Kámnjongi mérén tawafrotangi 50nit." 15 Féyo waitogh árém yafrotaménd, a méinyotio ár námnjotaménd. 16 Yesum euyoi 5 féskaf flawé a sómbio wagif, a néwewoi njafar tukét, kánjo yaramai a etfotam. Féyo eramai waitogh árfefaf tawarametawét árét. 17 Méinyotio árém enetaménd eremngoi, a waitogh árém ekafayénd 12 yáf afoghare njamke mbarmbar árkéméné ninamoyénd. Fité kwayaghé noraye nde Yesu siyém Keriso
bt: There were about(?) 5,000 men. But he said to his following men, "Have them sit down groups make about(?) 50 each." 15 So following men did, and all the people sat down. 16 Yesu took 5 breads and two fish, and looked up the heaven, gave thanks and broke them. Then he handed them to the following men to give to the people.

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum efrangoi ár mérén Ngánjánafé oraighét yambia a yánjo waitogh árfé tamorwén, yémon emotai fá, "Efe mérén korayawét yénd efe wém?"
bt: One day Yesu left the crowd for talking with God alone and his diciples were with him, he asked them, "Who do people say I am?"

nama: Yémofem yawangoyénd, "Ámb korayawét Jon walitogh ár, ámb árém korayawét Elaija, wénde ámb árém korayawét ndené tándáwár ámb Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár kénangote ghérsat."
bt: They answered, "Some say Jon the baptist, other people say Elaija, but other people say one of God's man who make know(prophet) of long ago has come back to life."

nama: Yémon emotai, "Wénde fém wét? Fém korayangi yénd efe wém?" Fitam yawangoi, "Ngánjánene Keriso." Yesu korayau yánjo kérmén
bt: He asked them, "But what about you? Who do you say I am?" Fitè answered, "God's Keriso." Yesu spoke about his death

nama: Yesum táikér si tawawéngsotau yéfe ende yau taufrongi yénémamén. 22 A fá norayai, "Ároene Ambumom so fété kérté yéngfande tárfár yaman a yékarotat sénko árém, limánégh ghéngén wárogh árém a nák watamegh árém, a fá so fété yésmetat a nambio efogh soramé Ngánjánam so tanongé ghérsat."
bt: Yesu strictly warned them not to tell about this to anyone. 22 And he said, "The Son of Man must suffer many things and the head people, the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) and the teaching men of the law rejected him, and they surely kill and after the three day he will rise to life."

nama: Féyo yémo emndayeng méinyotio: "Njam efe waitoghét kénufngo ta soramé, fá so fété kakenangé fáyo a tanangé yánjoyo wafatarégh wén méinyotio efghon a kwénmaitongé yénd. 24 Efe ménde njam yém yánjo ghérsé wanjarghét so yétarne, wénde emo njam yáne ghérsé yétarna tanemén so tanjarngé. 25 A nderé mer yém ároe sái tanangé méinyotio mband marat, wénde yétarna o nawasérmbota fáyo? 26 Njam wáiwáiafé efe yém tanemén a tane simén, Ároene Ambum so yém wáiwái yánemén njam fá nénangota yáne ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún a Yárefane ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún a kétándmé njúnnjúnofnar njafar si résagh ár. 27 Yéndon efalo ewaufrotan, ámb efe ewakai yéna, yémofem so yéfandati Ngánjánene limánégh soramé so kér em." Nambio waitogh árém yinjoyénd Yesuene ndimbal kwayaghé
bt: Then he said to them all: "Whoever start to follow me, they must deny themselves and take their cross every day and follow me. 24 Whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life because of me will save it. 25 And how good is it someone to gain the whole world, but lose or destroy themselves? 26 Whoever is with shame about me and my talk, the Son of Man will be with shame of him when he comes back in his strong light(big light) and in the big light(glory) of his Father and of the without.dust(holy) men carrying heavenly message(angels). 27 I tell you the truth, some who are standing here, they will see the ruling of God before they will die." Three following men saw Yesu's big light(glory)

nama: 8 efogh soramé Yesum ewasroi Fité, Jon a Jems yémafé néngwanoyénd mband tunakfot Ngánjánafé koraiwét. 29 Njamandio fá Ngánjánafé koraiwét, yáne sáf natambnai, a yáne sáláme náyátoi mé kwayagharo nálálai toyanit. 30 Sómbio ár, Moses a Elaija, ndimbal kwayaghé nufaryéng, Yesufé korayawét. 31 Yémofem korayawét yáne álighmén, árké témorwén Ngánjánene ménde so kufrotau sái kér témorwén Jerusalemén. 32 Fité a yáne mérén kémégh kér tákmangéwén, wénde njam fá nanoyénd, yémofem yinjoyénd yáne ndimbal kwayaghé a sómbio ár tawakayarwén yémafé. 33 Njamandio Moses a Elaijafem téfrangowét Yesu, Fitam yémndai, "Yuré, mer nérete fémafé sái yéna yéném. So erametam nambio méngo, ámbiro feyot, ámbiro Mosese a ámbiro Elaija." Fá mafina témorwén némamén korayau.
bt: 8 days later Yesu took Fitè, Jon and Jems with him [and] went [up] on a mountain to talk with God. 29 While he was talking with God, his face changed [its appearance], and his clothes became like as bright as lightening. 30 Two men, Moses a Elaija, appeared with big light(glory), [and they] talked with Yesu. 31 They spoke about his journey, which he would fulfill God's wanting by dying in Jerusalem. 32 Fitè and his group were very sleepy, but when they woke up, they saw his big light(glory) and two men standing with him. 33 As Moses and Eliaja were about to leave Yesu, Fitè said to him, "Lord, it looks good we are here with you. We will make three house, one for you, one for Moses and for Elaija." He didn't know what he said.

nama: Njamandio fá korayau, njafar kwamkwam nufaryéng a fá engwafaryéng, a fá férfér tamorwén njam ewafaryéng njafar kwamkwaman. 35 Si kém yénmormén njafar kwamkwamta, nénorayai, "Yéné siyém tane ambum, efe yéndon yaferyén, yiyáretati fá." 36 Si njam nefoi, Yesuro yinjoyénd yambia. Endefet ámb yau ewafroyénd yéné efghon némé yémofem yinjoyénd. Yesum yéfrendai yaufi mbérmbérafé ambum
bt: While he was speaking, a cloud appeared and covered them, and they were afraid when they entered the cloud. 35 A voice came from the cloud, said, "This is my son, whom I have chosen, listen to him." 36 When talk finished, they saw Yesu alone. They did not tell anyone at that time what they saw. Yesu Healed a child with evil spirit

nama: Ámb efghon, njam fá kénangotawét mband tunakéfta, ndimbal mérnam yéfángoyénd. 38 Ár nutnai mérén kénjúta, "Watamegh ár, yéndon némamnendan ménát tane ambum so tinjo, ménamén tane yénaro ambum fiyém. 39 Yaufi mbérmbéram yarsote fá a sunsune kutnangé, yémo tétarmangé táikram yafrote a féfokam ewétote táf sénfneta. Yaufi mbérmbéram yau téfrangongé fá mé yasérmbote. 40 Yéndon ewamnendan fene waitogh ár ménát yaufi mbérmbér witérmboghét, wénde fá féyotarofnar em."
bt: The next day, when they came back from the mountain, a large crowd met him. 38 A man cried out from the crowd, "Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, because he is my only child. 39 The evil spirit attacked him with a sudden shout, he throws him make strict and make him to foam at the mouth. The evil spirit does not leave and kept on destroying him. 40 I begged your following men to drive it out, but they are impossible [to do]."

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, "Fém ndimbal mwighafnar a áyáréghofnar ár, nderé teke fémafé so yénmor a ewamarotan fém? Ténémnjo fene ambum yénan."
bt: Yesu answered, "You without big mind(faith) and without hearing people, how long must I stay with you and endure you? Bring your son here."

nama: Njamandio ambum ténmorwén, yaufi mbérmbéram yitam mbandot kosénau. Wénde Yesum yaufi mbérmbér yifnai, yéfrendai ambum a yangoi yárefafaf. 43 A fá sélngwél emormén Ngánjánene ndimbal yam wafroghan. Yesu korayau yánjo kérmén yénamayo
bt: While the child was coming, the evil spirit threw him to the ground twisting. But Yesu rebuked the evil spirit, healed the child and gave him back to his father. 43 And they were amazed at God's big things doing. Yesu spoke about his death a second time

nama: "Mer kéráye káyárangi yéndon némé waufroghét féfe ewawafngon. Ároene Ambum so sén yérametat árfene ngángat." 45 Wénde fá yau mat náyátoyénd némé tésérmbnau. Ménamén ténoghe témorwén yéfefaf, fá yau mat náyátoyénd, a fá férfér tamorwén ámoghét yáfaf yénémamén. Efe so yém ndimbal
bt: "Listen carefully to what I am about to tell you. The Son of Man is going to be gave teeth(betrayed) into the hands of men." 45 But they did not understand what is meaning. Because it was secret to them, they did not know it, and they were afraid to ask him about it. Who will be big

nama: Yáne waitogh ár mwighayan ásnanéghan tamorwén efe so yém ndimbal. 47 Wénde Yesu mat náyátoyéng yéfene mwighé, féyo yémon yakayoi némbnar ambum tosé kété yá téndon. 48 Féyo yémon emndai fá, "Emo njam tumyongé yéné némbnar ambum tosé tane yétkwénan kumyongé yénd, a emofem yénd umyotat fá kumyongé emo wémáfáretam. Efe némbne yém féfefaf méinyotio fá siyém ndimbal."
bt: His following men were arguing who will be big. 47 But Yesu knew their thoughts, so he had a little child stand there beside him. 48 Then he said to them, "Whoever welcomes this little child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me. Who is little among all of you, he is big."

nama: Jon norayai, "Yuré, yéndfem ár yinjongém yaufi mbérmbér tawifnau fene yétkwénan a yéta tékmbotawém, ménamém fá yau téfene yém."
bt: Jon said, "Lord, we saw the man driving out evil spirits in your name and we tried to stop him, because he is not one of us."

nama: Yesum emndai, "Yau yékmbotati ménamén emo njam yau nékarota fá siyém téfeta." Samariamén árém yékenayénd Yesu
bt: Yesu said, "Don't stop him because whoever is not against us, he is for us." Samaria people rejected Yesu

nama: Yesuene efogh kaka kénamndetau angoghét njafarot, Yesum sotafé mwighé yékmai álighét Jerusalemét. 52 Yémon engwáfáretam si résagh ár frondeyo, efe engmormén Samaria méngotuot rokar frengéghét yáyot, 53 wénde árém kété yau umyoyénd fá, ménamén fá tiotau Jerusalemét. 54 Njam waitogh ár Jems a Jon yinjeyayénd yéné, yémofem yémotangeyayénd, "Yuré, fém ménde ném yénd fésot kutnangém njafarot yéfe wasérmboghét." 55 Wénde Yesu nawengoi ewárnam fá. 56 A fá néwanoyénd ámb méngotuot. Efalo Yesuene waitogh ár
bt: Yesu's day became close to go back to the heaven, Yesu strongly decided to travel to Jerusalem. 52 He sent messengers on ahead, who went to a Samaria village to prepare things for him, 53 but the people there did not welcome him, because he was going to Jerusalem. 54 When the following men Jems and Jon saw this, they asked, "Lord, do you want us to shout to the heaven fire down to destroy them?" 55 But Yesu turned and rebuked them. 56 And they went to another village. Real following men/followers of Yesu

nama: Fá njam endmé tangmormén árom yémndai Yesu, "Yéndon so némaitotan árkamé fém njam niota."
bt: As they were walking along the road, a man said to Yesu, "I will follow you wherever you go."

nama: Yesum yawangoyéng, "Ághéághafene féne té em a menafene njén té em, wénde Ároene Ambumoene sifayé yau yém kéméghét a enághét."
bt: Yesu answered, "Wild dogs have holes and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to sleep and to rest."

nama: Yémon yémndai ámb ár, "Kwénmaito yénd." Wénde árém yawangoi, "Yuré, yénd so fronde nangotan méngot kétán tane afé kér njam yém yéndon so yiyátotan soramé yéndon so kémaiton."
bt: He said to another man, "Follow me." But man answered, "Lord, first I will go back to home. After my father dies I will bury him, after I will foloow you."

nama: Yesum yémndai fá, "Kér ár yémofem so eyátotat efe em mbérmbére kérwén, wénde fém nguwano Ngánjánene limánéghmén si waitoghét."
bt: Yesu said to him, "Those who are spiritually dead will bury the dead, but you go and proclaim talk of the ruling of God."

nama: Wéi ámb norayai, "Yéndon so kémaiton fém, Yuré, wénde so fronde néngangotan tane mérén watembghét."
bt: Another still said, "I will follow you, Lord, but first let me go back to my family to say goodbye."

nama: Yesum yawangoyéng, "Emo tarégh ásáfogh térnyangé a néreta sémta, fá féyotarofnar yém Ngánjánene limánégh ásáfoghét."
bt: Yesu answered, "Anyone who starts to work to plow and looks back, he is not able to work for the ruling of God."

nama: Yéné soramé Yuram ewaferyéng 72 ámb waitogh ár a sómbiosómbioe ewáfáretam méinyotio tesenot a yuwot ándan fá álighét ménde témorwén. 2 Yémon emndai, "Wau yu njamke téfén tarégh siyém tárfár, wénde ásáfogh ár em nambio. Yénémaménnde témndangi wau yuwan njamke téfén Yuré, so ewáfárete ásáfogh ár yáne njamke téfén ndawot. 3 Kuwanongiǃ Yéndon fém ewáfáretan ndené wáutáréghofnar tosé sifnit yéfe kénjún munjan ághé. 4 Yau ersati mani yáf a áwogh yáf. Ámb sandol yau ersati. Yau teke árfé asneghét endén.
bt: After this the Lord chose 72 other following men and sent them two by two to every town and place where he wanted to go. 2 He told them, "Harvest season original food are ready to Dig are many but workers are few. Therefore ask the Lord of the harvest in the harvesting season, to send out workers into the origin food field. 3 Go! I am sending you out like helpless baby sheeps(lambs) among wild dogs. 4 Do not take money bag and traveling bag. Do not take other sandols, do not [spend] long [time] to greet with people on the road.

nama: "Njam fém kélawangi méngon fronde korayangi, 'Frengégh yém yéné méngon.' 6 Yéné frengégh ár njam kété yém, féfene frengégh so nene yáfaf, yau njam, féfefaf so kénangongé. 7 Kété táf emor yéné méngon, njamke nu némé njam ewarametat nénet, ménamén ásáfogh árém táf ersat yéfenjo fai. Yau nusanati wélafé méngomé.
bt: When you enter a house first say, 'Blessed to this house.' 6 If this blessing is [with] man there, your blessing will rest on him, if not, it will return to you. 7 Stay that house, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers should take their pay. Do not move around from house to house.

nama: "Njam tesenot kélawangi a taumyongi, enetati féfeyot némé em frengéghkaf. 9 Enjne téfnár mer eyátotati efe kété em ewaufrotati ndené, 'Ngánjánene limánégh siyém kaka yém féfefaf.' 10 Wénde njam tesenot kélawangi a yau taumyongi, endmé kuwanongi a korayangi, 11 'Féfene tesenmén ngwérméng nakayongi téfene kafkafan, yéndfem ewainangém féfe matot. Wénde nawafarotati yénémamén: Ngánjánene limánégh kaka yém.' 12 Yéndon ewaufrotan, falogh efghon Sodommén némbnare yénmormén wénde minde tukén falogh so yém yéné tesenan.
bt: When you enter a town and are welcomed, eat what is ready to you. 9 Heal the sick who are there and tell them that, 'The ruling of God is near to you.' 10 But when you enter a town and they don't welcome you(are not welcomed), go into the road and say, 11 'We wipe the dust of your town [that] stick to our fee, we give warning to you. But remember about this: The ruling of God has been near.' 12 I tell you, it comes little on the judging day for Sodom, but there will be very high judgement in that town.

nama: "Nderé yaufi so yém féfeyot, Korasinǃ Nderé yaufi so yém féfeyot, Betsaidaǃ Yéné sélngwélafé yam árké kufrotawét féfefaf té kufrotawét kété Taire a Sidonén, fá fiya kawengongayénd tándái, fá tamorangérwén saghafé a sakwéfafé. 14 Falogh efghon Taire a Sidon yéfene tékrat so yém minde tukén, wénde féfene tékratam so taindangé yéfene tékrat. 15 A fém, Káfenam, so kuyongi njafarot? Yau, fém so keworngi kémboghofnar yékwét."
bt: How bad will be to you, Korasin! How bad will be to you, Betsaida! For if the special workings(miracles) were did to you had been did in Taire and Sidon, they would have turn away long ago, sitting with sackcloth and ashes. 14 On the judgement day Taire and Sidon, their punishment will be very big, but your.p punishment will surpass their punishment. 15 And you, Kapenam, will you lift to the heaven? No, you will go down to never ending hole(hell).

nama: Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, "Emo fém njam niyáreta, yénd yémon so wiyárete, Njam emo fém nékena, yénd yémon wékene, wénde emo wékene yénd, fá yékene emo yénd wénmáfáretam."
bt: Yesu said to his following men, "Whoever listens to you, he listens to me, but whoever rejects you, he rejects me, but whoever rejects me, he rejects him who sent me."

nama: 72 waitogh ár kénangotawét áuwéghafé fá nafroyénd, "Yuré, yaufi mbérmbér mé kénamnyongayénd téfefaf fene yétkwénan."
bt: 72 following men returned with joy and they told, "Lord, even evil spirits obeyed us in your name."

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, "Yéndon yinjon Sáifér nénuyai toyanit njafarta. 19 Káyárangiǃ Yéndon muyé ewaramayénd álighét kamban a ndérkamé yátmé a waingéghét mék téfnárfene wafté, némé yam yau so némaghne. 20 Wénde yau náuwétati ménamén yaufi mbérmbér namnyongi féfefaf, wénde áuwégh féfeyot ménamén féfene yétkwén ekai njafaran."
bt: Yesu answered, "I saw Sáifèr fall like lightening from heaven. 19 Listen, I gave you authority to step on snakes and scopions and to overcome the power of the enemy, anything will not hurt you. 20 But do not rejoice because evil spirit submit you, but it is joy for you because your name are written in heaven."

nama: Yéné efghon, Ngánján Mbérmbéram Yesu áuwéghe yafoi norayai, "Yéndon némuwetan fém, Afé, Yuré njafarmén a mbandmén, ménamén fémon némé etnoi yéné yam kétándmé mer mwighé ár a matyam ár tambén wénde sáfét tawakmetau kor matyamofnar árét ambumnit. Néméwét, Afé, yéné témorwén fene mer frengéghafé.
bt: At that day the God Spirit filled with joy to Yesu and he(Yesu) said, "I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and intelligent, but revealed them to without-knowledge/intelligent men like children. Yes, Father, this was with your good blessing.

nama: "Méinyotio yam enramai tafaf tane Afam. Efe ámb yau mat yém Ambumoenemén mé Afaro, a efe ámb yau mat yém efe Afé yém mé Ambumro a fáro efe Ambumom ewaferyéng yá uyafoghét."
bt: My Father has given me all things. No one knows the Son only the Father [knows], and no one knows who the Father is only Son [knows] and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him."

nama: Féyo Yesu nawengoi yánjo waitogh árfefaf emndai yéfembiaye, "Frengégh yém yéfeyot emofem yinjoyénd fémofem némé yéfandati. 24 Ménamén yéndo emndetan ndené tárfár Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a warsagh ár ménde em fandaghét fémofem némé yinjongi, wénde yau yinjoyénd, a náyárangi fémofem némé náyáretati, wénde yau náyárayénd." Rengerenge si mer Samariamén ároenemén
bt: Then Yesu turned to his following men and said privately, "Blessed are them who see what you see. 24 For I tell you that many God's people who make know(prophets) and ruling men(kings) wanted to see what you see, but they didn't see, and to hear what you hear but didn't hear it." The parable of the good Samari man

nama: Ámb efghon ámb nák watamegh ár nokayoi Yesue ufoghét a yémotai, "Watamegh ár, yéndon némé so yafrotan efalo tuot ghérsé résaghét?"
bt: One day a teaching man of the law stood up to test Yesu and asked, "Teacher, what I must to do to receive the eternal life?"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Némé si yékai nák kénjún? Fémo ndernáye yafene?"
bt: Yesu answered, "What is written in the law? How do you interpret it?"

nama: Yémo yawangoi, "'Yuwetang Yuré fene Ngánján méinyotio fene tikfe, méinyotio fene mbérmbére, méinyotio fene waftaye, méinyotio fene mwighaye,' a 'Yuweta fenjo kaka ár ndenéyameyo fémyo ndernáye náuwete.'"
bt: He answered, "'Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your spirit, with all your power and with all your mind' and 'Love your neighbor like as yourself how do you love.'"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Efalo, yéné yafrota a fém so nakete."
bt: Yesu answered, "Correct, do this and you will live."

nama: Wénde fá ménde témorwén fáyo sái kauyafongai, féyo yémo Yesu yémotai, "A efe yém tane fútar?"
bt: But he wanted to justify himself, so he asked Yesu, "And who is my neighbor?"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Ár nuwanoi Jerusalemta kétán Jeriko, endén yésmetamén oro téfnáram. Yémofem sáláme yéfitérnaménd, yésmetaménd yéfrangoyénd krotro fá nuwanoyénd táf, 31 ghéngén wárogh ár téngmorwén yéné endmayo wénde njam yinjoi ár, yamnjai téndomé. 32 Yéné endmayo Lefi mérénmén ár ténmorwén yinjoi fá yamnjai téndomé. 33 Wénde Samariamén ár nénwanoi yéné endmayo njam yinjoi karémbkarémb a tikfan ghérghérsisé nawerai. 34 Fá néwanoi yáfaf, tiyátotau olif wékére a wéri nue, tumbwetau ngifafmé. Féyo yémon yéfayoi yánjo ndonkiyan a térsau fá kétán yánjo wanjengégh méngot a tanjetau. 35 Ámb efghon yémo sómbio silfé mani mbwe yaramai méngo wanjengégh téfnárai. Fá noyarai, 'Yanjeta fá, yénd njam nénangotan, fémon njam tárfár mani etarna, yéndon so kémawasongén.' |SPP-231.tif|col||Nan Pollard © 1998 Standard Publishing.|Mer Samaria ár (Luk 10.31-32)|10.31-32
bt: Yesu answered, "A man went to from Jerusalem to Jeriko, on the road robbers attacked him. They stripped him of clothes, beat him and leave him almost died and went away, 31 A burning sacrifice men was going that road but when he saw the man, he passed by through the other side. 32 Like this a man belong to Lefi family came and saw him, passed by the outside. 33 But Samari man was going like as, when he saw half dead [man] and he felt pity for him. 34 He went to him, painted on olive oil and alcohol, bandaged wounds. Then he put him on his donkey and brought him to an inn and looked after him. 35 The next day he gave two silver coins them to the house manager. He said, 'Look after him, when I return, if you use many money, I will pay you.

nama: "Yéné nambio mérénta fém ndernáye nemne efe fútar yém yáyot efe oro téfnáram yésmetaménd?"
bt: Which of these three do you think become a neighbor to the man who was striked by robbers?

nama: Nák watamegh árom yawangoi, "Yéné ár fiyém emon ghérghérsisafé yinjoi." Yesum yémndai, "Nguwano a yénéyamyo yafrota." Mata a Meri
bt: The teaching man of the law answered, "The man who had mercy to him." Yesu told him, "Go and do like this." Mata and Meri

nama: Yesu a yánjo waitogh ár tanmorwén néndkéfan, nufaryénd méngotuot, amaf yétkwén Mata Yesu yumyoi yánjo méngot. 39 Matane yánngén yétkwén Meri efe námnjoi Yurane kafkaffaf tiyáretau fá némé korayau. 40 Wénde Mata mwighayam tawérsotau ásáfogh tárfármén. Féyo Mata yénmormén Yesuefaf yémotai, "Yuré, fém mat ném Merim yénd té wéfrango tambia ásáfoghan? Ténémnde ta wáutárghét."
bt: Yesu and his following men were on the way, they arrived to the village, woman name Mata welcomed Yesu to her house. 39 Mata's small sister name Meri who sat the Lord's feet listening to what he said. 40 But Mata worried about her many works. So Mata came to Yesu and asked, "Lord, you know Meri has left me to do the work by myself? Tell her to help me!" 41 The Lord answered, "Mata, Mata, you are worried and made a trouble to mind about many things, 42 but one thing is necessary. Meri has chosen what is good, and it will not be taken way from her."

nama: Ámb efghon Yesu Ngánjánjafé koraiwét fénaté sifayan. Njam yifoi, ámb yánjo waitogh árém yémndai fá, "Yuré, ténmatameta Ngánjánafé orayégh, ndené fété Joném ndernáye tawatametau yánjo waitogh ár."
bt: One day Yesu was talking with God in a different/certain place. When he finished, one of his following men said to him, "Lord, teach us to talk with God, just as Jon taught his following men." 2 Yesu said to them, "When you talk with God, say like: Father, Be honored your without.dust(holy) name, your kingdom will come.

nama: Méinyotio efghon njamke so yénmaramete.
bt: Will give us food in every day.

nama: Kénmawalindangé téfene yaufi, ménamén yénd yéfene yaufi yénmawalinda emofem ewafrotat téfefaf. A yau yénd yénérsa ufoghan."
bt: Forget us our sins, because we forget their sins those who did against us. And do not bring us in temptation."

nama: Féyo Yesum emndai fá, "Fém ámb njam ném fútarafé, a fá nuwanoi yáfaf sémbár néndkéfan a norayai, 'Fútar, kwaram nambio féskaf flawé, 6 ménamén tane fútar té téném nayuta tafaf, a yéndon némé ámb yau téfrendan yáyot!'
bt: Then Yesu said to them, "If one [of you] have a friend, and you should go to him at midnight and say, 'Friend, give me three loaves of flawè, 6 because my friend has come to me from long distance, and I have no [food] to prepare for him!'

nama: "Efe kénjún témorwén yawangoi, 'Yau wawérsota. Sésafne siyém manéghkaf yém, tane ambumtértér a yénd kémégh yénkém. Yénd yau so kanon a némé yau so kémaraman.' 8 Yéndon némndetan fém, fá yau njam kanongé a kémaramangé féskaf flawé ménamén fém ném yáne fútar, wénde fá so kanongé a kémaramangé méinyotio fém némat ménde ném, fém yau wáiwái ném ménamén mé sái nómnenda.
bt: Who was inside answered, 'Don't bother me. The door is already locked, my children and I went to sleep. I can't get up and give you.' 8 I tell you, if he will not get up and give a bread because you are his friend, but he will get up and give you all what you need, you are not shame because you continue to ask him.

nama: "Féyo yénd norayan féfefaf: Kamota a so kémaramangé; yéronja a so tinjo; náutána a feyot sésafne so kasawérngé. 10 Ménamén méinyotio ár emofem namotat so yanangi, méinyotio ár efe yéronjat so tinjongi; fá efe náutánat, sésafne so kasawérngé.
bt: So I say to you: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door wll be opened to you. 10 Because every people who ask will receive, every people seek will find; the one who knocks, the door will be opened.

nama: "Fém afé, fenjo ambumom njam kénmamnendangé wagifot, némé so taram kamban? 12 O njam kénmamnendangé ghakér kowi mbweyot, némé so taram ndérkamé yátmé? 13 Féyo fém ndemamén yaufi ném, fém mat ném mer rokar ramégh fenjo ambumafet. Nderé mer yém minde tukén fene Afé njafaran tawaramangé Ngánján Mbérmbér yéfeyot efe nómnendat yáfafǃ" Yesu a Beljebab
bt: You father, if your child asks for fish, will you give him a snake, is it possible? 12 Or if he asked an egg, what will you give him a scorpion? 13 So even though you are evil, you know [how]to give good things to your child. How much more good will your Father in heaven give the God Spirit to those who beg him!" Yesu a Beljebab

nama: Ámb efghon Yesum yaufi mbérmbér yutoryéng babé ároeta, a njam yaufi mbémbéram yéfrangoi, ár efe babé témorwén si nufngoi. Ár mérén sélngwél emormén. 15 Wénde ámb ár norayayénd, "Yaufi mbérmbérafene sénko téfnároene yétkwénan Beljebab, Yesum yaufi mbérmbér eutone." 16 Ámb árém yufotam Yesu ámogh sie yémotayénd njafar tukmén yam wiyafoghét.
bt: One day Yesu drove out a evil spirit from mute man, and when evil spirit left, the man who was mute began to speak. The crowd was surprised. 15 But some of them said, "By name of Beljebab, the head man of evil spirit, Yesu is driving our evil spirits." 16 Others tested Yesu [and] they asked asking talk doing somethings from heaven.

nama: Yesu mat namndai yéfene mwighé, féyo emndai fá: "Árké yu afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot so kirorngé a árké méngo mérén afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot so kuyangé. 18 Njam Sáifér afaloghét kufngongé yambiayot, ndernáye yáne yu so yakai? Yéndon norayan yéné ménamén fém nemnati ndené Beljebabene yétkwénan yéndon kar yaufi mbérmbér eutonan. 19 Njam yéndo eutonan yaufi mbérmbér Beljebabene yétkwénan, wét endene yétkwénan eutonat fene waitogh árém? Fenjo waitogh árém efalo niyátotat fém yau mbarkánd ném. 20 Wénde njam yéndon eutonan yaufi mbérmbér Ngánján waftaye táf nde Ngánján limánégh yéna yém féfefaf.
bt: Yesu knew their thoughts, so he said to them: "Which country began to divide against itself will ruined, and which house began to divide against itself will fall down. 18 If Sáifér began to divide himself, how will his country stand? I say this because you think that I might cast out evil spirit by name of Beljebab. 19 If I cast out evil spirit by the name of Beljebab, by whose name your followers drive them? Your followers prove [that] you are not straight. 20 But if I cast out evil spirit by the power of God Spirit, then the ruling of God is here to you.

nama: "Njam muyafé ár yakai umaneghkaf yambwete yánjo méngo táf yáne rokar mer so em. 22 Wénde njam minde muyafé árom tésmangé a taindangé fá, yémon tangwanangé mék rokar árkéménayan yuré kamyotau a efaloi némé yémon ewanai oroe.
bt: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home when everything he owns are safe. 22 But if very strong man strikes and defeats him, he carries away the fight things which the owner was depending on and divides up what he stole.

nama: "Efe yau yéndfé yénre, fá siyém ta kenagh ár, a emon yénd yau sái wáutána umengan, fá so nironat.
bt: Who is not with me, he is man rejecting me, and who does not gather with me, he is scattering.

nama: "Yaufi mbérmbér njam kéntorngé ár tambén, fá nguwanongé fanfne yuwot sifayé ronjaghét enághét. Wénde yau njam sifayé tinjongé, so korayangé, 'Yénd so nangotan kétán árké méngota wénmormén.' 25 Njam kénangongé tinjongé méngo sámburéghkaf njúnnjúnofnar a mer frengéghkaf. 26 Féyo yaufi mbérmbér kuwanongé a tanwasrongé ámb 7 mbérmbér minde yaufi ndené fáyo, fá kélawangi a emor kété. A ároene ghérsé minde mámáfi kamndangé kétándmé fronde ndernáye témorwén."
bt: When evil spirit comes out from a man, it goes clear place to search for rest. But when it does not find, will say, 'I will go back to the house I lived.' 25 When it returns and finds house is swept clear and good prepared. 26 Then evil spirit goes and brings other 7 spirits more wicked than itself, they enter and live there. And man's life became more worse than before how he was.

nama: Yesum njam korayau yénémamén, amaf nutnayéng ár mérén kénjún, "Frengéghkaf amaf yém emo náráftam a endene nonoyan kanotau."
bt: When Yesu was saying these things, a woman called out [to him] in the crowd, "Blessed is the woman who gave birth to you and [let you] nurs at her breasts!"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Frengéghkaf fá em efe náyáretat Ngánjánene si a wafroghan tékmangi." Jonane yam ufarogh
bt: Yesu answered, "Blessed are those who hear God's talk and put it in practice." The surprising thing of Jona

nama: Njam ár mérén tárfár kénáyátotau, Yesu norayai, "Yéné yaufiyaufi ár mérén em. Fá namotat Ngánjánene wafroghyamot, wénde némé yau so tawaramangan mé Jonane ufarogh yamro. 30 Ndené Jona témorwén Ninéfa tesenmén árét yam ufarogh, ndené endeyo Ároene Ambum so wéi yém yam ufarogh yéné ár mérnat. 31 Sifa ndimbal yuoene warsagh amé so kokayongé moghét yéné ár mérnafefaf a so tawafalngonga ndené fá srem yaufiyamafé; ménamén yéné amaf yénmormén yu nayu Solomonene káfeté mwighé áyárghét, a ámb ár yéna yém yá tukén Solomon. 32 Ár Ninéfamén so kokayongi moghan yéné ár mérénfaf a so tafalngongi, ménamén fá nawengoyénd Jonane si waitoghan, a ámb ár yéna yém yá tukén Jona. Fifioene Kwayaghé
bt: When the crowds became many, Yesu said, "These are very wicked crowd. They are asking God's surprising thing, but I will not give anything [but] only the surprising thing of Jona. 30 As Jona was a surprising thing to Ninèfa people, like as the Son of Man will be a surprising thing to this people group. 31 The queen of the Sifa country will stand at the judgement this people group and will condemn them that they are sinner; because this woman came to Solomon from the long country. And one man are here big, over Solomon. 32 Ninèfa people will stand at the judgement to this people group and will condemn them because they turn away at Jona's preaching. And one man are here big over Jona. The lamp of the body

nama: "Árké árom kwayaghé yau so tékmangé kété ánde so yétno, o mbáfyáf mbanmbanan. Wénde yémon téfayongé yánjo fayogh sifayan, ménát efe njam eném yémofem kwayaghé so yéfandat. 34 Fene ghékéf sem kwayaghé em fene fifiot. Fene ghékéf njam mer ere, fene méinyotio fifi so yém kwayaghayan. Wénde njam mámáfi ere fene ghékéf, fene fifi so yém méinyotio kwéfkwéfan. 35 Kor fém aném ménát kwayaghé fene kénjún yau táf yém kwéfkwéf. 36 Féyo táf fene méinyotio fifi sái yém ndimbal kwayaghayan, ámb fifi kafe yau so yém kwéfkwéfan, so yém méinyotio kwayaghayan njam lambefane kwayaghé kátremngongé fefaf." Yesum táikér si tamndetau Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár.
bt: No one lights will not put where will be hidden, or under the big basket. But he put it on its stand, so that when [people] who come, they can see the light. 34 Your eyes are a light for your body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be in the light. But when your eyes are bad, your body will be in the darkness. 35 Be careful that the light in you should not darkness. 36 So if your body is in the light, any part of body will not be in the darkness, it will be full of light as when light of lamp shines to you. Yesu said strong talk Farasi men and teaching men of the law

nama: Yesum njam si yifoi Farasi árom yumyoi yémafe nénet. Féyo Yesu naflai a námnjoi nénet. 38 Wénde Farasi ár sélngwél yémormén ménamén ngángé umbarghofnar Yesum néne ewafngoi.
bt: When Yesu had finished speaking, a Farasi man invited him to eat with him. 38 But Farasi man came to surprise because Yesu began to eat without washing hands.

nama: Yuram yémndai Farasi ár, "Fém Farasi ár kafusi a sin faruro esénnati, wénde kénjún fém sem kwénjta a yaufi kér ro. 40 Fém afrengéghofnar árǃ Fém mat em emo faru yafrotam, wéi ndenáyo yafrotam kénjú? 41 Wénde tawaram némé enyau fene kénjún yauyau árét, méinyotio so em féfeyot njúnnjúnofnar.
bt: The Lord said to Farasi man, "You Farasi man clean the outside of the cup and pot, but inside of you are [full of] greed and wickedness. 40 You foolish men! [Didn't] you know the one who made the outside, also made inside? 41 But give away what is inside you to the poor, everything will be clean for you.

nama: "Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén féfene ramégh siyém Ngánjáneyot méinyotio ndau tambén, wénde fémofem yékenati Ngánjánene uwégh a mbarkánd yam. Fémofem so otameghét yékmetati Ngánjánene uwégh a mbarkánd yam, a yau nékmbotati ndau njamke raméghta.
bt: Woe/how terrible to you Farasi men, because your offering is to God from all the garden, but you neglect God's love and justice. You should have practiced the love of God and justice, and don't stop offering of garden food.

nama: "Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén fémofem méndefé yéfandati mer ámnjogh sifayé áumengégh méngon, a áuwégh si utmanégh térarégh sifayan.
bt: Woe to you Farasi men, because you like to find the good(important) seat in meeting house, and respectful greeting in the marketplaces.

nama: "Nderé mámáfi so yém Farasi ár féfeyot, ménamén fém ndenanit em wayamngoghofnar mbéwé, árkamé ár eyota matofnar."
bt: Woe/how terrible to you Farasi men, because you are like unmarked graves, which people walk on without knowing it.

nama: Ámb nák watamegh árom yawangoi, "Watamegh ár, njam fém noraye yénémamén, fémon mámáfi yénd yényátote ndenáye."
bt: One of the teaching man of law answered, "Teacher, when you say these things, you spoil/attack us also."

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Fém, nák watamegh ár, nderé mámáfi so yém féfeyot, ménamén fémofem árét ewafayotati kérté résaghét, wénde fémofem yau so ewáutánati kérté résaghét ámbiro tombé min ngángaye yéfe wáutárghét.
bt: Yesu answered, "You, the teaching men of the law, woe to you, because you put people down with burdons they are heavy to carry, but you will not lift on finger to help them.

nama: "Nderé mámáfi so yém féfeyot, ménamén fémofem mbéwé méngo erametati yéfeyot Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfet, efe féfene ákifem té esmetaménd. 48 Fém nafrotati efalo yiyátotati féfene ákifem némé té yafrotaménd; yémofem esmetaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a fémofem yéfene mbéwé méngo erametati. 49 Yéné téfnan, Ngánján yánjo káfeté mwighé norayam, 'Yéndon so ewáfáretan Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a Yesuene muyafé ár, wénde ámb so esmetat, ámb wéi so ewifnat.' 50 Féyo ár mérén yénanotio sénonjo efogh so em téfnot ménamén Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfene férék eréghmén kétándmayo rényégh, 51 kétándmé Abelene férék kétán Jakarayane férék, efe yésmetaménd ghéngén wárogh kitaré a njúnnjúnofnar sifayé yéné néndkéfan. Néméwét, yéndon némaufrotan fe, ár mérén yénanotio sénonjo efogh so em téfeyot méinyotioat.
bt: Woe/how terrible to you, because you build the graves for the God's people who make know(prophets), whom your ancesters had killed. 48 You testify that it appove what your ancesters did; they killed God's people who make know(prophets) and you built their graves. 49 In this reason, God with his wisdom said, "I will send them God's people who make know(prophets) and men with Yesu's authority(apostles), but they will kill some and will also chase other people. 50 So the people of this time will be responsible because God's people who make know(prophets)' blood bleeding from the beginning, 51 from blood of Abel to the blood of Jakaraya, who was killed between the table for burning sacrifice and without.dust(holy) place. Yes, I tell you, the people of this time will be held responsible for it all.

nama: "Nderé mámáfi so yém nák watamegh téfnárét, ménamén fémofem mé té tamndawét yéné ki matyam sésafne sauréghmén. Fémyo yau so kélawangi kénjút, a fémofem ekmbotati fá efe élaughét narametat."
bt: Woe/how terrible to you the teaching man of the law, because you have kept the key to open the door of knowledge. You yourselves have not entered inside, and you have stopped those who were entering.

nama: Njam Yesu nuwanoi kété, Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár nékwe tasotawét a ámogh si tarametawét tárfár yammén, 54 kawanjetawét sái terangayénd Yesu ménáyan némé yaufi si sái korayangai.
bt: When Yesu left there, Farasi men and the teaching men of law became angry and they made a asking talk about many things, 54 they waited, [they] will catch Yesu through what he will say wrong talk.

nama: Njam ár mérén náumendaménd, féyo fárofáro kafkafe katumndawét. Yesum si yérnyai fronde yánjo waitogh árfefaf korayau, "Fém so nawarawenati kétándmé Farasi árfé unyégh ghérsé tambén. 2 Efogh yéném némé méinyotio yafar so kanendangé, a némé ténoghe yém so nufanat. 3 Fém némé noraye kwéfitayan so kufarngé kwayaghat, a fémon némé yawanjai ténganan kénjúnro so yaitote kétándmé méngo sémsota.
bt: When crowd had gathered, so they were stepping on each other. Yesu began to talk first his following men [and] said, "You must guard yourself from Farasi men's lying life. 2 The day is coming everything what is covered will be discovered, and what is hidden will be revealed. 3 What you have said in the dark will reveal to the light, and what you have whispered into the ear will be proclaimed to the roofs of house.

nama: "Yéndon ewaufrotan, tane fútaréf, férfér yau em emofem fifi esmetat a yéné soramé yémofem yau némé so yafrotat. 5 Wénde yéndon ewauyafotan féfeyot efe so férférafé yéfandati: férférafé yéfandati Ngánján, féfene ngi soramé, fá muyafé yém emo so ewite fését. Néméwét, fá yéné yém efalofifi fémofem efe so férférafé yéfandati. 6 Némé 5 némbne men tamorwén 20 toyaye? Wénde némé yau wawalingé yém Ngánjánene kénjún. 7 Néméwét, féne sénko séf sem Ngánjánene waféghkaf em. Fém yau férférafé em. Fémofem ewaindati némbne men tértér.
bt: I tell you, my friends, don't be afraid those who kill the body and after this they cannot do anything. 5 But I show you who you should look with fear: look God with fear, after killing you, he has authority who throw you into fire(hell). Yes, he is truly the one who you should see with fear. 6 Are it 5 small birds sold for 20 toya? But one of them is not forgotten in God. 7 Yes, your hairs are counted by God. Don't be afraid. You.p surpass small birds.

nama: "Yéndon ewaufrotan, emofem yénd wiyátotat safén nde fá em tane, ndenéyameyo Ároene Ambumom so efalo tayátongé nde fá em tane kété Ngánjánene njafar si résagh árfene sáfrére. 9 Wénde emofem njam wékenat yénd ár safén wéi so ekenan Ngánjánene njafar si résagh árfene sáfrére. 10 Efe njam noraya Ároene Ambumoeta so tawalindangé, wénde efe njam noraya yaufi si Ngánján Mbérmbéroefaf yau so tawalindangé.
bt: I tell you, anyone who declare publicly that they belong to me, the same way the Son of Man will declare they belong to me before the men carrying heavenly message(angels) of God. 9 But anyone who denies me before people I will also deny them before the men carrying heavenly message(angels) of God. 10 Anyone who speaks against the Son of Man can be washed, but anyone who speaks bad talk(blasphemes) to the God Spirit will not be washed.

nama: "Njam fém nénémnjat áumengégh méngot, limánégh ár a muyafé árfefaf, fém yau mámé mwighé em ndernáye fém so nawamarotati o fém némé so norayati, 12 ménamén Ngánján Mbérmbéram so ewatamete fém yéné efghon némé so norayati." Wemár mámáfi ároene rengerenge si
bt: When you are brought to meeting houses, to rulers and authorities, do not worried about how will you defend yourself or what you will say, 12 because God Spirit will teach you at that time what will say." The parable of rich fool

nama: Ámb ár norayai mérén kénjún yáfaf, "Watamegh ár, témnde tane ané faloghét némé tane afam euroi."
bt: Someone in the crowd said to him, "Teacher, tell my big brother to divide what my father's left things."

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Fútar, emo wékafai yénd ménát yéndon efalotan rokarrokar féfene néndkéfan sómbio ár?" 15 Ndenaye fá norayai yéfefaf, "Kor fété! Ménamén féfene méinyotio fénatafénaté kwénjtamén; ménamén féfene ghérsé yau so rokaram tawanjarngé."
bt: Yesu answered, "Friend, who appointed me in order that I divide things between two men?" 15 Then he said to them, "Be careful! Because of your every kind of greed; because possessions/things do not save your life.

nama: Féyo Yesum emndayéng yéné rengerenge si: "Kété témorwén ámb wemár ár efe témorwén mband yuré némé kanotawét mer rokar ndawan. 17 Fá nemnayéng, 'Yéndo némé so yafrotan? Tane yau kor sifayé yém ánde tane méinyotio rokar so ewamndan.'
bt: Then Yesu told them this parable: There was a rich man who was land owner what produce good crops. 17 He thought, 'What shall I do? My place is no enough where I will store all my things.' 18 Then he said, "This is what I will do. I will tear down my crops storage house and build very big, and there I will store my farm crops and other things. 19 And I will say in my mind, "There are many good things to me to keep many years. Take life easy; in eating food and drink alcohol and be happy."

nama: "Wénde Ngánjánam yémndai fá, 'Fém mámáfi árǃ Yéné sémbáran fémon fene ghérsé so téram. Féyo emon so tawanangé méinyotio rokar fémon némé té táfrende fendio?'
bt: But God said to him, 'You fool! This night your life will be demanded from you. So who will take everything what you have prepared for yourself?

nama: "Yéné fiyém ndernáye so yém ámb ár emon rokar ewamnde yandio wénde yau em wemár ár Ngánján kénjún." Mámé mwighé yau
bt: This is how will be anyone who stores things for himself but is not rch in God." Don't worry

nama: Féyo Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, "Yénémamén yéndon emndetan, mwighayam yau ewawérsota ménamén féfene ghérsamén, némé so enetati; ménamén féfene fifimén a némé so ewawo. 23 Ghérsé siyém minde tukén, wénde njamke siyém yuwanyo yém, a fifi siyém minde tukén, wénde sáláme siyém yuwanyo yém. 24 Kéretangi kétán kwagh kés em: yémofem yau eitat o etanat, yéfene wamángégh méngo yau ewam; wénde Ngánjánam emanjne fá. Fém em minde tukén Ngánjánene ghékfan a ewaindati yéné men! 25 Fém so féyotaro em ndené teke sái emor ménáyan mwighé wawérsoghan yénémamén? 26 Táf njam yau ewafrotati yéné némbne rokar, fém némamén mámé mwighé so nemnati ámb rokarmén?
bt: Then Yesu said to his following men, "Therefore I tell you, don't worry because of your life, what you will eat; because of your body and what you will wear. 23 Food and clothing are important but behavior is very important. 24 Look at the crows: they do not plant and dig, there is no their storage house; but God feeds them. You are of high regard in God's eyes and surpass those birds! 25 Can you live how long by worrying about it? 26 If you cannot this small things, why do you worry about other things?

nama: "Kéretangi ndernáye kéghat mbumbu nanotat. Fá yau násáfotat minde yéfenjo sálámet. Wénde yéndon emndetan, yau mer ndené Solomon yáne méinyotio merfifi rokar kénjún fá umaneghkaf témorwén ndené mbumbu kés em. 28 Fá Ngánján yém emo ewamanete kéghat yus, árkéméné sénonjo yéna em káye kawaindangi a ewite fését. Nderé minde tukén yémon némamanete fém? Nderé némbne mwighafé fém emǃ 29 Fém yau féfenjo tikéfan nuitati njamkemén a numén, fém yau mwighayam ewawérsota yénémamén. 30 Ménamén téndo ár yéné mband maratan nérsat yéné rokarot, a fene Afé mat yém féfene némé ménde em. 31 Wénde yéronjati Ngánjánene limánégh, a yéné rokar so tawaramangé féfeyot féyotaro.
bt: Look how wild flowers grow. They do not make their clothes. But I tell you, even Solomon in all his beautiful things [that] he dressed are not good but flower are good. 28 If God clothes the grass of the bush, which are here today and tomorrow are thrown into the fire. How much more he will clothe you? What little big mind(faith) you have! 29 You do not downheart about food and water, do not worry about it. 30 Because outsiders of the world carry having this things, and your Father knows what you need. 31 But seek the kingdom of God, and these things will be given to you enough.

nama: "Férfér yau em, némbne mérén, ménamén féfene Afé áuwéghan yém so tawaramangé Ngánjánene limánégh. 33 Fémofem tátérnangi féfene némé ewam a tawaramangi yauyau árét. Néusnati mani yáf féfendio árkéméné yau so kefongi, féfene ngonjél so njafaran yém kété ánde yau so kefongi, ménamén yau oro téfnáram so tawanangi a yau so ghénjam ewasérmbotat. 34 Ménamén ánde féfene ngonjél yém, féfene tikéf kété so em. Kor ghékéf sénté em
bt: Don't be afraid, little group, because your Father is in pleasure to give you the ruling of God. 33 Sell your what you have and give to the poor. Provide money bag for yourselves which it will not be finished, your treasure will be in heaven where will be not finished away, because there is no thief can come and no moth can destroy. 34 Because where your treasure is, your heart will be there. Be Watchedness

nama: "Túmén afrengéghkaf aem Ngánjánene ásáfoghét méinyotio efogh a sémbár, 36 ndenanit ásáfogh ár kawanjetawét ménát yéfene ndimbal ár so nénangote awerégh téméndta, fá njam nénangota a náutána, yémofem sésafne táfandio so tasawérngi yáyot. 37 Nderé áuwégh so yém yéfeyot yéné ásáfogh árét yéfenjo yuram tawinjongé fá táf afrengéghkaf njam em fá sái nénangota. Yéndon féfe ewaufrotan efaloe, yémon so téfitrarngé awerégh sáláme ménát sái efrenda faiofnar ásáfogh ár, so tamndangé ámnjoghét njamke sifayan a sái ewarameta yéfeyot njamke. 38 Nderé áuwégh so yém yéfeyot ásáfogh árét yéfene yuram njam tawinjongé afrengéghkaf, fá njam nénangota yéné efghon sémbár néndkéfan o akwan faurghan. 39 Wénde fém so mat em: Méngo yuré mat njam té témorwén árké efogh fénefénean oro téfnár so ténmorwén, yémo yau so tumyongé oro téfnár yáne méngo fétrarghét. 40 Fém so afrengéghkaf em, ménamén Ároene Ambum sái yéném yéné efoghféneféneyan fém yau njam nawanjeta afrengéghofnar."
bt: Be always ready for God's work all the days and nights, 36 like workers waiting for that his master will return from wedding feast, when he returns and knocks they can immediately open the door for him. 37 How happy will be those workers their master finds they are ready when he comes back. I tell you the truth, he will take off wedding clothe to serve workers, [and] he will tell to sit food table and will give them food. 38 How happy will be those workers their master finds them ready, when he returns this day in the middle of the night or at daybreak. 39 But you must know: If owner of house had known at which hour the thief will come, he would not let the thief to break his house. 40 You must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at this hour when you do not expect him without ready.

nama: Fitam yémotai, "Yuré, Fémo yéné rengerenge si noraye téfendro o méinyotio árét?"
bt: Fitè asked, "Lord, are you speaking this parable only for us or every people?"

nama: Yuram yawawangoi, "Efe ndené yém ndimbal mwighafé a mer wanjengégh ár? Fá yéné féyém yánjo yuram so tékmangé méngo wanjengét sái ewarameta njamke efoghféneféneyan? 43 Nderé mer yém yéné ásáfogh téfnárai yáne yuram tinjongé yáne ásáfoghan fá njam nénangota. 44 Yéndon némaufrotan efaloe, yémo so tékmangé fá wanjengét yáne méinyotio rokaran. 45 Wénde táf faiofnar ásáfogh ár norayai yandio, 'Tane yuram yérse teke efogh angoghét,' a yémon ngi ewafngoi ásáfogh ár a amaf, a njamke nénet a wéri nu nénet ménát wériot. 46 Yéné ásáfogh téfnárane yuré so nénangote yéné efghon njam fá yau yánjeta fá a yau yéné efghon mat yém. Yáne yuram so tékrátan tékmangé kéran a yémon so ewite yémafé áyáréghofnar árfé.
bt: The Lord answered, "Who is with big mind(faith) and good manager? He is the one his master will put to manage the house to give them food at [proper] time? 43 How good is to this worker when his master finds him doing his work when he returns. 44 I tell you truly, he will put him to manage all his things. 45 But worker might say to himself, 'My master take a long day to come,' and he begins to beat working men and women, and eats food and drinks alcohols to get drunk. 46 The master of that worker will return on that day when he does not know. His master will punish him by death and he will put him with the people who do not truly approve.

nama: "Ásáfogh téfnár efe mat yém yáne ndimbaloene ménde wénde yau náfrenda o yau yafrota yáne yurane ménde, fá so efmote minde. 48 Wénde ásáfogh téfnár efe yau mat yém a emo yaufi yafrote, fá so efmote némbnare. Kétándmé méinyotio árét endefet ewaramete ndimbal yéfeyot, ndené ndimbalyo so taramangé. A kétándmé emon njam efalo yiyátota ndimbale, ndené ndimbaleyo so taramangé. Yau krufrát yam wénde falogh
bt: The worker who knows the master's desire but does not ready or does not do his master's desire, he will be beaten a lot. 48 But worker who does not know and he does badly, he will be beaten few. From every people who has been given many, same big will be demanded. And from who has been entrusted a lot, same big will be given. Not peace but division

nama: "Yénd wénmormén fés yénérsamén yéné mbandot, yénd nemnan fés té nawaro wénde yau nawaroǃ 50 Fronde yénd táf so wém ndimbal tékrátan, a nderé kérté yamamé so wiota kétánotio tane tékrát kefongéǃ 51 Fém nemne yénd wénmormén krufrát yénérsamén yéné mbandot? Yau, yau krufrát, wénde falogh. 52 Yénandmé sénonjo ámbiro mérén 5 so nafalotat mé fáro, nambiota kétándmé sómbio a sómbiota kétándmé nambio. 53 Fá so nafalotat, afé kétándmé ambum a ambum kétándmé afé, amé kétándmé mérés a mérés kétándmé amé, tanat alé kétándmé nakum alé a nakum alé kétándmé tanat alé." Mat áyátogh efogh
bt: "I came to bring fire on the earth, I think fire were already burning but it does not burn! 50 Firstly I must suffer bigly, how terrible am I until my suffering will be finished! 51 Do you think I come [to] bring peace on the earth? No, not peace but division. 52 From now one family of 5 will be divided, three against two and two against three. 53 They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in -law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law." Understanding the time

nama: Fá norayai mérénfaf, "Njam fémofem tawinjongi nu kwéf nénarmbotat mbormborta, efaloe fém so korayangi, 'Nuwét yém,' a so nafamnde. 55 A njam náikái mbormbor yénéfnje, fém so korayangi, 'So yém wafté,' a so nufrote. 56 Efe farun mer néretati wénde kénjún yauǃ Fém mat em ndernáye so tayamngongi yam mbandan a njafaran. Fém yau mat em ndernáye so tayamngongi sénonjo efoghmén.
bt: He said to the crowd, "When you see a black cloud rising from west, really you say, 'It's going to rain,' and it rains. 55 And the south wind blows, you say, 'It's going to hot,' and it happen. 56 Liar! You know how to interpret the surprising thing of the earth and sky. You don't know how to interpret the present days.

nama: "Fém fémyo mat em némé yém mbarkánd. 58 Emon njam tawinjongé fém kérté wafroghan mato kérté efrendati soramé so engém mogh sifayat. Yau njam, so tawerangi mogh sifayat. Féyo nák wanjengégh árém taramangi kétán félismanefaf a félismaném tákmangi kwéfkwéf sifayan. 59 Yéndon emndetan, fem yau so kéntorngi kétánotio fém njam méinyotio fai nufrotati."
bt: "You yourself know what is straight. 58 If anyone find your guilty, settle the case before you go to the court. If not, he may drag you court. So judging man will hand you to the police and the police throw you into darkness house, 59 I say, you will not get out until you have paid all the money."

nama: Táf yéné efghon ámb ár tamorwén emofem Yesue yaufroyénd ménamén Galili yu árfene férék Failetém térémnau yéfene ghéngénafé. 2 Yesum yawangoi, "Fém nderé nemnati yéné Galili yu árfene yaufiyam árké ewafrotat yémofem ewaindat yaufiyam yéfene ámb ár kés em Galili yumén, yéné ende yémofem tékrát téfandawét? 3 Yéndon emndetan, yauǃ Wénde fém yau njam Ngánjánefaf kawengongi, fém méinyotio wéi kér so em. 4 O efe 18 kér emormén njam tuk méngo Siloam kénjún nuyai yéfefaf, fém némé nemnati yéfene yaufiyamam ewaindayénd yéfene yaufiyam ámb kés tamorangérwén Jerusalemén? 5 Yéndon emndetan, yauǃ Wénde fém yau njam Ngánjánefaf kawengongi, fém méinyotio wéi kér so em." 6 Féyo yémo tamndetau yéné rengerenge si, "Ár témorwén wén mbwe tiyangérwén yáne géreif mbwe ménj ndau kénjún, a fá téngémorwén wén mbwe ereghét, wénde yémon yau ewinjoi. 7 Féyo fá norayai ároefaf emo tanjetau géreif mbwe ménj ndau, 'Yénanotio nambio yunjéf sénonjo yénd té kwénmorwén wén mbwe ereghét yéné wén mbweyot, wénde yéndo yau yinjon. Ténfend! Némamén mé anogh so nanote yéné mbandan?'
bt: At that day some people were there who told Yesu about the Galili region people whose blood Failet mixed with their sacrifices. 2 Yesu answered, "How do you think those Galillans' sin which they did surpass the sins that other men from Galili region did, that's why they saw pains this way? 3 I tell you, no! But If you don't turn away to God, all of you will die. 4 Or those 18 who die when tower of Siloam fell to them, what do you think their sin surpass of the other men who were in Jerusalem? 5 I tell you, No! But if you don't turn away to God, all of you will die way."

nama: Ámb Safatan Yesum tawatametau ámb áumengégh méngon, 11 a amaf témorwén kété yaufi mbérmbéram yékeindam 18 yunjéf. Fá témorwén kanémafé a yau tawalinjotau. 12 Njam Yesum yinjoi fá, yémo yémotai fá a yémndai, "Amaf, fém ném mer." 13 Féyo Yesum ngángé efayoi yáfaf, a táfandio nolinjoyénd a Ngánján tuwetau.
bt: One Safat Yesu was teaching in one of meeting houses, 11 and a woman was there who had been crippled by evil spirit for 18 years. She was bent over and can't straighten up. 12 When Yesu saw her, he called her and said, "Woman, you are good." 13 Then Yesu put his hands on her, and immediately she straighten up and praised God!

nama: Áumengégh méngo limángégh ár nékw témorwén ménamén Yesum mer táyátotau Safatan, fá korayau árfefaf, "Kété sem 6 efogh ásáfoghét. Féyo eném frengéghét yéné efghon, yau Safatan."
bt: The meeting house head man was angry because Yesu healed her on the Safat, he said to the people, "There are 6 days for working. So come to heal on those days, not on the Safat."

nama: Yuram yawangoi yá, "Fém efe farun mer néretati wénde kénjún yauǃ Fémofem túmén taférngi kau o ndonki kétándmé kwélalta a talimángi nuot Safatan. 16 Féyo yéné amaf, Ebraamene mérés, efe Sáifram yumbai yékeindam 18 yunjéf. Fá fété nawanjar Safatan kétándmé némé yam yékeinda."
bt: The Lord answered him, "You liar! You always untie cow and donkey from the stall and lead it out to give it water on the Safat. 16 Then this woman, a daughter of Ebraam, whom Sáifèr has kept to bound for 18 years. She should be saved on the Safat from what bound her."

nama: Yesum njam korayau yéné, yáne mék téfnár wáiwái yam ewifoi, wénde ár káuwetawét némé mer Yesum tawafrotau. Rengerenge si némbne mbwe a yist
bt: When Yesu said this, his enemies was full of shame, but the people rejoiced what Yesu did good. Parables of the small seed and the Yeast

nama: Féyo Yesum namotai, "Ndernáye nérete Ngánjánene limánégh? Némayafé so yétémndan? 19 Ndenanit némbne mbwe, árom yéngwanai yiyai yánjo ndawan a nanotam wén náyátoi, a menam njén tarametawét yéné kafeyamé."
bt: Then Yesu asked, "How is like the ruling of God? What shall I compare it to? 19 [It is] like small seed, a man took and planted in his garden and grew and became a tree, and the birds make nest in this branches."

nama: Wéikor Yesu namotai, "Yéndon némayafé so yétémndan Ngánjánene limánégh? 21 Ndené fété yist amafom yanai a yérémnam nambio flawé baik. Féyo méinyotio flawé néminam ndimbal kofékofé namndai." Némbne sésafne
bt: Again Yesu asked, "What shall I compare the ruling of God to? 21 It is like yeast a woman took and mixed into 3 bags of flour. So all the flour rises [and] become big dough." The narrow door

nama: Yesu njam tiotau Jerusalemét, yémo tangwatametau tesenamé a méngotuamé. 23 Ámb árom yémotai Yesu, "Yuré, féyotaro nambio árro so tawanjar?"
bt: When Yesu went to Jerusalem, he taught them through the town and village. 23 Someone asked Yesu, "Lord, are only a few people possible to be saved?"

nama: "Minde násáfotati ménát fém so kélawangi némbne sésafnemé, ménamén yéndon emndetan, tárfár ár yéta so nufotati élaughét a yau so nufrote. 25 Táf méngo yuré njam so kénangongé a so témnangé sésafne, fém so ewakai sésafne téndota náutánati a norayati, 'Yuré, sésafne tésawér téfe.' "Wénde yémon so tawawangongé, 'Yénd mafina wém fém, o fém árkamé eném.'
bt: "Work hard in order that you will enter small door, because I tell you, many people will try to enter and will not happen. 25 When house owner will get up and will close the door, you will stand outside, knocked and said, "Lord, open the door for us.' But he will answer, 'I don't know you or where you come from?

nama: "Féyo fém so korayangi, 'Yéndfem njamke a nu tánetawém fémafé, a fémon ténmatametau endmé.'
bt: Then you will say, 'We ate and drank with you, and you taught us on the roads.'

nama: "Wénde yémon so tawawangongé, 'Yénd mafina wém fém o fém árkamé eném. Kitrongi ta tambén, fém méinyotio yaufiyam wafrogh téfnárǃ'
bt: Then he will answer, 'I don't know you or where you come from. Get away from me, all of you evil doing people!

nama: "Kété siyém yengam a sén krarégh, njam fémon tawinjo Ebraam, Aisek a Jeikof a méinyotio Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár efe em Ngánjánene limánéghan, wénde fém té kéméwitat. 29 Ár so eném kétándmé wérwérta a mbormborta a náikáita a yambérméta, a so tawanangi yéfenjo sifayé kété téménd kénjún Ngánjánene limánéghan. 30 Efe soramé em fá so kénáyátongi frondeyot, efe fronde em fá so kénáyátongi soramat." Yesuene ghérghérsisé Jerusalemét
bt: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when you see Ebraam, Aisak and Jeikof and all the God's people who make know(prophets) in the ruling of God, but you are thrown. 29 People will come from east and west and north and south, and will take their places there at the feast in the ruling of God. 30 Who are last they will be first, who are first they will be last. Yesu' sorry for Jerusalem

nama: Ámb efghon ámb Farasi ár enmormén Yesuefaf a yémndayénd, "Koro yéné yu a kuano ámb sifayat. Erodém ngiot némafngo fém." 32 Yesum yawangoi, "Nguwano taufro yéné ághéághai, 'Yéndon so ewitrotan yaufi mbérmbér, a engéfrendan ár sénonjo a káye, a nambio efogh soramé yéndon so tifon tanjo ásáfogh.' 33 Wénde yénd mé so wéngém sénonjo a káye a ámb efghon, ámb Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár yau so kér em Jerusalem sémta.
bt: At that time some Farasi men came to Yesu and said to him, "Leave this place and go the other place. Erod wants to kill you."

nama: "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, fém emofem esmetat Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a ewafiyátotat wíme efe enwáfáretam féfefaf, yénd ménde wém fene ár so eumendan, ndenéyame amé ghakér kowiam yáne tosétosé yénau tawamongé njambe, wénde fém yau ménde kénémǃ 35 Kéreta, fene tesen so kafrangongé. Yéndon némaufrotan feyot, fémon yau so kwinjo wéi kétánotio fém njam koraya, 'Nderé frengégh yém efe yéném Yurane yétkwénan!'"
bt: "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the God's people who make know(prophets) and strike whom sent to them by stone, I wanted to gather your people, like as hen gather her chicks under her wings, but you did not want it! 35 Look, your city will be left. I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!"

nama: Ámb Safatan, njam Yesu téngmorwén Farasi árfene utmanégh ároene méngot nénet, árém kor téfandawét Yesu kakafifi. 2 Ámb ár efe témorwén kafkaf a ngángé mbwefé yénmorméng Yesuefaf. 3 Yesum emotai Farasi ár a nák watamegh téfnár, "Némé nák yém ménát ár frengéghét Safatan o yau?" 4 Wénde fá métar nufrotaménd. Yesum yéngwasroi ár yéfrendai a yéngwatembai.
