bt: One Safat, when Yesu went to eat in the house of respected Farasi men, the people were watching him very close. 2 There was a man whose arms and legs were swollen came to Yesu. 3 Yesu asked Farasi men and the teaching men of the law, "What is lawful to heal on the Safat or not?" 4 But they remained silent. Yesu took man and healed him and sent him.

nama: Yesum emotai, "Njam endene ámbiro ambum o kau kuyangé kétán yékwét Safatan, fémo wékeye yau so turto?" 6 A yéfeyot ámb yau si.
bt: Yesu asked them, "If one of you has child or cow fallen into a well on the Safat, will you not rush to get it out? 6 And they didn't say any talk.

nama: Njam fá mat náyátoi wan téfnáram yaferyéng mer sifayé njamke sékian, yémon tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si: 8 "Njam ámb árom kémumyongé fém kétán awerégh téméndfaf, yau tané yéné tuk sifayé. Yau aem ndené yéné sifayé kés yém nawamnde ároe yémo té yumyo endene em tukén yam a kémaindangé fém. 9 Njam nufrota, emon njam taumiye fém so kénaflend a tamndend fém, 'Fene sifayé taram yéné ároe.' Nderé wáiwáiafé yém, fémon so tané yuwon sifayé. 10 Wénde fém njam kémumyongi, yuwon sifayé tané, fene téménd yuré njam yéném, yémon so kénémndangé, 'Fútar, kimnda tuk sifayat.' Fém so némutémndat wan téfnárafene sáfan. 11 Efe nawarmbotat fáyo so newonat yuwot, efe njam néwonat fá so kayátongi ndimbal."
bt: When he noticed the guests chose the good place at the table, he told them this parable: 8 "When someone invites you to a wedding feast, don't take a high place. May it not be that that place is already reserved for a man that he has invited who has a high reputation & surpasses you.s 9 If he did, one who invited you will come and say to you, 'Give this person your place.' How he is shameful, you should take last place. 10 But when you are invited, take low place, [so that]when your host comes, he will say to you, 'Friend, move up to high place.' You will be honored in front of the guests. 11 Those who make themselves big will come down low, those who come down themselves will go up high."

nama: Féyo Yesu norayai yánjo téménd yurafaf, "Fémon njam ewarameta efoghméf néne o séite néne, fémo yau taumyo fe fútaréf a fene anéf a fene mérén o fene wemár fútaréf, fémon njam yafrota, fém so kémumyongi a so némawafángotat. 13 Wénde fémon njam tawaram njamke, taumyo yauyau ár, aknyégh ár, wawér, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár. 14 Yémofem yau so fai némawafángotat. Wénde Ngánjánam so néfrende a fai kémawangogé njam yáne ár nénanotat kérta." Rengerenge ndimbal téméndmén
bt: Then Yesu said to his host, "When you give a lunch or dinner, you don't invite your friends and brothers and relatives or the rich friends, if you do, you will be invited and will be repaid. 13 But when you give a food [banquet], invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, [and] the blind. 14 They cannot repay you. But God will bless you and repay when his people rise from death. Parable about big feast

nama: Soramé Yesum si waitogh yifoi, ámb wan ár norayai, "Frengégh yém yáyot emo so enete téménd kénjún Ngánjánene limánéghan!"
bt: After Yesu finished teaching, one of guests said, "Blessed is the one who will eat at the feast in the ruling of God!"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Ámb árom téfrendau ndimbal téménd a eumyoi tárfár wan téfnár. 17 Téménd efghon, yémo ewáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh ár tanwaufrongi efe té eumyon, 'Taném, sénonjo méinyotio rokar siyém frengéghkaf yém.'
bt: Yesu answered, "A man prepared a big feast and invited many guests. 17 On the feast day, he sent his workers to tell those who had been invited, "Come, everything is now ready.'

nama: "Wénde yémofem si waufrogh ewafngoyénd. Fronde ár norayai, 'Yéndon mband té yanan, a yénd so wéngém fandaghét. Ghérghérsisé kwawalo tayot.'
bt: But they began to make excusing talk. The first man said, 'I have bought a field, and I will go to see it. Excuse me.'"

nama: "Ámb norayai, 'Yéndo té ewanan 5 kau, a yénd álighan wém so eufotan. Ghérghérsisé kwawalo tayot.'
bt: Another said, "I have bought 5 cows, and I'm on the way to try them out. Excuse me.'

nama: "Wéi ámb norayai, 'Yénd somé naweran. Féyo yénd yau so wéném.'
bt: Still another said, 'I just got married, so I can't come.'

nama: "Ásáfogh téfnár nangotam a kafrotau yéné yánjo yurafaf. Féyo méngo yuré nékwafé naféghetam a yáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh téfnár, 'Nguwano kétor wékeye némbne endmé a kéghat endmé yéné tesenan a tanwasro yauyau ár, aknyégh ár, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár a wawér.'
bt: The workers returned and told to his master. Then house's owner became angry and ordered his workers, 'Go out quickly small roads and ouside/bush roads in the town and bring the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.'

nama: "Ásáfogh téfnárom yémndai 'Yuré, fémon némé wémndai té kufrote, wénde kété mé em sifayé.'
bt: The worker said, 'Lord, what you said has been done, but there are still place(room).'

nama: "Féyo yuram yémndai yánjo ásáfogh téfnár, 'Nguwano kétor némbne endmé a kéghat endét a tanwaurémnau, 'Kénaflangi kénjút,' ménát tane méngo so yém afoghare. 24 Yéndon némndetan, efe fronde eumyongén yémofem yau so tufongi tane njamkeǃ'" Waitogh ároene yam
bt: Then the master told his workers, 'Go out to small roads and bush roads and demand strong them 'Come in,' so that my house will be full. 24 I tell you, those who were invited first will not get taste of my food(banquet)'" Being a following man

nama: Ndimbal mérén tayotau Yesufé, a nawengoi yéfefaf yémon emndai: 26 "Efe njam yéném tafaf sái kamndangé tane waitogh ár, yénd so uwete mindefifi, yénamayo so euwete yánjo afé a amé, yánjo amaf a ambumtértér, yáne ghakér a mérésmérés, néméwét, ndenáyo yánjo ghérsé. Yau njam, fá yau so yém tane waitogh ár. 27 A emo yau njam yérsa yánjo wafatarégh wén a kwénmaitongé yénd fá yau so yém tane waitogh ár.
bt: A large crowd went with Yesu, and he turned them and said to them. 26 "If anyone comes to me to be my following man, he should love me a lot, secondly he should love father and mother, wife and children, brothers and sisters, yes, even his life. If not, he cannot be my following man. 27 And if anyone who don't carry his cross and follow me and he can't be my following man.

nama: "Njam efe ár ménde yém ndimbal méngo ramghét, fá fronde so kámnjongé a nufénmeta yéfanda njénamb mani yém wifoghét. 29 Yémon so tafngongé méngo ramégh, wénde yau so tifongé. Féyo méinyotio árém yéfandat némé nufrote so yurtat fá. 30 Fá korayawét, 'Yéné árom yérnyai ramégh wénde yau yifoi.'
bt: "If the man who wants to build a big house, he will sit first and estimate and see how much money to finish it. 29 He will start to build the house, but he cannot finish. So every people [who]see what he does will make fun of him. 30 They will say, "This man began to build but could not finish."

nama: "Némé so yafrote warsagh árom táf yáne njam em 10,000 fiyaré yandio awamaroghét kétamé ámb warsagh ár emafé so násmét 20,000 fiyarafé? Fronde njam kuwanongé mékot, féyo fá so kámnjongé mwighé so yéfarone waingéghét? 32 Fá yau njam yém féyotarofnar, yémon so yáfárete si résagh ár táfandio ámb mé esio a fá so kwómnendangé krufrátot. 33 Yéné endeyo, fémon tafrango méinyotio rokar, fém so ném tane waitogh ár.
bt: What king will do if he has 10,000 soldiers could defend himself against a king who is about to attack him with 20,000 soldiers? Before he goes out to battle, he should first sit down and decide if he can win? 32 If he is not able to, he send messanger while other is still a long way and he will asked terms of peace. 33 Like this way, you leave everything, you will be my following man.

nama: "Sol siyém mer yém, wénde yáne ménde njam kefongé, ndernáye so sol nawafarnjé wéikor? 35 Yau mer yém mbandot o ámb rokarot, árém táf yéngwitat. Efe njam ténganafé yém, so káyárangé yéné si."
bt: Salt is good, but if its flavor is finished, how can it be made to taste salty again? 35 It is not good for the soil or for anything, [so]people throw it out. Whoever has ears, he must hear this talk."

nama: Mani kafégh ár a yaufiyam téfnár méinyotio káumendawét áyárghét yáfaf. 2 Wénde Farasi ár a nák watamegh ár mike nufngoyénd, "Yéné árom eumyote yaufiyam ár ménát yémafe nénet."
bt: The tax collectos and sinners were all gathering to hear him. 2 But Farasi men and teaching men of the law began to gramble, "This man welcomes sinner to eat with them."

nama: Féyo Yesum tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si, 4 "Njam ámbiro ároene em 100 sif a kolindangé ámbiro, némé so yafrote? Yémon efaloe so tafrangongé 99 marat sifayan a nguwanongé olingégh sif ronjaghét kétánotio yémo tinjongé. 5 Táf yémon njam tinjongé, yémo áuwéghafé tambnangé sif téfayongé yáne fader tukén. 6 Fá so nguwanongé méngot. Féyo yémon emotai yáne fútaréf a ámb kaka ár a norayai yéfefaf, 'Áuwégh yangotam yéndfé. Yéndon té yinjon tane olingégh sif.' 7 Yéndon emndetan fém kété njafaran ndenéyameyo kété so yém ndimbal áuwégh. Efaloe áuwégh 99 mbarkánd árfenemén efe em yaufiyamofnar, yéné siyém némbnarfifi, wénde minde áuwégh yém ámbiro yaufiyam ár efe njam kawengongé yánjo yaufita. Olingégh mani mbwe
bt: Then Yesu told them this prarble, 4 "If one man has 100 sheep and loses one, what does he do? He surely leave 99 in open place and go to find lost sheep until he finds. 5 When he finds it, he is happy, [and] put it on his shoulder. 6 He will go the house. Then he called his friends and his neighbors and told them, "Rejoice with me. I have found my lost sheep." 7 I say to you.p, in heaven the same kind of big happiness will happen when a sinner turns away. Truly the rejoicing about 99 straight men who have no sin, it is very little, but the rejoicing about one sinner who turns away will be very big. The lost coin

nama: "Némé so yafrote amafom táf yáne 10 silfé mani mbwe em a kolindangé ámbiro? Yémon táf lambefé tuwarongé a sámburégh méngo tafngongé ronjagh merkéráye kétánotio yémon tinjongé. 9 Féyo yémon njam yinjoi, yémon emotai fútaréf a ámb kaka ár a norayai yéfefaf, 'Áuwégh yangotam yéndfé, yéndon té yinjon tane olingégh mani mbwe.' 10 Yéndon emndetan fém, ndené endeyo, kété so yém áuwégh Ngánjánene njafar si résagh ár sáfrére ámbiro yaufiyam ár efe njam kawengongé yaufita." Olingégh ambum
bt: "What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one. She surely lights a lamp and begens to sweep the house [and] looks carefully until she finds it. 9 Then when she find it, she calls friends and neighbors and told to them, "Make a feast with me, I have found my lost coin." 10 I said to you in the same way there is rejoicing in the front of the men carrying heavenly message(angels) of God when one sinner who turns away The lost son

nama: Yesum wéikor si yéngwafngoi, "Kété témorwén ár sómbio ambumafé. 12 Kertrom yémndai yárefé, 'Afé, yénd yau so nawanjetan kétán fém kér ném. Sénonjo kwaram fene rokarta tane némé so em.' Féyo yémon ewafaleyai rokar néndkéfan yánjo sómbio ambumat.
bt: Yesu began to say again, "There was a man with two sons. 12 The younger son said to his father, 'Father, I will not wait until you die. Now give me your things what will belong to me.' Then he divided things(property)between his two sons.

nama: "Féyo tekofnaye, kertrom ewanai yáne némé tamorwén, náfrendam nuwanoi nayu yuwot a mani taranégh yéngwafngoi yaufi ghérsayan. 14 Yéné soramé yémon njam ewifoi méinyotio rokar, kété nuyai káukáu yéné yuwon, féyo fá áronjagh nufngoi sioghmén. 15 Féyo fá nuwanoi ásáfoghét nómnendai ámb árfefaf yéné yuwon, a árom yéngwáfáretam fá kimb wanjengét. 16 Fá minde siogh témorwén, fá ménde témorwén kimbafene njamke mbarmbar nénet. Wénde emo yau taramangai yáyot némndafi.
bt: So not a long later, the younger took his [things] what had, packed up and went to long country and began to waste money in bad living. 14 After this when ran out everything, there was famine in the land, so he began to feel starving. 15 So he went to work and asked to a man in that land, and the man sent him to feed pigs. 16 He was very hungry, he want to eat pigs' food. But no one who gave him anything.

nama: "Fá njam nénangoi mwighat, fá norayai, 'Méinyotio tane afane faiofnar ásáfogh ár féyotaro njamkeyan em, a yéna yénd swém krotro wémǃ 18 Yénd so nangotan tane afafaf a témndan, "Afé, yéndon yaufiyam té tafrotan njafarot a fefaf. 19 Yénd yau féyotaro wém sái wémndeta fene ambum. Yénd kuyáto ndenanit fene faiofnar ásáfogh ár."' 20 Féyo fá nokayoi a nuwanoi yárefafaf. "Njam kété mé ténmorwén nayu, yárefam yéngwinjoi fá a ghérghérsisé yam yafoi yánemén. Fá néngengoi yánjo ambumoefaf a yambnai a tésnetau.
bt: When he came back to thought, he said, 'All of my father's workers have enough food, and here I am dying! 18 I will go back to my father and say, "Father, I had sinned to the heaven and you. 19 I am not enough to be called your son; treat me like your worker. 20 So he stood up and went to his father. But while he was still a long way, his father saw him and full of mercy for him; he ran to his son and hugged him and kissed him.

nama: "Ambumom yémndai yárefé, 'Afé, yéndon yaufiyam té tafrotan njafarot a fefaf. Yénd yau wémndeta fene ambum yénd wém.' 22 "Wénde yárefam yémndai yáne faiofnar ásáfogh ár, 'Wékeyeǃ Mer totér sáláme ténmawanangi a tawongi. Talawend ghil yáne ngángayan tawend sandol kafkafan. 23 Ténmanengi yarsafé kau tésmangi. So téménd yafrotam a áuwégh yangotam, 24 ménamén yéné tane ambum efe kér yémormén sénonjo ghérsé yém. Fá té nolindai a sénonjo té yinjongém.' Féyo yémofem áuwégh tangotawét. |GR-gn33n_noLabel.tif|col||© Global Recordings Network, globalrecordings.net.|Olingégh ambum: yárefé nérse kertér ambumoefaf (Luk 15.20)|15.20
bt: The son said to his father, 'Father, I had sinned to the heaven and you. So I can't be called your son.' 22 But his father said to his workers, 'Hurry! Bring new clothes and put it on him. Get a ring for his finger and sandols on his feet. 23 Bring the fattened cow and kill it. Let's make feast and rejoice, 24 for this son of mine who was dead and now he is alive; He was lost and now he has been found.' So they rejoiced.

nama: "Táfandio, fronde sénko ambum témorwén ndawan. Fá njam nénáyátoi méngo kaka, fá náyárayéng wagh ke a ermángégh. 26 Féyo yémon yanmoi ámb faiofnar ásáfogh ár a yémotai némé yam nufrote. 27 Yémon yawangoi, 'Fene néngén té nufar, a fene afam yarsafé kau té yésém ménamén fá yéna yém a merro yém.'
bt: At that time, the first son was in the field. When he came close the house, he heard singing sound and dancing. 26 So he called one of the workers and asked him what was happening. 27 He answered, 'Your small brother has arrived, and your father has killed fattened cow because he is here and is good.' 28 So first born son became angry and would't go into the house. His father came out and begged him to enter. 29 But he answered his father, 'Look! All years I have worked for you and have never disobeyed your talk. But you didn't give us to young goat in order that we will do feast with friends. 30 But when this your son who wasted your money in bad living [and] come back to the house, you killed the fattened cow for him!

nama: "Yárefé norayai, 'Tane ambum, fém túmén yéndfé ténre. Méinyotio rokar tane sem fene em. 32 Wénde yéndfem ásneghafé a áuwégh tafrotam ménamén fengén kér yémormén a sénonjo ghérsé yém. Fá nolindai a sénonjo yéna yém.'"
bt: His father said, "My son, you are always with me. Everything I have is yours. 32 But we celebrate and rejoice because your younger brother was dead and now is alive. He was lost and now is here."

nama: Yesum emndai yáne waitogh ár, "Ámb wemár árom yékafai ár yáne rokar wanjengét. Wénde wemár ároe yaufroyénd ndené rokar wanjengégh árom fene mani té ewalinda. 2 Féyo yémo yanmoi fá a yémotai, 'Némé yéné yém yénd náyáran fenemén? Kwaram fene ásáfoghmén mat ndernaye tanjete, ménamén fém yau so ném wanjengégh ár yéné soramé.'
bt: Yesu told his following men, "A rich man chose a man to look after his things. But the rich man was told that his manager had wasted your money. 2 So he called him and asked him, "What is this I heard about you? Give me knowledge(report) about your work how did you look after, because you cannot be manager after this."

nama: "Wanjengégh ár norayai yáfafétio, 'Yéndon némé so yafrotan sénonjo? Tane yuram tane ásáfoghta wutér. Yénd muyafnar wém targhét, a yénd wáiwái wém ómnengéghét. 4 Yénd mat wém némé yéndon so yafrotan ménát árém mé so umyotat yénd yéfene méngomé njam yéndon tanjo ásáfogh koron.'
bt: The manager said to himself, 'What shall I do now? My master is taking away my work. I'm not strong to dig, and I am shame to beg. 4 I know what shall I do so that people will continue to welcome me into their houses when I lost my work'

nama: "Féyo yémon tamotau ámbiroámbiroe yáne yurane fai wangoghét. Yémo yémotai fronde ár, 'Njénamb fai ekmang tane yurane?' 6 Yémo yawangoi, '20 dram olif wékwér.' Wanjengégh árom yémndai fá, 'Téngwan fenjo fefé, kámnjo wékeye, a tafarota 10 dram.'
bt: So he called one by one his master's people to pay back. He asked the first man, 'How much pay do you put my master's? 6 He answered, '20 drums of olive oil.' Manager told him, 'Take your paper, sit down quickly, and write 10 drums.'

nama: "Wéi yémo yémotai yénamayo ár, 'Fefaf njénamb em?' Yémon yawangoi yá, '1,000 flawé baik.' Yémon yémndai fá, 'Téngwan fenjo fefé. Tafarota kétán 800 flawé baikot.'
bt: He asked again the second man, 'How much is to you? He answered, '1,000 flawè bags .' He told him, 'Take your paper. Write 800 flawè bags.'

nama: "Yuram tuwetau yáne unyégh rokar wanjengégh ár ménamén fá kawarawenau merkéráye. Ár yéné mbandan nawarawenat merkéráye wénde kwayaghane ár yau ndené em. 9 Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe, fútar náyátotati fémyo mband rokere, njam tafrango, fém so kémumyongé tuot méngotuot.
bt: The master praised his dishonest manager because he deal with well. The people of this world deal well but the people of the light are not like them. 9 I tell you, become friend for yourself with worldly things. When it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal village.

nama: "Efe njam ndimbal mwighafé yém némbne yaman, ndenáyo wéi so yém ndimbal yaman. Efe njam unyégh ár némbne yaman yém, ndenáyo wéi ndimbal yaman so yém. 11 Féyo fém yau njam efalo ném mbandoene rokar wanjengét, ndernáye fém so niyátotat efalo wemáran? 12 A fém yau njam efalo kéném ámb ároene rokar wanjengét, emon so kémaramangé fenjo rokar?
bt: Whoever is faithful in small things, he is also [faithful]in large things. Whoever lies in little things, he is also[lying] in large things. 11 So if you are not true(faithful) to manage worldy things, how will be true(faithful) with true riches? 12 And you are not true to manage someone's things, who will give you your own things?

nama: "Ásáfogh ár yau so yém sómbio yurafene limánégh mbanmbanan. Fémon so yékene ámb a so yuwete ámb, o fém so kamnyong ámbfaf a tékena ámb. Yénémamén fém yau so sómbio limánégh mbanmbanan ném, Ngánján a mani."
bt: A worker cannot stay under two owners ruling. He will hate the one and love the other. You will obey to the one and deny the other. Therefore you cannot stay under two ruling, God and money.'

nama: Farasi ár sem mani méndero ár em. Yémofem njam náyárayénd yéné si a fén yafngoyénd Yesu. 15 Yesum emndai fá, "Fém yéné féyém efe mer nitaro kénamndangi árfene ghékfan, wénde Ngánján mat yém féfene tikéf. Némé minde tukén árfene mwighayan fifiofnar yém Ngánjánene ghékfan.
bt: The Farasi men are money lovers. When they heard this talk and made fun of Yesu. 15 Yesu said to them, "You are ones who make yourselves look good in the eyes of people, but God knows your heart. What is value highly to people's thought is without.meaning to God's sight.

nama: "Mosesene nák a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ároene watamegh si táitotawét kétánotio Jonene efogh. Kétándmé yéné efghon, Ngánjánene limánéghmén mer si waitogh yérnyai, méinyotio árém end táronjawét kétán Ngánjánene limánéghfaf. 17 Áfráte yém njafar a mband so kawaindangend, wénde némbne si Ngánjánene sian yau so titrém.
bt: Moses' law and God's people who make know(prophets)' teaching were proclaimed until Jon's day. From that time, the good talk about the ruling of God started to preach, every people is trying to get in to the ruling of God. 17 Heaven and earth will disapper, but little talk in God's talk will not remove.

nama: "Emon njam yánjo amaf tatembangé a kawerangé ámb amaf kamndangé amafanyo wanégh ár a emon njam watembégh amaf kawerangé káyátongé áranyo wanégh ár. Wemár ár a Lajarus
bt: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another man commits adultery and man who marries a divorced woman commits adultery. The rich and Lajarus

nama: "Kété témorwén wemár ár efe kumanetau minde tukén sáláme a témorangérwén yánjo meran méinyotio efghon. 20 Yáne méngo sésafnefaf kété témorwén ómnengégh ár yétkwén Lajarus efe témorwén fénend kér ro 21 a ménde témorwén nénet némé kuitawét kétándmé wemár ároene njamke kitarata. Táfandio ághé tanmorwén tasambnawét yáne fénend. |IB-048gr.tif|col||by Farid Faadil. © Biblica, Inc. Used with permission. All rights reserved worldwide.|Lajarus wémároene sésfnefaf ághafé (Luk 16.20-21)|16.20-21
bt: There was a rich man who wore very good clothes and lived in good every day. 20 At the gate of his house there was a beggar name Lajarus who was with sores, 21 and wanted to eat what fell from the rich man's table. At that times the dogs came and licked his sores.

nama: "Ómnengégh ár kér yémormén a njafar si résagh ár yénmormén a yéngwasroyénd fá kétán Ebraamefaf. Féyo wemár ár kér yémormén a yiyátotaménd. 23 Efoghofnar yékw sifayan, wemár ár tékrátan témorwén, njam fá néretai tukét yinjoi Ebraam nayuta, yémafé Lajarus yáne téndota. 24 Féyo fá nutnai, 'Afé Ebraam, tayot ghérghérsisé a témnde Lajarus ngángé tauwarngé nuan a tane kánké krufrát tiyáto ménamén yénd fés kénjún tékrátan wém.
bt: The begger died and men carrying heavenly message(angels) took him there to Ebraam. So the rich man died and was buried. 23 In the without-sun hole(hell), the rich was suffering, when he looked up and saw Ebraam far off, with Lajarus by his side. 24 So he cried out, 'Father Ebraam, have pity on me and send Lajarus to dip his hand in water and become cool my tongue, because I am suffering in this fire.'

nama: "Wénde Ebraamém yawangoi, 'Ambum, kawafarota kété fene ghérsé ámnjogh efghon, fém meran kénmorwén, táf Lajarus yauyauan, wénde sénonjo fá enaghan yém yéna a fém tékrátan. 26 Ámb téfén siyém, féyotarofnar yém yéné ménamén yékw ndimbalam yénfalowé, ménát efe ménde éférmanéghét yénandmé fefaf siyém táikér yém.'
bt: But Ebraam answered, 'Son, remember there your living days(lifetime), you lived in good [things], while Lajarus had nothing, but now he is resting here and you are suffering. 26 Another reason, it is impossible because big hole seperate between us, anyone who want to cross from here to you are difficult.'

nama: "Yémo yawangoi, 'Féyo yéndon némamnendan fém, afé, téngwáfáreta Lajarus kétán tane afane méngot, 28 ménamén tane em 5 néngnéf. Mat so táyátongé fá yau so eném yéné tékrát sifayat.'
bt: He answered, 'Then I beg you, father, send Lajarus there to my father's house, 28 because I have 5 small brothers. Let them know they will not come to this suffering place.'

nama: "Ebraamém yawangoi, 'Buk kété em Moses a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár efarotaménd, fá sái náyáretat yéfendio.'
bt: Ebraam answered, 'There are books Moses and the God's people who make know(prophets) wrote, they should listen/obey to them.'

nama: "Fá norayai, 'Yau, afé Ebraam, ámb kér ár njam néngangota kétán yéfefaf, fá so kawengongi.'
bt: He said, 'No, father Ebraam, if the dead returns to them, they will turn away.'

nama: "Fá norayai yáfaf, 'Yau njam náyáretat Moses a Ngánjánene mat yátogh ároefaf, fá yau so nawengotat wéi ndenéyameyo kér tambén njam kénangongé ghérsat.'"
bt: He said to him, 'If they do not listen/obey to Moses and the God's people who make know(prophets), they will not turn away also same way if someone returns from the dead to life.'"

nama: Yesu norayai yánjo waitogh árfefaf, "Árké yaman eméinde ár túmén yaufiot, wénde tékrát yém yéné ároe emo eméinde. 2 Yéné ároefaf ndimbal wím so yumbetat kéman a yitat arakafot, yéné mé té yém némbne kérté. Wénde emo taméinde némbne ambumtértér yaufiot, fene kérté so yém ndimbal. 3 Féyo kor kafandangi fémyo.
bt: Yesu said to his following men, "Which things always cause people to sin, but[how] painful to this person whom people cause. 2 To tie the person a big stone around neck and throw into the sea, even this would be a small heavy for you.s. But Who cause this children to sin, your.s heavy would be big. 3 So watch yourselves.

nama: Njam yémo yaufi yafrota fefaf 7 mbinotmé ámbiro efogh kénjún, a 7 mbinotmé nénangota wéikor fefaf a korayangé, 'Yénd té nawengon,' fémon tawalinda."
bt: If he do sins against you 7 times in a day and 7 times come back again to you and saying, 'I had turn away,' you wash them."

nama: Yesuene muyafé ár norayayénd Yurafaf, "Yuré, ténmamina téfene ndimbal mwighéǃ"
bt: The men with Yesu's authority(apostles) said to the Lord, "Lord, add our big mind(faith)!"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Njam fene ndimbal mwighé yém ndenanit némbne mbwe, fém féyotaro so koraya yéné ndimbal wénfaf, 'Karufang a kayang arakafan,' a so kényárángé fém.
bt: Yesu answered, "If your big mind(faith) is like a small seed, you can say to this big tree, 'be uprootes and planted in the sea,' and it will obey you.

nama: "Njam fene faiofnar ásáfogh ár nénangota tarégh tambén o sif wanjengégh tambén, ndimbal ár so korayangé, 'Kénaf kámnjo njamke nénet'? 8 Wénde fá so korayangé, 'Tane njamke kwanegh, kwáfrenda a kwanjeta njam enetan. Yéné soramé fémo so enete.' 9 Kánjo yaramai faiofnar ásáfogh ároe ménamén yáne áyáréghmén némat yémon yémndai wafroghét? 10 Ndenéyameyo fémofem njam ewafrotati méinyotio némat té emndai wafroghét, norayayénd, 'Yénd siéném faiofnar ásáfogh árro yéném. Yéndfem tanwafrotam téfenjo ásáfoghro.'" 10 ár yaufi ghéngan enjnefé
bt: If your worker comes back from plowing or from looking after the sheep. Will master say, 'Come [and] sit down to eat.'? 8 But he will say, 'Cook my food, and get ready and wait on me while I eat. After that you may eat.' 9 Will he give thank his worker because of his work what he was told to do? 10 It is same with you, when you have done everything what you were told to do, say, 'We are only your workers. We have only done our work." 10 men with leprosy

nama: Yesu álighan témorwén Jerusalemét, tiotau Samaria a Galili yu néndkéfan. 12 Fá njam kufanau méngotuot, 10 ár yaufi ghéngan enjnefé tamorwén yéfángoyénd fá. Fá nénokayoyénd nayu 13 a nutnayénd minde tukén, "Yesu, Yuré, ghérghérsisé téfeyotǃ"
bt: Yesu were walking to Jerusalem, traveled between Samaria and Galili region. 12 When he entered a village, 10 lepers met him. They stood at a distance, 13 and shouted loudly, "Yesu, Yure, have mercy on us!"

nama: Yesum ewinjoi fá a norayai, "Nguanongi, ngauyafongi fémyo ghéngén wárogh árfefaf." A njamandio néngwanoyénd, fá mer néngamndayénd.
bt: Yesu saw them and said, "Go, show yourselves to the burning sacrifice man." And while they went, they were made clean.

nama: Ámbiroam, njam yinjoi frengégham yanai, nénangoi, Ngánján tuwetawét minde tukén. 16 Fá budme niyai Yesuene kafkaffaf, kánjo yaramai yáyot. Fá témorwén Samariamén ár.
bt: One [of them], when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God loudly. 16 He knelt down at Yesu's feet, gave thank him and he was man from Samaria.

nama: Yesum yémotai, "Némé 10 ár méinyotio frengéghkaf em? Ánde ámb 9 ár em? 18 Némamén yéné Téndo árro nénangoi Ngánján frengéghét?" 19 Féyo Yesum yémndai fá, "Kokai a nguwano. Fenjo ndimbal mwighayam mer té niyáto." Ngánjánene limánégh
bt: Yesu asked, "What were cleased all of ten? Where is other 9 men? 18 Why only this foreigner came back to praise God?" 19 Then Yesu said to him, "Stand and go. Your big mind(faith) has made you heal." ' The ruling of God

nama: Ámb efghon Farasi árém yémotai Yesu, "Njam so Ngánjánene limánégh yéném?" Yesum ewawangoi, "Ngánjánene limánégh yau yéném yéné ende fémon so tinjo fenjo ghékfe. 21 Fém yau so norayati, 'Siyém' o 'Kété yém' ménamén Ngánjánene limánégh siyém féfene kénjún yém."
bt: One day Farasi men asked Yesu, "When the ruling of God would come?" Yesu answered, "The ruling of God does not come this way you see with your eyes. 21 You can't say, 'Here it is' or 'There it is' because the ruling of God is inside you.

nama: Féyo Yesu norayai yánjo waitogh árfefaf, "Efogh yéna yéném fém njam so ménde em winjoghét ámb efghon Ároene Ambumoenemén, wénde fémofem yau so tinjongi. 23 Árém so tawaufrongi féfe, 'Kété fá yém,' o 'Yéna fá yém.' Yau néwaitotati yéfe soramé yéfefaf. 24 Ménamén Ároene Ambum njam nénangota, méinyotio árém so yéfandat ndenanit toyé kwayaghanit so télálángé witmé njafar kwéndkwéndta kétán witmé njafar kwéndkwénd. 25 Wénde fronde yémon so tárfár tékrát efande a so yékenat yéné saté ár mérnam.
bt: Then Yesu said to his following men, "The day comes here when you will wish to see one of days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it. 23 People will tell you, 'There he is, or 'Here he is.' Don't follow after them. 24 Because when the Son of Man comes, all the people will see him like the lightening flashes [and] light up from one side to the other. 25 But first he will see/have many suffering and be denied by this generation.

nama: "Ndenaye fété Noane efghon kés tamorwén, ndenéyameyo so yém Ároene Ambumoene efghon. 27 Árém njamke a nu tánetawét, amaf ár kaweretawét kétán efogh nawerai Noa nélawai minde ndimbal ngárndot. Féyo táf kéfér nakmai a yérambaryéng méinyotio ár.
bt: Just as it was the days of Noa, it will same way in the days of the Son of Man. 27 People ate food and [drinking] water, men married to women up to the day Noa entered very boat. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.

nama: "Ndenéyameyo tamorwén Lotene efghon. Árém njamke a nu tánetawét, kéufnamotawét rokar, njamke yéghan a méngo raméghan. 29 Wénde yéné efghon Lotém yéfrangoi Sodom, fés a fésafé wím kénuitawét njafarta nunit kafamndau, a yérambaryéng méinyotio ár.
bt: It was the same in the days of Lot. People were eating food and [drinking] water, exchanging things, planting food and building house. 29 But in that day Lot left Sodom, fire and buring stone fell down from heaven like a rain, and destroyed them all.

nama: "Ndenéyameyo Ároene Ambumoene efghon so nufrote. 31 Yéné efghon efe méngo téndon em yau so kényawangi méngo kénjút rokar résaghét. Ndenéyameyo, efe ndawan em yau so néngangotat rokar résaghét. 32 Yafarotati Lotene amafǃ 33 Emo njam yéta yánjo ghérsé yambeta so yétarne, emo njam yétarna yánjo ghérsé so kawanjarngé. 34 Yéndon némaufrotan fe, yéné sémbáran sómbio ár so ekmare ámbiro kémégh sifayan, ámb Ngánjánam so téngwanangé ámb so téfrangongé. 35 Sómbio amaf so néusénanét njamke mérne, ámb so yémon téngwanangé, ámb so téfrangongé." 36 {o{ ...f* {o{17.36 Yau siménj.
bt: It will like this on the day of the Son of Man is revealed. 31 On that day those who are outside of house must not go inside to get things. In the same way, who is in the field must not go back to take things. 32 Rembember Lot's wife! 33 Whoever try to keep his life will lose it, whoever loses his life will save it 34 I tell you, on that night two people will be sleep on the one sleeping place, God will take away one, [and] the other will be left. 35 Two women will prepare food together, he will take one and the other leave. 37 They asked, "Where, Lord?" Yesu answered, "Where is the dead body, there the eagles will gather."

nama: Yesum tawaufrotau yánjo waitogh árét a rengerenge si ewauyafoi yéfe fá so túmén Ngánjánafé norayat a yau so kékmbongi. 2 Yesu norayai, "Ámb tesenan kété témorwén mwighé farorégh ár efe Ngánján férférofnar témorwén utmanéghofnar árfefaf. 3 Kété témorwén ámb kwiré yéné tesen kénjún efe katérnau túmén yáfaf siafé, 'Ta kwaram mbarkánd yéné si éwighmén mék téfnárafé siyénmare.'
bt: Yesu told his following men a parable to show them they should always talk with God and not to stop. 2 Yeus said, "In a town there was a decision maker(judge) who was without-fearing God and without-respecting people. 3 There was a widow in that town who continue to [come] always to him with talk, 'Give me justice in this arguing with enemy.'

nama: "Nák sénko árom yékenai ámb efghot, wénde ambinomén norayai yánjomén, 'Yénd férférofnar wém Ngánjánefaf a utmanéghofnar árfefaf, 5 wénde yéné kwiram túmén wawérsote yénd, yéndon so tinjon yáfaf mbarkánd, ménát yau so nénatérne tafaf túménǃ'"
bt: The judge ignored her to some day, but finally he said to himself, 'I am without-fear to God and without-respect to men, 5 but this widow keeps on bothering me, I will see it that she gets her justice, so that she won't keep on coming to me!'

nama: Féyo Yuré norayai, "Káyárangi kétán némé yéné mbarkándofnar mwighé farorégh ár norayai. 7 Ngánjánam so fété enwarse mbarkánd yánjo waferégh árét, efe nendat yáfaf efogh a sémbár. Yémon yau so túmén ekarote fá. 8 Yéndon némaufrotan fe, yémon so tinjongé nde yémafém mbarkánd yanangi wékeye. Wénde njam Ároene Ambum nénangota, yémon so tawinjongé efe em ndimbal mwighafé yéné mbandan?" Farasi ár a mani kafégh ár
bt: Then the Lord said, "Listen to what that unjust judge says. 7 God surely will bring about justice for his chosen men, who cry out to him day and night. He will not continually deny them. 8 I tell you, he will see that they receive justice quickly. But the Son of Man comes back, will he find anyone with big mind(faith) on earth? Farasi men and tax collectors

nama: Yesum tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si ámb ároefaf efe tawewonawét ndené kemnawét ndené yénd yéném mbarkánd ár a fá yau: 10 "Sómbio ár nuwaneyayénd kétán wáwefnogh méngot Ngánjánafé oraighét, ámb témorwén Farasi ár a ámb témorwén mani kafégh ár. 11 Farasi ár nokayoi Ngánjánafé koraiwét, 'Ngánján, kánjo fe ndené yénd yau wém ndené ámb ár kés em, oro téfnár, yaufi wafrogh ár, amaf wanégh ár, o ndenanit yéné mani kafégh ár kés em. 12 Yénd nawafarmetan sómbio efogh ámbiro Safatan a yéndon túmén ramégh yam so yafrotam Mosesene nákam noraye Ngánjáneyot.'
bt: Yesu told this parable to some one who look down as they think, 'I am straight man and he is not,' 10 "Two men went to the worshipping house to talk with God, one was Farasi man and the other is tax collecter. 11 Farasi man stood [and] talked with God, 'God, thank you that I am not like other men, robbers, sinners, adulterers, or like that tax collecter over there. 12 I fast two days a week, and I always give offering [that] Moses law require for God.

nama: "Wénde mani kafégh ár takayongérwén nayu. Fá yau néwewoi ndenáyo njafarot, wénde yánjo kafan nasmai a norayai, 'Ngánján, ghérghérsisé tayot, yénd yaufi ár wém.'
bt: But the tax colletor stood a distance. He would not even look on to heaven, but beat his breast and said, 'God, mercy on me, I am a sinner.'

nama: "Yéndon némndetan fém, yéné yaufi ár, yau ndené Farasi ár, fá kangotau méngot áuwéghafé Ngánjánene sáfrére. Fém njam nawarmbota ámb árfefaf, fém so kémeworngi, a fém njam yuwan ném, fém so ném tukén." Ambumtértér a Yesu
bt: I tell you, this sinner, not Farasi man, he went back home with joy in front of God. If you exalt yourself to other people, you will be put down, and if you are down yourself, you will be ontop." The children and Yesu

nama: Árém tanérsawét ambumtértér Yesuefaf ménát frengéghét. Njam waitogh árém yinjoyénd yéné, yémofem ekmboyénd yéfefaf. 16 Wénde Yesum emotai ambumtértér yáfaftio norayai, "Tanumyongi ambumtértér kénaflangi tafaf, yau ekmbotati ambumtértér ménamén Ngánjánene limánégh siyém ndené árét ambumtértér kés em. 17 Yéndon efalo si ewaufrotan, ámb árom emo yau tanangé Ngánján limánégh ndenanit ambumtértér sem yau so kényawangi." Wemár ár
bt: People brought children for Yesu to bless. When the following man saw this, they stop them. 16 But Yesu called children to himself and said, "Let children to come to me, don't stop children because the ruling of God is for the people like a children. 17 I tell you the truth, anyone who does not receive the ruling of God like a children cannot enter it." The rich man

nama: Ámb Ju sénko árom yémotai fá, "Mer watamegh ár, némé yéndon so yafrotan tuot ghérsé résaghét?"
bt: A Juwish head man asked him, "Good teaching man, what should I do to take the eternal life?"

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Némamén wémndete yénd mer? Efe yau mer yém fá Ngánjánro. 20 Fém mat ném náknák, 'Amafanyo wanégh yau, ár ngi yau, oro yau, unyégh yau, eutmanwé afé a amé.'"
bt: Yesu answered, "Why do you call me good? Who is no good only God himself. (There no good except God alone.) 20 You know the commandments, 'No adultery, don't kill people, don't steal, don't lie, respect father and mother.'"

nama: Yémo yémndai, "Kétándmayo yénd tosé kwémorwén, yéndon yéné méinyotio nák tawarawénawén." 22 Yesum njam náyárai yéné, fá norayai yáfaf, "Fefaf mé náláite ámb yam wafroghét. Etérna maniot fene némé em méinyotio, ewarameta yéfeyot yauyau árét, a fém so ném wemár njafaran. Féyo kénaf, kwénmaito yénd."
bt: He said, "Since I was baby, I obeyed all these laws."

nama: Yémon njam náráyai yéné si, fá nawinjoi ghérghérsisafé ménamén fá témorwén minde wemár ár. 24 Yesum yéfandai a yémndai, "Nderé táikér yém ndené wemár árét Ngánjánene limánégh élaughétǃ 25 Taikre kemole élawégh nidul fénemé siyém ndimbal, wénde taikre wemár ároe élaughét Ngánjánene limánéghét yéné yam yáinde."
bt: When he heard this talk, he saw with sadness because he was very rich man. 24 Yesu looked at him and said, "How hard it is for the rich to enter the ruling of God! 25 The difficulty of a camel to go through the needle's hole is big, but the difficulty of a rich man to enter the kingdom of God surpasses that thing."

nama: Fá efe náyárayénd yéné ámoghan, "Efe so wét kunjarngé?"
bt: Those who heard on this asking, "Who can be saved?"

nama: Yesum ewawangoi, "Némé táikér yém árét Ngánjáneyot siyém áfrat."
bt: Yesu answered, "What is hard to man is easy to God."

nama: Fité norayai yáfaf, "Yéndfem enfrangongém méinyotio téfene némé tam fém némaitongémǃ"
bt: Fitè said to him, "We left all of what we have, [and] followed you!

nama: Yesu norayai yéfefaf, "Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe efaloe, yéndon mat eyátotan méinyotio ár emo efrango méngo, amaf, yanaf, yárefaf, ambumtértér Ngánjánene limánégh téfnan 30 so kor ewinjotati tárfár mbinotmé sénonjo yéné ghérayan, a so akegh ghérsé tinjongi Ngánjáneta soramé yuwon." Yesu wéikor korayau yánjo kérmén
bt: Yesu said to them, "I tell you truly, I know every people who has left house, wife, big brothers, parents, children for the reason of the ruling of God 30 will see many times today in this life, and will see eternal life from God on next age(in the future)." Yesu said again his death

nama: Yesum ewasroi 12 waitogh ár téndot a emndai fá, "Yénd kuwanongém kétán Jerusalemét, a méinyotio yéné yéfarotaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém Ároene Ambumoenemén so nufrote. 32 Fá so téramangi Téndo árfefaf, a yémofem so si yawafrotat, yasalitat fá, mbérme yéfarndat. 33 Yémofem so yéfmotat a yésmetat krot. Nambio efogh soramé fá wéikor so kanongé."
bt: Yesu took 12 following men aside, and told them, "We are going to Jerusalem, and everything is written this by God's people who make know(prophets) about the Son of Man will happen. 32 He will be handed over the outside men, and they will mock him, insult him, spit on him. 33 They will flog him and kill him. After three day he will rise again." 34 But following men didn't understand about this. Its meaning was hidden from them, and they did not know what he was talking about. Yesu healed a blind beggar

nama: Njamandio Yesum tánetau Jeriko, ghékéf kwéfkwéf ár témorangérwén end téndon kómnendau. 36 Njam yémon eyárai ár mérén álighan kawaindawét, fá namotai némé yam nufrote. 37 Yémofem yaufroyénd yá, "Yesu Najaretmén siyém nawainde."
bt: As Yesu approched Jeriko, a blind man was sitting beside the road, begged. 36 When he heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening. 37 They told him, "Yesu of Najaret is passing by."

nama: Fá nutnayéng, "Yesu, Deifidene ambum, so ghérghérsisé ném tafafǃ"
bt: He cried out, "Yesu, Son of Deifid, have mercy on me!"

nama: Fá efe tangmorwén endmé yarnayénd fá a yémndayénd, "Métar kékmboǃ" Wénde fá yéméindayénd minde keyot, "Deifidene ambum, so ghérghérsisé ném tafafǃ"
bt: Those who was in the way rebuked him and told him, "Be quiet!" But he shouted with more big sound, "Son of Deifid, have mercy on me!"

nama: Yesu nékmboi a tamndangé ár yáfaf ménjaghét. Njam nénaflai kaka, Yesum témotangé fá, 41 "Némé wafroghét ménde ném tafaf?" Yémon yawangoi, "Yuré, yénd ménde wém éreghét."
bt: Yesu stopped and ordered the man to bring him to him. When he came near, Yesu asked him, 41 "What do you want me to do for you?" He said, "Lord, I want to see!"

nama: A Yesum yémndai fá, "Féyotaro so kéretaǃ Fenjo ndimbal mwighayam némanjar fém." 43 Táf fété fá ghékéf néretai a yémon yáitoi Yesu, tuwetau Ngánján. Njam méinyotio árém yinjoyénd, yémofem wéi ndenáyo Ngánján tuwetawét.
bt: Yesu said to him, You will able to see! Your big mind(faith) saves you." 43 Immediately his eyes was opened and he followed Yesu, praised God. When all the people saw it, they praised God in a same way.

nama: Yesu téngmorwén Jerikomé. 2 Ár yétkwén kété témorwén Jekies, fá témorwén mani kafégh sénko ár efe témorwén wemár. 3 Fá ménde témorwén Yesue fandaghét, wénde ndémbndémb ármén yau yinyoyéng ménamén ár mérén kénjún takayongérwén. 4 Féyo fá frondeyo nengoi a narmboi ndimbal wénan ndenanit wanj wén Yesue winjoghét, táf Yesu mé téngmorwén yéné endmé.
bt: Yesu was going through Jeriko. 2 There was a man name Jekies, he was the chief tax collector who was rich. 3 He wanted to see Yesu, but he was a little man [so] he can't see him because he surrounded in crowd 4 So he ran ahead and climbed on a big tree like a wanj tree to see Yesu, while Yesu was coming that way.

nama: Njam Yesu nufaryéng yéné sifayan, Yesu néwewoi tukét a yémndai fá, "Jekies, kénewor wékeye yuwot. Fene méngon sénonjo so wémor." 6 Féyo fá néneworyéng wékeye a Yesu áuwéghafé yumyoi yánjo méngot.
bt: When Yesu arrived at that palce, Yesu looked up him and said to him, "Jekiyes, come down quickly. I must stay at your house today." 6 So he came down quickly and welcomed him to his house with joy.

nama: Méinyotio árém yinjoyénd yéné yam a mikemike nufngoyénd, "Fá néngwanoi wan téfnár náyátoi yaufi ároefaf."
bt: All the people saw this thing and began to grumble, "He went to become the guest to a sinner."

nama: Wénde Jekies nokayoi a norayai Yurafaf, "Kéreta, Yuréǃ Sénonjo yénandmé yéndon efalotan néndkéfan tane rokar yauyau árét, a yénd njam té kuindan árfefaf rokarmén, so ewangotan yéfene fai fo mbinotma."
bt: But Jekies stood and said to Lord, "Look, Lord! Now and here I divide half my things [to give] to the poor, and if I have cheated to the people anything, I will pay back 4 times their payment."

nama: Yesu norayai yáfaf, "Sénonjo wanjarégh yém yéné méngot, ménamén yéné ár siyém Ebraamene ákimén. 10 Ménamén Ároene Ambum yénmormén ronjaghét olingégh ár wanjarghét." Rengerenge si ten faiofnar ásáfogh árfenemén
bt: Yesu said to him, "Today salvation is into this house, because this man is Ebraam's descendant. 10 For the Son of Man came to look for and to save the lost." The parable of ten workers

nama: Njam fá káyáretawét yénémamén, yémon tawawaitotau rengerenge si ménamén fá témorwén Jerusalem kaka a ár kemnawét ndenénde Ngánjánene limánégh kar sélngwéle so kufarngai. 12 Fá norayai, "Ámb sénko ár nuwanoi nayu yuwot ménát fáyo náyátoi warsagh ár a so wéi nénangote. 13 Féyo yémon emotai ten faiofnar ásáfogh ár a yéfeyot ewarametam ámb rérero ndimbal mani. Fá norayai, 'Takmangi yéné mani ásáfoghét kétánotio yénd nénangotan.'
bt: When they were listening to this, he preached the parable because he was close to Jerusalem and people thought that the ruling of God might appear suddenly. 12 He said, "A head man went away to a distant country so that he himself become a king and will come back again. 13 So he called ten workers and gave each of them same big amount money. He said, 'Fut this money to work until I come back.'

nama: "Wénde yánjo árém méndméndat tafandawét a ewáfáretam fénaté ásáfogh mérén soramé fá norayayénd, 'Yénd méndefnar yéném yéné ár sái kamndangé téfene warsagh ár.'
bt: But his people saw him with hate and sent different woking group(delegation) after they said, 'We don't want this man will become our king.'

nama: "Fá náyátoi warsagh ár féyo fá nénangotam méngotuot. Féyo yémon ewáfáretam faiofnar ásáfogh ár kétán endefet mani ewarametam ménát matot njénamb mani ewafrotaménd.
bt: "He became a king so he return to village(kingdom). Then he sent for the workers to whom he had given the money in order to know how much money they made.

nama: "Fronde ámbiro ár yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene maniam té efuna ámb ten.'
bt: The first one came and said, "Lord, your money has earned ten times more.'

nama: "Yáne yuram yawangoi, 'Mer yém. Tane mer faiofnar ásáfogh árǃ Ménamén fém té efalo náyáto némbne mani funaghan, fémon so ewanjete ten ndimbal tesen.'
bt: His owner answered, 'That's good, my good worker! Because you have become true(faithful) in a very small money work, you will take care of ten cities.'

nama: "Sómbiomén yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene maniam té efuna ámb 5.'
bt: The second came and said, 'Lord, your mina has earned 5 times more.'

nama: "Yáne yuram yawangoi, 'Fémon so ewanjete 5 ndimbal tesen.'
bt: His owner answered, 'You will take care of 5 cities.'

nama: "Nambiomén faiofnar ásáfogh ár yénmormén a norayai, 'Yuré, fene mani siyém. Yéndon fene mani tamndan a etnoyén sáláme sutafé. 21 Yénd fenemén férfér kwémorwén ménamén fém ném táikér ár. Fémon totér némé yau yélawai a yétane némé yau yiyáyéng.'
bt: The third worker came and said, "Lord, here is your money. I kept your money and hid them with clothe. 21 I was afraid of you because you are a hard man. You take what did not put in and reap what you did not plant.'

nama: "Yánjo yuram yawangoi, 'Yéndon so kémawitérmbon fe fenjo sieyo, fém yaufi faiofnar ásáfogh árǃ Fém mat ném ndené yénd wém táikér ár a yéndon ewangotan árkéméné yau em tane a etanan yéné árkéméné yéndon yau eitamén. 23 Némamén wét tane mani yau yélawai mani funagh sifayan ménát yénd njam nénangotan, yéndon so tinjongén minaghkaf?'
bt: His owner answered, 'I will judge you by your own talk, you are wicked worker! You knew that I am hard man and I take back what isn't mine and I harvest what I have not planted.' 23 Why didn't put my money in the place of producing money(bank) so that when I come back, I can collect it with interset?'

nama: "Féyo yémon fá emndai efe tawakayongérwén kaka, 'Takawangi yáne mani yá tambén a taramangi yá efe ten maniafé yém.'
bt: Then he said to those who were standing close, 'Take his money from him and give it to the one who has ten moneys.'

nama: "Fá norayayénd, 'Yuré, té náyátongi ten mani yáneǃ'
bt: They said, 'Lord, his has already become ten moneys!'

nama: "Yémon tawangongé, 'Yéndon némndetan ndenáye méinyotio endene tárfár em, yá so taramangi tárfár, wénde endene yau em, so takawangi. 27 Wénde tane mék téfnár efe em méndefnar fá em yénd sái wém yéfene warsagh ár, tanwasrongi a tásmetangi tane sáfrére.'" Yesu warsagh ár nélawete Jerusalemét
bt: He answered, 'I tell you that to every people who has many, will be given to them many, but those who has nothing, will be taken away. 27 But my enemies who without-want methat I will be their king, bring them and kill them in front of me.' Yesu enter Jerusalem as a king

nama: Yéné si soramé Yesu téngmorwén frondeyo Jerusalemét. 29 Njam kaka náyátoi Betefes a Betani mband tunakéf yu yétkwén olifén, yémon ewáfáretangeyai yánjo sómbio waitogh ár, emndeyai fá, 30 "Kuwanend méngotuot fronde, njam kélawend, so tinjend ndonki rényéghkaf, efe yau yéfayongérmén. Ténferend a yénémnjét yénan. 31 Ámb árom njam tamotangé, 'Némat fémofem yéferét?' taufrend yá, 'Yuré ménde yém.'"
bt: After Yesu said this he went in front of [them] to Jerusalem. 29 When he became close to Betefes and Betani on the mountain called Olif, he sent his two following men, said to them, 30 "Go into to the village first, when you enter it, you will find donkey tied, who didn't ride. Untie it and bring it here. 31 If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying it?' say to him, 'The Lord needs it.'"

nama: Yéné efe ewafareméng nuwaneyayénd a yinjeyayénd ndernáye té engémndeyai fá. 33 Njamandio yémofem téferwét ndonki, yéné yuram emotangeyai fá, "Némamén fémofem yéferét ndonki?"
bt: Those who were sent went and found it how he had told them. 33 As they untied donkey, that owners asked them, "Why are you untying donkey?"

nama: Yémofem yawangeyayénd, "Yurane ménde yém."
bt: They answered, "It is Lord' need."

nama: Yémofem yénémnjeménd Yesuefaf, yéfenjo sáláme efayeyayénd ndonkiyan a Yesu nafayoi yéné tukén. 36 Njamandio Yesu téngmorwén, árém yéfenjo sáláme tangéfyangotawét endmé.
bt: They brought it to Yesu, threw their clothes on the donkey and Yesu got on that. 36 As Yesu went, people spread their clothes on the road.

nama: Fá njam téngmorwén kaka néngamndai yéné sifayan ánde end ngewonau Olif Mband tunakfot, méinyotio Yesuene waitogh ár nufngoyénd wagh áuwéghafé Ngánjánefaf marat kéme, téfén ménamén sélngwélafé ásáfogh téfandawét Yesuefaf:
bt: When he came near and reached on that place where [road] sloped down to the Olive mountain, all of Yesu's following men began song/praise with joy to God in loud voices, reason because they had seen special workings(miracles) from Yesu:

nama: "Frengégh ném warsagh ár efe néném Yurane yétkwénanǃ Krufrat njafar tukén a ndimbal kwayaghé minde tukén!"
bt: "Blessed are you the king who comes in the name of the Lord! peace in heaven and big light(glory) in the very high(highest)!"

nama: Ámb Farasi ár kénjún norayayénd Yesuefaf, "Watamegh ár, táwárna fene waitogh árǃ"
bt: Some of the Farasi men in the crowd said to Yesu, "Teacher, rebuke your following men!"

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, "Yéndon emndetan fém, njam fá métar ekmbo, wím so nutnatǃ"
bt: He answered, "I tell you, if they keep silent, the stones will cry out."

nama: Njam Yesu kaka ufarghétro témorwén Jerusalemét, yéngwinjoi yéné tesen, Yesu táf ye nufngoi yénémamén 42 a norayai, "Jerusalemén ár, yénd ménde wém fém mat kamndangi sénonjo némé yam krufrát yénérse féfefaf, wénde yau mat em. Ménamén ténoghe yém kétándmé féfene ghékéfta. 43 Efogh yéna eném féfeyot njam féfene mék téfnáram so tawarendangi fém a tawanawangi féfe méinyotio téndomé. 44 Yémofem so tawalindangi fém kétán mbandot, fém a ambumtértér fene sifayan. Yémofem ámb yau so taurongi wím kwéndkwéndan yaufifi, ménamén fémofem yau yáyamngongi Ngánjánene wanjarégh féfeyot." Yesu wáwefnogh méngon
bt: When Yesu was arriving close to Jerusalem, he saw the city, Yesu began to weep about this [city] 42 and said, " Jerusalem people, I want you to know today what things bring peace to you, but you didn't know. Because it is hidden from your eyes. 43 The days will come to you when your enemies will surround you and block you from every side. 44 They will destroy you to the ground, you and your children in your place. They will not leave a stone on another, because you did not recognize God's salvation for you. Yesu in the worshipping house

nama: Féyo Yesu nélawai wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a wifarégh ewafngoi emofem rokar téwáfnamotawét. 46 Fá norayai yéfefaf, "Buk sian yékai, 'Tane méngo so yém Ngánjánafé orayégh méngo,' wénde fémofem yiyátongi oro téfnárafene átnogh yékw."
bt: Then Yesu entered into the worshipping house courts and began to chase out [the people]who were exchanging things. 46 He said to them, "It is written in the God's talk, 'My house will be house of talking with God.' but you made it robber hidding hole.'

nama: Méinyotio efghon fá témorwén watameghan wáwefnogh méngon. Wénde limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár, nák watamegh ár a limánégh ár ménde tamorwén Yesue ngiot. 48 Wénde yémofem yau end ewinjoyénd yá ngiot, ménamén méinyotio árfene mwighé tarnyangérwén yáne sifaf.
bt: Everyday he was in teaching at the worshipping house. But the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests), the teaching men of the law and the head men wanted to kill Yesu. 48 But they couldn't find a way to kill him, because all the people's mind tied up his talk.

nama: Ámb efghon yémon njam tawatametau ár wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a táitotau mer si, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a nák watamegh ár nafghayénd yémafe ámb sénko árfé, enmormén yáfaf. 2 Yémofem norayayénd, "Ténmaufro némé muyaye fémon ewafrote yéné ásáfogh. Emon fe yéné muyé némaramai?"
bt: One day when he was teaching the people in the worshipping house courts and preaching the good talk, the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) and the teaching men of the law with head people, came to him. 2 They said, "Tell us by what authority you are doing these work. Who gave you this authority?"

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, "Yéndon so ndenéyameyo fém témotangén ámogh si. Ta kwaufrongi, 4 'Jonene walitogh yénmormén njafarta o árta?'"
bt: He answered, "I will also ask you a asking talk(question). Tell me, 4 Jon's baptism comes from heaven or from human?"

nama: Yémofem táruindawét yéfe kénjúnro a korayawét nde, "Njam yénd korayangém 'Kétándmé njafarta,' fá so kamotangé, 'Némamén yau wét fá efalo yiyátongi?' 6 Wénde njam yénd korayangém, 'Kétándmé árta,' méinyotio árém so yénamndat yénd wíme ménamén fá em mat nde Jon témorwén Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár."
bt: They discussed it among themselves and said that, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will ask, 'Why didn't you truly approve(believe) him?' 6 But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us because they knew that Jon was a God's man who make know(prophet)."

nama: Féyo yémofem yawangoyénd, "Yénd mafina yéném fá árkémamén yém."
bt: So they answered, "We don't know where it come from."

nama: Yesu norayai, "Yéndon wét yau so tawaufron féfe némé muyaye yéndon ewafrotan yéné ásáfogh." Rengerenge si ndau wanjengégh téfnárafenemén
bt: Yesu said, "I will not tell you by what authority I am doing these work." Parable about people looking after field(Tenants)

nama: Fá téngmorwén tawawaitotau árét rengerenge si, "Árém yeitam géreif mbwe ménj ndawan, noroi faiofnar ásáfogh árfefaf a nuwanoi teke. 10 Wau yuwon yémon yáfaretam faiofnar ásáfogh ár kétán ndau wanjengégh árfefaf ménát yémofem so téramangi yáyot ámb géreif mbwe ménj mbwe yéné ndaumén, wénde wanjengégh árém tésmetawét fá a yátembayénd firo rokarofnar. 11 Yémon yáfáretam yénamayo faiofnar ásáfogh ár, wénde yéné árém ndenéyameyo tésmetawét, mámé wáiwáiafé yátembayénd firo rokarofnar. 12 Yémon yáfáretam nambiomén a tésmetawét férkafé yitaménd fá.
bt: He went to tell the people this parable, "A man planted a vine tree in the field, left it to the workers and went away for a long time. 10 At harvesting time he sent a worker to people [who] look after the field(tenants) so that they will give him some fruit of vine tree from that field, but the tenants striked him and sent him back empty without things(empty-handed). 11 He sent second worker like that, but they also beated this man and treated with shame and sent him empty-handed. 12 He sent third man and they hit him with blood and threw him.

nama: "Géreif mbwe ménj ndau yuré norayai, 'Yéndon némé so yafrotan? Yéndon so yéngwáfáretan tane ambum efe yéndon yuwetan. Efalo yiyátoi yémofem so yutémndat.'
bt: The owner of the grape fruit vine garden said, 'What shall I do? I will send my son whom I love. Surely they will respect him.'

nama: "Wénde njam ndau wanjengégh árém yinjoyénd fá, yémofem korayawét yéfenjo kénjúnro, 'Yéné siyém ndau yurane ambum yém. Wét yésmetam krotio fá, a ndau rokar so kénamndangi téfene.' 15 Féyo yémofem yitaménd géreif mbwe ménj ndau kénjúta a yesmayénd krotio fá.
bt: But when the tenants saw him, they said to each other, 'This is the son of the owner of the grape fruit vine garden. Let's kill him, and farm things will be ours.' 15 So they threw him out of the grape fruit vine garden and killed him.

nama: Fá so yéném a engésmete krotio yéné ár mérén a tawaramangé géreif mbwe ménj ndau ámb árét." Njam árém náyárayénd yénémamen, fá norayayénd, "Yénandmé yaufifi so yémǃ"
bt: He will come and kill those men and give the grape fruit vine garden to other people." When the people heard about this, they said, "From here it won't be."

nama: Yesu néngretai yéfefaf mbarkándaye a emotai, "Féyo némé fifi yéné yém árkéméné yéfarotaménd, 'Sérasér árké méngo ramégh téfnáram yékenayénd a namndayéng néndkéf sérasér.'
bt: Yesu looked to them straightly and asked, "Then what is the meaning of that which is written, 'The post which the builders rejected and became the middle post.

nama: Méinyotio ár efe njam nénuitat wíman so karéfkaréf nufrotat, a njam kénuyangé árfefaf fá so tásénangé."
bt: Everyone who falls on the stone will be broken pieces, and when it falls on to them, they will be destroyed.'"

nama: Nák watamegh ár a limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár end táronjawét werghét wékeye, ménamén fá mat náyátoyénd nde fá korayau yéné rengerenge si yéfenemén. Wénde fá férfér tamorwén árfenemén. Kaisare mani kémégh
bt: The teaching men of the law and the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) looked for a way to arrest him quickly, because they knew that he spoke this parable about them. But they were afraid of the people. Paying taxes to [Rom high ruling man] Kaisar

nama: Yémofem ghékéf tafayotawét yáyot, a ewáfáretaménd ufénmanégh téfnár efe mermer kuindawét. Fá yéta kemnawét Yesue werghét ménát ámb si so korayangai, ménát yémofem so tramangayénd gafanafaf. 21 Ufénmanégh téfnáram yémotayénd fá, "Watamegh ár, yénd mat yéném ndené fém noraye a ewatamete némé mbarkánd yém a fém yau namnyote árfene mwighafaf, wénde ewatamete Ngánjánene ende efaloyero. 22 Téfeyot mbarkánd yém mani sái ekmetam Kaisare o yau?"
bt: They watched him, and send checking man(spies) who pretend to be good. They tried to think to catch Yesu so that he will say diffent saying, so that they might hand him over to the governor. 21 The spies asked him, "Teacher, I know that you speak and teach what is straight and you are not influenced by people's thinking, but you teach the way of God truthfully. 22 Is it straight for us to put the money to [Rom high ruling man] Kaisar or not?"

nama: Yesum ewawinjoi yéfene unyégh a norayayéng yéfefaf, 24 "Kwauyafongi yáne mani. Endene ár sáf a yétkwén ekayong?" Yémofem yawangoyénd, "Kaisarene."
bt: Yesu saw their lying/trick and said to them, 24 "Show me his money. Whose face and name are these on it?" They reanswered, "[Rom high ruling man] Kaisar's"

nama: Yesum emndai, "Taramangi Kaisare yáne némé yém, a Ngánjáne taramangi némé yém yáne."
bt: Yesu said, "Give to [Rom high ruling man] Kaisar what is his, and give to God what is his."

nama: Fá endofnar namndayénd Yesue werghét némé yaufimén korayau kété árfene sáfrére. Sélngwél emormén yáne uwangoghan, a fá métar namndayénd. Anogh a awerégh
bt: They became without-way to catch Yesu saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer, and they became silent. The rising and marriage

nama: Ámb Saduki ár enmormén efe norayayénd ndené kété ámb yau anogh yém. Fá enmormén Yesuefaf moghét. 28 Fá norayayénd, "Watamegh ár, Mosesém yéfarotam nák téfeyot ndené njam yánngénoene yané kér yém tatembangé amaf wénde ambumofnar, yánngnom fété so kwiré tékayongé a ambum eunjeta yanai.
bt: Some of Saduki men, who say that there is no rising, came to him. They came to ask Yesu. 28 They said, "Teacher, Moses wrote a law for us that if a small brother's big brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, his small brother must marry the widow and must produce children for his big brother.

nama: Kété tamorwén sefen ghakér ambum. Fronde sénkom nawerai a kér yémormén ambumofnar. 30 Yénamayom kwiré nawerai, wénde fá ndenéyameyo kér yémormén, yéfrangoyéng ambumofnar. 31 A nambiomén nawerai kwiré, ndenéyamanro sefen kér emormén, yéfrangoyéng ambumofnar. 32 Soramayo amaf kér yémormén. 33 Anogh efghon endene amaf so yém? Ménamén yéné sefenam té naweretaménd."
bt: There were 7 sons. The first one married and died childless. 30 The second married the widow, but he also died, leaving no children. 31 And the third married the widow, in the same way that 7 died, leaving no children. 32 Lost of them, the woman died. 33 At the resurection whose wife will she be? Because the 7 married her.

nama: Yesum ewawangoi yéfe, "Árém amaf so fété naweretat yéné mbandan. 35 Wénde soramé efghon, fá efe so tawanongé kérta yau so nawerétat. 36 A fá yau kér so em. Fá so ndené kamndangi njafar si résagh árnit. Fá so em Ngánjánene ambumtértér ménamén Ngánjánam so tawanongé kérta. 37 Wénde Mosesém njam yufarotaméng kéghat fés awároghan, yémon yuyafoi ndené kér so nanotat ghérsat. Yémon témotau Yuré 'Ebraamene Ngánján, Aisekene Ngánján a Jeikofene Ngánján.' 38 Fá yau yém kér árfene Ngánján, wénde ghérsé árfene Ngánján, ménamén fá méinyotio ghérsé em yáfaf."
bt: Yesu answered them, "Men will marry in this earth. 35 But in the future, those who can rise from death will not marry. 36 And they will not die. They will be like the men carrying heavenly message(angels). They will be God's children because God will rise them from death. 37 But when Moses wrote about burning bush fire, he showed that the dead will rise to life. He called the Lord 'The God of Ebraam, the God of Aisak and the God of Jeikof.' 38 He is not God of the dead, but God of the living, because they all are alive to him. 39 Some of the teaching men of the law responded, "Well said, teaching man!" 40 And no one[dared to] ask him any question talk. Whose son is Keriso

nama: Yesum emotai fá, "Némamén ndené árém norayat nde Keriso yém Deifidene ambum? 42 Deifid fáyo safén nakmai Sams bukan a norayai, 'Yuré noyarai kétán tane Yurafaf: "Kámnjo yénandmé tane tambén ngángata.
bt: Yesu asked them, "Why [do] people say that Keriso is the son of Deifid? 42 Deifid himself put in clear in the book of Psalms and said, 'The Lord said to my Lord: Sit here at my right hand.

nama: Kétánotio yéndon tákman fene mék téfnár fene kafkaf mbanmbanan."'
bt: until I put your enemies under your feet.'

nama: Deifidém tanmotau fá 'Yuré.' Ndernáye fá yáne ambum so yém?"
bt: Deifid called him 'Lord.' How can he be his son?"

nama: Njamandio méinyotio árém tiyáretawét, Yesum emndai yánjo waitogh ár, 46 "Kor nawarawenati nák watamegh árfenemén. Fá ménde em tombé sálámefé arengét a sái eutémndat térarégh sifayan, a yéfene mwighé témorwén minde mer sifayan ámnjoghét áumengégh méngo kénjún a yémofem méndefé tafandawét limánégh ámnjogh sifayé témndan. 47 Yémofem unyéghe kwirafene rokar tawarsawét a fá mermer kuindawét tombé sie Ngánjánafé orayégh ár sáfan. Ndené árém tékrát so yérsat minde tukén."
bt: As all the people were listening, Yesu said to his following men, 46 "Protect yourselves from the teaching men of the law. They like to walk around with long clothes and to be respected in the markets, and their mind were sitting in the respecting places in the synangogues and they like to find the respecting places at feasts. 47 They take widows' things with cheating and they pretend to be good by making long talking with God in front of people(in public). These men will carry the pains most high.

nama: Njamandio Yesu néngretai, yémon ewinjoi wemár árém takmetawét ramégh rokar wáwefnogh méngon. 2 Yémon yinjoi yauyau kwiré sómbio némbne mani mbwe kémghan. 3 Yémon emndai, "Yéndon efalo ewaufrotan, yéné yauyau kwiram ewaindai fá emofem mani takmetawét. 4 Méinyotio yéné árém takmetawét némbnare tárfár rokarta, wénde kwiram ekmeyai méinyotio yánjo ghérsé ámnjoghmén." Kerker efoghene ufarogh yam
bt: As Yesu looked up, he saw rich people putting offering things in the worshipping house. 2 He saw a poor widow putting in two small coins. 3 He said, "Truly I tell you, this poor widow surpasses them who put the money. 4 All these people put the little things from many things, but widow put every thing for his living." Signs of last days

nama: Ámb yáne waitogh ár tamorwén sian ménamén ndernáye wáwefnogh méngo yamanetaménd mer wíme a ramégh yam wateghkaf Ngánjáne. Wénde Yesu norayai, 6 "Méinyotio fémo némé yéfande yéna, efogh yéna yéném ánde ámbiro wím yau so kafrangongé kétándmé ámbta, méinyotio so ewite yuwot."
bt: Some of his following men were talking about how worshipping house decorated with good stone and offered things to dedicate God. but Yesu said, 6 "Everything what you see here, the day come where one stone will not leave from another people, everything will be thrown down to land."

nama: Fá namotayénd, "Watamegh ár, njam so yéné yam nufrotat? A némé yamam so tufarotangé ménát yéné yam ufroghétro em?"
bt: They asked, "Teacher, when will these things happen? And what will be the surprising thing in order that this things are about to happen?"

nama: Yémon ewawangoi, "Kor fété aem fém warsoghmén. Mwighé sénté ménamén tárfár ár so eném tane yétkwénan, ndené siafé, 'Yénd wém Yesu,' a 'Efogh siyém kaka yém.' Yau ewaitotati fá. 9 Njam fém náyáretati mékmén a sélngwélafé yammén, yau táf férfér em. Yéné yam fété so nufrotat frondeyo, wénde mbandoene yátogh yau mé so yéném."
bt: He answered, "Be careful of deceiving. Be alert because many people will come in my name, with talk that, 'I am Yesu,' and 'The day is near.' Don't follow them. 9 When you hear of wars and surprising things, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but land's end will not come yet.

nama: Féyo fá norayai yéfefaf, "Ámb yu a ámb yu, fá so násmét, ámb ndimbal yu a ámb ndimbal yu, fá so násmét. 11 Kété so yém ndimbal sokrere, káukáu, ndimbal enjne tárfár yuwomé a férférafé yam a ndimbal sélngwélafé yam njafaran.
bt: Then he said to them, "One nation and other nation, they will fight, one kingdom and other kingdom, they will fight. 11 There will be big earthquakes, and famines, serious deseases in many places and fearful events and big surprising thing in heaven.

nama: "Wénde yémofem so kémerangi a kértayan kénékmangi fém soramé yéné yam so nufrotat. Fém so némumbetat áumengégh méngon a kémarmbnangi kwéfkwéf méngot, a néngemnjat warsagh ár a gafanafene sáfrére tane yétkwén téfnan. 13 Wénde yéné so fiyém efoghféneféne si waitoghét fene ndimbal mwighamén. 14 Wénde fém mwighayam yau némaworsota frondeyo ndernáye fém so nawamarote fémyo. 15 Yéndo so kémaramangén si a káfeté mwighé ménát fene mék téfnáram yau so kémitrombongé o kénémndangé fém ném unyégh ár. 16 Fém so sén erametat afafemyo, amafemyo, anafemyo, mérnamyo, fútaramyo a yémofem fém so ámb krot ekmetat. 17 Méinyotio árém fém so méndméndat efandat ménamén tanemén. 18 Wénde fene ámbiro sénko séf yau so kolindangé. 19 Fém so taikrataye ewakai ménát fémo so tinjo efalo ghérsé.
bt: But they will hand you over and put you into suffering after [this] these things will happen. You will be tied in meeting houses and you will be put in darkness house, and you will be taken in front of ruling men(kings) and governers for my name. 13 But this will be time to tell about your big mind(faith). 14 But don't worry in the mind beforehand how will defend yourselves. 15 I will give you talk and wisdom in order that your enemies will not remove you or tell you are liar. 16 You will be gave teeth(betrayed) by fathers, mother people, brothrs, family, friends and they will put some [of you] to death. 17 Everyone will see you with hate because of me. 18 But your one hair of your head will not be lost. 19 You will stand firm in order that you will see real life. 20 When you see Jerusalem being surrounded by soldiers, you will know that its destruction is not.far. 21 Know [those] who are Judia people must go to the mountain, [those] who are in the city must go out, [those] who is outside of city must not enter the city. 22 Those day become with body(fulfilled) which it has been written about suffering. 23 How painful it will be pregnant women these day and nursing mother people! There will be big suffering in the land and anger to this people. 24 They will be cut by the bush knife to kill and other people will be taken as darkness house men(prisoners) to all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled by Side people until outside man's day are finished. 25 There will be surprising things in the sun and moon and stars. On the earth, through all nations will be mental pain and in worring because wave roaring sound of the sea. 26 People fell down with fear, because pain what comes to the land, because the body of heaven will be shaken. 27 In that day they will see the Son of Man coming back in a cloud with power and big big light(glory). 28 When these things began to happen, stand up and lift up your head, because your salvation is near.

nama: Yémon tawaufrotau yéné rengerenge si, "Téfandangi fig wén a méinyotio wén. 30 Njam wén mbambam éwiwogh kufngongi, fémofem so tinjongi féfendio a mat kamndangi nde efogh yu siyém kaka yém. 31 Yéné endeyo, njam yefandati yéné yam ufroghan, fém mat so kamndangi nde Ngánjánene limánégh siyém kaka yém.
bt: He told them this parable, "Look at the fig tree and all the trees. 30 When they begin to come up new leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that sunny season is near. 31 Like this, when you see these things happening, you will know that the ruling of God is near.

nama: "Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe efalo si, ámb ár mérén yéné satamén so mé em ghérsé njam méinyotio yam nufrotat. 33 Njafar a mband so kawaindangend, wénde tane si yaufifi so kawaindangé.
bt: I tell you the truth, some of the people of this generation will still be arive when all the things happens. 33 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my talks will never pass away.

nama: "Kor nawarawenati, féfenjo tikéf yau ewaghnati wéri nuye, wagh témnde a mbandoene méghméghe ménamén yéné efgham so táf kémerangé sunsune ndenanit urérnit. 35 Ménamén yéné efogh so yéném méinyotio yéfefaf efe emor yéné mbandan. 36 Aya túmén ghékéf sénté aem néretati a Ngánjánafé orayégh ménát fém so kawanjarngi kétándmé némé ufroghét em, a fém so taikrataye ewakai Ároene Ambum sáfrére."
bt: Be careful, don't let your hearts be dulled by drinking alcohol and singing feast and by world mind because this day will arrive suddenly like a trap. 35 Because this day will come on all those who live in this earth. 36 Always watch out and talk with God that you can be saved from what are happing, and you can stand firmly in frnt of the Son of Man."

nama: Méinyotio efghon Yesu témorwén watameghan wáwefnogh méngon, a fá tangmorwén méinyotio sémbáran Olif Mband tunakfan. 38 Méinyotio ár tanmorwén túmén tándái akwan áyárghét yáfaf wáwefnogh méngot.
bt: Every day Yesu was in teaching at the worshipping house, and he spent every night on the mount of Olif. 38 All the people came early each morning to hear him at the worshipping house.

nama: Minaghofnar flawamén téménd, yétkwén Waingégh téménd kaka kénamndetau. 2 Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a nák watamegh árém ámb end táronjawét Yesue werghét, ménamén fá férfér tamorwén árfenemén. 3 Féyo Sáifér nélawai Judas Iskariotefaf, efe ámbiro nafaloi 12 ármén. 4 A Judas téngmorwén kétán limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfefaf a wáwefnogh méngo wanjengégh árfefaf a taruindawét yémafé ndernáye yémon so sén téramangai Yesu. 5 Fá káuwétawét a nuwangoyénd mani ramghét yáyot. 6 Judas nuwangoi a kéretau end táronjau Yesue werghét árofnar sifayan. Soramé njamke
bt: The festival of unleavened/without yeast flawè, called Passover became near. 2 The leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) and the teaching men of the law were looking for catching Yesu, because they were afraid of the people. 3 Then Sáifèr entered Judas Iskariot who was one of 12 following men. 4 And Judas went the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) and worshipping house guarders and discussed with them how he might give teeth(betray) Yesu. 5 They were pleased and offered to pay him some money. 6 Judas went back and looked for the way to catch Yesu in place without-people. The last feast

nama: Efogh nawerai Minaghofnaye flawamén árké yém Waingégh sif tosane ghéngénmén. 8 Yesum ewafareméng Fité a Jon a norayai, "Kuwanend yéfrengéghét Waingégh njamke téfe nénet."
bt: The day came for unleavened flawè which is Passover lamb being sacrifice. 8 Yesu sent Fitè and Jon and said, "Go to prepare for us to eat Passover food."

nama: Yémofem yémotangeyayénd, "Ándan fene ménde yém téfeyot frengéghét?"
bt: They asked, "Where do you want us to prepare for it?"

nama: Yesum ewawangeyai, "Njam ngélawend tesenot, ár so tinjend nu séfkwénafé yémon so tafange. Fémofem téngwaitend árké méngot njam ngélawangé, 11 a témndend méngo yuré, 'Watamegh árém namote, "Ánde yém wan téfnároene méngo saté, ánde yéndon so enetan Waingégh njamke tane waitogh árfé?"' 12 Yémon so tawauyafé féfe ndimbal méngo saté tukén, méinyotio teghkaf yém. Afrengégh kété ayém."
bt: Yesu answered, "When you enter the city, you will see a man carrying a jar of water. You follow him the house that he enters, 11 and say to the owner of the house, 'Teacher asks, "Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover food with my following men?"' 12 He will show you a largh room upstairs, all furnished. Be ready(Prepare) there."

nama: Fá néwaneyayénd rokar ewinjeyayénd ndenáye fété Yesum ndernáye tamndéwéng. Féyo yémofem yéfrengéménd Waingégh njamke.
bt: They left [and] found things like as Yesu how told them. So they prepared the Passover food.

nama: Njam efoghféneféne nawerai, Yesu a yánjo muyafé ár nénámnjoyénd njamke sifayan. 15 A Yesum emndai fá, "Yénd minde ménde kwém njamke nénet fémafé soramé tékrát so yéfandan. 16 Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe, yéndon yau so enetan wéikor kétánotio yéné nufrota Ngánjánene limánéghan."
bt: When the time came, Yesu and his men with Yesu's authority(apostles) sit at the eating place. 15 And Yesu said them, "I wanted very much to eat the Passover food with you after [this] I will see the suffering. 16 I tell you, I will not eat again until this fulfill in the ruling of God.

nama: Soramé kafusi yerai, yémon kánjo yaramai a norayai, "Tawanangi yéné a tafalotangi féfene kénjúnro. 18 Yéndon ewaufrotan féfe yéndon yau so yéfértan wéri nu kor kétánotio Ngánjánene limánégh kénufarngé."
bt: After taking a cup, he gave thanks and said, "Take this and divide it among you. 18 I tell you I will not repeat [to drink] alcohol again until the ruling of God arrives."

nama: Yémon féskaf flawé yerai, kánjo yaramai a etfoi, a ewaramai, yéné siafé, "Yéné siyém tane fifi, árkéméné so téraman féfeyot. Yéné yafrotati awafaroghét tanemén."
bt: He took flawè(bread), gave thanks and broke it, and gave it, with this talk, "This is my body which is given for you. Do this for remembering me."

nama: Ndené endeyo, njamke soramé yémon yerai kafusi, ndené siafé, "Yéné kafusi siyém totér rényégh tane férék kénjún árké so nasune féfeyot. 21 Wénde yéné ár emo yénd so sén wéramuete siyém yéna yéndfé yénmorare. 22 Ároene Ambum so kér yém Ngánjánene méndeyan, wénde nderé mámáfi yém yéné ároe emo sén yéramete." 23 Fá ámogh nufngoyénd yéfenjo kénjúnro efe yéna yafnyang téfefaf emon so sén yéramete.
bt: In the same way, after the supper he took a cup, saying this, "This cup is the new without.lying talk(promise) in my blood which is poured out for you. 21 But the man who will be give teeth(betray) me is with me here. 22 The Son of Man will die on the God's want/will, but how terrible that man who betrays him!" 23 They began to ask among themselves who is here who would give teeth(betray).

nama: Féyo éwigh nufngoyénd yéfenjo kénjúnro efe so yém ndimbal. 25 Yesu norayai yéfefaf, "Téndo árfene warsagh ár yuré kamndangi waftafé yéfenjo ár limánghét, wénde fá emndetat, 'Fútar em árfene.' 26 Wénde fém yau so ndené em. Wénde efe ndimbal yém so yém ndenanit ambumtosé, a efe limánégh ár yém fá so yém ndenanit faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 27 Efe yém ndimbal, yéné ár efe yémor kitarayan o yéné ár efe faiofnar ásáfogh ár yém? Yéné ár efe yémor kitarayan. Wénde yénd wáféin féfefaf faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 28 Fém fá em efe tanwakai tafaf tane ufoghan. 29 A Yéndon ewaraman féfe wafté Ngánjánene limánghét ndené fété tane Afam wafté waramai ta, 30 ménát fémofem so enetati njamke a nu tane kitarayan Ngánjánene limánéghan, a so emorang limánégh ámnjogh sifayan, Israilmén 12 ár mérén moghan so ewakai. Yesu norayai Fitane kenaghmén
bt: Then they started to argue among them who will be big. 25 Yesu said to them, "The ruling men(kings) of Side people become owner with power to rule their people over, but they said, 'People's friend are.' 26 But you will not be like that. But who is big must be like a child, and who is head man must be like worker. 27 Who is big, the one who is at the table or the one who is worker? It is the one who is at the table. But I am among you who is worker. 28 You are those who have stood by me in my trials. 29 And I gave you the power for the ruling of God just as my Father gave me the power, 30 so that you will eat and drink in my table in the ruling of God, and will sit on high places, [and] will stand to judge 12 tribes of Israil. Yesu say Fite's denial

nama: Yémon yémndai, "Saimon, Saimon, Ngánjánam muyé yaramai Sáifére fe ufoghét ndenanit ndau téfnáram yafrote witrémboghét kétándmé mer mbweta yaufiot. 32 Wénde yénd Ngánjánafé koraiwam feyot, Saimon, nde fene ndimbal mwighé yau so kuyangé. Fém njam kénawengo tafaf, fémon so muyafé eyátote fengénéf."
bt: Yesu said, "Saimon, Saimon, God gave authority for Sáifèr to test you as a farmer do to remove bad [seeds] from good seeds. 32 But I talked with God for you, Saimon, that your big mind(faith) may not fall. When you have turned back to me, you must strengten your younger brothers. "

nama: Wénde yémon yawangoi, "Yuré, yénd afrengéghkaf wém fémafé álighét kwéfkwéf sifayat a kétán krot."
bt: But he answered, "Lord, I am ready to go with you to darkness house and to death."

nama: Yesum yawangoi, "Yéndon némndetan fém, Fité, fémon so kwékena nambinotmé nde fém mafina ném tanemén soramé ghakér kowi kutnangé sénonjo."
bt: Yesu answered, "I tell you, Fitè, you will deny me three times that you don't know about me after [this] the rooster crows today."

nama: Féyo Yesum emotai fá, "Yéndon njam ewáfáretan fém mani yáfofnaye, awogh yáfofnaye o sandolofnaye, némé rokar ámb náláite?" Yémofem yawangoyénd, "Yau."
bt: Then Yesu asked them, "When I sent you without money bag, without big(traveling) bag or without sandols, what did you miss? They answered, "No[thing]"

nama: Yémon emndai fá, "Wénde táf sénonjo mani yáf tanangi a awogh yáf tawanangi, a njam yau ewam féfene náifé, sálámeyafé tétérnangi ámbiro náifat. 37 Buk si yékai, 'A fá témorwén waféghkaf yaufi árfe kénjún.' Yéndon ewaufrotan féfeyot nde yéné yam efalo so nufrote tafaf. Néméwét, némé yéfarotaménd tanemén so efalo nufrote."
bt: He told them, "But now if you have a money bag and small bag, take it, and if you don't have a knife, exchange clothes with a knife. 37 It is written by God's talk, 'And he was number(was counted) among the sinners.' I tell you that this things must happen to me. Yes, what is written about me must happen."

nama: Waitogh ár norayayénd, "Téfanda, Yuré, sómbio náifé sere." Yémon ewawangoi, "Féyotaro ere." Yesuf Ngánjánafé koraiwét Olif Mband tunakfan
bt: The following men said, "See, Lord, here are two knife." He answered, "That's enough." Yesu talks with God on the mount of Olif

nama: Yesu tiotau túmén mband tunakéf yétkwén Olif, a yánjo waitogh árém yénamayo táitotawét fá. 40 Njam nufaryénd yéné sifayan, yémon emndai fá, "Ngánjánafé norayatati ménát fém yau so kuyangi ufoghan." 41 Fá nitroi nayuofnaye yéfetambén a budme niyai Ngánjánafé oraighét, 42 "Afé, fene ménde téu yém, yéné tékrát titro ta tambén, tane ménde yau, wénde fene méndero so nufrote."
