nama: Fol mat náyátoi ndené ámb ár tamorwén Saduki ár a ámb Farasi ár, fá néngutnayéng ár mérnafefaf, "Tane fútaréf, yénd swém Farasi ár, Farasi árfene ambum. Yénd wakayong ufoghamé ménamén tane méghmégh kérta anoghmén."
bt: Fol realized that some men were Saduki men and other people Farasi men, he shouted to the people, "My brothers, I am Farasi man, the son of a Farasi man. I stand on trial because of my hope of the resrrection of the dead.

nama: Njam fá norayai yéné si, Farasi ár a Saduki ár nafaloyénd a éwigh yérnyayénd. 8 Ménamén Saduki ár korayawét ndené kér ár yau so nanotat ghérsat, yémofem efalo yau tiyátotawét ndené njafar si résagh ár a mbérmbér kété em. Wénde Farasi árém efalo tiyátotawét méinyotio yéné yam.
bt: When he said this talk, Farasi men and Saduki men were divided and started to argument. 8 Because Saduki men say that dead people will not rise from death to life, they don't truly approve(believe) that there are men carrying heavenly message(angels) and spirits. But Farasi men truly approve(believe) all these things.

nama: Yénémamén kété témorwén ndimbal sarake, ámb nák watamegh ár efe tamorwén Farasi ár tawakayongérwén éwigh nufngoyénd, yémofem norayayénd, "Yéndfem yau yinjongém yéné ároene yaufi, némé mbérmbér o njafar si résagh ár korayat yáfaf?" 10 Yéné éwigh minde tukén namndai, yénémamén fiyarafene ndimbal sénko ár férféram yifoi ménamén Fol mété téftérnawét árém. Yémon ewáfáretai fiyaré yéngwanayénd Fol yéfenemén wékeye kétán fiyarafene sifayat.
bt: So there was a lot of noise, some of the teaching men of the law who were Farasi men stood up and started to argue, they said, "We do not see any sin of this man, what if a spirit or man carrying heavenly message has spoken to him?"

nama: Yéné sémbáran Yuré nokayoi kaka Folefaf a yémndai, "Férfér yau némǃ Ndené fété kés noraye tanemén Jerusalem kénjún, ndenéyameyo tanemén so wéi nafrote kété Romén." Yémofem mwighé téfaronawét Fole ngiot
bt: That night the Lord stood near to Fol and said, "Don't be afraid! As you have told about me in Jerusalem, you also speak there in Rom about me. They planned to kill Fol

nama: Ámb akwan ámb Ju ár náumendayénd a unyéghofnar si norayayénd, "Njamke a nu yau so enetam kétánotio Fol kor yésmetam krotio." 13 Kété tamorwén tárfáryo 40 Ju ár tambén emofem mwighé téfaronawét mérne. 14 Fá néngaflayénd limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfefaf a norayayénd, "Yéndfem unyéghofnar si norayam ndené yau so mé enetam kétánotio Fol kor yésmetam krotio. 15 Fém a áumengégh ár tangém kétán fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf a taunyangi ndené ménde yéném kor ronjaghét matot Folenemén. Tamnendangi fá a ténmasrongi fene sáfrére moghét. Yénd yéna so nawanjetam end néndkéfan Fole ngiot."
bt: The next morning some Ju people gathered and said without.lying talk(agreed), "They would not eat and drink anything until they killed Fol." 13 There were many more from(more than) 40 Jews who planned this together. 14 They went to the chief burning sacrifice men and said, "We said without.lying talk(promised) that we will not eat until we have killed Fol. 15 You and the Ju head men's meeting go to the soldiers' big head man and pretend that you want to find information about Fol. Ask for him and bring him before your court. We will be waiting on the way to kill him."

nama: Wénde njam Fole yánjafam náyárai ndernáye Ju ár mwighé kémnawét yá ngiot, fá táf néngaflai fiyarafene sifayat a Fole yaufroi.
bt: But when Fol's nephew(sitster's son) heard how Ju people had decided to kill him, he went to the soldier's place(fortress) and told Fol.

nama: Féyo Folém yémotai ámb fiyaré sénko ár a témndangé, "Téngwasro yéné totér ár kétán fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf, ménamén yáfaf ámb si em yá waufroghét."
bt: Then Fol called one of the soldiers' head man and said, "Take this young man to soldiers' big head man, because he has some talk to tell him."

nama: Féyo yémo yéngémnjoi fiyaré ndimbal sénko ároefaf. Fiyaré sénko árom témotangé, "Fol, kwéfkwéf méngo árom wémnde. Fá ténmasro yéné totér ambum fefaf, ménamén yáfaf ámb si em fe sái kémaufrongé."
bt: So he took him to the soldiers' big head man. The soldiers' head man asked, "Fol, the darkness house man(prisoner), spoke to me. He asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has some talk to tell you."

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom yéngwasroi totér ár téndot ménát ár yau so káyárangayénd a témotangé, "Fene ménde némé yém kwaufro?"
bt: The soldiers' big head man took the young man beside so that people could not to hear and asked, "What do you want to tell me?"

nama: Yémon korayangé, "Ámb Ju árém mwighé téfaronat ménát fém sái kénmotangi Fole wasroghét Ju sénko árfene áumengégh sifayat kaitau. Fá ménde em kor sái tawinjongi mbarkánd si yánemén, wénde fá so nuindat. 21 Yau yafrota némat fá namotangi wafroghét, ménamén tárfáryo 40 árta nawanjetat Fole ngiot. Yémofem unyéghofnar si norayayénd yau njamke a nu nénet kétánotio Fol yésmetat. Sénonjo fá nawanjetat fene uwangogh siotro."
bt: He said, "Some Ju people planed to ask you to bring Fol to the Ju head men's meeting place(Sanedrin) tomorrow. They want to see accurate talk about him but they will tell lies." 21 [Please] don't do what they ask to do, because more than 40 men are waiting for him to kill. They said without.lying talk(promised) that they will not eat and drink any food until they kill Fol. They are now waiting for your answering talk.

nama: Fiyaré ndimbal sénko árom yémndai totér ár, "Yau taufro ende ndené fémo té waufro yéné ta." Féyo yémo yéngwatembai totér ár. Fiyaré sénko árom yáfáretam Fol kétán Kaisariat
bt: The soldiers' big head man said to young man, "Don't tell anyone that you have told this to me." Then he sent the young man away. Soldiers' leader sends Fol to Kaisaria

nama: Yémon emotangeyayai yánjo sómbio fiyaré sénko ár a emndeyai, "Takafend 200 fiyaré, 70 os wérse ár a 200 námb táikér ár so kuwanongi Kaisariat 9 aklok sénonjo sémbár. 24 Ámb os taramend Fole ménát fá merro so néngérse gafana Feliksefaf."
bt: He called two of his soldiers' head men and told them, "Pick 200 soldiers and 70 horse riding men and 200 bow strong people(spearmen) to go Kaisaria at 9 tonight. 24 Privide some horses for Fol so that he may be taken safely to Governor Felix."

nama: Yémo yéné férén yéfarotam ndené,
bt: He wrote this letter like that,

nama: Klaudius Lisias, Kétán fene utmanégh yétkwén Gafana Feliks, Áuwégh yém.
bt: Klaudius Lisias, To your honorable name Governor Felix, Greetings.

nama: Yéné ár yerayénd Ju árém a yémofem ngiot térametawét, wénde yénd tanjo fiyaré mérnafé wénmormén wanjarghét, ménamén yénd mat namndayén ndené fá témorwén Rommén ár. 28 Yénd ménde kwémorwén matot némamén yémofem tánangotawét, yénémamén yéndon yéngwasroyén yéfene sénko árfene áumengégh sifayat. 29 Yéndon yinjon yéné wátaranégh siyém moghan so yém yéfene nákan, wénde kété ámb téfén yau témorwén yáfaf árkémamén krot so yém o kwéfkwéf méngon tényawangi. 30 Njam yénd mat wiyátoyénd yéfene wátaranéghmén krot yéné ároenemén, yéndon táf fété yéngwáfáretayén fefaf.
bt: Ju people hold this man and they were about to kill him, but I came with my troops to rescue him, for I had learned that he was a Roman citizen. 28 I wanted to know why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their Sanedrin/head man's Ju head men's meeting. 29 I saw that this accusation is in the court about their law, but there was no reason to him that he should die or put him into darkness house(Jail). 30 When they let me know their complain to kill him, I immediately sent him to you."

nama: Fiyaram yéfenjo si ewaitoyénd. Yémofem yéngwasroyénd Fol sémbáran a yéngémnjoyénd Antifatris yuwot. 32 Ámb efghon ámb fiyaré nénangotaménd Jerusalemét fiyarafene sifayat, wénde os wérse ár mé tangmorwén Folfé. 33 Njam fá nufaryénd Kaisariat, yémofem yaramayénd férén Gafana Felikse, a yafnamoyénd Fol yáfaf. 34 Gafanam férén yaftam a yémotai árké yumén fá témorwén. Njam fá mat náyátoi ndené Silisiamén témorwén, 35 yémon yémndai, "Yénd so náyáretan fene kérté njam fene wátaranégh ár kufarngi." Féyo yémon ewáfáretai fiyaré Fole wanjengét warsagh ár Erodene méngon.
bt: The soldiers followed their talk. They took Fol in the night and took him to Antifatris area. 32 The next day some soldiers returned to the soldiers place(fortress) in Jerusalem, but horse riding men continued to go with Fol. 33 When they arrived in Kaisaria, they gave this letter to the Governor Feliks and handed Fol over to him. 34 The Governor read the letter and asked him what province he was from. When he learned that he was from Silisia, 35 he said, "I will hear your problem/case when your complaining men/accusers arrive." Then he sent/ordered soldiers to guard in ruling man(king) Erod's house/palace.

nama: Faif efogh soramé limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár Ananias néwanoi Kaisariat yémafé ámb sénko árfé a néndkéf ár Tétulus, yémofem yénérsaménd yéfenjo áwátaranégh Folenemén gafanafaf. 2 Njam Fol yanmoyénd kénjút, Tétulusém yáne áwátaranégh yéramai Feliksefaf. Fá norayai, "Fémo té yénmaramai tombé yu krufráte, a fene mwighé té yénérsam mer téfene yuwot. 3 Utmanéghafé Feliks, méinyotio yéné yammén yéndfem kánjo némarametam mindefifi yénémamén feyot. 4 Wénde yénd yau ménde wém yongnayafé sái kényátongén fém, yéndo némamnendan fémo so kwumyo sénonjo siot.
bt: Five days later the leading burning sacrifice man Ananias went to Kaisaria with some of the head people and a middle man/lawyer name Tètulus, and they brought their complaints about Fol to the governor. 2 When they called Fol in, Tètulus gave his complaint to Feliks. He said, , “You gave us a long period of peace, and your thinking has brought us goodness to our nation. 3 Respectful Feliks, for all of this things we thank you very much about this. 4 But I don't want to make you tired, I ask you to allow me to say now.

nama: "Yéndfem té yinjongém yéné ár ndené kérté wafrogh ár yém, kérté té tawafrote Ju árfefaf méinyotio yuwon. Fá siyém yéné mérénmén sénko ár yémndetat Najaretmén ár, 6 wéi téfé yafngoi wasérmbogh wáwefnogh méngo, féyo yéndfem yerayém fá. 7 Wénde fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár Lisias yénmormén a yéngwasroi fá téfe tambén minde waftafé. 8 Fémon so tinjo efalo si téfene wátaranéghmén ménaye fémonyo sái yafénmeta fá."
bt: We have seen this man is a troublemaker, he made problem to the Ju people all over the world. He is a head man of this group called Najaret men, 6 [and] even tried to spoil the worshipping house, so we caught him. 7 But the soldiers' big head man Lisias came and took him from us with strong power. 8 You can find out the truth of our complaints(accusations) by examining him yourself.”

nama: Ámb Ju ár nufnyayénd wátaranégh kénjút, fá norayayénd ndené méinyotio yam Tétulusém árké noyarai efalo yém. Fol nawamarote fáyo
bt: The other Ju people joined in the complaint, he told that everything Tètulus said was true. Fol defended for himself

nama: Njam Gafanam yéméindai fá siot, Folém yawangoi, "Yénd mat wém yéné yunjéf tárfár fém té kéném mwighé farorégh ár yéné ndimbal yuwot, féyo yéndon áuwéghafé tanjo awamarogh yafrotan fene sáfrére. 11 Fémon so wékeye tayamngo ndené 12 efogh soramé yénd nufaryén Jerusalemét wáwefnoghét. 12 Tane wátaranégh árém yau winjoyénd yénd éwighan ámb árofé wáwefnogh méngon, yau taméindan awifaréghét ár mérén Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé o árkamé ámb yau ndimbal tesenan. 13 Fá féyotarofnar em yátoghét safén fefaf yéné yammén árkémamén yénd watarnat.
bt: When the governor caused/commanded for him to speak, Fol answered him, “I know this many years you have been a mind spliting(deciding) man over this nation, so I am glad to my defense before you. 11 You can quickly discover that I arrived in Jerusalem before 12 days to worship. 12 My complaint men never found me arguing with anyone in the worshipping house, and I did not cause to group discussing(riot) people group in the meeting house or anywhere in the city. 13 They cannot prove to you clearly these things which they complain me.

nama: "Wénde ámb si kafe némé fá norayat siyém efalo, árké yém ndené yéndon yáwefnotan tane ákifene Ngánjáne ndené fété End waitogh ár. Fá norayat yémofem wáwefnogh yafrotat efalo Ju árfene ende, wénde fá norayat yéndo wáwefnogh yafrotan fénaté efalo yátoghe. Wénde yéndon efalo eyátotan méinyotio némé ekai nákan a némé efarotaménd Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém. 15 Tafaf yém ndené méghméghyo Ngánján kénjún yéné árfefaf kés em, kété so anogh yém mbarkánd árfefaf a yaufi árfefaf. 16 Yénémamén yénd so nufotan túmén ménát tane emarégh kwayaghé so yém Ngánjánrére a árfefaf.
bt: But one part of what they say is true, which is that I worship the God of our ancesters as a follower of the Way. They say they worship the Ju people's way, but they say I worship in a different truly approving(belief). But I truly approve(believe) everything that is written in the law and what the God's people who make know(prophets) wrote. 15 To me is the same hope in God as these men have, there will be rising of [both] the straight men and the evil men. 16 So I do always to keep my mind/conscience bright/clear before God and men.

nama: "Tárfár yunjéf yukér soramé, yénd wénmormén Jerusalemét engérsamén tane árfene mani yauyau árét a ghéngén ramghét. 18 Tane wátaranégh árém winjoyénd yénd wáwefnogh méngon njamandio yénd kwémorwén wányányégh wifogh tárfáran. Kété yau ár mérén tamorwén yéndfé, yau ámb usérmbogh. 19 Wénde kété ámb Ju ár tamorwén kétamamén Esia yumén. Táf yéfene njam ewam áwátaranégh tafaf, fá fiya tanmorwén yéna fene sáfrére a tanérsawét wátaranégh. 20 Tamotangi yéné ár yéna, yémofem so tawaufrongi ndené mámáfi yau yinjoyénd njam yémofem wéngwanayénd moghfaf Ju sénko árfene áumengégh kénjút. 21 Ámbiro yaman yénd nutnayén njam yénd kwakayongérwén yéfene sáfan: 'Yénd ufoghan wém féfene sáfan sénonjo ménamén tane efalo yátogh yém árfene anoghmén kérta!'"
bt: After several years away, I came to Jerusalem to bring my people money for the poor and giving sacrifice. 18 My complaint men saw me in the worshipping house as I was completing a cleaning. There was no crowd with me, and not any disorder. 19 But there are some Ju people from the province of Eisia. If they have complaint to/against me, they should be here in front of you.s and bring complaints. 20 Ask these men here, they can tell you that they didn't find me badness(guilty) anything when they took me the court in Ju head men's meeting. 21 For one thing I shouted out when I stood in their presence: 'I am on trial before you.p today because my truly approving(believing) is people's rising from the dead!'"

nama: Feliks mer mat náyátoi Yesuene Endmén, féyo njam náyárai yéné sioene fifi, yékmboi mogh a yémndai, "Njam fiyaré ndimbal sénko ár Lisias kénaflangé, yéndon so mwighé yéfaronan fene awataranéghmén." 23 Yémon yáfáretam fiyaré sénko ár Fole wanjengét wénde yaramai ámb enagh a yumyoi yáne fútaram so tawarametawét yáne méndefé rokar.
bt: Feliks knew well about Yesu's Way, so when he heard the meaning of this talk, stopped the court and said, "When the soldiers' big head man Lisias comes, I will decide your complaint." 23 He ordered the soldiers' head man to look after Fol but gave him some rest/freedom and allowed his friends to give his needed things.

nama: Tárfár efogh soramé Felikséf enérmén yánjo amaf Drusila, efe témorwén Jumén amaf. Yémo yáfáretam yánjo ásáfogh ár Fole wasroghét. Fá ménde tamorwén sái káyáretawét Folefaf árfene ndimbal mwighamén Keriso Yesuene kénjún. 25 Fol korayawéng mbarkánd ghérsamén, fémyo awaraweréghmén a mogh efoghtaumén árkéméné yéném, Feliks férfér témorwén a norayai, "Féyo tém sénonjoyot. Fém téu kuwano. Efogh njam wam yéndon so kéngmotangén ménát sái néném tafaf wéikor." 26 Yéné efghonyo fá témorwén emarghan ndené Folém so mani taramangai, féyo fá túmén yémafé kafángowét a koraiwét yémafé.
bt: Many days after Feliks came with this wife Drusila, who was Ju woman. He sent working man to bring Fol. They wanted to listen from Fol about people's big mind(faith) in Keriso Yesu. 25 Fol talked about straight life, self-countrol and the judgment day to come, Feliks was afraid and said, "That's enough for now. You may go. When I have time I will ask you to come to me again." 26 At the same time he was in thought that Fol would give him some money, so he often met with him and they talked with him.

nama: Sómbio yunjéf soramé, Forsius Festus nosoi Gafana Feliksene sifayan. Wénde ménamén Feliks ménde témorwén Ju árfene ménde sái tawawafrotau, yémo yéfrangoi Fol kwéfkwéf méngon.
bt: After two years, Forsius Festus replaced/succeeded Feliks's as governor. But because Feliks wanted to do Ju people's desire, he left Fol in darkness house.

nama: Festus namndai gafana, nambio efogh soramé fá nuwanoi Kaisariata kétán Jerusalem. 2 Jerusalem kénjún, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a Ju árfene sénko ár nufaryénd yáne sáfrére a yéramayénd wátaranégh si Folenemén. 3 Yémofem yamnendayénd Festus yéfene méndeyot Fole wasarghét Jerusalemét, ménamén fá káfrendawét yá ngiot endén. 4 Festusém ewawangoi, "Fol siyém té yangongi kwéfkwéf méngo ár yém Kaisarian, a yéndio so wéngém tekofnaye. 5 So ámb féfene limánégh árfé yénngém yéndfé, a njam yéné árom némndafi mámáfi yafrota, yémofem so wátaranéghan tékmangi fá."
bt: Festus became governor, three days later he went from Kaisaria to Jerusalem. 2 In Jerusalem, leading burning sacrifice men and Ju head people arrived before him and made complaints(accusations) about Fol. 3 They asked Festus as a favor to them to bring Fol to Jerusalem, for they were preparing a plot to kill him on the way. 4 Festus answered them, "Fol is have been taken back a darkness house man(prisoner) in Kaisaria, and I myself will go not.far(soon). 5 Let some of your leading men come with me, and if this man has done anything wrong, they can put in complaint(accusation) him."

nama: 8 o 10 efogh ámnjogh soramé yémafé, Festus néwanoi Kaisariat. A ámb efghon yémon yanmoi mogh a emndai Fol sái ténémnjongayénd yáfaf. 7 Njam Fol nélawai kénjút, Ju ár efe enmormén kétándmé Jerusalemta yarendayénd fá. Yémofem enérsaménd yáfaf tárfár kwérnarafé wátaranégh si, wénde fá féyotarofnar emormén yátoghét yéfene wátaranéghan.
bt: After staying 8 or 10 days with them, Festus went down to Kaisaria. And the next day he called the court and ordered that they brought Fol to him. 7 When Fol went inside, the Ju people who had come from Jerusalem stood around him. They brought many strong complaints to him, but they are without.enough to prove their complaints.

nama: Féyo Fol fáyo nawamarotai, "Yéndon yau ámb mámáfi tafrotan kétán Ju árfene nákfaf o wáwefnogh méngofaf o Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarefaf."
bt: Then Fol defended himself, "I haven't done any wrong to Ju people's law or against the worshipping house or Rom high ruling man Kaisar."

nama: Festus ménde témorwén Ju ár sái áuwéghét taméindau, Folefaf norayai, "Fém ménde té ném álighét Jerusalemét a sái némakai tane sáfrére yéné wátaranéghan?"
bt: Festus wanted to make Ju people happy, he asked Fol, "Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and stand before me there on these complaints?"

nama: Folém yawangoi, "Yénd sénonjo wakai Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarene mogh sáfrére, árké yém mbarkánd sifayé tayot moghét. Fém fémyo mer mat em ndené yéndo ámb yau mámáfi tafrotan Ju árfefaf. 11 Wénde yéndon njam té tafrotan mámáfi, féyo moghan yénd so wésmetat kérotio, yénd yau nawatnan kérot. Wénde njam fá nuindat moghan féyo emo yau so kwengongé yénd kétán yéné árfefaf ménát sái wésmetat kérotio. Yénd mé nawatarnan Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisar sái náyáreta tane mogh!"
bt: Fol answered him, "I now am standing before Rom high ruling man Kaisar's court place, which is straight place to judge me. You.p yourselves well known that I didn't have done any wrong to the Ju people. 11 But if I have done something bad, so in the court they would want to kill me, I don't refuse to die. But if they lie in the court then no one should turn me over to these men to kill me. I complain/appeal to Rom high ruling man Kaisar so that he will listen to my court!"

nama: Festus yánjo árfé korayawét, yéné soramé fá norayai Folefaf, "Fém té norayai, 'Kaisarém so náyárete tane mogh.' Yénémaménnde, fém so kuwano Rom tuk warsagh ár Kaisarefaf!" Festusém yaufroi warsagh ár Agrifai Folenemén
bt: Festus talked with his people and then he said to Fol, "You.s have said, 'Kaisar will hear my court.' Therefore, you.s will go to Rom high ruling man Kaisar!" Festus told the ruling man(king) Agrifa about Fol

nama: Nambio efogh soramé warsagh ár Agrifa a Bernisa nufareyayénd Kaisariat. Fá enérmén ménát sái tumyeyayénd Festus. 14 Njam fá emorarmén tárfár efogh kété, Festusfem taruinwét Agrifafé Ju árfene wátaranéghmén Folefaf. Fá norayai, "Ár yéna yém kwéfkwéf méngo ár efe Feliksém yéngéfrango. 15 Njam yénd wéngmormén Jerusalemét, limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár a Ju árfene sénko árém enérsaménd wátaranégh a wémotayénd ménát Fol sái kértayan tékmangayén.
bt: A few days later the ruling man(king) Agrifa and Bernisa arrived at Kaisaria. They.d came to welcome Festus. 14 When they.d were spending many days there, Festus discussed with Agrifa about Ju people's complaint to Fol. He said, "There is a darkness house man(prisoner) here whom Feliks left. 15 When I went to Jerusalem, the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests) and Ju head people brought complaints and asked me to put Fol pain/punishment.

nama: "Yéndon ewawangoyén, 'Fém ndené norayati yéné árom té tafrote mámáfi yam. Fá fronde so kokayongé féfene sáfrére efe nawatarnat yáfaf. Féyo yémon so tawawangongé féfe, féyo yéndo so táf moghan tákman efe yém mbarkánd. Yéné fiyém téfene Rom nák.' 17 Njam yáne wánangogh ár yénmormén yénan, yéndon yau yalayén yéné kérté. Wénde yénd náumendayém moghét ámb efghon a emndayén yéné ár yénmasroyénd kénjút. 18 Yáne wánangogh ár nokayoyénd a norayayénd. Yénd kemnan ndené fá so norayat ndené Folém té ámb minde mámáfi yam tafrote. Wénde fá yau yéné si norayayénd. 19 Wéndende, fá yémafé kéwitawét Folfé ménamén némé Ju árém tawatametawét Ngánjánenemén. Fá kéwitawét yánemén efe kér té yémormén Yesu. Wénde Fol ndené korayau fá ghérsé mé yém. 20 Yénd yau mat wém ndernáye so tawafénmetawén yéné áwátaranégh. Féyo yéndon yémotayén fá, 'Fém té ménde ném ménát álighét Jerusalemét a némakai kété ufoghan yéné wátaranéghan.' 21 Wénde Folém wawangoi ta, 'Yau, yénd ménde wém Kaisarém sái náyáreta tane si.' Yénayan yénd norayayén tane fiyarafaf, 'Ménát Fol sái tumbetau yéna kétánotio yéndon téngwatemban Kaisarefaf.'"
bt: I answered them, “You are saying that this man has done bad things. He must first stand in front of you.p who complained against him. Then he can answer you.p, then I will put him in court who is straight. That is our Rom law.” 17 When his complaining men came here, I did not delay the case. But I gathered to the court the other day and ordered this man to be brought in. 18 His complaining men got up and spoke. I thought they would say that Fol had done some very bad thing. But they did not say that. 19 Instead, they were arguing with Fol about what the Ju people teach about God. They were arguing about who was died Yesu. But Fol was saying that he is still alive. 20 I don't know how to examine this complaint. So I asked him, "Would you like to go to Jerusalem and stand for trial on these complaints." 21 But Fol answered me, "No, I want Kaisar to listen to my talk." So I said my soldiers, "Guard Fol here until I send him to Kaisar."

nama: Féyo Agrifa norayai Festusefaf, "Yéndon so yiyáretan fá némé si njam noraya." Yémon yawangoi, "Káye fémo so yiyárete." Fol noraye Agrifafaf
bt: Then Agrifa said to Festus, "I will listen to him what he is saying." He answered him, "Tomorrow you will listen to him." Fol apeaks to Agrifa

nama: Ámb efghon Agrifa a Bernisam numaneménd mer sáláme. Fá nélaweyayénd Gafaman méngot yémafé fiyaré sénko árfé a utmanégh ár tesenmén. Festusém emndai yánjo ár, "Ténmasrongi Fol yénan." 24 Festus norayai, "Warsagh ár Agrifa a méinyotio efe yéna em yéndfé, fémofem téfandangi yéné ár! Méinyotio Ju árém wamnendangi yénd Jerusalemén a yéna Kaisarian, kutnawét ndené fá yau so nakete. 25 Wénde yéndon yau yinjoyén yáne yaufi wafroghyam árkémamén fá krot yékmetat. Wénde ménamén fá wéikor norayai fá ménde témorwén Kaisar sái káyáretau yáne áwátaranégh. Féyo yéndon mwighé yafrotamén yá watembghét kétán Rom.
bt: The next day Agrifa and Bernis wore good clothes. They.d entered into Government house with head men of soldiers and respected people from the city. Festus ordered to his people, "Bring Fol here." 24 Festus said, "King Agrifa and all who are here with me, you.p see this man! All the Ju people asked me in Jerusalem and here Kaisaria, shouting that he ought not to live. 25 But I have not found his bad action which he is put to death. But because he said again he wanted Kaisar to his his complaint(case). So I have made mind(decided) to send him to Rom.

nama: "Yénd yau mat wém némé yéndon so yéfarotan Rom tuk warsagh árfefaf yánemén. Yénémaménnde yéndon té ténémnjan fá méinyotio féfefaf. Warsagh ár Agrifafaf, yéndon némamnendan fém kor merkéráye náyáreta némé Fol noraye. Féyo yénd so táf norayam yéné kértamén mérne. Soramé yéndon so mbarkánde férén yéfarotan kétán Kaisarefaf. 27 Yénd norayan yéné ménamén njam yéndfem tatembangém kwéfkwéf méngo ár kétán Rom tuk warsagh ároefaf téfnofnar yuwon, féyo yéné yau so mer yém."
bt: I don't know what I should write to the Roman Rom high ruling man(emperor) about him. Therefore I have brought him to all of you. The ruling man(king) Agrifa, I ask you to listen carefully to what Fol says. Then we can talk about the problem together. Later I will write a straight letter to Kaisar. 27 I say that because if we send a darkness house man(prisoner) to the Roman high ruling man without reason, then that would not be good."

nama: "Warsagh ár Agrifa, yénd áuwéghafé wém fe sáfrére sénonjo táf yéndon norayan awamarogh si méinyotio Ju árfene wánangoghmén, 3 ménamén fém mer mat ném ndernáye Ju ár emor a yéfene fénatayam. Féyo fém náyáreta tafaf métare!
bt: "King Agrifa, I am glad before you.s today as I make defence against all complaints of all the Ju people, 3 because you know well about how Ju people live and their different doing. So you listen to me patiently!

nama: "Méinyotio Ju ár mat em tanemén kétándmayo yénd tosé. Fá mat em némé sáfot ghérsafé yénd kwémorangérwén tanjo yuwon a ndenáyo Jerusalemén. 5 Fá mat té tamorwén tanemén tekefifi. Fá so nafrotat ndené yénd kwémorwén Farasi ár. Yéndo mbarkánde ewaitotan Ngánjánene nák ndenanit méinyotio Farasi árém kés ewafrotat. 6 Tane méghmégh siyém ménáyan némé Ngánjánam té nénorayai téfene ákiafefaf, yéné téfnan yénd ufoghan wém sénonjo. 7 Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si norayam ndimbal yammén kétán téfene 12 Israil ár mérénfaf. Yéndfem méinyotio yánjetam ménát so tanangém némé Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si néngorayai téfefaf. Yéné téfnan yéndfem Ju árém túmén yáwefnotam Ngánjáne sémbár a efogh. Ndené yéné Ju ár sem yéna, yéndo efalo yiyátotan Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si. Wénde yéné yém téfén ndené fá nawatarnat tafaf. 8 Ngánjánam so taméindangé kér ár sái kanongi ghérsat wéikor. Fémofem so efalo wiyátotati yénd njam norayan yéné si.
bt: "All the Ju people know about me since I was a child. They knew what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. 5 They knew about me for a long time. They will tell that I was a Farasi man. I straightly followed God's law like as all Farasi men are doing. 6 My hope is by what God had said to our ancesters, this is the reason I am on trial today. 7 He promised a big thing to our 12 Israil families. We all expect to receive what God has promised to us. That is why we Ju people always worship God in the day and in the night. Like these Jews here, I believe in God's promise. But that is the reason that they complained to me. 8 God can cause dead people to become alive again. You should believe me when I say this.

nama: "Yénd yéndio ménde kwémorwén sái kásáfotawén tékarotawén Yesu Najaretmén. 10 Yéné fiyém yéndon némé tafrotawén Jerusalemén. Limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfene si muyayan, yéndon efalo yátogh ár tárfár takmetawén kwéfkwéf méngot. Njam téfene limánégh árém korayangayénd, 'Yéné efalo yátogh ár táf kér em,' Yéndon yéfene mwighé ámbiro tiyátongayén. 11 Tárfár mbinotma yénd kwiyotau Ju árfene áumengégh méngomé ménát yéfe tékrátan kémghét. Yéndon tawáfáretawén fá yaufi siot Ngánjánefaf. Yéndon tawakeindawén nékw kwémorwén yéfefaf a tawifnawén dábi árfene tesenamé.
bt: I wanted to work and to oppose Yesu of Najaret. 10 That is what I did in Jerusalem. On the authority of the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests), I put many of God's people into darkness house. When our leading men said, "These truly approving people must die," I became one thought/mind(agreed) [with] them. 11 Many time I went around meeting house to punish them. I forced them to wicked talk to God. I was pursuing to angry with them and chased them through foreign people cities.

nama: "Ámb efghon yénd álighan kwémorwén Damaskosét limánégh ghéngén wárogh árfene si muyafé a wáfáretayénd. 13 Warsagh ár Agrifa, séite karéfta, njam yénd kwéngmorwén endmé, yéndo yinjoyén kwayaghé njafarta, yau ndené efogh kwayaghé, wénde yéné yáindai, a warendai yénd a yéndfé áligh ár. 14 Yénd méinyotio nuyáyém mbandot, a yénd náyárayén kém korayau Ibru sie, 'Sol, Sol, némamén fémo yénd wesmete? Fémo njam kafkaf tautar wím minan, fémon ndémke so yéfande.'
bt: One day I was on the way to Damaskas with the authority and sending of the leading sacrifice burning people(high priests). 13 Ruling man(King) Agrifa, about noon, when I was on the road, I saw a light from heaven, not like the light of the sun, but surpassing that, shone and surounded me and traveling people with me. 14 We all fell down to the ground, and I heard a voice saying in Ibru language, "Sol, Sol, why are you beat me? When you bump your foot for sharp point of stick, you will feel pain."

nama: "Féyo yéndon yémotayén, 'Efe fém ném, Yuré?'
bt: Then I asked him, "Who are you, Lord?"

nama: Kano a kokai fenjo kafkafe. Yénd té nufarén fefaf fém némuyafon tane ásáfoghét a fandagh ár. Fémon so ewaufrote tanemén némé fémon téfande a so mé yéngfande. 17 Yéndon so kémanjarén kétamé Ju ár a Téndo ár. Yéndon némáfáretan téndo árfefaf 18 ménát yéfene ghékéf sauréghét a so ewangote kwéfkwéfta kwayaghat, kétamé Sáiféroene waftata kétán Ngánjánefaf. Féyo yémofem so tanangi yaufiyam wighét yaufita a sifayé tawaramangi Ngánjánene árfefaf emofem yénd efalo wiyátotat.'
bt: Get up and stand on your feet. I have appeared to you and chosen you for my works and a witness. You will tell about me what you have seen and will see contiually. 17 I will save you.s from the Ju people and the Side people. I am sending you.s to Side people 18 to open their eyes and turn them from the darkness to light, and from the power of Sáifèr to God. So they will receive throwing the sin and be given a place among God's people who truly approve(believe) me."

nama: "Féyo warsagh ár Agrifa, yénd áyárégh kwémorwén ghékéf waingégh yaman njafarmén. 20 Fronde ewaufrotan kétán efe tamorwén Damaskosén, ndenáyo yéfefaf Jerusalemét a méinyotio Judiat a méinyotio Téndo árfefaf. Yéndon táitotawén ménát fá so nawengotat yaufita Ngánjánefaf a so yufarotat yéfenjo awengogh yam wafroghe 21 Yéné téfnan ámb Ju árém wérayénd wáwefnogh méngo sifayan a kwérametawét ngiot. 22 Wénde Ngánjánam kwáutáne ta méinyotio efogh kétánotio sénonjo. Féyo yénd wakai yéna a nafrotan efalo si yétkwénofnar ár a yétkwénafé árfefaf. Yéndo yéné norayan némé Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár a Moses korayau so nufrotat: 23 Kerisom so tékrát yáyárete, a fronde so yém anoghét kétamé kérta, so yénérse si kwayaghé yánjo árfefaf a Téndo árfefaf."
bt: So King Agrifa, I was obedient in the eye pass thing(vision) from heaven. 20 First I told to whom were in Damaskos, also to them in Jerusalem and all Judia and all the Side people. I preached in order that they must turn from sins to God and do the things that would show by thier turning action. 21 For this reason some Ju people held me in the worshipping house courts and tried to kill me. 22 But God helped me every day until now. So I stand here and testify the true talk to without.name people and with.name people. I am saying this what the God's people who make know(prophets) and Moses said would happen: 23 Keriso would suffer, and be first to rise from the death, would bring the talk of light to his own people and to the Side people.

nama: Njam Folém mé korayau, Festusém nutnai, "Fol, fém onon ném! Fenjo ndimbal otamegham néméinde ononot!"
bt: While Fol was still speaking, Festus shouted, "Fol, you are crazy. Your big learning caused you crazy!"

nama: Folém yawangoi, "Minde utmanéghafé Festus. Yénd yau onon wém. Yénd némé norayan siyém efalo a fifiyafé. 26 Warsagh ár mat yéné yammén yém. Yénd so norayan férférofnaye fefaf. Yénd mat wém ndené warsagh ár mat yém némé yénd norayan, ménamén yéné yam yau nufrotam átnoghe kwéndkwéndan. 27 Warsagh ár Agrifa, fémon efalo té eyátote Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár? Yénd mat fémo fété."
bt: But Fol answered him, "Big respectful Festus. I am not crazy. What I am saying is true and with.meaning/reasonable. 26 The ruling man(king) knows about these things. I speak without fear to you. I know that the ruling man(king) know what I am saying, because this thing did not happen secretly in a corner. 27 King Agrifa, do you truly approve(believe) the God's people who make know(prophets)? I know you do."

nama: Féyo Agrifa norayai Folefaf, "Fém ndené nemne yéné tekofnare fémon so kwiyáto wékeye yénd ndené fété efalo yátogh ár?"
bt: Then Agrifa said to Fol, "Do you think in a not.long(short time) you can make me quickly a truly approve man(believer)?"

nama: Folém yawangoi, "Ténjfi o tombé kéráye, tane orayégh Ngánjánefaf feyot a méinyotio emofem wiyáretat yénd sénonjo so kénamndangi efalo yátogh ár ndené yénd swém, wénde yénd yau ménde wém fém sái kamndangi kwéfkwéf méngo ár ndené yénd."
bt: Fol answered, "Short or long time, my talking with God for you and all who are listening to me today may become truly approve people(believers) like I am, but I do not want you to become darkness people(prisoners) like me."

nama: Warsagh ár, gafana, Bernis a méinyotio efe tamorangérwén yémafé fá nokayoyénd. 31 Soramé yémofem yéfrangoyénd a fá nufngoyénd si yéfe kénjúnro, "Yéné árom némé yau tafrote némndafi árkémamén krot so tékmangi o kwéfkwéf méngot tényawangi."
bt: The ruling man(king), the governor, Bernis and all who was with them stood up. 31 After they left and they began saying among themselves, "This man is not doing anything that put do death or putting into darkness house."

nama: Agrifa norayai Festusefaf, "Njam yéné ár yau té norayai Kaisarefaf ménát sái káyáretau yane si, féyo yéndfem so téngwurtongém."
bt: Agrifa said to Festus, "If this man had not spoken to Kaisar to hear his talk, then we could take him out."

nama: Njam mwighé nufaryéng nde yénd so kuwanongayém ndimbal boute kétán Italia, yémofem eramayénd Fol a ámb kwéfkwéf méngo ár yáfaf Rom fiyaré sénko ár Juliés. Fá tafnyangérwén tukén warsagh ároene fiyaré mérnan. 2 Yénd nuwanoyém ndimbal boutfaf árkéméné yénmormén kétamé Adramitum. Yéné bout témorwén kafrendau. Yéné so kuwanongai yénamé tesenamé arakaf téndomé Esia ndimbal yuwon. Yénd nuflayém yéné boutan. Ár yétkwén Aristakus efe témorwén Tesalonika tesenmén Makedonia yuwon nuwanoyém yémafé. |nmx_225wbt_Paul to Rome [bwf-light] @en_v3.tif|col||© 2025 Wycliffe Bible Translators, Inc.|Folene áligh Romét (Acts 27.1—28.14)|27.1—28.14
bt: When it was decided that we would go to Itali, they gave and some other darkness house men(prisoners) to a soldiers' head man (named) Juliès . He belonged to high ruling man's soldiers group(Emperor's Army). 2 We went on to a big bout that had come from Adramitum. This ship was prepared [to go]. It would travel to towns on the coast of Esia region. We got on that ship. A man name Aristakus who was from Tesalonika city in Makedinia province, we went with him.

nama: Ámb kwéfon nufaryém Sidonét. Juliésém mere Fol yumyoi fandaghét yá fútarfefaf ménát yémofem so taramangayénd yáne méndefé rokar. 4 Njam yéndfem yéfrangoyém Sidon tesen, ménamén ndimbal mbormboram wangogh yénmafngoi, féyo yénd kérsawém Saifrés fér téndomé árkamé mbormbor yau téfnjawéng muyafé. 5 Yéné soramé néférmayém marat arakaffaf kaka kétán arakaf téndot Silisia a Famfilia yuwon, nénufaryém kétán Mirat tesen Lisia yuwon. 6 Mira tesenan, fiyaré sénko árom yinjoi Aleksandria árfene ndimbal bout árké kafrendawét álighét Italiat, féyo yémo yénmaflai boutan.
bt: The next day we arrived at Sidon. Juliès was kind to Fol and allowed him to see to the friends so that they could give him his wanting things. 4 When we left Sidon city, [and] because strong wind were pushing us back, so we sailed through the side of the island of Saifrès where the wind did not blow strongly. 5 After this we crossed over the sea to the coast of Silisia and Famfilia area, we arrived at Mira town, in Lisia region. 6 In Mira town, there the soldiers' head man found an Aleksadria people's bout which it was preparing go to Italia, so he put us on the bout.

nama: Yénd áfráe kérsawém tárfár efogh, a táikér mé téfandawém ufarghét Sénidus yuwot. Yau mbormboram ténmumyotau yénd álighét yéné sifayamé, féyo yénd téndomé nuwanoyém kétán náikái tambén Krit fér, yéngwaindayém Salmone ghém. 8 Yénd áfráe kérsawém arakaf téndomé a nufaryém yéné sifayé yétkwén Wanjarégh Sur, yau nayu témorwén kétándmé Lasia tesenot.
bt: We traveled slowly for several days, and had a hard time arriving off Sènidus region. The wind would not let us go that place, so we traveled to the southern of the island of Krit, passing by Cape(point) Salmone. 8 We went slowly along the coast and arrived at the place name Safe Harbors, not far from the town of Lasia.

nama: Yénandmé yéndfem té tárfár efogh tanérsam, féyo yéné so yém mámáfi njam mé nérsam boute ménamén Ju árfene ndimbal awafarmanégh efogh soramé arakaf namndai minde mbormborafé. Féyo Folém emndai ár boutan, 10 "Fútaréf, káyárangi tafaf yénd njam kuwanongém sénonjo téfene ndimbal bout rokarafé so kértayan yém, a tárfár ghérsé so etarnam." 11 Wénde fiyaré sénko ár yau náyárai Folefaf némé fá norayai, wénde ewaiteyai ndimbal bout wawerégh ár a ndimbal bout yurane si. 12 Ménamén sur yau mer témorwén yunjfan yéfe kémghét, tárfár ár ménde tamorwén awaingéghét kétán sur yétkwén Finiks yunjéf ámnjoghét kété. Finiks sur témorwén sauréghkaf kétán arakafot sómbio ende a muyafé mbormbor yau kor ténéfnjau kété. |CN02042B.tif|col||© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission.|Fol korayau Boutan mbormboran (Acts 27.9-10)|27.9-10 Ndimbal Mbormbor
bt: By now we had spent a lot of time. so it would be bad(dangerous) if you still traveled by boat because after Ju people's big fasting day sea became very stomy. So Fol said to people in the boat, 10 "Friends, listen to me if we go now our big boat(ship) with cargoes will be problem, and we will lost many lives." 11 But the soldiers' head man not listen to Fol what he said, but followed the so boat driver(captain) and its owner's talks. 12 Because the harbor was not good to spend the raining season(winter) for their staying, many people wanted pass to the harber name Foniks to spend the raining season(winter). Foniks harbor was open to the sea in two ways and strong winds did not blow there. The big wind

nama: Njam métar náikái mbormbor waufégh yénmafngoi, yémofem yinjoyénd mer témorwén álighét. Féyo táf yurtoyénd werégh téfén ndimbal boutoene a nuwanoyénd Krit arakaf téndomé. 14 Wénde ndimbal mbormbor yétkwén Yambérmawér Mbormboram eutfoi fá a yénmaufetai kétamé férta. 15 Mbormbor témaufetau minde muyafé ndimbal boutfaf. Féyotarofnar tamorwén wawerégh téfnáram sái taweretawét ndimbal bout mbarkánde kétán mbormborfaf. Féyo yumyoyénd ndimbal bout mbormboram yéngwanai. 16 Yénd yénamé yéngmormén Kauda fér néndkéfamé. Bout wawerégh téfnáram wénmauyoyénd Boutoene ndingi nutambén ménát wanjarghét. 17 Féyo yémofem yarmboyénd ndingi ndimbal bout tukét. Yéné soramé yémofem wumbégh yafngoyénd ángéghe ndimbal bout ménát taikrot. Ménamén fá férfér tamorwén nde mété táf kásmetawét wíman Siritisén, yémofem yeworyénd boutoene wérégh tot téfén yumyoyénd ndimbal bout yéngwanayéng mbormboram. 18 Ndimbal mbormbor mindefifi taufetau, féyo ámb efghon ámb rokar taranégh ewafngoyénd ndimbal boutta. 19 Nambiomén efghon yémofem taranégh ewafngoyénd ámb bout ásáfoghmén rokar. 20 Tárfár efghon yéndfem yau tafandawém efogh a kénd. Ndimbal mbormbor mé ténmaufetau muyafé a yénd féyo yénmormén a téfene kamnang nefoyénd awanjarghét.
bt: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they saw good time to go. So they pulled up anchor and went along the coast of Krit. 14 But a big wind(storm) name Northeast wind suddenly came them and blew from the island. 15 The wind blew strongly against the boat. It was not possible for the bout drivers(sailors) to sail the boat straight into the wind. Then they let boat the wind took it. 16 We went along the island of Kauda. The sailors lifted the boat's ndingi out of water for the safety. 17 so they lift ndingi up to big bout. After this they started tying around big bout with rope to make strong. Because they were afraid that might be hitting on the stone off the Siritis, they lowered the holding the holing metal of bout(anchor) and let the big boat(ship) be carried by the wind. 18 The storm brew so strong, so the next day they began to throw the thing(cargo) from the big boat(ship). 19 On the third day they began to throw some big boat(ship) working things. 20 For several days we could not see the sun and star. The storm kept blowing strongly and so we came to finish our hope of being saved.

nama: Yéné téfnan yéndfem yau njamke tánetawém ndené teke. Fol nokayoi emndai ár: "Fém fai káyáretawét tafaf njam tamorwén kété Krit féran, fémofem yau yéné wasérmbogh so tinjongayénd a té kawaghnat. 22 Wénde sénonjo yéndon ewamnendan fém, yau férfér em ménamén emo ámb yau ghérsé so yétarne, mé ndimbal boutro so nawasérmbote. 23 Yénd wém Ngánjánene, a yéndon yáwefnotan yáyot. Sémbáran yémon ténmáfárete njafar si résagh ár 24 ta waufroi, 'Fol, yau férfér ném! Fém so némakai ufoghan Rom tuk warsagh ároene rére. Ménamén fenemén Ngánjánam so tawanjarngé efe méinyotio em ndimbal boutan.' 25 Féyo férfér yau em! Yénd mat wém ndené Ngánjánam so yafrote mbarkánd némé fá norayai. 26 Wéndende, ndimbal bout so nirone ámb féran." Ndimbal bout niroyéng
bt: In this reason we did not eat any food for a long time. Fol stood and said to the people: "You should have listened to me when you were in island of Krit, you would not see this damage and spoiled/lost things. 22 But now I ask you, don't be afraid because no one will be lost, only the big boat(ship) will be destroyed. 23 I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent a man carrying heavenly message 24 said to me, "Fol, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Rom high ruling man(Roman emperor). Because of you.p God will save all who were on the big boat(ship)." 25 So don't be afraid! I know that God will do exactly what he said. 26 But big bout will bestroyed we will on some island." The big boat(ship) destroyed

nama: 14 sémbáran yénd ténmimndau mbormboram yénamé Mediterian arakafamé. Wénde sémbár néndkéf ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár mat náyátoyénd yénd kaka náyátoyém mbandfaf. 28 Yémofem yufotaménd nuoene kénjkam ndené 40 mité. Yéné soramé wéi yufotaménd a yinyoyénd 30 mité kénjkam témorwén. 29 Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár férfér tamorwén ménamén mété táf kásmetawét wíman. Féyo yémofem ewitaménd 4 werégh tot téfén ndimbal boutoene kwémb tambén a fá Ngánjánafé orayégh nufngoyénd efogh kwayaghat.
bt: In 14th night wind drove us around over the Mediterian sea. But midnight the bout working men(sailers) realized we were getting near land. 28 They measured depth of water was 40 meter. After this they measured again and found there was 30 meter. 29 the bout working men(Sailers) were afraid because we might hit some rocks. Then they let down 4 anchors from the back and they started talking with God for daylight.

nama: Bout ásáfogh ár ménde tamorwén sái téfrangongayénd ndimbal bout. Féyo yémofem yeworyénd ndingi arakafot. Fá yéta kwankwane álighét nufngoyénd. Fá norayayénd, "Yénd yéngém kétán ndimbal bout sénkofaf ménát ndimbal boutoene werégh tot téfén so taweworngém arakafot." Wénde kuindawét. 31 Wénde Folém emndai fiyaré sénko ár a fiyaré, "Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár yau njam emor ndimbal boutan, féfene kamnang ámb yau yém ménát awanjarghét." 32 Féyo fiyaram yénfayénd ángégh árké teretawét ndingi a nuyai arakafot.
bt: The sailers wanted to leave the big boat(ship). So they lowered the ndingi into the sea. They tried to go away secretly. They said, ‘We are going to the front of the big bout to put big bout's holding metal(anchors) down into the sea.’ But they cheated. 31 But Fol said to the soldiers' head man and the soldiers, "If the boat working men(sailers) don't stay on the big boat(ship), you have no hope for us to be saved. 32 Then the soldiers cut the ropes which held the nding and let it fall into the sea.

nama: Fauréghétro témorwén Folém ewamnendai njamke nénet, a emndai, "14 efogh fém té tam mámé mwighé a ámb njamke yau tánetati. 34 Yéndon ewamnendan fém, féyo, njamke tánetangi, ghérsé sái mé em. Yéné yafrotati a fém so mer em."
bt: Just before dawn Fol asked them to eat food, and said. He said to them, "For 14 days you have been so worried and you haven't eaten food. 34 I ask you, then, eat food, your so you will live. Do this and you will be fine."

nama: Si soramé, Folém yanayéng ámb féskaf flawé karéf a kánjo si yaramayéng Ngánjáne. Féyo méinyotio árfene sáfrére, yémo yétfoi féskaf flawé a ténetau. 36 Fá méinyotio náuwétaménd a ámb njamke enetaménd. 37 Kété tamorwén méinyotio 276 ár ndimbal boutan. 38 Njam yémofem enetaménd a eremngoi, yémofem tawitawét flawé baik kétán arakafot ndimbal bout ofat.
bt: After talk, Fol took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of every people, he borke the bread and ate it. 36 They all peased and ate some food. 37 There were 276 people on the big boat(ship). 38 When they eat and satisfied, they threw the flawabaik(bag of wheat) into the sea to make (bout)light.

nama: Ámb akwan, yémofem yau yáyámngoyénd mband, wénde yémofem yinjoyénd trewar ménáyafé miri mbandafé. Féyo yémofem mwighé yinjoyénd ménát wérset miriamé. 40 Yémofem enfayénd werégh téfén ángégh féyo engéfrangoyénd arakafan. Táfandio yémofem eferyénd ángégh árkéménayam teretawét ndimbal bout waknyéghmén. Yénamayo yémofem yakayoyénd sáimai ndimbal bout sénko tambén a mbormboram ndimbal bout résagh yafngoi kétán mirifaf. 41 Wénde ndimbal bout miri mbandot náyátoi. Féyo ndimbal bout sénko nékmboi kété yau kimndau, a ndimbal sófam yésmai ndimbal bout kwémbta méinyotio yiroryéng.
bt: In the next morning, they did not recognize the land, but they saw a bay with sandy beach. So they saw mind(decided) to run through the sand. 40 They cut the ropes of anchors so left them in the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes which were holding the stears. Next they stand up the saimai(Note. this word means sail from Nemo language) at head/front of the big boat(ship) and the wind started to carry the big boat(ship) to the beach/sand. 41 But the big boat(ship) hit a sandbank. The front of the big boat(ship) stuck there and could not move, and strong waves struck the back of the big boat(ship) and everything was destroyed.

nama: Fiyaré méndefnar tamorwén efe yau so kakmetawét tunat a kéngongayénd, féyo yémofem mwighé téfrendawét kwéfkwéf méngo árfe ngiot. 43 Wéndende, fiyaré sénko ár ménde témorwén Fole wanjarghét a yémo yau taumyotau yéné yam wafroghét. Wéndende, "Méinyotio ár efe mat em akmégh, katnangi arakafot, a kakmetangi tunat. 44 Ámb árém táf ewaitotat. Nawéretat wén karfan o ndimbal bout karéfkaréfan." Féyo fá nufaryénd merro kétán tunat.
bt: The soldiers did not want to no one swam to land and ran way so they planed to kill the darkness house men(prisoners). 43 But the soldiers' head man wanted to save Fol and he didn't allow doing this things. Instead he said, "Everyone who can swim, jump into the water first. Then swim to the land. 44 You other people must follow them. Hold on to pieces of wood, or pieces of the ship.’ So they were reached to the land safely.

nama: Njam yénd merro nufaryém, yéndfem yáyamngoyém fér yétkwén Malta. 2 Fér ár efe tákmangérwén yémofem yénmumyoyénd mere a fútar náyátoyém. Yémofem fés yétayaménd a yénmumyoyénd yénd méinyotio ménamén nu kafamndau a kurférmén.
bt: When we safely arrived, we learned the island name Malta. 2 The island people who were living they accepted well and became friend. They made a fire and accepted us all because it was raining and cold.

nama: Folém ámb fés sut enwanai a efayoi fését. Fés waftayam yéméindai kamban étorghét a yararyéng yáne ngángé. 4 Njam fér árém yinjoyénd kamban térnyangérwén yáne ngángayan, fá korayangi yéfe kénjúnro, "Yéné ár mété yém ár ngi téfnár! Fá yau kolitote arakafan, wénde ngánjánam yau yumyo ghérsat." 5 Wénde Folém yéfnendai kamban fését yéneghoi a ámb némé kérté yau yanai. 6 Ár kemnawét yáfaf ndené mbwe kar so tarenau o sune kér témorwén. Wénde teke awanjengégh soramé yau némndafi nufrotam yáfaf, yéfefaf mwighé nufaryénd a norayayénd, "Fá siyém ngánján."
bt: Fol carried some fire sticks and put them on the fire. The heat of the fire caused a snake to came out and bit him on the hand. 4 When the island people saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said among themselves, "This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but god has not allowed him to live." 5 But Fol shook a snake off into the fire and there was no problem. 6 The people kept thinking that he would swell up from wound or suddenly dying. But after waiting a long time and nothing happened to him, they reached(changed) their mind and said, "He is a god."

nama: Gafana témorwén Fablis yéné féran, yáne ámb mband kété témorwén. Fablis témorwén ngárám ár téfefaf a yénmumyoi yánjo méngot nambio efogh ámnjoghét. 8 Yárefé enjne tékmangérwén yameyan fifi waftamén a oghanemén. Fol nénglawai kénjút Ngánjánafé orayéghét a ngángé yéfayoi yáfaf a mer yiyátoi. 9 Njam yéné yam nufrotam, yéné férta enjne téfnár tanmorwén a mer táyátotau. 10 Yémofem ténmutémndawét yénd tárfár ende, a yénd njam álighétro ténmorwén, yémofem ténmarametawét téfene méndefé rokar. Fol nufaryéng Rom
bt: There was governor Fablis in the island, he owned some of the land around there. Fablis was graciful to us and invited us into his home for three days to stay. 8 His father was sick in matress(bed) with fever and stomach troblem. Fol went into talking with God and he placed his hands on him and was became good. 9 When this thing happened, the sick people from the island came and became good. 10 They were honoring us in many ways, and when we were [time] to go, they gave us [everything] we needed. Fol arrived at Rom

nama: Nambio ghérare soramé mbormboram yiroyéng ndimbal bout. Ndimbal bout yéméghangérmén Maltan mbormbormén yunjfan. Yéné témorwén Aleksandriamén ndimbal bout a kété tárwén sómbio wafroghmén ngánján ndimbal bout sénkoyan. Yénd néngufaryém ndimbal boute. 12 Yénd ngérsawém arakafamé nungufaryém Sirakus tesen a yénmorangérmén kété nambio efogh. 13 Kétándmé yénd nufaryém Regium tesen. Ámb efghon náikái mbormbor waufégh yafngoi, sómbio efogh soramé yénd nufaryém Fotioli tesen. 14 Kété yéndfem ewinjoyém Yesuene waitogh ár, emofem yénmumyoyénd yémafé ámnjoghét 7 efghot. A féyo yénd néngwanoyém Rom. 15 Ámb Yesuene waitogh ár Rommén náyárayénd téfenemén a enmormén téfe wanféghét Afius térarégh sifayat a méngotu yétkwén Nambio wan téfnár méngot. Njam Folém ewinjoi fá, yémon kánjo yaramai Ngánjáne a frengégh yanai. 16 Yénd njam nufaryém Rom, Fol yumyoyénd yambia ámnjoghét fiyaram tambetau. |CN02055B.TIF|col||© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission.|Folém efangoi efalo yátogh ár Rommén (Acts 28.16)|28.16 Folém táitotau mer si Romén wambégh mbanmbanan
bt: Three month after wind destroyed the big boat(ship). The big boat(ship) docked at Malta for wind in the winter. It was Aleksandrian big boat(ship) and there were two gods on the head of the big boat(ship), a we traveled in a big boat(ship). 12 We traveled through the sea and arrived Sirakus town and stayed there three days. 13 From there we arrived at Regium town. The next day the south wind began to blow up, and two days later we arrived at Fotioli. 14 There we found some Yesu's following men, who invited us to spend 7 days with them. And so we came to Rom. 15 Some of Yesu's following men in Rom heard about us and came to meet us at the market of Afius and at the village name Three guesthouse. When Fol saw them, he thanked God and received blessing. 16 When we arrived at Rom, they allow Fol to live by himself with a soldier was guarding him. Fol preached the good talk at Rom under guard

nama: Nambio efogh soramé yémo eumendai Ju árfene sénko ár a Fol norayai yéfefaf, "Tane fútaréf, yéndon némé yau tafrotan téfene árfefaf o téfene ákifene túmén yam, wénde yénd werayénd Jerusalemén a wéramayénd Rom árfene ngángat. 18 Yémofem kwémotawét yénd a ménde tamorwén ta utorghét, ménamén yaufi yau yinjoyénd tafaf árkémamén kér so kwémorwén. 19 Ju sénko ár korayawét tafaf, féyo yéné yamam kwéméindangé yénd sái korayangén Kaisar náyáreta tane si, ndemamén tane ámb yau témorwén wánangogh tane árfefaf. 20 Féyo yéndo té kénmarawetan fém yénan álighét ménát yéndo so némaufrotan némamén yénd wém kwéfkwéf méngon. Yéné ménamén yém yéndo efalo yiyátotan Keriso, efe yém Israil árfene kamnang té yénmormén."
bt: Three days later he called together Ju head men and Fol said to them, "My friends, I have done nothing to hurt our people or the everyday doings(customs) of our ancesters, but they held me in Jerusalem and gave me into the hands of the Rom people. 18 They asked me and wanted to take me out, because they could not see/found sins to me which I should die. 19 The Ju head men spoke against me, so this thing caused/made me say Kaisar listen to my talk, even though I have no accusation to my people. 20 So I have requested you to come here so that I can tell you why I am in darkness house. It is because I truly approve(believe) in Keriso, who is the hope of Israil people has already come."

nama: Yémofem yawangoyénd, "Yéndfem ámb yau némé férén ewinjoyém Judiamén fenemén. Ámb yau téfene ár efe enmormén kétándmé nafroyénd o norayayénd yaufi si fenemén. 22 Wénde yénd ménde yéném áyárghét fene si némé em, ménamén yénd mat yéném árém norayat yaufi si marataye yéné totér mérénmén."
bt: They answered him, "We have not any letters from Judia about you. None of our people who have come from there has reported or said anything bad talk about you. 22 But we want to hear your.s talk what you have, because we knew people are saying bad things about this new group widely."

nama: Yémofem yufoyénd ámb efogh Fole álighét. Njam yéné efogh nawerai, tárfár ár enmormén mérne yéné sifayat ánde fá tékmangérwén. Yémo táitotau yéfefaf akw kétán férégh, tawawafenau Ngánján limánéghmén, a kétamé Mosesene nák a kétamé Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfenemén. Yémo tamndetau ménát yaufiyamta awengoghét a ménát awengoghét Yesuefaf. 24 Ámb árém efalo tiyátotawét némé fá korayau, wénde ámb árém yau efalo tiyátotawét. 25 Fá kéwitawét yénémamén yéfenjo kénjún. Njam yémofem frangoghét yafngoyénd, Fol norayai, "Ngánján Mbérmbéram norayai efalo si Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár Aisaya kénjúmé féfene ákifefaf:
bt: They set another day to go to Fol. When that day arrived, many people came together to this place where he was staying. He preached to them from morning to late in the afternoon(evening), explained about the ruling of God, and from the law of Moses and from the God's people who make know(prophets). He told them to repent from sin and to turn to Yesu. 24 Some people truly approved(believed) in what he said, but other poeple did not truly approve(believe). 25 They argued about it with each other. When they began to leave the house, Fol said, "The God Spirit spoke a true talk through the God's man who make know(prophet) Aisaya to your ancesters:

nama: "'Kuwano yéné árfefaf a koraya, "Fém so náyáretati wénde mat yau so náyátotati. Fémofem so yéfandati wénde yau so tayamngongi.
bt: "Go to this people and say, "You will be hearing but never understand. You will look but you never recognize.

nama: Ménamén yéné árfene tikéf té nénamndai táikér, yémofem táikér yéfandat áyárghét, a yémofem yéfenjo ghékéf té eumbértongi. Féyo mété táf yau néretat yéfenjo ghékfe, a yau náyáretat yéfenjo téngane, a efalo yau yiyátotat yéfenjo tikfe a kawengongi ménát yéndon so mer táyáton fá."'"
bt: Because this people's heart has become stubborn, they became hard hearing [with their ears], and they have closed their eyes. So they might not see with their eyes, and couldn't hear with their ears, and they couldn't truly approve(believe) with their heart and turn so that I would heal them.

nama: Yénémaménnde yénd Fol ménde wém fém mat so em Ngánjánene wanjarégh té yéngramai kétán Téndo árfefaf, a fá so náyáretat! 29 {c{ ...f* {c{28.29 Yau siménj
bt: Therefore I Fol, want you know that God's salvation has been given/offered to the Side people, and they will listen!

nama: Sómbio yunjéf Fol yémorangérmén kété yáne mani faimén méngon a taumyotau fá efe tanmormén yá fandaghét. 31 Yémo tawaufrotau Ngánján limánéghmén a tawatametau Yuré Yesu Kerisoenemén férférofnar a emo yau tékmbongai.
bt: For two years Fol stayed there his lenting house and accepted them who came to see him. 31 He was telling about the ruling of God and taught about the Lord Yesu Keriso without fear and no one stopped him.

nama: Fol, Yesu Kerisoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, nénamndai Yesuene muyafé ár a fénataye náyátoi Ngánjánene mer si waitoghét.
bt: Fol, a without.payment working man(servant) of Keriso Yesu, became a man with Yesu's authority(apostle) and become defferently to preach God's good talk.

nama: Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si yiyátoi yéné mer si tándái, soramé nufrotam. Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém yéfarotawét yénémamén Bukan. 3 Yéné mer si siyém yánjo Ambumoenemén, fá náráftam Deifidene mérnan fifie. 4 A njam nanoi kérta ménaye Ngánján Mbérmbér waftaye, fá té yuyafoi sái témorwén Ngánjánene Ambum Yesu Keriso téfene Yuré. 5 Yá kénjúmé Ngánjánam yénmaramai ngárám Kerisoene yétkwén téfnan yáne muyafé ár, ménát sái elimndam Téndo ár áyárghét árké yéném kétamé ndimbal mwighata. 6 Fém wéi fété em yéfefaf, efe té náyátoyénd Yesu Kerisoene.
bt: God did without.lying talk(promise) this good talk long ago, before it happened. God's people who make know(prophets) wrote about this in the Buk(scripture) 3 This good talk is about his Son, he was born in family of Deifid bodily. 4 And when he was raised from death by the power of the God Spirit, he was shown to be the Son of God Yesu Keriso our Lord. 5 Through him God gave us grace men with Yesu's authority(apostle) for the sake of Keriso's name,{s{ ...f* {s{1.5 men with Yesu's authority(apostle) for the sake of Keriso's name, and appointed us to do the work of apostle so that we lead the Side people(Gentiles) to hear which comes from big mind(faith){t{ ...f* {t{1.5 so that we lead the Side people(Gentiles) to hear which comes from big mind(faith) in order to lead people of all nations to believe and obey

nama: A yéndon yéfarotan méinyotio féfeyot kétán efe em Romén efe euwete Ngánjánam a namndetat yáne njúnnjúnofnar ár. Ngárám a krufrát féfeyot Ngánjáneta téfene Afé a kétamé Yuré Yesu Keriso. Folene ménde yafoghét Romét
bt: And I am writing to all of you.p to whom are in Rom whom God loves and became{u{ ...f* {u{1.7 became whom he called and you became his without.dust(holy) people: Grace and peace to you.p from God our Father and from the Lord Yesu Keriso. Fol's longing to visit Rom

nama: Fronde, yéndon kánjo ewarametan tanjo Ngánjáne Yesu Keriso kénjúmé féfeyot ményotio, ménamén féfene ndimbal mwighé téfnan mband marat afrogh té néngufngo. 9 Ngánjáne yéndon yausáfotan tane tikfe ménáyan yáne Ambumoene si waitoghan, fá siyém tane ghékéf fayogh ár ndernáye ekmboghofnar yéndon ewafarotan fém 10 tane Ngánjánafé orayégh kénjún ményotio efghon. A yénd Ngánjánafé noraiwam ndené sénonjo soramé Ngánjánene méndeyan end so kasawérngé tayot sái wéném féfefaf. 11 Yénd minde ménde wém féfe fandaghét, ménát yéndon so eférmetan féfefaf ámb mbérmbére tambén ménát so táyátongé muyafé. 12 Ndené fété fém a yénd sái nawáutánam mere téfene ndimbal mwighaye.
bt: First, I gave thanks my God through Yesu Keriso, for all of you.p, because your.p big mind(faith) had started preaching the world.{v{ ...f* {v{1.8 for all of you.p, because your.p big mind(faith) had started preaching the world. because the whole world is hearing about your faith.

nama: Tane fútar, yénd ménde wém fém sái mat em ndené yénd ménde yéta kwém tárfár mbinotma sái kwénmorwén féfefaf, wénde yénanotio sénonjo, ámb yamam wékmbote. Yénd ménde wém yéndo sái násáfotan féfe kénjún a so yéfandan mer mbwe, ndené fété yéndon kés yéfandan ámb Téndo ár kénjún. 14 Yéndo táf so ewaufrotan yáne mer si méinyotio árét. Yéndo so ewafrotan yáne mer si árét, Grikmén árét o yau Grikmén árét, a endefaf em káfeté, o endefaf em káfetafnar. 15 Yéné téfnan yénd ménde wém mer si waitoghét wéi féfefaf efe em Romén. Mer sioene wafté
bt: My friends, I want you.p to know that I have tried many times to come to you.p, but until now, something has stopped me. I will see a fruit of my work among you.p{y{ ...f* {y{1.13 I will see a fruit of my work among you.p I want to work among you and see good fruit

nama: Yénd yau wáiwái wém yéné mer simén, ménamén yéné siyém Ngánjánene wafté wanjarghét méinyotio árét emofem efalo yiyátotat, fronde yéfeyot Israil árfet a yénamayo Téndo árét. 17 Ménamén yéné mer sian mbarkánd yam kétamé yéném Ngánjáneta nénufane, a mbarkánd yam árké yém ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye kétamé frondeta kétán soramé,{a{ ...f* {a{1.17 Ménamén yéné mer sian mbarkánd yam kétamé yéném Ngánjáneta nénufane, a mbarkánd yam árké yém ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye kétamé frondeta kétán soramé, This Good News tells us how God makes us right in his sight. This is accomplished from start to finish by faith.(NLT)-translate it again ndené fété kés yéfarotaménd: "Mbarkánd ár so emor ndimbal mwighaye." Ngánjánene nékw méinyotio árfefaf
bt: I am not shame this good talk, because this is God's power to save all who truly approve(believe), first to them the Israil people and also the Side people. 17 For in this good talk the straight thing came from God revealed and a straight thing which is by big mind(faith) from first to last, just as they wort it, “The straight people will live by big mind(faith).” God's anger to all the people

nama: Ngánjánene nékw nufrote njafarta yéfefaf méinyotio ngánjánofnar ghérsafé ár a yaufiyam wafrogh téfnár emofem yengwitrotat efalo yéfene kénjúta. 19 Fá so fété mat em Ngánján efe yém, ménamén Ngánjánamyo té ewauyafoi sáfotro yéfeyot. 20 Kétamé mband marat nufrotam, árém té yinjoyénd mband a njafar. Yénayan méinyotio rokar Ngánjánam ewafrotam, yémofem féyotaro so efandat safénro yáne fandaghofnar yam, yáne akegh wafté a ménde. Féyo kété ámb yau endeyot si yém sái noraya, yénd yau mat ténmorwén Ngánjánenemén efaloyam.
bt: God's anger showed from heaven to them all the people without.god life and evil doing people who remove the truth from their inside. 19 They should know who God is{z{ ...f* {z{1.19 who God is need 'what God is like'

nama: Ndemamén fá mat em Ngánjánenemén, wénde yémofem yau kor táwefnotawét ndené fété Ngánján, o yau kor tarametawét kánjo si yáfaf. Wénde fá yau kor kemnawét safénro. Yéfene mwighé nénamndayénd kwéfitayan a orsaghan. 22 Ndemamén fá namndetat káfeté ár em, wénde fá em mwighémwighafnar; 23 Yémofem yékenat sái yáwefnotat ndimbal Ngánjáne efe yau so kér yém. Wénde, yémofem ewafrotat unyégh ngánján yéfendio. Yémofem ewáwefnotat mewangat árké em árnit efe kér so em. Yémofem wéi ewafrotat árké nérétat mennit, wérárnit a kambannit, a yémofem ewáwefnotat.
bt: Even though they know God, but they do not worship him well as God, nor do they give thank talk well to him. But they did not even think clearly any more. Their minds became dark and in confusing. 22 Even though they say they are wise, but they are without mind(foolish). 23 They refused to worship the big God who will not die. Instead, they made lying gods for themselves. They worshipped idols which were like people who must die. They also made idols which looked like birds, animals and snakes, and they worshipped them.

nama: Féyo Ngánjánam eumyoi yaufiyam méndeyotro tikfan kétán máméghéraye walot ménde wifogh yéngwafngoyénd yéfene fifiyan fárofáro. 25 Fá nákenayénd efalo yátoghét Ngánjánenemén.efaloyam. Wénde yémofem yanayénd unyégh. Yémofem táwáwefnotawét a kásáfotawét rokarat Ngánjánam árké ewafrotam. Wénde yémofem nakenayénd Ngánján wáwefnoghét. Fá yéné yém emo ewafrotam yéné méinyotio rokar. Méinyotio árém táf yuwetat fá tuot! Efalo. Yéné siyém efalofifi.
bt: So God allowed them in the sinful desires in their hearts to fill the desire for bad sexuality in their bodies with each other. 25 They refused to believe the true thing about God. But they took lies. They worshiped and worked things that God has made. But they refused to worship God himself. He is the one who made all those things! Everyone should praise him eternal! Amen. This is really true.

nama: Yéné téfnan, Ngánjánam eramai kétán wáiwáiafé ménde yam wafroghét. Yéfene amafam yengoyénd árfé kémégh yam kétán amafro kémégh yamfaf. 27 Ndené yameyo árém yéfrangoyénd amafafé kémégh yam a nufngoyénd ár ghakér ro yam fárofáro. Ár wáiwáiafé yam nefngoyénd fárofáro, a yanayénd fai yéfenjo yaufimén.
bt: Because of this, God gave them for doing shameful desires. Their women changed sleeping with men into women sleeping with women. 27 In the same way the men left sleeping with woman and started men sleeping with men each other. Men started shameful things with each other, and received penalty for their sins.

nama: Táf yéné ár nakenayénd sái tafarotawét Ngánján, féyo Ngánjánam eumyoi yéfene mwighé ewasérmbotam. Féyo yéné téfnan, yémofem tawafrotawét yaufi yam árkéméné yau so tawafrotawét. 29 Fá té ewafoi méinyotio fénaté áyáréghofnar yamam, yaufi, nité a mámáfi. Fá sem waramnjaghro, ngi, ásmégh, wasogh a ár fayogh. Fá sem si tiyárégh, 30 a unyégh si wafrogh árfenemén, Ngánján méndménd, utmanéghofnar, ndimbalndimbal mwighé, a awarmbogh mwighé. Yémofem nemnat tárfár ende yaufi wafroghét, a yémofem yau eyáretat yéferafaf. 31 Fá yau mat em némé yém mbarkánd. Yémofem eráfnat yéfene unyéghofnar si. Yémofem yau euwetat ámb ár a yau em ghérghérsisé ámb árfefaf. 32 Fá mat em Ngánjánene mbarkánd yam yénat emofem ewafrotat yéné yam so em kér. Wénde yémofem ménjtaye mé ewafrotat yéné yaufi yam. Minde mámáfiot, yémofem ewawanjat ámb ár ménát sái ewafrotat wéi.
bt: These people refused to remember God, so God allowed their minds become spoiled. So in this reason, they do bad things that they should not do. 29 They are filled with all kinds of without.hearing, evil, greed, and wrong. They are only jealousy, murder, fighting, deceit, and piling people up(blame). They are gossips 30 and make lying talk about people, hate God, without.respectful, big mind(proud), and boastful mind. They think of many ways to do evil, and they don't obey their parents. 31 They did not understand what is straight. They break their without.lying talks(promises). They do not love other people and do not mercy to other people. 32 They know that God's straight thing for those who do these things will be die. But they continue to do these bad things. Very badly, they instruct other people to do them, too.

nama: Yénémamén, fém mé nemne fém sái moghan ekmeta ndené ár, wénde fém ndené mámáfiyo ném, a féfeyot ámb yau so ném uwangogh! Njam fém noraye fá em yaufi a so tékratan em, fém fémyo moghan nakmeta, ménamén fémo emofem njam moghan ekmeta ámb ár ewafrote yéné yamyo wéi. 2 Yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánján siyém mbarkánd yém njam yémo moghan ekmeta fá emofem ewafrotat yéné yam. 3 Fémo yéférmete ámogh yéfefaf a ewafrote wéi yéné yamyo. Fém nemne so kátnongi Ngánjánene moghta? 4 Nawafarotati ndené Ngánján siyém ramégh kér ro a métar téfefaf. Fá nawanjete teke soramé yémo so tékratan yénékmete yénd. Fém té nemne yéné siyém fifiofnar fefaf? Fém fiya mat kamndé ndené Ngánjánam ewaramete fe efoghféneféne awengoghét. Fá nawanjete métare, ménát fém so kawengo yaufiyamta.
bt: Therefore, you.s still think you can put in judgement such people, but you.s are just as bad, and to you.s will be no other reply(excuse)! When you.s say they are wicked and should be in punishment, you.s put yourself in judgment, because you.s who put other people in judgment do these things too. 2 We know that God is straight when he put them in judgment who do these things. 3 You.s pass judgment on them and do also these things. Do you.s think you.p will escape Godʼs judgment? 4 Remember that God is really giving(very kind) and patient to us. He waits a long time before he will put us in suffering. Do you.s think this is without.meaning to you.s? You.s should understand that God is giving you.s a time(chance) to change. He waits patiently, so that you.s may turn away from sin.

nama: Wénde fém nakena awengoghét yéné ende fém némor. Fém yau ghérghérsisé ném ménamén yaufi yammén ndené fémofem tawafrote. Yéné téfnan, fémo téméinda Ngánján nékwét féfendio. Féyo, yémo so tékratan tékmangé fém minde yéné efghon njam yémo moghan tákmangé méinyotio ár. Yéné efghon, yémo so yuyafote ndernáye nékw so yém yéné árfefaf emofem ewafrotat yaufiyam. Méinyotio ár mat so em ndené yáne mogh árét siyém mbarkánd. 6 Ngánjánam so ewaramete méinyotio fénatayero fai ároe yénayan yémofem némé tawafrotat. 7 Yéfeyot emofem ménjtaye yéngwafrotat mer, yéronjat ndimbal yétkwén, utmanghét a kérofnar ghérsat ndené Ngánjánam so eramete, Ngánjánam so tawaramngé tuot ghérsé. 8 Wénde yéfeyot efe fifi ménde wifarghan tém a emofem tékarotat efalo si a táitotat yaufi, kété so yém minde nékw. 9 Kété so yém kértayam a tékrát méinyotio árét emofem ewafrotat yaufi: fronde so yém Ju árfefaf, yénamayo Téndo árfefaf. 10 Wénde ndimbal yétkwén, utmanégh a krufrát yém yéfeyot méinyotio árét emofem tawafrotat mer: fronde Ju árfeyot, yénamayo Téndo árfeyot. 11 Ménamén Ngánjánam moghan ekmete méinyotio ár ámbrérero.
bt: But you.s refuse to change this way that you.s live. You.s are not sorry about the wrong things that you.s have done. In this reason, you.s cause God's anger for youeself. So, he will put you.s in suffering even more on this day when he will put every people in judgment. On that day, he will show how angry he is against these people who do bad things. Every people will know that his judgment is straight to people. 6 God will give all different pay[reward or punish] to man according to what they did. 7 To those who continue to do good, to seek big name(glory), honor and without.dead that God offers, God will give them eternal life. 8 But for those who are chasing of the bodily desire and who reject the true talk and follow evil, there will be strong anger. 9 There will be trouble and suffering for every people who does evil: first for the Ju people, second for the side people. 10 But big name(glory), honor and peace for every people who does good: first for the Ju people, second for the Side people. 11 Because God puts all the people in judgment same way.

nama: Ár emofem yaufiyam tawafrotat nákofnaye wéi so kér em nákofnaye, a ár emofem yaufiyam tawafrotat nákanyo so moghan em náke. 13 Ménamén yau fá mbarkánd em efe áyáréghro náyáretat nákfaf Ngánjánrére, wénde fá emofem ewaitotat nák fá sof tiyátongé mbarkánd Yáne sáfrére. 14 Téndo árfefaf yau em Ngánjánene nák. Wénde yémofem mé té ewafrotat mbarkánd yam ndené yéné nák ndernáye norayat. Yémofem ewafrotat mbarkánd yam ménamén fá ndernáye nemnat yém mbarkánd. Yéné ende fá nauyafotat ndené yéfefaf em yéné nák yéfene mwighayan, ndemamén fá mafina em Mosesene nák. 15 Yémofem ndené yufarotat Ngánjánene nák nafarotaménd yéfene tikfan. Yéfene emargham tawaufrongé ánde yém mer a yaufi. Ámb efghon yéfene mwighé noraye: "Fémo té tafrote ámb yaufi.' A ámb efghon yéfene mwighé noraye: "Fémo tafrote némé mbarkánd yém.' 16 Yéné fiyém si ndené yéndo ewaufrotan árét. Ngánján mat yém méinyotio árké ár kwankwane néngemnat. Ámb efghon Ngánjánam so moghan tákmangé méinyotio ár Yesu Keriso kénjúmé. Ju ár a nák
bt: Men who do sin without the law will also die without the law, and men who sin under the law will be judged by the law. 13 Because they are not straight those who only hear the law in Godʼs sight, but those who follow the law will become straight before Him. 14 The Side people do not have God's laws. But they may still do the straight things that those laws say. They do straight things because of what they think is straight. In that way they are showing that they have a this law in their minds, even though they do not know Moses' laws. 15 They show that God's law are written on their hearts. Their consciences show where is good and evil. In other days their mind said: "You did some sin." And other days their mind said: "You did what is straight." 16 That is the talk that I tell people. God knows everything that people think secretly. One day God will put every people in judgment through Yesu Keriso. The Ju people and the law

nama: Fém njam namndeta Ju ár ném, fém njam námyota kétán Ngánjánene nákfaf a nawarsa fene fénaté fútaryammén Ngánjánafé. 18 Néméwét, fém mat ném némé ménde Ngánján yém. Fém mat ném ndené mbárkándyam siyém mer em ménamén fém té ewatametaménd yáne nák. 19 Fém njam féyotaro ném ndené fém wambégh ném yéfeyot kwéfkwéf árét, a kwayaghé yéfeyot efe em kwéfitayan, 20 watamegh ár onon árfene, a watamegh ár ambumtértérafene, ménamén fém féyotaro ném ndené Ngánjánene nákam ewaramete féyotio mat a efalo. 21 Mer fété, njam fémo ewatamete ámb ár, némamén wét fémyo yau nawatameta? Fémo emndeta ámb ár oro yau, wénde fém oro té nufrote? 22 Fém efe noraye ndené amafanyo awanégh yau, wénde fémo amafanyo ewaneta? Fém noraye fémo méndméndat efanda mewang, wénde fémo engérsa rokar kétándmé mewangoene wawefnogh méngota? 23 Fém efe nawarsa ménamén nák mat ném, wénde yau yutémndati Ngánján ménaye eráfnati nák. 24 Bukam norayai yénémamén: 'Ménamén fém Israil ár, Téndo ár norayat yaufi si Ngánjánefaf.'
bt: If you.s are Ju man, you.s are relying on God's law and you.s boast about your.s special friendship with God. 18 Yes, you.s know what God wants. You.s know that straight things are good because his law already taught. 19 If you.s are possible{b{ ...f* {b{2.19 possible need be, convinced

nama: Ghéngan néfégh yam tukényo yém táf fémo njam ewaitota nák, wénde fémo njam eráfna nák, fém ndenanit nénamnde fém yau ghéngan néfégh yaman némormén. 26 Ár efe njam ghéngan néfghofnar yém sái ewaitota nák, fá so tiyátoi ndené ghéngan néfégh yaman yémormén. 27 Efaloye, fá efe yau ghéngan yénfayénd fifie a mé ewaitota nák yémo so moghan ekmete fém mé nák rafarégh ár ném, ndemamén fene faroghmén nák yém a ghéngan néfégh yam. 28 Ár efe Ju ár ghérsé yémor fifiyero yau yém efalo Ju ár. A ghéngan néfégh yau fifiménro yam yém. 29 Yau, efalo Ju ár siyém endene tikéf yém mbarkánd Ngánjánafé a ghéngan néfghét yam siyém ghéngan néfghét yam tikfan, Ngánján Mbérmbére, yau faroghmén náke. Yéné sáfot árfene áuwégh yau yéném árta, wénde kétamé Ngánjánta.
bt: Skin cutting thing(circumcision) is high if you.s follow the law, but if you.s break the law, you.p have become like that you.s are not in cutting skin. 26 If a man who is not cut skin(circumcised), he keeps the law, he will be regarded as he was in cutting skin. 27 Truly, the one who is not cut skin(circumcised) physically and yet obeys the law will put you.s in judgment are a lawbreaking man, even though you.s have the written law and cutting skin. 28 The man who lives as a Juda only in the body is not a true Ju man. And cutting skin is not only something of the body 29 No, the true Ju man is one who is straight with God and cutting skin is cutting skin of the heart, by God's Spirit, not by the written law. That kind of man's praise is not from men, but from God.

nama: Némé mer yém Ju árét, o némé fénataye yém ghéngan néfégh yammén? 2 Tárfár yaman! Frondefifi, Ngánjánam ekafai a ewaramai yánjo si Ju árét. 3 Ndernáye wét ámb Ju ár kés tamorwén ndimbal mwighafnar? Némé yénayan so Ngánján ndimbal mwighafnar kamndangai? 4 Yaufifi! Ngánján siyém efalo, a méinyotio ár em unyégh ár. Ndené kés yéfarotaménd, "Ngánján, fém efalo so niyátote mbarkándaye njam noraya a so nawainde njam moghan ném."
bt: What is good to a Jew, or what is different/special in cutting skin? 2 In many things! First of all, God chose and gave his talk to the Ju people. 3 How about some Ju people were without.big mind(unfaithful)? Does this mean that God will become without.big mind(unfaithful)? 4 Never! God is true, and every people are lying people. As it is written: “God, you.s will be proved straight when you.s speak and you.s will win when you.s are in judgment.”

nama: Ámb ár korayawét ndené, 'Mámáfi yam árké yéndfem ewafrotam yuyafotat fanfnenro ndené Ngánján siyém túmén mbarkánd. Yénémamén, Ngánján siyém yau mbarkánd yém sái tékratan yénékmeta yénd? 6 Yéné yau yém efalo! Ngánján túmén mwighé yéfarone némé yém mbarkánd a féyotaro. Yéné téfnan yémo so moghan ekmete méinyotio ár mband maratan.
bt: Some people say that, ‘The wrong things which we do show more clearly that God is always straight. Therefore, God is not just he will put us in judgment? 6 That is not true! God always decides what is straight and fair. That is why he is able to put every people in the world in judgment.

nama: Ámb ár mété norayat, "Yénd njam nuindan, yéné yam yufarote nderé ndimbal yém a mer Ngánján. Tane unyégh yamam yuyafote méinyo safén Ngánján túmén noraye némé yém efalo. Yénémamén, Ngánján yau so noraye ndené yénd wém yaufi ár. Yémo yau so kwékmangé tékratan." 8 Wénde yéné njam yém efalo, fém so wéi noraye, 'Yéndfem mé so ewafrotam mindefifi mámáfi yam. Féyo ámb mer yam so nufrote yéné téfnan.' Ámb ár wéi néngorayat yéné yém si yéndon árké ewatametan. Wénde njam fá korayangi ndené, yémofem yénd wasalitat. Yéné yém mbarkánd Ngánjánam so tékratan tákmangé yéné ár. Méinyotio ár séném yaufiyam ár
bt: Someone might say, "When I tell lies, that thing shows how big and good God is. My lying thing shows more clearly that God always says what is true. Therefore, God should not say that I am a bad man. He should not put me in suffering. ” 8 But if that is true, you could also say, ‘We will keep on doing wrong things very much. Then some good thing will happen in this reason.’ Some people also say that is the talk which I teach. But if they say that, they are insulting me. It is straight that God should put those people in suffering. All the people are sinners

nama: Némé yénd so norayan yénamé? Yénd merro yéném? Yaufifi! Yénd té efalo nauyafongém ndené Ju ár a Téndo ár ndenaro em méinyotio yaufi mbanmbanan. 10 Ndené fété kés yéfarotaménd: Ámb efe yau mbarkánd yém, ámbiro ámb yau;
bt: What will we say about this? Are we well? Never! We have truly shown that Ju people and Side people same are all under sin. 10 As it is written: “No one is straight, not even one;

nama: efe ámb yau yém emo mat yiyátoté yau ámb emo yéronje Ngánján.
bt: No one understands no one is looking for God.

nama: Méinyotio té kangotat fá té nénafghangi nénáyátongi fifiofnar, efe yau yém ámbiro emo ewafrote mer, yau ámbiro ámb yém.
bt: All have turned away, they have combined and became without.meaning, there is no one who does good, not even one.

nama: Yéfene kémngaféne sem ásauréghkaf mbéwé. Yéfene mbírmbíram yafrote unyégh. Yéfene si sem ndenanit kamban rarégh.
