bt: Their throats are open graves. Their lips do lie. Their talk is like snake bite.

nama: Yéfene sénféne siyém waghanégh a njérmat si afoghare.
bt: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.

nama: Yéfene kafkaf wékeye em ár ngiot.
bt: Their feet are quick to kill people.

nama: Awirorghafé a usérmboghafé eyota,
bt: They walk with ruining and with destroying,

nama: krufrát ghérsé fá mafina em.
bt: life of peace they do not know.

nama: Kété yau yém Ngánján férfér yéfene ghékfan."
bt: There is no fear of God in their eyes.”

nama: Féyo, yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánjánene nák yéfene yém Ju árfene. Ngánjánam emndai fá ménát méinyotio waitoghét árkéméné yáne nákam ewatamete. Yéné fifiyan, ámb ár so korayangé: 'Ngánjánam yau so tékratan kwékmangé yénd.' Méinyotio ár mband maratan so ewakai Ngánjánene sáfrére a yémo so emote fá. 20 Ngánjánene nákam ewauyafote árét safénro ndené yémofem té tawafrotat yaufi yam. Efe ámb yau so tiyátongé mbarkánd Ngánjánrére nák waitoghan. Yesum yéngwanai téfene tékrát
bt: So, we know that God's law belongs to Ju people. God told them in order to follow everything that his law teaches. In this meaning, anyone can say: ‘God should not put me in suffering.’ All people in the world must stand in front of God and he will judge them. 20 God's law shows people clearly that they have done evil things. No one will be approved straight in front of God by following the law. Yesu took our pains

nama: Wénde sénonjo yau ménaye nák waitoghe, mbarkánd yam kétamé Ngánjáneta mat té yényátoi. Wénde Mosesene nák a Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfene si té ténmaufrotawét yénémamén. 22 Yéné mbarkánd yam kétamé Ngánjáneta yéném ndimbal mwighamé Yesu Keriso kénjún yéfefaf méinyotio emofem efalo yiyátotat. Yau em fénataye kétamé Ju ár a Téndo ár, 23 ménamén yénd méinyotio té nényawayém yaufiyam kénjún a nénamndayém ténjfi Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghata, 24 a yényátoi mbarkánd yánjo ngáráme yénamé wanjarégh árké yénmormén Keriso Yesuefafmé. 25 Ngánjánam yuyafotam ndené Yesu Keriso kér yémormén wafatarégh wénan ghéngén téfene yaufiyammén. Ngánjánam yénmawite téfene táf njam efalo yiyátotam Yesu a yáne ghéngén férék. Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam ménát yuyafotam safén ndené fá siyém túmén mbarkánd a féyotaro. Tándái, Ngánjánam yau tékratan takmetau ár emofem mámáfi yam tawafrotawét. 26 Yémo yau takmetau tékratan fá ménamén fá témorwén métar. Fá mat témorwén némé yémo so tafrotau sénonjo, yéné efghon. Yémo yuyafoi ndené fá siyém féyotio mbarkánd a féyotaro. Yémo eumyote ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf njam efalo yiyátotat Yesu.
bt: But now not by following the law, the straight thing from God has been made known us. But the law of Moses and the talk of the people who make to know God knowldge(prophets) have told us about this. 22 This straight thing from God came through big mind(faith) in Yesu Keriso to all who truly approve(believe). There is no difference between Ju people and Side people, 23 because we have all go into sin and became short of the big light(glory) of God, 24 and approve us straight by his grace that salvation came through Keriso Yesu. 25 God showed that Yesu Keriso died on the cross as a sacrifice for our sins. God washes us if we truly approve Yesu and his sacrifice blood. God did this in order that he shows clearly that he is always straight and enough/fair. In past, God did not put in suffering people who did wrong things. 26 He did not put them in suffering because he is very patient. He knew what he was going to do now, in this time. He has now shown that he is completely straight and fair. He accepts people as straight with him if they truly approve(believe) Yesu.

nama: Yénémaménnde, féyotaro yénd so nawarsam? Yau. Némé téfnan yénémamén? Téfene uwangogh yém meryam wafroghe? Yau, wénde ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye. 28 Ár káyátongé mbarkánd Ngánjánafé yénamé ndimbal mwighayan, yau yénamé sái ewaitota nák. 29 Ngánján yau yém Ju árfene Ngánján naro. Fá wéi yém Téndo árfene Ngánján. Néméwét, fá siyém méinyotio árfene Ngánján. 30 Yéna yém ámbiroro Ngánján. Yémo so taumyongé Ju ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf njam efalo yiyátotat Keriso. Yémo wéi so eumyote ámb ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf yémofem njam efalo yiyátotat Keriso. 31 Némé yéndfem so ewironam nák yéné ndimbal mwighayan? Yaufifi! Yéndfem njam efalo yiyátotam Yesu, yéndfem taikrataye eyatotam Ngánjánene nák.
bt: Therefore, can we boast about it? Nothing! What is the reason for this? Our answer is doing good things? No, but it is by the big mind(faith). 28 Man become straight with God through big mind(faith), not trough following the law. 29 God is not God only for Ju people. He is also God for the Side people. Yes! He is the God for all people. 30 There is only one God. He will accept Ju people as straight with himself if they truly approve(believe) Keriso. He will also accept other people as straight with himself if they truly approve(believe) Keriso. 31 How we are going to destroy the law by this big mind(faith)? Not at all! When we truly approve(believe) Yesu, we are making strong God's laws.

nama: Némé so yénémamén norayam táf téfene afé Ebraam? Némnati némé yémo té yifoi. 2 Táf Ebraam njam té yiyátoi mbarkánd yam mer wafroghan Ngánjánam, fá so kawarsau yénémamén. Wénde yéné yau yémormén Ngánjánene ende. 3 Ndernáye wét Buk si noraye? "Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau Ngánján, a yéné téfnan fá yiyátoi mbarkánd yam."
bt: What then shall we say that our father, Ebraam? Think about what he discovered. 2 If God made rightoues Ebraam in doing good, he can boast about it. But that was not God's way. 3 What does Scripture say? “Ebraam truly approved(believed) God, and in this reason he(God) made him straight thing.”

nama: Táf ár njam násáfotat, yéfenjo fai yau yém tambén, wénde tawanangi ménamén ásáfogh fá té tafrotat. 5 Wénde árké árém yau so ewafrotat ámb ásáfogh árkéménayam so eyátote mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. Wénde, yémofem njam efalo yiyátotat Ngánján, féyo Ngánjánam so taumyongé fá. Ménamén yéfene ndimbal mwighamén, Ngánján noraye yéné ár sem mbarkánd em yémafé. Yéné fiyém Ngánjánene tambén yémafayo emofem yiyáretat fá. 6 Deifid ndenéyameyo noraye yénémaményo njam korayau ároene frengéghmén endefaf Ngánjánam téramangé mbarkánd yam ndemamén yau téu ewafrotat ásáfogh yéné frengégh résaghét:
bt: When people work, their pay is not a gift, but they received because they have done work. 5 But nobody can do any work that will approve them straight with God. Instead, if they truly approve(believe) God, then God will accept them. Because of their big mind(faith), God says these people are straight with himself. That is God's gift even to those who have not obeyed him. 6 Deifid also says the this thing when he speaks of the blessedness of the one to whom God gives straight thing even though they do not work to receive the blessing:

nama: "Frengéghkaf fá em endefene yaufiyam tawalindangé, endefene yaufiyam rautoghkaf em.
bt: “Blessed are those whose sins he(God) washed, whose sins are covered.

nama: Frengéghkaf yéné ár yém endene yaufiyam Yuram so yau ewawafte yéfefaf!"
bt: Blessed is this man whose sin the Lord will never count against them.”

nama: Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam méinyotio árét, yéfe Ju ár a Téndo ár. Méinyotio ár efalo áuwéghan so em yénéyan, njam Ngánjánam taumyongé fá. Yénd té kénorayam ndené Ebraam ndimbal mwighayan Ngánjánam yiyátoi mbarkánd. 10 Yémofem ghéngan yénfayénd Ebraamene ménát yufarotayénd ndené fá témorwén Ju ár. Nemnati yéné efoghmén njam Ngánjánam yumyoi Ebraam. Yéné té nufrotam frondeyo yémofem ghéngan yénfayénd yáne, o yéné soramé? Yénd mat yéném ndené yéné yémormén frondeyo soramé ghéngan yanfayénd. 11 Ngánjánam yémndai Ebraam ndené ámb árém so ghéngan ténfangai. Yéné so témorwén mbor yáne fifiyan ménát so tufarotau ndené Ngánjánam té yumyoi. Ngánjánam té yumyoi Ebraam ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Ngánjánam yau yumyoi Ebraam ménamén yémofem ghéngan yanfayénd ndené Ju ár témorwén. Yéné yam yufarote ndené Ebraam siyém afé méinyotio árét árké Ngánjánam eumyoi. Ndené Ebraam, Ngánjánam eumyoi fá ménamén yémofem efalo tiyátotawét fá. Yéné yau yém ndimbal yam árém sái tanfetawét yéfene ghéngan. 12 Wénde Ebraam wéi siyém ndené afé méinyotio Ju árét emofem efalo yiyátotat Ngánján. Ámb árém té ghéngan tanfetawét yéné Ju ár, wénde fá kunfénmetawét Ebraamene ufaroghfaf. Yémofem efalo tiyátotawét Ngánján, ndené fété Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau frondeyo soramé yáne ghéngan yénfayénd.
bt: God does this for all people, for them Ju people and Side people. All people can be really happy like this, when God allows them. We have been saying that God made him straight in Ebraamʼs big mind(faith). 10 They cut skin(circumcised) Ebraam to show that he was a Ju man. Think about that time when God accepted Ebraam. Did it happen before they cut skin(circumcised) him, or after that? We know that it was before they cut skin(circumcised) him! 11 God told Ebraam that someone should cut skin(circumcise) him. That would be a mark on his body to show that God had accepted him. God had already accepted Ebraam because Ebraam truly approved(believed) him. God did not accept Ebraam because they had cut skin(circumcised) him as a Ju man. This thing shows that Ebraam is like a father to every people that God has accepted. Like Ebraam, God has accepted them because they have truly approved(believed) him. It is not big thing that nobody has cut their skin(circumcised) them 12 But Ebraam is also like the father of all Ju people who truly approve(believe) God. Someone has cut skin(circumcised) those Ju people, but they have copied Ebraam's showing. They have truly approved(believed) God, as Ebraam truly approved(believed) before anyone had cut skin(circumcised) him.

nama: Ngánján unyéghofnar sie norayai Ebraamefaf a yáne ákiambumat, ndené mband marat so kamndangé yéfeyot ámb efghon. Ngánjánam yau unyéghofnar sie norayai ménamén Ebraamém tawaitotau nák. Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie norayai ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Yéné téfnan Ngánjánam yumyoi Ebraam ndené fété mbarkánd ár yémafé. 14 Árém yau so tawanangi némat Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie emndai ménamén yémofem ewaitotat nák. Njam yémofem tanangi yénaye, féyo ndimbal mwighé Ngánjánefaf so yém fifiofnar. A némé Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si yém fifiofnar so yém. 15 Mosesene nákam noraye ndené Ngánjánam so tékratan ekmete ár emofem yau ewaitotat nák. Wénde nákofnar, kété ámb yau yém árét sái ewaitotat.
bt: God said without.lying talk(promise) to Ebraam, and to his descendants, that the world would belong to them one day. God did not say without.lying talk(promise) that because Ebraam followed rules. God said without.lying talk(promise) it because Ebraam truly approved(believed) him. That is why God accepted Ebraam as straight man with him. 14 People cannot receive what God said without.lying talk(promise) because they follow rules. If they could receive it by that, then big mind(faith) to God would be without.meaning. And what God did without.lying talk(promise) would be without.meaning. 15 Moses's law say that God will put people in suffering who do not follow the law. But without law, there is nothing for people to follow.

nama: Yénémamén, Ngánjánam yénmaramai unyéghofnar si ndené tambén, ménamén fá siyém kénjtafnar. Yéndfem tawanangém táf njam yéndfem yafarotam fá. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján ndené Ebraam kés témorwén, féyo Ngánjánam ténmumyongé yénd. Yéné fiyém Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si méinyotio Ebraamene mérnat. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján, yéndfem so yémndetam téfene áki. Yéndfem so méinyotio ersam némé Ngánján unyéghofnar si té norayai. Yéné yau yém mé yéfendro emofem ewaitotat Mosesene nák. 17 Ndenáye fété Buk si noraye, "Yéndon té niyáton yéferafé méinyotio mband maratamé." Yéné fiyém némé Ngánjánamyo unyéghofnar si norayai, ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Ngánján yéné féyém emo eméinde ár nanotat kérta. Fá noraye yéné yammén kété árké yau mé em táf yéné njam té tamorwén kété.
bt: Therefore, God gives us his without.lying talk(promise) as a gift, because he is without.inside(very kind). We receive it when we trust him. If we truly approve(believe) God like Ebraam did, then God accepts us. That is God's without.lying talk(promise) to all of Ebraam's family. If we truly approve(believe) God, we can call Ebraam our ancestor. We can all receive what God has said without.lying talk(promise). It is not only for those who have Moses' law to obey 17 As the Buk(scripture) says: “I have made you.s their father of many nations.” That is what God himself says without.lying talk(promise), because Ebraam truly approved(believed) him. God is the one who causes people raise from death. He speaks about these things which are not yet there as if they were already there.

nama: Njam Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie noyarai Ebraam ndené fá so kénamndangé yéferafé mband maratoene, Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau fá. Ngánján wéi té norayai, "Fene ákiambum so em ndené wafoghofnar kénd." Yáne mé yém méghmégh njam yau yáne yém mer téfén méghméghot. 19 Ebraam té nénamndai ndené 100 yunjéf. Yáne fifi nénamdai bulu. Yáne amafausé, Sara wéi ndenáyo féyotarofnar témorwén ambum unjghét. Ebraam mat méinyotio témorwén yénémamén, wénde fá yau nékmboi Ngánján wafarogh. 20 Fá yau kékmbongai efalo yátoghét Ngánjánene unyéghofnar sian. Wénde yémo efalo tiyátotau Ngánján minde muyaye. Yémo efalo tiyátotau Ngánjánene ndimbal wafté. 21 Ebraam efalo yiyátoi ndené Ngánjánam so tawafrotau némat fá unyéghofnar si yémndai wafroghét. 22 Yénémamén yéndfem yéfandam ndené yéné siyém efalo: Ménamén Ebraamene ndimbal mwighamén, Ngánjánam yumyoi fá ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé.
bt: When God says without.lying talk(promise) Ebraam that he will become their father of the world, Ebraam truly approved(believed) him. God have said again, “Your.s descendants will be like without.counting stars.” He continued to have a hope when he is not good reason to hope. 19 Ebraam has became about 100 years old. His body became old man. His (old) wife, Sarah, was also unable to have children. Ebraam understood all about that, but he did not stop remembering God. 20 He did not stopped believing the without.lying talk(promise) of God. But he truly approved(believed) in God very strongly. He truly approved(believed) God's big power. 21 Ebraam really approved that God can do what he had said without.lying talk(promise) to do. 22 Therefore we see that it is true: Because of Ebraam big.mind(faith), God accepted him as straight with him.

nama: Nemnati yéné simén, 'Ngánjánam yumyoi fá ndené fété fá mbarkándro yém. Fá yau yáneménro norayat Ebraam, 24 wénde wéi téfeyot. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján, yémo so ténmumyongé yénd ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé. Ngánjánam yanoi kérta téfene Yuré Yesu, ménát fá nénamndai ghérsat wéikor yáne kér soramé. 25 Ngánjánam eumyoi ár yésmetaménd Yesu wafatarégh wénan téfene yaufiyammén. Féyo Ngánjánam yanoi Yesu kérta, ménát yuyafoi ndené yémo so yénd ténmumyongé ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé.
bt: Think about those talks, ‘God accepted him just as he is straight.’ They are not only speaking about Ebraam, 24 but also for us. If we truly approve(believe) God, he will accept us as straight with him. God raised our Lord Yesu, so that he became alive again after his death. 25 God allowed people kill Yesu on the cross for our sins. Then God raised Yesu from death, to show that he would accept us as straight with him.

nama: Yénémaménnde, yénd efandam ndené Ngánján té yénmumyoi yénd ndené fété mbarkándro ménamén yéndfem efalo yiyátotam fá. Yéné téfnan, téfefaf yém krufrát Ngánjánafé ménamén némé téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisom té yafrotam téfeyot. 2 Téfene ndimbal mwighaye, Kerisom té yéné tuk sifayat yénérsam ánde yénd sénonjo yénmakai. A yénd féyotaroe méghméghafé néngretam kété sái yéném Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghayan. 3 Yénd áuwéghafé yéném mé njam yénd tékratan yéném. Yénd mat yéném ndené tékratam téfunangé awamarogh, 4 awamarogham yanote imángéghyam a imángéghyamam enyátote téfene méghmégh awanjarghét. 5 Yéné méghmégham yau so tinyátongé sotot yénd. Yénd mat yéném nderé uwégh yénd yénmuwetam, ménamén Ngánjánam té yénmaramai Ngánján Mbérmbér ménát sái tawafotau téfene tikéf yáne uwéghe.
bt: Therefore, we see that God has accepted us as straight because we truly approve(believe) him. In this reason, we have peace with God because of what our Lord Yesu Keriso did for us. 2 By our big mind(faith), Keriso has taken us this high place where we are now standing. And we confidently look with hope there we will be in the big light(glory) of God. 3 We keep on being with joy when we are in troubles. We know that troubles produces endurance, 4 endurance raises movement(character), and movement(character) makes our hope for salvation. 5 This hope will not make us empty. We know how much he loves us, because God has given us God Spirit to fill our heart with his love.

nama: Yénd njam mé ténmorwén muyafnar wáutáréghét yéndro, Keriso yénmormén mé yéné efghonfifi a kér yémormén téfe yaufiyam árét. 7 Tárfár ár yau so em sái kér em mbarkánd ároe, ndemamén ámb ár mété táf ménde yém sái kér yém árot efe yém minde mer. 8 Wénde Ngánjánam yuyafotam yánjo ndimbal uwégh téfefaf ménaye yénmáfáretam Keriso ménát kér sái témorwén téfeyot táf yaufiyaman mé sténmorwén. 9 Ngánjánam yénmumyoi ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé yánjo férke. Yénd féyotaroe mat yéném yémon so ténmanjarngé Ngánjánene nékwta. 10 Yémon té yényátoi fútar Ngánjánafé yánjo ambumoene kér kénjúmé táf yénd mé njam ténmorwén yáne mék téfnár. Sénonjo yénd Ngánjánene fútar yéném, yánjo Ambumom féyotaroe so ténmanjarngé yánjo ghérsaye. 11 Wénde yéné yau yém méinyotio! Yénd minde náuwetam sénonjo ménamén némé Ngánjánam té yafrotam téfeyot. Téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisom té yafrotam yéné féyotaroe. Yéné yém ménamén yánemén ndené yénd té sénonjo nénáyátongém Ngánjánene fútar. Kér Edémene kénjún, ghérsé Kerisoene kénjún
bt: When we were still without.power to help ourselves, Keriso came at just that time and died for us sinning people. 7 Many people would not be willing to die for an straight man, though some man might be willing to die for a person who is very good. 8 But God showed his big love for us by sending Keriso to die for us while we were still in sin. 9 God has accepted us as straight with himself by his blood. We possibly know he will save us from God's anger. 10 He made us friends with God through the death of his Son while we were his fighting men(enemies). Now we are God's friends, his Son will certainly save us by his life! 11 But that is not everything! We rejoice very much now because of what God has done for us. Our Lord Yesu Keriso has made this possible. It is because of him that we have now become God's friends. Death in Edèm, life in Keriso

nama: Yaufiyam nényawai mband maratot ámbiro ár kénjúmé, a yáne yaufiyamam yénérsam kér. Yéné ende, kéram méinyotio ár yanai, ménamén méinyotio árém yaufiyam nufrotam. 13 Soramé Ngánjánam nák eramai fronde yaufiyam témorwén mband maratan. Wénde yéné efghon, Ngánján yau ndené norayai ár kértat so tákmangai, ménamén kété yau tamorwén nák yéfeyot waitoghét. 14 Wénde mé kéram talimndau méinyotio ár, kétamé Edémene efoghta kétán Mosesene efogh. Méinyotio ár kér emormén, fá wéi efe yau nuyayénd ndené endeyo Edém snuyai.
bt: Sin entered into the world through one man, and his sin brought death. By that way, all the people received death, because every people did sin. 13 Before he gave the law sin was in the world. But at that time, God did not say that people put to pain(were guilty), because there was no law for them to follow. 14 But still death ruled all people, from the day of Edèm to the day of Moses. All people died, even those who did not fall down in the same way that Adam fell down.

nama: Wénde kété yém ndimbalfifi fénataye yéfene néndkéfan Edémene yaufiyam a Ngánjánene raméghmén ngárám. Yéné ámbiro ár, Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kér méinyotio árét. Wénde nderé mindefifi Ngánjánene ngárám a ramégh yémormén ámbiro ároene kénjúmé, Yesu Keriso emon enérsam fénaté ndimbalfifi frengégh méinyotio árét. 16 Wéikor Ngánjánene ramégh ndené yau yém Edémene yaufiyammén fai kés yém. Edémene yaufiyamam yélimndam waghanéghfaf, wénde téfeyot yéna yém yuwek ramégh árké yumyote Ngánjánam, ndemamén yénd kértafé yéném tárfár yaufiyammén. 17 Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kér, yéné ámbiro ár kénjúmé. Nderé mindefifi emofem so tumyongi Ngánjánene ngárám a yáne mbarkánd yam ramégh, ménamén yéfe méinyotio emofem tanangi so emor yénayan waingégh yaufiyam a kér yéné ámbiro ár kénjúmé, Yesu Keriso.
bt: But there is a very big difference between Edèm's sin and God's giving grace. This one man, Edèm's sin brought death to all the people. But how very big is God's grace and gift [that] came throuth the other one man, Yesu Keriso who brought very big blessing for all the people. 16 Also God's gift is not like the payment(result) of Edèm's sin. Edèm's sin led to spoil(condemnation), but we have free gift which God allowed, even though we are trouble(guilty) of many sins. 17 Edèm's sin brought death, through this one man. How much more will those who receive Godʼs grace and his gift of straight thing, because all who receive it will live in triumph over sin and death through this one man, Yesu Keriso!

nama: Féyo, Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kértafé méinyotio árfefaf, wénde Kerisoene ámbiro mbarkánd yam wafrogham yénérse mbarkánd fútaryam yémafé Ngánján a totér ghérsé yéfeyot méinyotio árét. 19 Ménamén ámbiro árom yékenai Ngánján, méinyotio ár nénamndayénd yaufiyam ár. Wénde ménamén ámbiro árom yiyárai Ngánján, méinyotio ár so mbarkándro táyátongé.
bt: So, Edèm's sin brought trouble(condemnation) to all people, but Keriso's one action of straight thing brings a straight friendship with God and new life for them every people. 19 Because one man rejected God, all the people became sinner. But because one man obeyed God, all the people will become straight.

nama: Ngánjánam ewaramai nák ménát yaufiyam narmboi. Wénde ánde yaufiyam narmboi, Ngánjánene ngárám narmboi minde ndimbal. 21 Ndené fété yaufiyamam talimndai ár kéran, sénonjo Ngánjánene ngárámam yénlimnde ndenáye, yénmaramete mbarkánd okayogh Ngánjánafé a yénérse yénd tuot ghérsat Yesu Keriso kénjúmé téfene Yuré.
bt: God gave them the law so that sin might increase. But where sin increased, God's grace increased very big. 21 Just as sin ruled people in death, now God's grace rules us like this, giving us straight standing with God and bringing us to eternal life through Yesu Keriso our Lord.

nama: Ámb ár mé té noraya, 'Yéndfem so mé engwafrotam yaufiyam ménát Ngánjánene ngárám mé so narmbote.' 2 Yaufifi! Yénd kér té yénmormén yaufiyamta, ndernáye wéi yaufiyaman mé so yéngém? 3 Fém so mat em ndené méinyotio yénd efe té yénmálitotaménd Keriso Yesuene kénjút yénd nufnyayém yáfaf yáne kérfaf. 4 Yénd té yényatotaménd yémafé walitogh kénjúmé yáne kérfaf ménát, ndenáye Keriso yéngwano kérta Yárefafene ndimbal muyé kénjúmé, yénd wéi so yénmor totér ghérsaye.
bt: Someone might say, 'We should keep on doing sin so that grace may increase.' 2 Never! We have died from sin, how can we continue to sin? 3 You.p should know that all of us who were baptized into Keriso Yesu joined him into his death. 4 We have been buried with him through baptism into his death in order that, just as Keriso was raised from the dead through the big power of his Father, we also may live a new lives.

nama: Ménamén njam yénd yémafé ámbiro yéném mérne yáne kér kénjúmé, yénd wéi ndenéyameyo so kérta nanotam ndené fá kés kérta nanoi. 6 Yénd mat yéném ndené téfene tándáwár fifioene ménde té enamndaméng wafatarégh wénan Yesufé. Yéné nufrotam ménát fifioene méndeyamam yau so yénlimnde yaufiyamot, a ménát yénd ndenayu yau so yéném yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 7 Ménamén árké ár njam kér yém yaufiyamta té nékmbo.
bt: Because if we are one with him together through his death, we will also rise from death just as he rose from death. 6 We know that he(God) nailed our old bodly desire in the cross with Yesu. This happened so that bodily desire will not lead us sin, and so that we are no longer without.payment working men(servants) of sin. 7 Because anyone who died from sin has stopped it.

nama: Féyo, yénd njam té kér yémormén Kerisofé, yénd nemnam ndené yénd wéi ghérsé so yéném yémafé. 9 Yénd mat yéném ndené ménamén Yesu kérta kés yéngwano, fá wéi yau so kér yém. Kér waftafnar yéngém yáfaf. 10 Njam Keriso kér yémormén, fá kér yémormén ámbirotaro ménát yironam yaufiyamoene wafté ambino. Sénonjo ndené fá yém ghérsé wéikor, fá yémor kété néuséne Ngánjáneyot.
bt: So, if we have died with Keriso, we think that we will also live with him. 9 We know that because Keriso was raised from the dead, he cannot die again. Death go without.power over him. 10 When Keriso died, he died once to destroy the power of sin forever. Now that he lives again, he lives to serve God.

nama: Ndené endeyo, fémyo nafandati ndené kér té emormén yaufiyam wafroghta wénde ghérsé em Ngánjáneyot Keriso Yesuene kénjún. 12 Yénémaménnde, yau yumyotati yaufiyam sái elimnda féfene orngégh fifi ménát fémofem sái ewaitotati yaufioene ménde. 13 Yau eumyotati ámb fifi kafe yaufiyam wafroghét, ndené ásáfogh rokarnit mámáfiot, wénde karamangi fémyo Ngánjánefaf, ndené fété efe té kérta nénangoi ghérsat. A eumyotati féfenjo fifi kafe Ngánjánene mbarkánd yam wafroghét. 14 Yaufiyam yau so yém féfene Yuré, ménamén fém yau em nák mbanmbanan, wénde Ngánjánene ngárám mbanmbanan em. Mbarkánd yam faiofnar ásáfogh árfet
bt: In the same way, look to yourselves that you have died from doing sin but live for God in Keriso Yesu. 12 Therefore, do not allow sin lead your.p mortal body so that you.p follow desires of sin. 13 Do not allow any part of your body to do sin, as working tool for wickedness, but offer yourselves to God, just as those who came back to life. And allow your body part for God's straight thing doing. 14 Sin shall not be your.p lord, because you.p are not under the law, but under God's grace. Without.payment working men(slaves) to straight thing

nama: Némé yénd so norayam ndené? Yéndfem yaufiyam mé so engwafrotam ménamén yénd yau nák mbanmbanan yéném wénde Ngánjánene ngárám mbanmbanan? Yau ámb fifi! 16 Fém efaloe mat té em ndené fém kénáyátongi faiofnar ásáfogh ár táf fémofem njam tiyátongi áyárghét. Fém so kénamndangi yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, árkéménayam so elimndat krot, o fémofem téu tékafangi áyárégh Ngánjáne, árkéménayam elimnde mbarkánd yamfaf. 17 Wénde kánjo Ngánjáne, ndemamén fém tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fémofem tikfan tékmetawét yéné watamegh árkéméné yéndfem ewaramayém. 18 Fém yuwek em yaufiyamta a té nénamndayénd mbarkánd yamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár.
bt: What shall we say then? Shall we contine doing sin because we are not under the law but under God's grace? Not at all! 16 You truly know that you.p become the without.payment working man(servant) if you.p become to obey. You.p can become a without.payment working man(servant) of sin, which leads to death, or you can choose to obey God, which leads to straight thing. 17 But thanks to God, though you.p were without.payment working men(servants) of sin, you.p put in heart this teaching which we have given you. 18 You.p are free from sin and have become without.payment working men(servants) to straight thing.

nama: Yéndon yéné mbanmban yafrotamén méinyotio efoghta ménamén yéné yém táikér matot. Fronde fémofem té taumyotawét féfenjo fifi kafe ménát tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fém yaufiyamotro tamorangérwén. Wénde sénonjo fém táf karamangi mbarkándoene faiofnar ásáfogh árot ménát wafroghét yéné árké yém njúnnjúnofnar.
bt: I am using this picture from everyday life because it is hard to understand. Previously, you.p have allowed your.p body parts to be without.payment working men(servants) of sin, you lived for sin. But now you must give yourselves as without.payment working men(servants) of straight thing to do that which is without.dirty(holy).

nama: Fém njam mé tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fém mé yau so kémnawét ménamén némé mbarkánd témorwén. 21 Némé mbwe yanayénd yéné efghon yéné rokarta? Yaufifi. Fém sénonjo wáiwái sem yénémamén. Yéné yam nufrotat kérfaf! 22 Wénde sénonjo fém yuwek em yaufita wénde té nénamndayénd Ngánjánene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. Yéné yam yénérse féfeyot ghérsé árké yém Ngánjánetro, a yéné yam ewaramete féfe tuot ghérsé. 23 Ménamén yaufiyamoene fai siyém kér, wénde Ngánjánene raméghmén siyém tuot ghérsé yáne kénjún Keriso Yesu téfene Yuré.
bt: When you.p used to be without.payment working men(servants) of sin, you.p did not have to think about what was straight. 21 What fruit/result did you.p receive at that time from those things? None. You.p are now ashamed of. Those things happened to death! 22 But now you.p are free from sin but became without.payment working men(servants) of God. Those things bring you life which is only for God, and those things give you enternal life. 23 For the pay of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Keriso Yesu our Lord.

nama: Tane Fútaréf, fém mat té em yénd néngorayan árfefaf efe mat em nák, ndené nák waftafé ároefaf yém njamandio fá ghérsé yém? 2 Ufogh si, náke awerégh amaf mé yém yánjarefaf táf yánjar njam mé ghérsé yém, wénde njam yánjar kér yém, fá siyém awerégh nákta kefongé. 3 Njam wéi amafanyo ámb árfé ekmare njam yánjar ghérsé mé yém, amafom amafanyo nák eráfne. Wénde njam yánjar kér yém, fá té nafitrar yéné nákta a fá yau yém amafanyo nák rafargh ár njam ngawerangé ámb ár.
bt: My friends, do you.p not know I am speaking to the people who know the law, that the law has power for a man only while he lives? 2 Showing talk(example), by law a married woman is still with her husband while his husband is alive, but if her husband dies, she finish from the marriage law. 3 If she has sexual relations with another man while her husband is still alive, woman break adultery law. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law and is not an breaker of adultery law if she marries another man.

nama: Tane fútaréf, fém wéi kér té emormén nákfaf Kerisoene fifi kénjúmé, ménát fém so em ámb ároene, yáne efe té yéngwano kérta, ménát yéndfem so efunam mbwe Ngánjáne. 5 Táf yénd njam ténlimndawét yaufiyam méndeyam, nák karmbotau yaufiyamoene ménde árké témorwén ásáfoghan téfene fifiyan. Kerkeran yaufiyamam yénlimnde kérfaf. 6 Wénde sénonjo, kér kétamé árkéménayam ténmumbetau yénd, yénd té yénferyéng nákta ménát yéndfem so yausáfotam Ngánjáne totér ende Ngánján Mbérmbéran, a yau tándáwár ende faroghmén náke. Nák a yaufiyam
bt: My friends, you.p also died to the law through the body of Keriso, so that you.p might belong to another man, to him who was raised from the dead, so that we might produce fruit for God. 5 When sin's desire led us, the law arouse the desire of sin which were at work in our body. In the end, sin lead us to death. 6 But now, death from which bound us, we have been released from the law so that we will serve God in the new way in the God Spirit, and not by old way of the written laws. The law and Sin

nama: Féyo, mé té ámb ár noraya, 'Nák yém yaufi. Yau! Yéné nákofnar, yénd yau mat so kwémorwén némé yaufiyam yém. Ámb nák noraye, 'Yau méndefé yéfanda ámb ároene rokar." Yéné nákonfar, yénd yau so mat kwémorwén ndené yéné yém mámáfi méndefé fandaghét ároene rokar. 8 Wénde yéné nákam yaramai yaufiyamoe féneféne ménát sái kényawangai tane mwighafaf. Yéné téfnan, yéndo yafngoyén ménde méinyotio yaufifaf. Njam kété yau yém nák sái yénmaufrotat némé yém mámáfi, féyo yaufiyam siyém waftafnar.
bt: So, someone might say, ‘The law is sinful.’ No! Without that law, I would not have known what sin really is. One rule says, ‘You must not covet other people's things.’ Without this law, I would not have known what it is wrong to covet other people's things. 8 But that rule gave sin a chance to come into my thoughts. In this reason, I started to want all the bad things. If there is no law to tell us what is wrong, sin is without.power.

nama: Tándái yénd ghérsé mé kwémorwén frondeyo soramé yénd mat náyátoyén nák, wénde soramé nák nénufaryénd, yaufiyam sénjonjo muyafé témorwén tane ghérsayan, 10 a yénd kér wémorwén. Ngánjánam ewaramai yéné nák árét ménát mer ende ámnjoghét, wénde yéné nákam yénérsam kér tayot. 11 Yéné nákam yaramai yaufiyamoe féneféne ménát sái wasotam yénd. Féyo, yénd kér wémormén yéné nák téfnan. 12 Yénémamén, nák a yáne wafarégh em njúnnjúnofnar, a mbarkánd a merro.
bt: In the past I used to live before I know law, but later the law came, sin now had power in my life, 10 and I died. God gave that law to people so that they could live in a good way, but that law brought death to me. 11 That law gave sin a chance to deceive me. So, I died in the reason of that rule. 12 Then, the law and its commands are without.dust(holy), and straight and good.

nama: Féyo, ámb ár mé té noraya, 'Yéné fifi ndené yém ámb meram yénérsam kér féfefaf.' Yaufifi! Yaufiyamam yénérsam kér tayot. Yaufiyam Ngánjánene mer nákan nauyafotam ndené yénd kwémorwén kértafé. Yéné téfnan, yéndfem yéfandam ndené yaufiyam fété yém minde mámáfi. Ngánjánene mer nák kénjúmé yaufiyam násáfotam minde yaufiot.
bt: So, someone might say, ‘This means that something good brought death to you.p.’ Never! Sin brought death to me. Sin used God's good law to show that I was in trouble(guilty). Because of that, we can see that sin is really bad. Through God's good law sin works for very evil.

nama: Yénd mat yéném nák siyém mbérmbérafé em; wénde yénd wém njangén ár. Yénd ndenanit wém yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 15 Yéndon yau kor yérsan némé yéndon ewaufrotan. Némat yénd yéta ménde wém wafroghét yéndon yau yafrotan, wénde némé yéndon méndméndat yéfandan yéndon yafrotan. 16 A yéndon njam yafrotan némé méndefnar efandan, yéndon yiyátotan ndené nák siyém mer yém. 17 Féyo yéndon njam ewafrotan, yéné yau yéndonyo ewaufrotan, wénde yéné siyém yaufiyam yényau tafaf.
bt: We know that the law is spiritual, but I am a weak man. I am like a without.payment working man(servant) of sin. 15 I do not carry what I say. For what I want to do I do not do, but what I hate I do. 16 And if I do what I do not want to do, I agree that the law is good. 17 So when I do things, this is not me myself doing them, but that is sin living in me.

nama: Yénd mat wém ndené yéna yau ámb mer yém tayot. Yénd wém njangén ár. Yénd yéta ménde wém wafroghét némé yém mer. Wénde yénd féyotarofnar wém wafroghét. 19 Yéndo yau ewafrotan mer yam árkéméné yénd yéta ménde wém, wénde yéndon mé ewafrotan yaufiyam árkéméné yénd méndefnar wém wafroghét. 20 Yéndon njam ewafrotan némé yénd yau ménde wém wafroghét, yéndon yau yéné ewafrotan, wénde yaufiyam yafrote tane kénjún.
bt: I know that there is nothing good in me. I am a weak man. I want to do what is good. But I am unable to do it. 19 I don't do good things which I am willing to do, but I continue to do the bad things that I do not want to do. 20 If I do what I do not want to do, I do not do this, but sin in me does it.

nama: Féyo yénd mat té namndan némé nufrote tane kénjún. Njam yénd yéta ménde wém sái yafrotan mer yam, yéndon mé yénaro taferén yaufiro. 22 Méinyotio tane tikfe yéndon euwetan Ngánjánene nák. 23 Wénde yéndo yéfandan ámb nák ásáfoghan tane kénjún, yafrote mék nákfaf tane mwighayan a wiyátote yénd yaufiyam nákoene kwéfkwéf méngo ár árkéméné násáfote tane kénjún. 24 Némé afrengéghofnar ár yénd wém! Emo so kwanjarngé yéné fifita siyéngém kér? 25 Kánjo Ngánjáne, emo kwanjane tane kénjún Yesu Keriso téfene Yurane kénjúmé! Yénémaménnde, tane mwighaye yénd ménde wém sái ewaitotan Ngánjánene nák. Wénde táfandio, wénde yéndo yásáfotan yaufie ndené tane yuré, ménamén yénd wém njangén a ár.
bt: So I understand what is happening in me. Whenever I want to do good thing, I can only choose bad thing. 22 With all my heart I love Godʼs law. 23 But I see another law at work in me, making war to the law in my mind and making me darkness house man(prisoner) of the law of sin which is working in me. 24 What an miserable person I am! Who will save me from this body going to die? 25 Thank God, who saves me through Yesu Keriso our Lord! So then, with my mind I want to follow God's law. But, immediately, I serve sin as my lord, because I am weak and human.

nama: Yénémaménnde, yéfeyot ámb yau kértafé yém efe em Keriso Yesuene kénjún. 2 Ménamén ghérsé Ngánján Mbérmbéroene nákam Keriso Yesuene kénjúmé té yuwek tiyátoi fém kétamé yaufiyamoene nák a kroene nák. 3 Yéné nák árké Ngánjánam yaramai Mosese yau kor yényátoi yénd ndené kés. Yénd ténmorwén muyafnar ménát waitoghét yáne nák ménamén yénd ménd ár. Wénde Ngánjánam té yafrotam némé yáne nákam yau kor so tafrotau. Yémo yénmafaretam yánjo Ambum sái nénamndai ár ndené yénd siyéném. Yáne fifi témorwén ndené ár fifi emofem engwafrotat mámáfi. Ngánjánene Ambum kér yémormén ndené fété ghéngén ménát sái tawitau tékrat yam téfene yaufiyammén. Yéné ende, Ngánjánam yironam yaufioene wafté yéné árfene efe em fifie muyafnar. 4 Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam ménát nákoene ménde némat yém so nufrotat téfefaf, efe yau emor yaufioene ménde wafroghyaman wénde Ngánján Mbérmbér limánéghan.
bt: Therefore, for them there is not with trouble/judge who are in Keriso Yesu. 2 Because living God Spirit's law through Keriso Yesu has made you.p free from the law of sin and death. 3 The law which God gave to Moses could not make us well like that. We were without.power to follow his laws because we are ordinary people. But God has done what his law could not do well. He sent his own Son to become a man like us. His body was like the body of people who do wrong things. God's Son died as a sacrifice to take away the suffering for our sins. In that way, God destroyed the power of sin over people who are bodily without.power. 4 God did this so that what the law require might be happened to us, who don't live in action of sinful desire but in leading of the God Spirit.

nama: Fá efe elimnde yaufioene méndeyamam nemnat mé yaufi yamotro. Wénde fá efe emor Ngánján Mbérmbéran nemnat némé Ngánján Mbérmbér ménde yém. 6 Yaufioene ménde yamam yénlimndat kérot, wénde Ngánján Mbérmbéroene mwigham yénlimndat ghérsé a krufrát yamot. 7 Yaufioene ménde yamafé túmén násmét Ngánjánafé, ménamén yau ewaitote Ngánjánene nák, a yau féyotaro em waitoghét. 8 Fá efe elimndé yaufioene ménde yamam yau yéremngote Ngánján.
bt: Those who sinful desire ruled only think about only evil things. But those who live in the God Spirit think about what the Spirit wants. 6 Sinful desire leads us to death, but thought of God Spirit leads us life and peace. 7 The sinful desire is always fighting with God, because it does not follow God's law, and it cannot follow it. 8 Those who is led by sinful desire cannot please God.

nama: Wénde fém yau elimndé yaufioene ménde yam, fém elimndé Ngánján Mbérmbéram, táf njam Ngánjánene Mbérmbér yényau féfefaf. Endefefaf yau yényau Kerisoene Mbérmbér yéfene kénjún yau em Kerisoene. 10 Wénde Keriso njam yényau féfefaf, ndemamén féfene fifi kér so em yaufiyammén, wénde Ngánján Mbérmbéram ewaramete ghérsé féfeyot ménamén Kerisom té mbarkánd eyátoi Ngánjánafé. 11 A njam Ngánjánene Mbérmbér yém féfefaf emo yanoi Yesu kérta, fá emo yanoi Keriso kétamé kérta wéi so téramangé ghérsé kétán féfene ornégh fififaf yánjo Mbérmbére efe yényau féfefaf.
bt: But you.p are not led as sinful desire, God Spirit leads you.p, if God's Spirit are inside you.p. To whom the Spirit of Keriso are not inside them do not belong to Keriso. 10 But if Keriso is in you.p, even though your.p bodies are going to die because of sin, but the God Spirit gaves life for you.p because Keriso has made you straight with God. 11 And if the Spirit of God who raised Yesu from death lives you, he who raised Keriso from death will also give life to your.p mortal bodies by his Spirit who is in you.p.

nama: Yénémamén, tane fútar, yénd yau so yénmor sái yénremngota fifioene ménde yaman. Yénd yau so ndenau yénmor ndenáye 13 Fém njam emor fifioene méndeye, fém so kér em. Wénde njam Ngánján Mbérmbére krot tákmangi féfene yaufiyam wafrogh, fém so ghérsé engém. 14 Ménamén fá efe elimnde Ngánján Mbérmbéram sem Ngánjánene ambum. 15 Fémo yau yéné mbérmbér yanai sái niyátota férférafé wénde fémo yanai ménát sái ném Yáne ambum a Yáne kénjún yénd nutnam, "Afé! Tane Afé!" 16 Ngánján Mbérmbéram wéi yémonyo efalo yiyátote téfene mbérmbérafé ndené yénd siéném Ngánjánene ambumtértér. 17 Ménamén yénd yéném Ngánjánene ambumtértér, yénd wéi mat yéném ndené yéndfem so tawinjongén mer rokar yá tambén. Yéné rokar em yémon árké té unyéghofnar si ewaramai yánjo ambumtértérat. Mérne yémafé Keriso, yéndfem so tawanangém rokar árké Ngánjánam té ewamnde yáyot. Yéné siyém efalo táf njam yéndfem eyátotam tékrat yam ndené Kerisom kés yafrotam. Féyo yéndfem wéi so yayamnetam ghérsé njafaran ndené Kerisom kés yafrote. Sénonjo tékrat a soramatau ndimbal kwayaghé
bt: Therefore, my friends, we will not to live to satisfy bodily desire. We will not live like that. 13 If you.p live by the bodily desire, you.p will die. But if by the Spirit you.p put to death your.p sinful actions, you.p will live. 14 Because those who God Spirit leads are God's children. 15 You.s have not received this spirit to make you.s fear but you.s received him become His child and in Him we shout, “Father! my Father!” 16 God Spirit also agrees with our spirits that we are God's children. 17 Because we are God's children, we also know that we will receive good things from him. Those things are which he has done without.lying talk(promise) to give to his children. Together with Keriso, we will receive the things that God has kept for him. That is true if we agree to have troubles like Keriso did. Then we will also enjoy life in heaven like Keriso does. Today suffering and future big light(glory)

nama: Yéndon nemnan ndené sénonjomén tékrát yamafé yau so katmangé kétán soramé ndimbal kwayaghé sái nufrota téfefaf. 19 Méinyotio rokar Ngánján ewafrote nawanjete kétán ánde Ngánjánene ambumtértér so safén tákmangé. 20 Méinyotio rokar Ngánjánam ewafrotam nawengoyénd fifiofnarot, yau ménáyan yánjo méndeyan wénde ménamén Ngánjánene ménde témorwén. Wénde kété témorwén méghmégh, 21 yéné wafrogh rokar néretat kétán yéné efghot ánde so kafghangi yéfefaf Ngánjáne ambumtértérafefaf ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún yuwek kétamé kérta orngéghta.
bt: I am thinking that today suffering will not match with later big light(glory) will happen to us. 19 All thing God made waits to where God's children will put clearly. 20 All things God made turned without.meaning, not by his will but because God's will. But there was a hope, 21 created thing(creature) looks forward to this day when it will join God's children in big light(glorious) freedom from death and mortal.

nama: Yénd mat yéném ndené méinyotio wafrogh rokar tékratan yém ndené fété ambum áráfghan yénau tékrát yéfandat yénanotio sénonjo. 23 Yéné wéi yém ndenéyameyo téfe efe yéném efalo yátogh ár. Yéndfem té ewanyém Ngánján Mbérmbér ndené fété fronde yáne tambén téfeyot, wénde yénd wéi nendam yéndo kénjúm.Yéndfem té yanayém Ngánján Mbérmbér ndené fété fronde ramégh téfeyot, wénde wéi yénd ye nendam téfenjo kénjún. Yénd nawanjetam yéné efghot ánde Ngánjánam so tifongé yánjo ndimbal ásáfogh. Féyo yénd so yéném yáne féyotio ndené fété yáne ambumtértér. Téfene fifi so em yuwek kétamé yaufiyamoene waftata. 24 Yéné méghmégh kénjún yénd té yénmanjayéng. Wénde méghmégh árké efandam yéné méghmégh siyém fifiofnar yém. Efe méghmégh em yéné rokarotio táf té ewam? 25 Wénde yénd njam méghmégh yéném yéné rokarfaf yéndfem árkéméné yau efandam, yénd nawanjetam yénat métare.
bt: We know that all of created thing are in pain just like the pain which they experience in childbirth until today. 23 This is the same for us who are approving truth people(believers). We have received God Spirit as the first of his gifts to us, but we also cry inside ourselves. We are waiting for the time when God will finish his big work. Then we will belong to him completely as his children. Our bodies will be free from the power of sin. 24 In this hope he had saved us. But hope which we see is this hope without.meaning. Who hopes for these things they already have? 25 But if we hope for this thing which we do not see, we wait for it patiently.

nama: Yéné endeyo, Ngánján Mbérmbéram wéi yénmáutáne téfene muyafnar sifayan. Ménamén yénd mafina yéném ndernáye so Ngánjánafé norayam. Wénde Ngánján Mbérmbér fáyo Ngánjánafé norayet téfeyot. Fá nutne Ngánjánefaf téfene téfnan yéné ende ár ánde yau kor so noraye. 27 Ngánjánam efande némé em téfene tikfan, a fá mat yém némé mwighé yém Ngánján Mbérmbéroene, ménamén Ngánján Mbérmbéram noraye yánjo ár téfnan Ngánjánene méndeyamé.
bt: In the same way, the God Spirit also helps us in without.power place. Because we do not know how to talk with God. But God Spirit himself talks with(prays) God for us. He shouts to God on our behalf in a way that nobody could say with words. 27 God sees what is in our hearts, and he knows what the thought of the God Spirit is, because the God Spirit talks with God on behalf of his people according to the will of God.

nama: Yénd mat yéném ndené méinyotio yaman Ngánján násáfote merot yéfeyot emofem yuwetat fá, efe té emotai yáne ménde wafroghét. 29 Ngánján fá mat yém túmén efe em yáne waferéghkaf, yémon té mwighé yéfaronam fá sái táyátongai ndenanit yánjo Ambumnit, ménát yáne Ambum so kénamndangai fronde sénko tárfár néngénfefaf. 30 Ngánjánam té ewaferyéng yéné ár sái em yáne ambumtértér. Yéné téfnan, yémo emote sái eném yáfaf. Yémo eumyote yéné ár ndené mbarkánd yémafé. A yéné efe eumyote, yémo wéi eméinde fá so kénamndangi ndimbal yétkwén. Ngánjánene uwégh Keriso Yesu kénjún
bt: We know that in all the things God works for good with those who love him, those whom he has called to do his will. 29 God always know who are his chosen, he had decided they will become like his Son, so that his Son will become the firstborn among many younger brothers. 30 God had already chosen those people to be his children. In this reason, he called them to come to him. He accepted those people as straight with him. And those whom he accepted, he also caused them to become big name. God's love in Keriso Yesu

nama: Féyo, ménamén méinyotio yam Ngánjánam kés ewafrote téfeyot, yénd so norayam ndené: Njam Ngánján té násáfota téfeyot, árké árom yau so ewafrote ámb yam téfe ásénanéghét. 32 Yémo efe yau yangoi yánjo Ambum, wénde yénramai yánjo Ambum kérot méinyotio téfe téfnan. Yénémamén, Ngánjánam so ténmarangé mérne yane Ambumafé méinyotio rokar némé ménde yéném. 33 Efe ár so noraye ndené Ngánjánene ár sem kértafé. Ngánjánam yémonyo té yénmumyoi yénd ndené fété mbarkánd yémafé. 34 Féyo efe yau so noraye ndené Ngánjánam so mé tékratan yénékmete yénd. Efe yau. Ménamén Keriso Yesu fáyo té kér yémormén téfene téfnan. A Ngánjánam yanoi ghérsat téfeyot. Sénonjo Keriso siyém yémor Ngánjánene tambénta njafaran. Fá fáyo Ngánjánefaf noraye téfene téfnan.
bt: Then, because of all the things [that] God does for us, we can say that: If God is working for us, anybody cannot really do anything against us. 32 He who did not even keep back his own Son, but gave his own Son to die for us all! So, God will give all the things what we need with his Son. 33 Nobody can say that God's people who will say that God's people are with trouble(accuse). God himself has accepted us as straight with him. 34 So nobody can say that God should still put us in suffering(condemn). No one. Because Keriso Yesu himself died on our behalf. And God raised him to the life for us. Now Keriso is sitting at God's right side in heaven. He himself is talking with God on our behalf.

nama: Emo so yénfalote yénd kétamé Kerisoene uwéghta? Féyotaro táikéryam o kérté yamam o ngiyam o siogh o firoyam o kér kakayam o witmafé sénté náifé? 36 Ndené Buk si noraye, "Fene téfnan yéndfem kér yéfandam méinyotio efghon; yénd ndenéyame yéném sif ngiot tawafngongi."
bt: Who can separate us from the love of Keriso? Can trouble or hardship or striking or hunger or nakedness or close to death or sharp knife with both side(sword)? 36 As the scripture say, “For your.p sake we see the death every day; we are like sheep start to be killed.”

nama: Yau, méinyotio yéné yam tambén yénd mindefifi waingégh ár yéném Yesue kénjúmé emo yénmuwetam yénd! 38 Yénd mat wém némé yam yau so ténfalongé Ngánjánene uwéghta, yau ghérsé yam o kéram, yau njafar si résagh árém o sénko árém njafarmén o tukén wafté njafarmén, yau sénonjoyam o soramé yumén, 39 yau njafar tukmén o mband yuwan, yau kétamé méinyotio wafrogh rokarta, so ténfalongé kétamé Ngánjánene uwéghta árké té yényátoi Keriso Yesuene kénjúmé téfene Yuré.
bt: No, from all these things we are very big conquerors through Yesu who loved us! 38 I know nothing can separate us from God's love, neither death nor life, neither men carrying heavenly message(angels) nor other heavenly lead people or heavenly high powers, neither the present nor the future, 39 neither heaven above nor in low of the land, nor from all created thing, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is revealed through Keriso Yesu our Lord.

nama: Yéndon nafrotan efalo si Keriso kénjún, yénd yau nuindan, tane emarégh efalo yiyátote Ngánján Mbérmbéramé. 2 Tafaf yém ndimbal ghérghérsisé a túmén kérté yém tane tikfan 3 yéfeyot tane árét, tane Ju fútaréf. Yéndio kemnawén ndené so tuot mbawar tanangayén, kanfangayén kétamé Kerisoeta, njam téu yéné yam tawanjarngé fá. 4 Israilmén ár, ewaferyéng sái em Ngánjánene rényégh ambumot. Ngánjánam yuyafotam yánjo ndimbal kwayaghé yéfefaf. Yémo árnyégh yam yafrotam yémafé a ewaramai yáne nák. Yémo ewaramai féyotaroe ménát yá wáwefnoghét a umyoghét yáne merfifi unyéghofnar si. 5 Yéfene ákif tamorwén tukfifi yétkwén, a yéné fifie áki kénjúmé yénmormén Keriso, efe fá yém Ngánján, a fá emo yélimnde méinyotio rokar, a yém tuot utmanégh áuwégh! Efalo.
bt: I speak the truth in Keriso, I am not lying, my conscience truly approves(confirms) it through the God Spirit. 2 To me are(I have) big sorrow and always trouble in my heart 3 for my people, my Ju friends. I myself think that take forever curse, cut off from Keriso, if that thing would save them. 4 The people of Israil, chosen to be God's adopted children. God showed his big light(glory) to them. He made covenants with them and gave them his law. He gave them the possiblity of worshiping him and receiving his very good without.lying talks(promises). 5 Their anscestors had very high up names, and through these bodily descendants Keriso came, who is God, the one who rules over everything, and is worthy of eternal praise! Amen.

nama: Ngánján unyéghofnar si norayai mer rokarot Israil árét. Wénde yénd yau norayan ndené némé Ngánján unyéghofnar si norayai yau nufrotam. Yéné safénro yém ndené yau méinyotio Israil ár em Ngánjánene efalo ár. 7 Yau méinyotio em efaloe Ebraamene ákimén. Ngánjánam yémndai Ebraam, "Fáro yém fene ambum Aisek yéndon so yémndetan fene ákiambumafene yéferafé.'
bt: God said without.lying talk(promise) good things to Israil people. But I am not saying that what God said without.lying talk(promise) did not happen. This is clear that not all of Israil people are God's true people. 7 Not all of them are true descendants of Ebraam. God told Ebraam, ‘It is only your.s son Aisek that I will call the father of your.s descendants.'

nama: Yéné fifi yém ndené yau em mé fifie ambum kétamé fifie ákita efe emndetat Ngánjánene ambum, wénde yáne efalo áki ambum fá em efe náráftat yáne unyéghofnar sian. 9 Yéné yém némé Ngánján unyéghofnar si norayai Ebraamefaf, "Kété kaféghmén efghon yénd so wéném, a Sara so yém ambumafé."
bt: This means that they are not bodily children from bodily descendants who are called Godʼs children, but his true descendants are born through his without.lying talk(promise). 9 This is what God said without.lying talk(promise) to Abraham, “At the chosen time I will come, and Sara will have a son.”

nama: Wéikor ewafarot yéné: Soramé, Aisekene amaf, Rebekam néslam sómbioe ambum. Yéné sómbio ambumafene tawarwén ámbiro afé, efe témorwén téfene áki, Aisek. 11 A Ngánján norayai Rebekafaf soramé sómbioe ambum eunjeyai, a fronde soramé ambumofem té ewafrotaménd némé yam téu mer o mámáfi. Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam yuyafotam safét ndené yémon ámbiro ambum yaferyéng. Yémo yau yaferyéng ámb ménamén yéné árom némé tawafrotau. Yémonyo mwighé yéfaronam yémo efe so taferngai. 12 Ngánjánam yémndai Rebeka, "Fronde sénkom so yásáfote yángnoe." 13 Ndené fété Buk si noraye, "Jeikof yéndon yuwetan, wénde Esau yéndo méndméndat yéfandan."
bt: Remember this too: Later, Isaac's wife, Rebeka, gave birth to twins. Those two sons had the one father, who was our ancestor Aisek. 11 And God said to Rebeka before twins were born, and before the boys had done what thing must good or bad. God did this to show clearly that he chose one child. He did not choose someone because of what that man did. He himself decided who he would choose. 12 God said to Rebeka, "The firstborn son will serve his younger brother.” 13 Just as the scripture says, “Jeikof I loved, but Esau I hated.”

nama: Ndernáye so norayam? Némé Ngánján yau yém mbarkánd? Yaufifi. 15 Ménamén fá té norayai Mosesefaf, "Tane mer yam so yém yáfaf endefaf mer yam yém, a tane ghérghérsisé so yém yáfaf endefaf ghérghérsisé yém."
bt: What shall we say? Is God not straight? Never! 15 Because he says to Moses, “My good thing will be to him on whom I have good thing, and my mercy will be to him on whom I have mercy.”

nama: Yau kété nufrote ároene méndefaf o ásáfoghan, wénde Ngánjánene ghérghérsisayan. 17 Ménamén Buk si noraye Farawanemén, "Yéndon némakayoyén fém yénat, yéndon so yuyafotan tane waftaye fém ménát ár méinyotio mband maratan so yuyotaménd tane yétkwén." 18 Yénémaménnde, Ngánján yém ghérghérsisafé yáyot endeyot fá ménde yém ghérghérsisafé, a yémon wéi térmbat eyátote fá endefefaf fá ménde yém térmbatot.
bt: It does not happen to man's desire and in working, but on Godʼs mercy. 17 Because Scripture says about Farawa: “I stand you.p up for this, I will show my power to you so that people in all the earth will lift my name.” 18 Therefore, God has mercy on whom he wants to have mercy, and he also hardens whom he wants to harden.

nama: Ámb so norayati fém tafaf, "Ngánjánam túmén ewafrote yéné némat fá ménde yém wafroghét. Árké árom yau so tawengongé némé Ngánjánam wafroghét tawafngongé. Féyo, Ngánján yau so noraye ndené árém té tawafrotat mámáfiyam." 20 Wénde fém em ménd ár. Féfefaf yau em muyé siot kétán Ngánjánefaf ndené kés. Ngánjánam té ewafrotam fém. Ngénd wafroghmén sin yau so noraye yéné ároefaf emo tafrote yéné! Yénayam yau so témotangé, 'Némamén fémo yénd kwafrote ndenáye?' 21 Ámb árom emo ewafrote ngéndmén sin so taferngé némé sáfot sin so ewafrote. Yémon so yénayo ngénd mbarmbare ewafrowé sómbio fénataye sin. Ámb sin sái yém ménát fénaté témndot. Ámb sin sái yém ménát njúnnjúnafé rokarot. 22 Ndernáye wét yénmatamete yénd Ngánjánenemén? Ámb ár em ndenanit ngénd wafroghmén sin árké em afrengéghkaf Ngánjáneyot wirorghét. Ngánján siyém ndené árfefaf nékwafé yém. Fá afrengéghkaf yém sái yuyafota yánjo wafté yéfefaf. Wénde yémo té yaferyéng ménát sái nawanjeta métare. Yémo yamnde yánjo nékw soramatau. 23 Ámb ár ndenanit em mer wafroghmén ngénd sin árkéméné Ngánjánam té ewaferyéng wafroghét. Ngánján ménde yém merro sái yém ndené árfefaf. Fá ménde yém yémo sái eméinda ménát so engwauyafotat árét nderé ndimbal yém fá. Yémo té efrendam fá ménát sái em yémafé fénataye témndot kété njafaran. 24 Yénd yéné ár yéném! Ngánjánam té yénmaferyéng yénd sái yéném yáne ár. Yau fá ewaferyéng Ju árro. Yémo wéi ewaferyéng Téndo ár. 25 Ngánján noraye Osiane Buk kénjún; 'Yénd so norayan árfefaf efe yau tamorwén tane ár, "Sénonjo fém em tane ár." Yénd so norayan árfefaf yéndo yau euwetan fém, "Yéndo euwetan."'
bt: One of you.p will say to me, "God always does what he wants to do. Nobody can change what God begin to do. So, God should not say that people have done wrong things." 20 But you.p are ordinary man. You.p have no power to speak to God like that. God has made you.p. A pot made with soil cannot speak to the man who made this! This thing cannot ask him, ‘Why did you.s make me like this?’ 21 Somebody who makes pots can choose to make what kind of pot. He can use the same piece of clay to make two different pots. One pot may be for special parties. The other pot is for dirty things.

nama: 'Ngánján té norayai yéfefaf, 'Fém yau em tane ár.' Yéné sifayanyo ánde fá norayai ndené, árém so sénonjo emndetat fá "Ngánjánene ambumtértér, efe túmén so em ghérsé."'
bt: ‘God had said to them, “You.p are not my people.” In that place where he said that, people will now call them “Children of God, who always lives.” ’

nama: Aisaya kutnau Israil ár téfnan: "Ndemamén Israil ár em tárfár ndenanit mirinit arakaf téndon, mé nambio árro so kunjarngi;
bt: Aisaya shouted out concerning Israil people: “Though the Israil peoples are many like the sand of the seashore, only few people will be saved.

nama: Yuré Ngánjánam so tifongé yánjo ásáfogh wékeye. Yémo té tawawéngsote yánjo ár némé yémo so ewafrote. A yémo so tékratan tákmangé fá féyotio.'
bt: The Lord God will finish his work quickly. He has warned his people what he will do. And he will put them in suffering completely.’

nama: Ndené fété Aisaya ndernáye norayai tándái, "Yau njam wafté kér ro Yuram ténéfrangongai yénd nambio, yénd so ténmalindangai ndenanit Sodom yénd so ténmalindangai ndenanit Gomora." Ndimbal mwighafnar Israil ár
bt: Just as Aisaya said long time ago, “If the Lord Almighty had not left us some, we would be destroyed like Sodom, we would be destroyed like Gomora.” Israil peopleʼs without big mind(faith)

nama: Némé wét yénd so norayam? Téndo ár yéta kufotawét ménát sái kamndangayénd mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. Wénde ámb yéfe tambén té nénamndayénd mbarkánd yémafé. Ngánjánam té eumyoi fá mbarkándmén, ménamén yémofem té efalo tiyátotawét Yesu Keriso. 31 Wénde Israil ár yéta kufotawét ménát sái tawinjongayénd nák káyátongayénd mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. Wénde fá nuyayénd sái kénamndangayénd mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. 32 Fá nuyayénd ménamén fá kakenawét ménát efalo sái tiyátowét Keriso. Wénde yémofem taufotawét ámb yam wafrogh ménát Ngánjánam so taumyongai fá. Yéné téfnan fá nuyayénd mbandot. Yéfene kafkafam tasmetawét wím árkéménayam eméinde ár uighét. 33 ndené fété Buk si noraye, "Kéretangi, yéndo yékmetan fénataye wím kété Jaion. Yéné wímam so eméinde ár uighét mbandot. Yéné wím yém árkéménayam so eméinde fá uighét. Wénde árké árom emo efalo yiyátota yau so mwighayan kuyangé.'
bt: What shall we say? The Side people were not trying to become straight with God. But some of them have now become straight with him. God has accepted them as straight, because they have truly approved(believed) in Yesu Keriso. 31 But Israil people tried to find the law that would make them straight with God. But they failed to become straight with God. 32 They failed because they were refusing to truly approve(believe) Keriso. But they were trying to do other things so that God would accept them. Because of that they fell to the ground. Their feet hit the stone which causes people to fall. 33 As the scripture says, ‘Look, I am putting a special stone in Jaion. That stone will cause people to fall to the ground. It is a rock that will cause them to fall. But anyone who truly approves(believes) in him will not fall in mind(be disappointed).’

nama: Mosesém yéfarotam ménamén ndernáye Ngánjánene nákam so ewáutáne ár ménát sái em mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. Fá norayai, "Njam árém ewaitota Ngánjánene méinyotio nák, féyo fá so yémor yénaye." 6 Wénde Buk si noraye wéi ámb ende. Yéné yufane ndernáye so kénamndangé mbarkánd Ngánjánafé ménamén fémo efalo yiyátota fá. Ndené noraye: 'Fémonyo yau noraya, "Ámb ár so fété kuwanongé tukét njafarot."' (Ndené táf yém: 'Ámb árom so ténmewérngé Keriso kétamé tukta.') 7 'Yau táf fémyo noraya wéi, "Ámb ár so fété yéngém mband marat mbanmbanot."' (Ndené táf yém: 'Ámb árom so fété ténmanongé Keriso yéné sifayata ánde kér téfnár em.') 8 Wénde Buk si noraye: "Ngánjánene si siyém kaka fefaf, fene mbírmbíran yém a fene tikfan." Yéné si siyém ménamén ndimbal mwighamén árké yéndfem néngafrotam: 9 Fém táf noraya safénro ndené Yesu siyém Yuré. Wéi ndenéyameyo fémo táf efalo yiyátota kénjkame fenjo kénjún ndené Ngánjánam yanoi fá kérta. Féyo Ngánjánam kémanjarngé fém. 10 Yéné siyém efalo yátoghe fene tikfan ndené fém té mbarkánd náyátend Ngánjánafé. A njam fém noraya safén ndené Yesu siyém Yuré, Ngánjánam kémanjarngé fém. 11 Ndené Buk si kés noraye, "Árké árom njam efalo yiyátote Yesu yau so wáiwái yém."
bt: Moses wrote about how God's law could help people so that they will be straight with God. He said, "If a man follows God's all laws, then he will live by them." 6 But the scripture says also about another way. This shows how to become straight with God because you.s truly approve(believe) him. It says: ‘Do not say to yourself, “Someone will have to go up into heaven.” ’ (That means: ‘Someone will have to bring Keriso down from up.’) 7 ‘Do not say to yourself either, “Someone will have to go down into the world below.” ’ (That means: ‘Someone will have to raise Keriso from that place where dead people are.’) 8 But the scripture says: “The talk of God is near you.s, it is in your.s mouth and in your.s heart.” This talk is about big mind(faith) that we proclaim: 9 You.s must say clearly that Yesu is the Lord. Also, you.s must truly approve(believe) deep inside yourself that God raised him from death. Then God will save you.s. 10 This is through truly approving(faith) in your.s heart that you.s have became straight with God. And when you.s say clearly that Yesu is Lord, God saves you.s. 11 As Scripture says, “Anyone who truly approves(believes) Yesu will never be put to shame.”

nama: Ndené noraye 'méinyotio ár.' Kété yau yém fénataye Ju ár a Téndo ár. Kété yém yénayo Yuré méinyotio yéfeyot. Yémo ewáutáne méinyotio árét emofem yamnendat fá ménaye tárfár mer rokare. 13 Ndené kés noraye Buk sian, "Yuré Ngánjánam so tawanjarngé méinyotio ár efe nomnendati yáfaf wáutárghét.'{b{ ...f* {b{10.13 Joel 2:32
bt: That says ‘every people’. There is no difference between Ju peope and Side people. There is the same Lord for all of them. He helps every people who asks him by many good things. 13 As it says in the scripture, ‘The Lord God will save every people who ask him for help.’

nama: Wénde ndernáye wét so nutnat yáfaf emofem yau efalo yiyátoyénd fá? A ndernáye so yémofem efalo yiyátotat fá endenemén yau náyárayénd? A ndernáye fá so káyárangayénd yánemén yau njam ámb ár ngafrotau yéfefaf? 15 A ndernáye so ámb ár yéngém waufroghét táf yau njam yéngwáfáretat? Buk siam yénémamén noraye, "Nderé mer em yéfene kafkaf emofem yénérsat mer si!"
bt: But how can they shout to him whom they have not truly approved(believed)? And how can they truly approve(believe) him whom they have not heard? And how can they hear about him if some man do not preach to them? 15 And how can anyone go to tell when he is not sent? As the scripture says about this, “How good are their feet of those who bring good talk!”

nama: Wénde yau méinyotio Israil árém yumyotat mer si. Aisaya Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár norayai, "Yuré, emo ámb efalo yiyáto téfene si?" 17 Yénémaménnde, ndimbal mwighé táf kétamé yéném njam náyárete si, a si káyárangém táf kétamé yéném Kerisoene sita.{c{ ...f* {c{10.17 Yénémaménnde, ndimbal mwighé táf kétamé yéném njam náyárete si, a si táf káyárangém si kénjúmé Kerisoenemén. Need be change: Therefore, by heyaring the message, people come to have faith, and by someone's preaching about Christ the people hear the message.
bt: But not all the Israil people accepted the good talk. The God's man who make know(prophet) Aisaya says, “Lord, who has truly approved(believed) our talk?” 17 Therefore, big mind(faith) comes from hearing talk, and hearing talk comes from the talk of Keriso. 18 But how about Israil people? Did they heard the talk? Yes, they heard: “Their talk has gone to all the people in this world, their talks reached out to the corners the world.”

nama: Wénde Israilmén ár yau mat tamorwén? Néméwét, fronde Moses korayau, "Yéndon so táyáton waramnjagh ár ménamén ármén efe yau em tane ár. Yéndon so eméindan nékwafé ménamén ármén efe yau em mat tanemén."
bt: But did Israil people not understand? Yes, first Moses says, ‘I will make you.p jealous people because of people who are not my people. I will cause you.p angry because of people who don't know me.

nama: Wéi soramé Aisaya marataye norayai, "Yénd yémofem winjoyénd yéné árém emofem yénd yau kwénronjawét; yénd nauyafoyén yéndio yéfefaf efe yau kamotawét tanemén."
bt: Also later Aisaya widely says, “They found me those people who were not looking for me; I showed myself to those who were not asking for me.”

nama: Wénde Israil árfenemén yémon norayai, "Méinyotio efghon yéndon tane ngángé tasafotan yéfefaf, wénde fá yau káyáretat tafaf a kamronam yéndfé."
bt: But about Israil people he says, “In all day I strecthed my hands to them but they did not hear me and argue with me."

nama: Féyo, yénd namotan ndené: Némé Ngánjánam ekene yánjo ár? Yaufifi! Yéndio wéi wém Israilmén ár, Ebraamene áki ambum, yéné mérénmén Benjamin. 2 Ngánjánam yau ekene yánjo ár, efe té ekafai tándái. Fém mat em ndernáye Buk si noraye yánemén Elaija, ndernáye fá kawatarnau Ngánjánefaf Israil árfe téfnan, 3 "Yuré, yémofem té tasmetat fene mat yátogh ár a taftérnat ghéngén wárogh kitaré. Yéndonro ámbiro nafrangon, a yémofem yéta wérametat ngiot." 4 A némé wét Ngánjánene uwangogh témorwén yáfaf? "Yéndon té euron tandio 7,000 ár emofem yau budme táwefnotat Baal mewangoe." 5 Wéi sénonjo efghon yéna em yáne árfifi ekafai ngáráme. 6 Yénayam yuyafote ndené Ngánjánam yau ewaferyéng fá ménamén ámb mer yammén ndené yémofem kés tawafrotat. Yéné njam témorwén efalo, yénd yau so korayawém ndené Ngánján témorwén kwénjtafnar yéfefaf. Yéné yau so témorwén yáne ramégh.
bt: So, I ask this: Did God reject his own people? Never! I myself am also a man from Israil, a descendant of Ebraam, from the family of Benjamin. 2 God did not reject his own people, whom he had chosen long ago. You.p know how Scripture says about Elaija, how did he complain to God in the reason of Israil people: 3 “Lord, they have killed your.s God's people who make know(prophets) and torn down your.s tables for burning sacrifice(altars). I am the only one left, and they are trying to make me to kill.” 4 And what was Godʼs answer to him? “I have left for myself 7,000 people who have not bowed the knee to idol Baal .” 5 Also today there are his real.people chosen by grace. 6 This thing shows that God has not chosen them because of any good things that they have done. If that was true, we could not say that God was without.greed to them. This would not be his gift.

nama: Féyo, ndenáye so norayam: Israil árém méinyotio yau yinjoyénd némat kéretawét. Mé yéné árémro efe Ngánjánam ewaferyéng yanayénd yémofem. Wénde ámb Israil ár féyotarofnar emormén ménát sái mat káyátongayénd Ngánjánene si. 8 Ndené fété Buk si noraye, "Ngánjánam té ewaramai kémégh kér. Sénonjo tawétio yémon té ewawamoi yéfene ghékéf ménát fá yau so néretat a ewawembái yéfene téngan ménát fá yau so náyáretat."
bt: So, we can say this: Israil people did not all find what they were looking for. Only those people whom God had chosen received it. But the other Israil people were not able to make known God's talk. 8 As the scripture says, “God have given them dead sleep. Until now he has blocked their eyes so that they will not see a blocked their ears so that they will not hear.”

nama: Deifid noraye, "Yéfene njamke kitaré so kénamndangé ndénndén a urér. Yéfene frengégham yéfune yéfeyot usérmbogh, a so tawanangi yémofem némat ménde em.
bt: Deifid says: “May their eating table become a long trap and a trap. May their blessings produce them to confuse, and they will receive they want.

nama: Yéfene ghékéf so kwéfkwéf náyátotat ménát fá yau so néretat, a yéfene sémsot so ewáwefno tuot!" Israil árfene kenagh yau yém ambino
bt: May their eyes become dark so that they cannot see, and their backs be bent forever.” All Israil people's rejection is not forever.

nama: Féyo, sénonjo yénd namotan ndené: 'Njam Israil ár nuyayénd Yesu Kerisoe umyoghét, té fá nafaloyénd kétamé Ngánjáneta tuot? Yau, yéné yau yém efalo. Ménamén yéfene áyáréghofnarmén, Ngánjánam ewanjane Téndo ár. Ngánjánam yéné yafrote ménát Israil ár so kénamndangi waramnjaghafé. 12 Téndo ár njam té namndayénd wemár ménamén Israil árém ámb ende tengotawét Ngánjánene ramégh awanjarghét, kemnangi nderé ndimbalfifi frengégh so tawanangayénd mband maratan táf njam Israil árém ambino tumyongayénd.
bt: So, now I ask that: ‘When Israil people failed to accept Yesu Keriso, did they make themselves separate from God forever?’ No, that is not true! Because of their without.hearing, God is saving Side people. God is doing that so that Israil people would become jealous. 12 If the Side people have become rich because the people of Israil turned other way from God's gift of salvation, think how really big blessing take into the world will receive when they finally accept it.

nama: Yénd sénonjo norayan Téndo ár féfeyot. Ngánjánam té wékaf yénd nénamndan Yesuene muyafé ár Téndo árét, yéndon yérsan tuktuk tane ásáfoghmén. 14 Tane méghmégh yém ndené tane ásáfogh féfene kénjún Téndo ár eméinde tanjo ár sái em waramnjaghafé. Yéné fifiyan, ámb yéfe kénjún so nawengotat Ngánjánefaf ménát yémo so tawanjarngé. 15 Njam Ngánján nawengoi kétamé Israil árfeta, yémo wéi eumyoi ámb ár mband maratan yáne fútarot. Yénémamén, njam Ngánjánam eumyota Israil ár, yéné fai so yém mer! Ndené so yém kér ár efe té nénano ghérsat wéikor.
bt: I now say to you.p Side people. God has chosen me that I became a man with Yesu's authority(apostle) to the Side people, I take pride in my work. 14 My hope is that my work among you Side people will cause my own people to be jealous. In this result, some of them may turn to God so that he will save them. 15 When God turned away from Israil people, he accepted again other people in the world as his friends. So, when God accepts Israil people, that result will be good! That will be like dead people who have risen to the life again!

nama: Táf fémo njam taram fronde féskaf flawé karéf Ngánjáne, féyo méinyotio féskaf flawé so yém Ngánjáne. Táf wén sérémb njam em Ngánjáne, féyo kafekefe wéi so em Ngánjáne.
bt: If you give the first piece of cooked flawé(bread) to God, then all the bread will be to God. If the roots of a tree belong to God, then the branches will also be God's.

nama: Israil ár ndenanit em olif wén árké ndau téfnáram eite. Wénde Ngánjánam eráfne ámb wén kafe. Féyo tanangé kafe kétamé kéghat olif wénta. Yémo tékmangé yéné kéghat wén kafe kétán ndau téfnárafene wénfaf, ménát so kénamndangé yéné wén sáf. Fém Téndo ár ndenanit em kéghat wén kafe. Sénonjo fémofem yérsati njamke sérémbta ndau téfnárafene wénta, ménát eyátote muyafé. 18 Israil ár ndenanit em kafe Ngánjánam té eráfar. Wénde fém yau táf nemnati ndené fémofem kar ewaindati yéné kafe. Nawafarotati ndené fém em mé kafero. Fémofem yau yafrotati sérémb taikrot. Yau, yéné yém sérémb árkéménayam némérete a neyátote fém muyafé.
bt: Israil people are like an olif tree that a farmer has planted. But God has broken off some tree's branches. Then he has taken a branch from a wild olif tree. He has put that wild branch into the farmer's tree, to become kind of that tree. You.p Side people are like the wild tree branch. Now you.p receive food from the root of the farmer's tree, to make you strong. 18 Israil people are like the branches that God broke off. But you must not think that you.p surpass those branches. Remember that you.p are still only the branches. You.p do not make the root strong. No, this is the root that holds you.p up and makes you.p strong.

nama: Fém mété norayati, "Ngánjánam tarafarngé kafe ménát yénd so kénamndan yéné wénoene." 20 Néméwét, wénde ewafarota yéné kafe, fá narafaryénd ménamén yau yémofem efalo tiyátotawét Keriso, a fém kété em ménamén ndimbal mwighamén. Féyo yau nemnati minde tukén féfenjo kénjún, wénde férférafé yéfandati némé so nufrote. 21 Ngánjánam yau njam té tawarawenau frondemén kafe, fém wéi ndenéyameyo yau so ewarawene?
bt: You.p might say, ‘God broke off the branches so that I could become [part]of this tree.’ 20 Yes, but remember those branches, they were broken off because they didn't truly approve(believe) Keriso, and you.p are there because your.p big mind(faith). So don't think highly of yourselves, but fear what could happen. 21 If God did not take care of the first branches, he won't take care of you.p in the same way?

nama: Féyo, fém mat so em ndernáye Ngánján yém mer, wénde wéi tékratan ekmete ár. Yémo tékratan takmete Israil ár, ménamén fá nawengoyénd yá tambén. Fá té tém mer féfefaf. Wénde fém miya engém ménát tanangi yáne mer ramégh. Njam yéné yafrotati, yémo so erafne fém kétándmé yánjo wénta ndenáyo. 23 A Israil ár njam kawengongi yéfenjo akenaghta, Ngánjánam so tanwangongé wénfaf wéikor, fá waftafé yém wafroghét. 24 Ngánjánam enfete fém ndenanit kafe kétamé kéghat olif wénta. Féyo yémo tákmangé fém kétán ndau téfnárafene olif wénfaf ménát fém so kénamndangi yénayo. Yémo so ewafrote yéné ndenamén yau tém féfenjoyo wén fronde. Féyo Ngánjánam so ewafrote yéné yéfeyot Israil árét. Yémo so afrátaye tákmangé fá kétánotio yéfenjoyo wénfaf, frondeyan kés tamorwén. Ngánjánene ghérghérsisé méinyotio árfefaf
bt: So, you.p must know how God is good, but he also put people into the suffering. He has put Israil people in suffering, because they turned away from him. He has been good to you.p. But you.p must continue to go to accept his good gift. If you.p do not do that, he will break you.p off from his tree as well. 23 And if Israil people will turn away from their denial, God will bring them back to the tree again, he has the power to do. 24 God cut you.p from the branches of the wild olif tree. Then he put to the farmer's olif tree so that you.p will become same. He will do this even though it was not your.p tree before. Then God will do this for them Israil people. He will put them softly until to their own tree, they might be in before. God's mercy on all the people

nama: Tane fútaréf, yénd ménde wém sái ewáutánan féfe mat kamndangi yéfenemén Israil árfenemén. Yéné siyém kwankwane yam ndené Ngánjánam té tuyafote téfeyot. Táf fémofem njam mat tiyátongi, fém yau so em tuktukan ndené fém em mwighé sénté. Tárfár Israil ár té nakenayénd té efalo tiyátotawét Keriso. Fá mé so engém sái yéngkenat fá kétánotio Téndo árém kor efalo tiyátongi waféghfaf árké Ngánján mwighé yéfaronam. 26 A yéné ende méinyotio Israil ár so kunjarngi. Ndené Buk si noraye, "Wanjarégh ár so yéném kétamé Jaion, a yémon so ewengote Israil kétamé méinyotio Ngánjánofnar ghérsé tambén.
bt: My firends, I want to help you.p to become knowldge about Israil people. It is a secret that God has shown to us. If you.p really understand it, you.p will not be proud that you.p are so sharp mind(wise). Many of Israil people have refused to truly approve(believe) Keriso. They will continue to refuse him until number that God decided for Side people have truly approved(believed). 26 And in this way all Israil people will be saved. As the scripture says, “The Saving man will come from Jaion and he will turn Israil from all without.god life.

nama: A yéndon so yafrotan yéné árnyégh yémafé njam yéndon ewawitan yéfene yaufiyam."
bt: And I will make this covenant with them when I take away their sins.”

nama: Tárfár Israil ár sénonjo sem mék téfnár mer sifaf, a yéné yémormén féfe téfnan. Té Ngánjánam ewaramai yáne tambén féfe Téndo árfet, wénde Israil ár mé em yáne kafegh ár ménamén yáne unyéghofnar si yéfene ákiffaf. 29 Ngánjánene tambén a ke yau so kitrongé. 30 Té fém Téndo ár fronde tamorwén áyáréghofnar Ngánjánefaf, wénde ménamén Israil árém yékenayénd yáne ghérghérsisé, wénde Ngánján nénamndai ghérghérsisafé féfefaf. 31 Ndené yameyo, Israil ár sénonjo nénamndayénd áyáréghofnar Ngánjánefaf a Ngánjánene ghérghérsisé té yénmormén féfefaf, wénde ámb efghon yémofem wéi so tawanangayénd Ngánjánene ghérghérsisé. 32 Ngánjánam té eyátoi méinyotio ár áyáréghofnar, ménát mé yáne ghérghérsisé so yém yéfefaf méinyotio. Áuwégh Ngánjánefef
bt: Many Israil people are now enemies of the good talk, and this comes for your.p reason. God have given his gift to you.p Side people, but Israil people are still his chosen people because of his without.lying talk(promise) to their ancestors. 29 Godʼs gifts and his voice can never be thrown away. 30 You.p Side people have first without.hearing God, but because the Israil people refused his mercy, but God become merciful to you.p. 31 In the same way, the Israil people now become without.hearing to God and God's mercy have come to you.p, but some day they may also receive God's mercy. 32 God has made all people to without.hearing, so that his mercy will be on them all. Praise to God

nama: O, nderé merfifi Ngánján yém téfene! Nderé kénjkam em yáne káfeté mwighé a matyam! Nderé táikér yém téfeyot mat yiyátongém yáne mwighé farorégh a yáne end! 34 "Efe ámb mat yém Yurane mwighé? Efe ámb yém yáne mwighé wáutárégh ár?
bt: Oh, how really good is God to us! How deep are his wisdom and knowledge! How hard to us become knowledge(understand) his decisions and his way! 34 “Who knows the mind of the Lord? Who is able to be his mind helping man?

nama: Emo ámb némé yaram Ngánjáne, ménát Ngánjánam sái fai taramangé?
bt: Who has ever given anything to God, so that God will give payment?”

nama: Yá tambén a yáne kénjúmé a yáyotro méinyotio rokar em. Yáyotro so yém ndimbal yétkwén tuot! Efalo.
bt: From him and through him and only for him are all things. Only for him be the big name(glory) forever! Amen.

nama: Yénémaménnde, tane fútar, yéndon ewamnendan fém Ngánjánene ghérghérsisé kénjún, ménát sái erametati féfenjo fifi ndené fété ghérsé ghéngénghéngén, njúnnjúnofnar a remngongafé Ngánjánefaf. Yéné siyém efalo a mer wáwefnogh ásáfogh. 2 Yau yaitotati féfene yam wafrogh a mband maratoene túmén yam wafrogh, wénde Ngánjánam a eyátota totér ár ménaye mwyghé wengoghe kétámé fém ndérmayé nemnati. Féyo fém mat so em Ngánjánene ménde féfeyot, árkéméné yém mer a méndefé a féwénféwénofnar.
bt: Therefore, my friends, I urge you.p in Godʼs mercy, in order to give your.p bodies just as a living sacrifice, without.dust(holy) and pleasing to God. This is true and good worshipping work. 2 Do not follow your.p action and always doing of the world, but you.p show God differently you.p become new people through that you.p prepare thought from what you are thinking. Then you.p should know God's will for you.p, which is good and pleasing and without.flaw(perfect).

nama: Ndené fété Ngánjánene si résagh ár, yéndon ewarametan féfe yéné mat si: Yau fémyo nawawindati a nawarmbotati fifiofnare. Fémofem táf mwighé efaronati fém fifi efe em yénaye ndimbal mwighaye árké Ngánjánam ewaramai. 4 Ndené fété téfene em ámbiro fifi tárfár fifi kafekafeyafé, a yéné fifi kafeyafene sem yau ámbiro ásáfogh ewam, 5 Kerisoene fififaf. Yénd méinyotio yéném yáne ásáfogh ár ámbiro fifiyan, téfeyot sem fénatayero em ásáfogh wafroghét, a ndené fété yénd ámbirofifi yéném Keriso kénjún, yénd yéndio so náfrendam, a yéndfem méndefé so efandam ámb ár. 6 Téfene em fénatayero tambén, yéné féyotaro ngárám kés yénmaramai téfe. Njam ároene tambén noraya yéné yam ufroghét, so yafrotati yáne ndimbal mwighé féyotaroye 7 njam násáfoghta ár wáutárghét so fété ewáutánati; njam yém watameghét, so fété ewatametati. 8 Njam yém wawéngsoghét, so fété ewawéngsotati. Njam yém ramégh kétán yéné árfene ménde némat em, so fété tangwaram marataye. Njam yém limánégh ár ayém táikér ásáfogh ár. Njam ghérghérsisé yuyafota, so yafrotati ásneghafé. Uwégh
