nama: Fá efe nemnat ndené fá mat em nauyafote ndené fla efalo yau mat em ndené fété fá so mat em. 3 Wénde njam ár uweta Ngánján, féyo Ngánján efaloe mat yém yéné ár.
bt: Those who think they know shows they really don't know as they ought to know. 3 But a man loves God, then God indeed knows that man.

nama: Féyo ménamén njamke nénemén mewangfaf raméghmén. Yénd mat yéném ndené mewang siyém yau efalofifi ngánján a kété siyém mé ámbiro Ngánján yém. 5 Njam kété em tárfár "ngánján" njafar kénjún o mbandan, a yéné em tárfár "njánján" a tárfár "yuré, " 6 wénde téfeyot siyém ámbiro Afé Ngánján, kétándmé ende tambén méinyotio rokar nufrotaménd a endeyot yénd naketam. A ámbiro Yuré, Yesu Keriso, ende kénjúmé méinyotio rokar nufrotaménd, a ende kénjúmé yénd naketam.
bt: So, about eating the food offered to idols. We know that an idol is not really a god and there is only one God. 5 Even if there are many “gods,” in heaven or on earth, and there are many “gods” and “lords,” 6 but there is for us only one God, the Father, from whom all things came and for whom we live. And one Lord, Yesu Keriso, through whom all things came and through whom we live.

nama: Wénde méinyotio ár yau mat em yéné matyam. Ámb ár ménj té em mewangfaf sénonjo tawotio njam enetat ndené sáfot njamke yémofem yéné mé ewafarenat mewangoene njamke árké em; ménamén yéfene emarégh siyém njangénnjangén, yéné siyém mámáfi ewayatote. 8 Wénde njamke yam yau so kaka yényátote Ngánjánefaf. Yéndfem yau so ewaghnam némndafi táf yéndfem yau njam enetam, yéndfem némndafi mer yau so ewinjotam njam yéndfem njamke enetam.
bt: But not everyone have this knowledge. Some people are still so used to idols that to this day when they eat such food they still think of it as food that belongs to an idol, because their conscience is weak, it make bad. 8 But eating food will not bring us near to God. We shall not lose anything if we do not eat, we gain don't anything if we eat food.

nama: Aya kor aem, yau eumyotati féfenjo yuwek yimángégh fá eméinde efe njangénnjangén ndimbal mwighayan nuitat yaufiyamot. 10 Njam ár endene emarégh yém njangénnjangén yéné yaman kénminjongé fém, endene matyam té yém, nénéyan sái kénminjongé mewang wáwefnogh méngon, yéné yam so téméindangé fá njamke nénet mewang ramégh njamket. 11 Féyo yéné njangénnjangén ár, fe fútar endeyot Keriso kér yémormén, so yaghne ménamén fene matyammén! 12 A yéné ende fémofem so yaufiyam yafrotati Kerisoefaf ménaye yaufiyam ewafrotati ámb efalo yátogh árfefaf a ewaghnati yéfene njangénnjangén emarégh. 13 Féyo njam njamke yam yéméinde efalo yátogh ár yaufiyamot, yéndon yaufifi so yénetan fenjen wéikor, ménát yau so yéméindan efalo yátogh ár sái kuyangé yaufiyamot.
bt: Be careful, not to let your freedom of action make those who are weak in the big mind(faith) fall into sin. 10 If a person whose conscience is weak in this things sees you.p, who have knowledge, eating in the worshipping house of an idol, this thing will encourage him to eat food offered to idols. 11 So this weak person, your friend for whom Keriso died, will perish because of your knowledge! 12 And in this way you.p will do sin against Keriso by doing sin to other truly approving man(believer) and spoiling their weak conscience. 13 So if eating food makes a truly approving man(believer) sin, I will never eat meat again, so as not to make a truly approving man(believer) fall into sin.

nama: Yénd yuwek ár wém. Yénd Yesuene muyafé ár wém. Yéndon té yinjoyén Yesu téfene Yuré. A fém em tane ásáfogh mbwe Yurai. 2 Táf ámb árém yau njam kumyongi yénd ndené fété Yesuene muyafé ár, yénd mé fété wém féfeyot! Ménamén fém em tane fagharéghkaf em yénd wém Yesuene muyafé ár.
bt: I am a free man. I am a man with Yesu's authority(apostle). I have seen Yesu our Lord. And you.p are the fruit of my work for the Lord. 2 Even if other people do not accept me as a man with Yesu's authority(apostle), surely I am to you.p! Because you.p are the seal(note: when people close the house of yam put the mud on the string to show that nobody will unclose the bark.) that I am a man with Yesu's authority(apostle).

nama: Njam árém waruindat yénd, yéné siyém ndenáye yénd nawémarotan yéndio. 4 Tane mbarkánd yém njamke a nu nénet ménamén tane ásáfoghmén. 5 Yénd mbarkánd wém efalo yátogh amaf wasroghét yéndfé tane álighan, ndenanit ámb Yesuene muyafé ár, Yurane yánngnéf a Fité. 6 A Banabas a yénd wéi mbarkánd yénre yau ásáfoghét ghérsé ámnjoghmén. 7 Némé fiyaré násáfotat yéfenjo fai kémghét ásmégh mérén kénjún? Némé ndau téfnáram yau so enete géreif mbwe kétándmé yánjo géreif mbwe ménj ndauta? Némé sif wanjengégh árém etrone nono kétándmé yéfenjo sifta?
bt: When people criticize me, this is how I defend myself. 4 I have the right to be given food and drink for my work. 5 I have the right to take a believing wife along with me on my trips, as the other men with Yesu's authority(apostles) and the Lord's younger brothers and Fitè. 6 And Banabas and I have also the right to not work for a living. 7 What soldiers use their own expenses in the army? What farmers do not eat the grapes from their own grape fruit vine garden? What sheep watching men do not use the milk from their own sheep?

nama: Yénd yau norayan ár mwighata yéné méinyotio efogh wafrogh yammén, ménamén nákam noraye ndené yam ménáyo. 9 Yéndfem yaftam Mosesene nák kénjún ndené, "Yau yumbeta kau sénféneyan njam etumnda flawé mbwe." Némé Ngánján mwighé yém kaumén? 10 Efalo fá noraye yénémamén téfefaf. Fété yéné yéfarotaménd téfeyot ménamén emo njam yétana a emo njam eita so yéfenjo ásáfogh yafrotat yéné mwighayan nde yéndfem so ámb tawanangém yéné njamke. 11 Yéndfem té eitamém mbérmbére mbwe féfefafmé. Némé mindefifi táf yéndfem tatanam fifie rokar féfe tambén? 12 Táf ámb árfene té ewam mbarkánd wamaroghét kétándmé féfe tambén, téfene mbarkánd siyém minde ndimbalfifi. Wénde yéndfem yau yaman yékmangém yéné mbarkánd. Wénde yéndfem yumyoyém méinyotio ménát yénd yau so nétér yéném Kerisoene mer sifaf.
bt: I am not saying you human idea of these everyday doing, because the law says the same thing. 9 We read in the law of Moses that, “Do not cover the mouthof an kau when you.p are using it to tread the grain.” Is God thinking about kau? 10 Surely he says this for us. Of course that was written for us because anyone who dig and anyone who reaps should do their work in the hope of getting a share of the crop. 11 We have planted spiritual seed among you.p. Is it too much if we reap physical things from you.p? 12 If other people have the right to expect this from you.p, our right is very big. But we didn't use this right. Instead, we have endured everything not to put any obstacle in the way of the good talk about Keriso.

nama: Fém mat em ndené yéné ár efe násáfotat wáwefnogh méngon so ersat njamke kétándmé yéné wáwefnogh méngota a fá emofem erametat ghéngén kitarayan némé yémofem ersat ghéngén kétándio. 14 Ndené endeyo, Yuram té yénémndayéng ndené fá emofem mer si yaitotat yémofem so tawanangi yéfenjo ghérsé ámnjoghét kétándmé mer sita.
bt: You.p know that those men who work in the worshipping house get food from that worshipping house and those who offer the sacrifices on the table for burning sacrifice get a share of the sacrifices. 14 In the same way, the Lord has ordered that those who preach the good talk should get their living from the good talk.

nama: Wénde yénd yau tane mbarkándan kásáfotan. A yéndon yéné yau yéfarotan sénonjo ménát ewatnan tanjo mbarkánd tandio. Yénd so kér wém fronde! Árké árom yau so tengongé tane mbarkánd fifiofnarot. 16 Njam yéndon yaitotan mer si, yénd féyotarofnar awarmboghét wém, ménamén yénd naneghan si waitoghét. Nderé mámáfi yém tayot njam yéndon yau yaitotan mer si! 17 Njam yéndon mé tafrotan tanjo méndeye, féyo yénd kéma kawanjetan faiot, wénde yéndon tafrotan yéné ásáfogh ménamén Ngánjánam té yumyoi yéné ásáfogh tafaf. 18 Némé fai so yéndon tanan? Mé yénaro yém: Mer si yafroghro yénd so karaman faiofnaye, a yau so tafrotangén tane mbarkánd ndené fété mer si waitogh ár.
bt: But I haven't used any of these rights. And I am not writing this now to claim such rights for myself. I will die first! Nobody is going to turn my rightful boast into empty! 16 When I preach the good talk, I cannot boast, because I am compelled to preach. How terrible for me if I do not preach the good talk! 17 If I did my work as my wanting, then I could expect to be paid, but I do this work because God has allowed me this work. 18 What pay do I get? Just this: Only preaching the good talk I may offer it without.payment, and I will not claim my rights as the preacher of the good talk.

nama: Yénd wém yuwek ár, árké ároene yau, wénde yénd náyátotan yéndio méinyotio árfene faiofnar ásáfogh ár ménát tárfáram so efalo tiyátongi mer si. 20 Njamandio yénd násáfotan Ju árfé, yénd ndenanit so wémor Ju árnit ménát yémofem so efalo tiyátongi mer si, ndemamén yénd yéndio yau so Mosesene nák mbanmbanan wém. Yénd ndené fété so wémor ndernáye yénd wém, njam yénd násáfotam yémafé ménát yémofem efalo so tiyátongi mer si. 21 Ndené ende, njam Téndo árfé yénd yanmor ndenanit Téndo ár kés em, Ju árfene nák téndota, ménát yémofem efalo so tiyátongi mer si. Yéné yau ndené yém ndené yéndon yau Ngánjánene nák náyáretan, yénd efalofifi swém Kerisoene nák mbanmbanan wém. 22 Muyafnar árét yénd kénáyáton muyafnar ménát muyáfnar ár sái ersan. Yénd méinyotio yamamé wafnyé ménát ámb ár wanjarghét Kerisoene sie.
bt: I am a free man, nobody's without.payment working man(servant); but I make myself everybody's without.payment working man(servant) to many people to believe good talk. 20 While I was working with the Ju people, I live like a Jew so that they believe good talk, even though I myself am not under the law of Moses, I lived as I were, when I was working with those who are so that they believe good talk. 21 In the same way, when working with Side people, I live like a Side man, outside of the Ju people's law, so that they will believe good talk. This is not like that I don't obey God's law, I am really under Keriso's law. 22 To the weak people I became weak to win them. I have became all things to people so that some people be saved by Keriso's talk.

nama: Méinyotio yéné ásáfogh yéndon sewaufrotan siyém mer si téfnan, ménát yéndon so yéné frengégh tawanan. 24 Fém mat em ndené kembneyan tárfár wérse téfnáram sifayé ersat wérset, wénde ámbiro áromro so tanangé yánjo fai tombemén. Ersati ménat sái tanangi fai. 25 Méinyotio kembne téfnár osaréghan so keworngé yuwot sotafé watameghét. Yémofem yéné yafrotati ménát so tanangi ákiki árkéméné yau so nakete, wénde yéndfem yafrotam ménát so tanangém ákiki árkéméné so nakete tuot. 26 Yénémamén yénd nérsan mbarkánde kétán wifogh fufaf. Yéné téfnan yénd wém faisé ásmégh árnit emon yau ewanete yánjo utarégh ásmégh muyé. 27 Yéndon tanjo fifi táikér yiyátotan tékráte ménát so méinyotio yém awamaroghan, yéndio ménát yau so kolangén kembnet.
bt: All this I do for the good talk's sake, so that I will receive that blessings. 24 You.p know that many runners take part in a race, but only one of them gets the prize. Run to get the prize. 25 Every athlete in training submits to strict discipline They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever. 26 Therefore I run straightly for the finish line. That is why I am like a boxer who does not waste his fight poweres. 27 I become hard my body with pains to endure completely, to keep myself from being failed to the play.

nama: Tane fútaréf, Yénd ménde wém mé kawafrotangi némé nufrotam téfene ákiafefaf emofem Moses táitotawét. Fá tamorwén méinyotio njafar kwamkwam warawerégh mbanmbanan, a méinyotio néférmayénd merro Férkat Arakaf kénjúmé. 2 Njafar kwamkwam kénjúmé a arakaf kénjúmé fá méinyotio ewalitotaménd ndené fété Mosesene waitogh árot. 3 Méinyotioyam enetaménd ámbiro sáfot mbérmbére féskaf flawé 4 a enetaménd ámbiro sáfot mbérmbére nu. Yémofem nu enetaménd kétándmé mbérmbére wímta árké tayotawéng yémafé, a yéné wím témorwén Keriso fáyo. 5 Wénde Ngánján témorwén yau kéuwetawéng tárfár árfefaf, yénémamén yéfene kérwén fifi wafaranéghe tamorwén fanfne yuwan.
bt: My friends, I want you.p to remember what happened to our ancestors who followed Moses. They were all under the protection of the cloud, and all passed safely through the Red Sea. 2 Through the cloud and through the sea they were all baptized as followers of Moses. 3 All ate the same spiritual bread 4 and ate the same spiritual water. They ate water from the spiritual rock that went with them, and that rock was Keriso himself. 5 But God was not pleased with many people, and so their dead bodies were scattered over the desert.

nama: Méinyotio yéné siyém matyamot téfeyot ménát matot aya yau so mwighé ekwnam yaufiyam, ndené yémofem takwnawét, 7 yau so ewáwefnotati mewangat, ndené ámb ár kés kufrotat, méné Buk si noraye, "Ár námnjoyénd témndot wéri nu nénet a nokayoyénd wagh ermangét a walot." 8 Yéndfem yau so yaufi wal yafrotam ndené ámb árém kés tafrotawét, a ámbiro efogh kénjún 23,ooo ár mérén kér emormén. 9 Yéndfem yau so yékmetam Yuré ufoghan, ndené fété ámb árém kés yafrotaménd, a fá tásmetau kambanam. 10 Yénd yau so nawatarnam, ndené ámb ár kés kufrotawét, a fá kér résagh árém esmetaméng.
bt: All of this is for knowledge for us to warn us not to desire evil things, as they did, 7 nor to worship idols, as some of them did, as the scripture says, “The people sat down to a feast for drinking alcoholic drink and got up to dancing and sex.” 8 We must not do sexual immorality, as some people did, and in one day 23,000 people died. 9 We must not put the Lord to the test, as some people did, and they were killed by snakes. 10 We must not complain, as some people did, and the angel of death killed them.

nama: Méinyotio yéné yam kufrotawét yéfefaf ndené matyamot téfeyot, a yéfarotaménd matot téfeyot efe yénmor yéné efoghon mbandoene yatogh wifoghétro yam.
bt: All these things happened to them as knowldges for us, and were written down as a warning for us who live at the day of the end of the world.

nama: Njam fém nemnati ndené fém taikrataye ewakayong mer em fém so nawarawene ndené fém yau so kuyangi. 13 Méinyotio ufogh fémofem kés efángotati fá ndené fété eném túmén árfefaf. Wénde Ngánján yakai yánjo unyéghofnar sian yém, a yémon yau so kémumyongé fém ufoghét árké ewainde féfene muyé. Wénde njam ufogh yéném féfeyot, Ngánjánam so muyé tawaramangé féfe awamaroghét.
bt: If you.p think you.p are standing strongly you.p are good be careful that you.p do not fall. 13 Every test you.p have experienced is the kind that normally comes to people. But God keeps his without.lying talk(promise), and he will not allow you.p to be tested that will overcome your power. Instead when a temptation comes to you God will give you.p the strength to endure it, and so provide you.p with a way out.

nama: Tane tikfafé fútaréf, mewang wáwefnoghta kékmbongi. 15 Fém mwighafé ár em, féyo Yénd norayan féfefaf yéné. Fém fété so wafénmanetati féfembia némé si yénd norayan. 16 Yéné frengégh kafusi yéndfem árkémamén kánjo yarametam Ngánjáne, yéndfem té yéfalotam Kerisoene férék kénjún. A yéné féskaf flawé yéndfem yatfotam, yéndfem yéfalotam Kerisoene fifi njam yéndfem yénetam. 17 Ménamén kété siyém mé ámbiro flawé kofékofé yénd méinyotio sieném mé ámbiro fifi yéném, ndemamén tárfár yéném, ménamén yéndfem yéfalotam yéné kofékofayo.
bt: My heartful friends, stop from the worship of idols. 15 You are knowledgable people, I speak to you.p this. You must judge for yourselves what I say. 16 This blessing cup for which we give thanks to God, we share in the blood of Keriso. And the bread that we break, we share in the body of Keriso. 17 Because there is the one loaf of bread, all of us, though many, are one body, because we all share the same loaf.

nama: Mé kemnangi Israil árfenemén. Emofem enetat némé erametat ghéngénot kafghangi ghéngén kitaré ásáfoghan Ngánjáne. 19 Yéndon némé norayan siyém ndené némé yém mewang o njamke ramégh kétán mewangfaf? 20 Yau némndafi! Némé yénd norayan féfefaf siyém ndené némé efayotat ngánjánofnar árfene ghéngén kitareyan erametat yaufi mbérmbrafefaf, yau Ngánjáne. A yénd méndefnar wém fém sái em awarnyégh ár yaufi mbérmbérafé. 21 Fém féyotarofnar em nu nénet kétándmé Yurane kafusita a ndenáyo kétándmé yaufi mbérmbérafeta; fém féyotarofnar em njamke nénet Yurane kitarayan a ndenáyo yaufi mbérmbérafene kitarayan. 22 O yéndfem yafngongém Yuré waramnjaghét? Yénd ndené nemnam ndené téfene muyé yam kar yáinde yáne muyé?
bt: Keep on thinking about the people of Israil. Those who eat what is offered in sacrifice share in the table for burning sacrifice's service to God. 19 What I am saying that what is an idol or the food offered to the idols? 20 Nothing! What I am saying to you.p is that what you are offered on without.god people's table for burning sacrifices is offered to evil spirits, not to God. And I do not want you.p to be partners with evil spirits. 21 You cannot drink from the Lord's cup and also from the cup of evil spirits; you.p cannot eat food at the Lord's table and also at the table of evil spirits. 22 Or do we want to make the Lord jealous? Do we think that our power surpasses his power?

nama: Féyo fá ndené norayat."Yénd féyotaro yéném méinyotio yam wafroghét, " Yéné siyém efalo yém, wénde yau méinyotio yam em wáutáréghafé. 24 Efe yau so náronje yéfenjo merot, wénde náronja ámb árfene merot.
bt: So they say, “We are allowed to do everything.” That is true, but not everything is helpful. 24 No one should seek their own good, but the good of other people.

nama: Fém sem yuwek em némndafi téu enetati wérár fenjen maket téraréghmén, yau némndafimén namotati, ménamén féfenjo mwighé. 26 Buk si noraye ndené, "Yéné mband marat a méinyotio rokar yéné kénjún sem mé Yuranero em."
bt: You.p are free to eat anything sold in the meat market, without asking anything, because of your conscience. 26 Scripture says that, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”

nama: Táf ámb efalo yátoghofnar árom fém sái tanwarawangi séite nénet a fém kemnangi álighét, enetati némé féfene sáfrére sái em, yau táf kamotangi némndafimén ménamén féfenjo emarégh so yarawenati. 28 Wénde táf ámb árom njam kémaufrongé féfe ndené, "Yéné njamke yém mewangafefaf wateghmén, " féyo yau táf némé njamke enetati, ménamén ároene téfnanro emon fe némaufro a yáne emarégh téfnan- 29 yéné siyém, yau féfene emarégh yém, wénde ámb ároene emarégh.
bt: If a man who do not truly approve(believe) invites you.p for dinner and you.p decide to go, eat what is set before you.p, without asking anythings because of your conscience. 28 But if someone tells you.p, “This food was offered to idols,” then do not eat that food, for the sake of the one who told you.p and for conscience' sake, 29 that is, not your.p own conscience, but the other person's conscience.

nama: Táf yéndon kánjo yarametan Ngánjáne ménamén tane njamkemén, némamén wét ámb árém waruindat yénd ménamén njamkemén árkémamén yéndon kánjo yarametan?
bt: If I give thanks to God for my food, why should anyone criticize me about food because of something I give thanks?”

nama: Merfifi, fémofem némé njam yafrotati, njamke yenetati o nu yenetati, yafrotati ménát yuyoghét Ngánjáne yétkwén. 32 Yau yéméinda ámb ár wuyéghét, téu em Ju ár, Téndo ár o Ngánjánene Yesuene mérén. 33 Yéndon yéné mwighé yaitotan mato méinyotio ár áuwéghét eméindan méinyotio yaman. Yéndon sewafrotan, yau nemnan tanjo mermén wénde méinyotio árfene merot, ménát fá so ghérsé kewortongi.
bt: Well, whatever you.p do, you.p eat or drink, do it to lift up God's name. 32 Don't cause anyone to stumble, to Ju people or Side people or to the Yesu's family(church) of God. 33 I follow this thought to know to cause please every people in every way. I do, not thinking of my own good but the good of many, so that they may be saved.

nama: Wufénmametati tafaf ndené fété yénd nufénmetan Kerisoefaf. Wafarégh sénko wáwefnogh kénjún
bt: Imitate me just as I imitate Keriso. Covering the head in worship

nama: Yénd náuwetan féfefaf ménamén fémofem túmén yénd wamarotati a ewaitotati tane watamegh si árkéméné efermayén féfefaf. 3 Wénde yénd ménde wém fém sái mat em ndené Keriso siyém fronde sénko yém méinyotio ár ghakrafefaf, amafoe yánjar siyém sénko yém yaténoefafá, a Ngánján siyém sénko yém Kerisoefaf. 4 Féyo njam ár ghakér Ngánjánafé oraighan yém o Ngánjánene si waitoghan yém maratan wáwefnogh kénjún sénko wafaréghafé yéné yame wáiwáiyot yékmete Keriso. 5 A árké amaf efe Ngánjánafé oraighan yém o Ngánjánene si yaitote marat wáwefnogh sifayan sénko wafaréghofnaye wáiwáiot yékmete yánjar. Kété siyém yau ámb fénatayo yém fá a amaf endene sénko séf té yétértaménd. 6 Táf amafom njam yau tafarngé yánjo sénko, mer yém yémon yánjo séf tanfangé, a táfyo wáiwáiafé yam yém amafoe yánjo sénko weranégh o séf térégh, yémon so tafarngé yánjo sénko. 7 Ár ghakrom yau so tafarngé yánjo sénko, ménamén fá yém mbanmban a ndimbal kwayaghé Ngánjánene. Wénde amaf yém ár ghakroene ndimbal kwayaghé. 8 Ménamén ár ghakér yau yafrotam kétándmé amafoeta, wénde amaf yafrotam kétándmé ár ghakroeta. 9 Yau ár ghakér yafrotam amafoe téfnan, wénde amaf yafrotam ár ghakroe téfnan. 10 Mé njafar si résagh árfene téfnanro, féyo, amafoene sénko so fété wafaréghkaf yém ménát yuyafote ndené fá siyém si wafté mbanmban yém. 11 Wénde Yurane árfene kénjún, amaf yau yambia yém ár ghakérta, o ár ghakér yau ár ghakér yambia yém amafoeta. 12 Ménamén amaf yafrotam kétándmé ár ghakéroeta, ndené endeyo ár ghakér siyém náráfte amafoeta, wénde méinyotio rokar eném Ngánjáneta.
bt: I praise you.p because you.p always remember me and follow the teachings that I have handed on to you.p. 3 But I want you.p to know that Keriso is the first head over every man, the husband is the head over his wife, and God is the head over Keriso. 4 So if a man who talks with God or proclaims God's talk in public worship with his head covered put the shame by this thing to Keriso. 5 And any woman who talks with God or proclaims God's talk in public worship with without.covered on her head put the shame to her husband. There is no difference between her and a woman whose head has been shaved. 6 If a woman does not cover her head, it is good if she cut her hair, and if it is a shameful thing for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair, she should cover her head. 7 A man has no need to cover his head, because he is the image and big light(glory) of God. But woman reflects the big light(glory) of man. 8 Because man was not created from woman, but woman was created from man. 9 Nor was man created for woman's sake, but woman was created for man's sake. 10 On account of the men carrying heavenly message(angels), then, a woman's head should be covered to show that she is under word power(authority). 11 But in the Lord's people, woman is not herself from man, nor is man himself from woman. 12 Because woman was made from man, in the same way man is born from woman, but everything comes from God.

nama: Yafénmetati yéné yam féfendio ndené mer nérete amafoe sái noraya Ngánjánefaf maratan wáwefnogh kénjún táf némndafi yau njam yéfai yáne sénkoyan. 14 Némamén Ngánjánene wafroghyamam ematamete fém ndené tombé séf ár ghakroefaf sem wáiwáiafé yém, 15 wénde njam amaf tombé séfafé me, yéné siyém yáne merro yém yáne sénkoyan. Njam Ngánjánam ewaramete tombé séf yáfaf, yémon yaramete ménát yáné sénko wafaréghmén. 16 Wénde efe njam éwighét kufngongé yénémamén, téfene ámb wafroghyam yau yém a Ngánjánene Yesuene mérnoene yau yém. Yurane njamke
bt: Consider this thing for yourselves that is good for a woman to talk with God to God in public worship with nothing to put on her head. 14 Why, God's creation way teaches you.p that long hair on a man is a shameful, 15 but if a woman has long hair, it is a beauty of the head. When God gave her long hair, he gave it for a covering of her head. 16 But if anyone wants to argue about it, we have no other acting way and there is no in Yesu's groups(churches) of God. The Lord's food

nama: Yénamayo watamegh sian, yéndon yau náuwétan féfefaf, ménamén féfene áumengégham wáwefnoghét yafrote minde tékrat, wénde yau mer yafrote. 18 Fronde sifayan, yénd náyáretan ndené njam fém naumendat ndené fété Yesuene mérén, kété afalogh em féfe kénjúnro, a yéné yéndon efalo yiyátotan ámb mé ewam. 19 Yau mwighé déudéu em yéné séno afalogh féfe kénjún yém ménát fá efe mbarkandan yém so safénro kawinjongi. 20 Njam fém nafángotati mérne, yéné siyém yau Yurane njamke yenetati. 21 Ndené njamke nénean, fém ámbiroámbirom fété so féfenjo njamke enetati, féyo ámb ár siogh em táfandio ámb wériafé em. 22 Féfene méngo yau té ewam njamke nénet a nu nénet? O fém ménde em wáiwáiafé Ngánjánene Yesuene mérén yiyátote a fá efe áronjaghan em? Némé so yénd korayangén féfefaf? Yéndon so áuwétan fém? Yéndon yaufifi!
bt: In the following teachings, I do not praise you.p, because your meetings for worship do more harm, but it does not good. 18 In the first place, I hear when you.p come together as a Yesu's group(church), there are divisions among you.p, and this I truly approve(believe) is partly true. 19 No doubt there must be divisions among you.p so that the ones who are in the straight may be clearly seen. 20 When you.p meet together, it is not the Lord's food that you.p eat. 21 For as you.p eat, you.p each go ahead with your own meal, so some people are hungry while other people get drunk. 22 Don't you have your own homes in which to eat and drink? Or do you.p want to make shame the Yesu's family(church) of God and to them who are in need? What shall I say to you.p? Shall I praise you.p? Never I do!

nama: Yéndon yanayén Yurata yéné watamegh yéndon árké yafnamoyén féfefaf. Yuré Yesu, sémbáran fá sén yérametaménd, ewanai féskaf flawé ndimbul, 24 a kánjo si yaramai Ngánjáne, yétfoi, norayai, "Yéné siyém tane fifi, árké yém féfeyot. Yafrotati awafaroghét tanemén." 25 Ndené endeyo, séite njamke néne soramé yémon kafusi yerai a emndai, "Yéné kafusi siyém Ngánjánene totér árnyégh fémofé, yetetai tane férke. Njam fémofem yéné nu yenetati, wafarotati yénd."
bt: I received from the Lord this teaching that I passed on to you.p. The Lord Yesu, on the night he was given teeth(betrayed), took a piece of bread, 24 and gave thanks to God, broke it, and said, “This is my body, which is for you.p. Do this in memory of me.” 25 In the same way, after eating afternoon food(the supper) he took the cup and said, “This cup is God's new covenant, sealed with my blood. When you.p drink it, remember me.”

nama: Njam fémofem yenetati yéné féskaf flawé a nu yenetati yéné kafusita, fémofem yaitotati Yurane kérmén kétánotio fá nénangota. 27 Féyo táf árké árém Yurane féskaf flawé o nu yenetat yéné kafusian yéné ende yau utémndat fá, fém yaufi yafrote Yurane fififaf a férékfaf. 28 Féyo mer yém fém so néwasufetati fémyo fronde, féyo soramé féskaf flawé a nu enetati kétándmé yéné kafusita. 29 Fá emofem enetat féskaf flawé a nu Yurane fifi awasufoghofnaye enérsat mogh yéfendio. 30 Ndené téfnan tárfár fém em enjne a njangénnjangén, a tárfár ár kér té emormén. 31 Wénde njam yéndio fronde nawasufetam merkéráye, yénd yau so Ngánjánene mogh mbanmbandan yéném. 32 Wénde yénd yénmote téfél yénramete Yuram, ménát ndené yénd yau so ténkmangé tékrat mérne yémafé mband árfé.
bt: When you.p eat this bread and drink from this cup, you.p proclaim the Lord's death until he comes. 27 So the man who eats the Lord's bread or drinks from his cup in this way that dishonors him, you.p do sin to the Lord's body and blood. 28 So you.p should examine yourself first, and then eat the bread and drink from the cup. 29 Those who eat and drink without discerning the body of Keriso bring judgment on themselves. 30 That is why many of you.p are sick and weak, and many people have died. 31 But if we would examine ourselves first, we would not come under God's judgment. 32 But we are judged and punished by the Lord, so that we shall not be put pain together with the world.

nama: Féyo, tane fútaréf, njam kufngongi mérne áumengéghét Yurane njamke nénet, fronde nawanjenati ámbiroámbiro árot. 34 Táf ámb ár siogh njam yém, fronde yémon so enete njamke méngon, ménát ndené fém yau so Ngánjánene mogh mbanmbanan em njam fém nafángotati mérne. Ndené ámb yammén, yéndon so téngfrendangén táf yénd njam kénaflen.
bt: So, my friends, when you.p gather together to eat the Lord's food, firstly wait for one another. 34 If anyone are hungry, firstly you.p should eat at home, so that you.p will not come under God's judgment as you.p meet together. As for the other things, I will solve them when I come.

nama: Tane fútaréf, sénonjo mwighé ménamén fokmén Ngánján Mbérmbéroeta.
bt: My friends, now concerning about the gifts from the God Spirit.

nama: Fém mat em ndené njam fém mé tamorwén Téndo ár, fém talimndawét a tawarsotawét ghérsafnar mewangot. 3 Yénd ménde wém fém so mat kamndangi ndené ámbiro efe yau Ngánján Mbérmbéram yélimnde so korayangé "Afalngogh yém Yesuefaf!" a ámb yau so marataye korayangé ndené "Yesu siyém Yuré, " táfandro Ngánján Mbérmbéram yau njam nélimnda.
bt: You.p know that while you.p were still Side people, you.p were led and deceived to lifeless idols. 3 I want you.p to know that no one who is led by God's Spirit can say “A curse on Yesu!” and no one can say publicly, “Yesu is Lord,” without being guided by the God Spirit.

nama: Kété em fénaté sáfot mbérmbére fok, wénde ndenéyameyo Ngánján Mbérmbéram ewaramete. 5 Kété em fénaté ásáfogh, wénde ndenéyameyo Yurai násáfote. 6 Kété em fénaté ásáfogh wafroghét, wénde ndenéyameyo Ngánjánam ewaramete fok méinyotio árfefaf, ménát yéfenjo ásáfoghét. 7 Ngánján Mbérmbéroene wafté nauyafote ámbiroámbiro árfaf fénaté ende méinyotio árfene merot. 8 Ngánján Mbérmbéram yaramete ámbiro ároe káfeté mwighé afoghkaf si, njamandio ámb ároe yéné Ngánján Mbérmbéram yaramet matyam afoghkaf si. 9 Ámb ároe yéné Ngánján Mbérmbéram yaramete ndimbal mwighé, ndenéyameyo ámb ároe yémon yaramete wafté mer yátoghét. 10 Ngánján Mbérmbéram ámb ároe yaramete wafté sélngwélafé yam wafroghét, ámb ároe yaramete fok Ngánjánene si wafroghmén, a wéi ámb árét, yéné fok ménát mat sái yém fénaté sáfot mbérmbér. Ámbiro ároe yaramete fok ménát fénaté sáfot kanké si orayéghét a ámb ároe fok yaramete ménát némé noraye kankaye yéné si waferghét. 11 Wénde yéné siyém ámbiro a yéné Ngánján Mbérmbéryo emo yéné méinyotio yafrote ásáfogh ndené fété fá nemne, yémon ewaramete fénaté fok ámbiroámbiro árét. Ámbiro fifi tárfár fifi kafeyafé
bt: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but the same God Spirit gives them. 5 There are different works, but the same Lord is served. 6 There are different works to do, but the same God gives gift to everyone for their work. 7 The God Spirit's power is shown in each person for the good of all. 8 The God Spirit gives one person a talk full of wisdom, while to another person the same God Spirit gives a talk full of knowledge. 9 One and the same God Spirit gives big mind(faith) to one person, in the same way to another person he gives the power to make good. 10 The God Spirit gives one person the power to work special workings(miracles), to another, gives the gift of proclaiming God's talk, and to another, the gift to know the various kinds of spirits. To one person he gives the gift to speak in strange tongues, and to another he gives the gift to explain what said with tongues. 11 But it is one and the same God Spirit who does all this as he thinks, he gives a different gift to each person. One body with many Parts

nama: Keriso siyém ndenanit ámbiro fifi, árké yém tárfár kafeyafé, wénde mé yém ámbiro fifi, ndemamén yafrote fénataye kafeyam. 13 Ndené endeyo, méinyotio yénd, Ju ár o Téndo ár, faiofnar ásáfogh ár a yuwek ár, té yénmalitotam ámbiro fifi kénjút yéné Ngánján Mbérmbére, a yénd méinyotio té yénramayéng yéné ámbiro Ngánján Mbérmbére nu nénet.
bt: Keriso is like a single body, which has many parts, but it is still one body, even though it is made up of different parts. 13 In the same way, all of us, Ju people or Side people, without.payment working men(servants) or free, have been baptized into the one body by the same God Spirit, and we have all been given the one God Spirit to drink.

nama: Fifi fáyo siyém yau yafrote mé ámbiro fifi kafeyam, wénde tárfár fifi kafeyam. 15 Njam kafkaf korayangé, "Ménamén yénd yau ngángé wém, yénd yau wafnyang fifiyan, " yéné yam yau yékmbote kafkaf sái yém fifi kafe. 16 A njam téngan korayangé, "Ménamén yénd yau wém ghékéf, yénd yau wafnyang fifiyan, " yéné yam yau yékmbote téngan sái yém fifi kafe. 17 Njam méinyo fifi yém mé ghékéfro, ndernáye so náyárete? A njam mé ténganro yém, ndernáye so nomnde? 18 Ndené fété Ngánjánam ekmetam fénaté kafe fifiyan yánjo méndeyan. 19 Njam méinyotio mé ámbiro fifi kafe yém, ánde yém fifi? 20 Ndené fété kété em tárfár kafe wénde ámbiro fifi.
bt: The body itself is not made up of only one part, but of many parts. 15 If the foot could say, “Because I am not a hand, I don't belong to the body,” that thing would not keep it from foot being a part of the body. 16 And if the ear could say, “Because I am not an eye, I don't belong to the body,” that thing would not keep it from for eye being a part of the body. 17 If the whole body were just an eye, how could it hear? And if it were only an ear, how could it smell? 18 As God put every different part in the body just as he wanted it to be. 19 If they were all one part, where could the body be? 20 As it is, there are many parts but one body.

nama: Féyo, ghékfam yau so korayangé ngángéfaf ndené, "Yénd méndefnar wém fém!" Yau sénko so korayangé kafkaffaf, "Mer, yénd méndefnar wém fém!" 22 Atéraréghe, yéndfem yau so némé ewafrotam yéné fifi kafeyofnaye árkéméné em njangénnjangénnit, 23 a yéné fifi kafemén ndené yénd nemnam ndené yau mer em wénde yéndfem árkéméné ewarawenam ndimbal kéráye, yéné fifi kafe árkéméné yau mer néretat fénaté utmanégh, 24 ndené warawerégh yau yém minde mer fifi kafeyat. Ngánjánam té fifi efghai yéné ende ndené sái ewarameta ndimbal utmanégh yéné fifiyat árkéméné fifiofnar néretat. 25 Féyo kété siyém yau falogh yém fifiyan, wénde méinyotio fénataye fifi kafe ámbiroámbiroe ndenáyo mwighafé nafandat. 26 Njam ámbiro fifi kafe kawambangé, méinyotio ámb fifi kafe wéi kawambangi. Njam ámb fifi kafe náuwéta, méinyotio ámb fifi kafe áuwéghan em yémafé.
bt: So, the eye cannot say to the hand, “I don't need you.p!” Nor can the head say to the feet, “Well, I don't need you.p!” 22 On the contrary, we cannot do without the parts of the body that seem to be weaker, 23 and those body parts that we think aren't good which we treat very big, the parts of the body which don't look good with special respect, 24 to the beautiful parts do not need special treatment. God has put the body together in such a way as to give big honor to those parts that looks without.meaning. 25 So there is no division in the body, but all its different parts have the same thought for one another. 26 If one part of the body suffers, all the other parts suffer with it. If one body part is praised, all the other body parts are happy with it.

nama: Fém méinyotio sem Kerisoene fifi, a fém ámbiroámbiro sném fifi kafe ném. 28 Yesuene mérén kénjún Ngánjánam té ekmetaméng méinyotio yéfenjo sifayan: fronde sifayan Yesuene muyafé ár, yénamayo sifayan Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár, a nambiomén sifayan watamegh ár, féyo fá emofem sélngwélafé ásáfogh ewafrotat, wafté mer yátoghét enjneta, o ámb ár wáutárghét, o ámb wambghét o ámb orayéghét fénatayo kanké sie. 29 Yénd ményotio yau em Yesuene muyafé ár, o Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár, o watamegh ár. Yau méinyotio árém so sélngwélafé yam ewafrotat, 30 o ménát enjneta mer yátoghét, o ménát sái norayat kanké si, o ménát sái yéfenat némé noraye. 31 Tikfe tékwnat minde tukén fok. Yéndon so kénmauyafon fá minde mer end.
bt: All of you.p are Keriso's body, and each one is a part of it. 28 In the Yesu's family(church) God has put all in their place: in the first place men with Yesu's authority(apostles), in the second place God's people who make know(prophets), and in the third place teaching men, then those who do special workings(miracles), powe to make good from the diseases, or to help other people, or to direct them, or to speak in strange tongues. 29 We all are not all men with Yesu's authority(apostles), or God's people who make know(prophets), or teaching men. Not every people can make special things(miracles), 30 or to make good diseases, or to speak in strange tongues, or to explain what is said. 31 Eagerly desire the very big gifts. I will show you.p the most good way.

nama: Njam yénd féyotaro wém so norayan árfene a njafar si résagh árfene sie, wénde tané uwégh yau, tane orayégh siyém mé sotro ndenanit sin kwar nau yaraunete ke kér ro o bel nau yaraunete. 2 Njam yénd wém yéné fokafé sái nafrotan Ngánjáne tambén si a yénd so méinyotio matyamafé a mwighé kwayaghé efandan méinyotio kwankwane yam, a yénd njam so ndimbal mwighafé mband tunakéf witérmboghét, wénde njam yénd uwéghofnar wém, yénd swém fifiofnar. 3 Njam yénd so ewaramayén méinyotio tanemén yauyau árét a tane fifi téraman fésén wakrarghét, wénde táf yénd wém uwéghofnar, yénd swém fifiofnar.
bt: If I could speak in the tongues of men or of men carrying heavenly message(angels), but do not have love, my saying is like resounding empty pot skin(drum) or a clanging bell. 2 If I have that gift of saying the talk come from God and I have all knowledge and understanding all secrets, and if I have a big mind(faith) to move mountains, but I do not have love, I am nothing. 3 If I give all I have to to poor people and give over my body to be burned, but I have no love, I gain nothing.

nama: Uwégh siyém métar, uwégh siyém mer. Uwégh siyém yau waramnjagh, tuktuk yam, awarmbogh yam. 5 Uwégh siyém wafareréghofnar yam, o awamanjarégh{g{ ...f* {g{13.5 awamanjarégh check it again , o nékwofnar wékeye, uwéghan siyém ámb wafarsogh yam yau yém.{h{ ...f* {h{13.5 uwéghan siyém ámb wafarsogh yam yau yém what is meaning?
bt: Love is patient, love is kind. Love is not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. 5 Love does not without.respect thing, without.meaning, without anger quickly, in love there is no blockage way; 6 Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. 7 Love always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always encure falling.

nama: Uwégh yaufifi kuyangé. wénde kété em Ngánjáne si afroghmén, wénde fá so kékmbongi, a kété em kanké si, fá so kolindangi, a kété em matyam, yéné so kawaindangi. 9 Yénd mat yéném ámbro a yéndfem norayam ámb Ngánjáne tambén siro, 10 wénde njam kénwanongé féwénféwénofnar wafté, némé yém féwénféwénafé so kolindangé.
bt: Love never fails. But where there are proclaiming God's talk, they will cease, and where there are tongues, they will be disappeared, and where there is knowledge, it will pass away. 9 For we know in part and we prophesy in part, 10 but when perfective power comes, what is in part disappears.

nama: Njam yénd kwémorwén tosé, tane orayégh, tane emarégh ndenanit tosanit, a mwighé méinyotio tamorwén tosétosanitro. Wénde yénd njam namndayéng ár sotafé, yéndon ekmayén tosétosafene yam wafrogh tane sémta. 12 Sénonjo yéndfem yéfandam ndenanit mbanmban glasan, ámb káye sáfenro so kawafotangém. Némé yénd mat wém siyém mbarmbar, féyo yénd so méinyo mat wém, ndené fété Ngánján mat yém tanemén mbarkánde.
bt: When I was a child, my speaking, my thought like a child, I think everything like a child. But when I became a strong man(adult), I put the ways of childhood behind me. 12 Now we see as in a mirror; then we shall see clearly that time. What I know is part, then I shall know fully, just as God knows me straightly.

nama: Sénonjo yéné nambioro nafrangongi: ndimbal mwighé, méghmégh, a uwégh. Wénde minde ndimbalfifi siyém uwégh.
bt: Now these three remain: big mind(faith), hope and love. But the very big of these is love.

nama: Uwégh fiyém árkéméné fémofem so yafarenati tikfe Wénde fém wéi yafarenati ámb fok árké Ngánján Mbérmbéram ewaramai, fénatayero fok Ngánjáne si afroghmén. 2 Fá efe norayat kankaye yau norayat ámb árfefaf wénde kétán Ngánjánefaf, ménamén yau ámbiroámbirom mwighé kwayaghaye yau yérse. Fá norayat némé kwankwane efalo si em Ngánján Mbérmbéroene waftaye. 3 Wénde yémofem efe Ngánjáne si nafrotat, yáne si yéngém kétán árfefaf a ewarametat wáutárégh, wawéngsogh, a wamarogh. 4 Fá efe norayat kankaye nawáutánat mé fáro, wénde fá emofem Ngánjáne si nafrotat ewáutánat méinyo Yesuene mérén.
bt: Love is that you.p seek with heart. But you also seek the other gifts from God Spirit, especially gift of proclaiming God's talk(prophecy). 2 Those who speak in tongues do not speak to other people but to God, because no one understands them. They are speaking what is secret truths by the power of God Spirit. 3 But those who proclaim God's talk, his talk goes to to people and give them help, encouragement, and comfort. 4 Those who speak in tongues help only themselves, but those who proclaim God's talk help the whole Yesu's family(church).

nama: Yénd ménde wém ménát méinyotio fémofem so norayati kankaye si, wénde yénd minde nemnan nde féfefaf yéné fok yém Ngánjáne si wafroghmén. Yéné ár emo Ngánjáne si nafrote siyém minde waftafé, yau yéné ár emon noraye kankaye siyém némbne waftafé, táfandro kété njam ámb ár yau yém emon so si yéfene némé noraye, ménát wáutárégh méinyo Yesuene mérnot so yém. 6 Tane fútaréf, féyo njam yénd kénaflen féfefaf, némé mer yéndon so yafrotan féfefaf, táf yénd snorayan kankaye? Táfandro yéndon njam yau enwarsan Ngánjánam némé eufane tafaf o ámb matyam o ámb Ngánjáne si wafroghmén o ámb watamegh.
bt: I want all of you.p to speak in strange tongues, but I really think that that you.p had the gift of proclaiming God's talk. For this man who reports God's talk is with strong power, but this man who speaks in tongues is with little power, unless there is someone present who can explain what said, so that the whole Yesu's family(church) may be helped. 6 My friends, so when I come to you.p, what is good I will do to you.p, if I speak in tongues? Even though I bring you.p what God showed me or some knowledge or some proclaiming God's talk or some teaching.

nama: Mé kemnangi ghérsafnar rokarafene kém rérkemén árké ewaufetat ndené fété mburaré o sifrafér ke sem. Ndernáye ámb ár so mat káyátongé árké ke nambnat, táfandro njam wayangoghe nutnat? 8 Wéikor njam suféroene ke yau njam nutne merkéráye, efe so nafrende ménát mékot? 9 Ndené endeyo, ndernáye so ámb ár mat kamndangé némé fém noraye ménamén fene si afrogh kankaye yau safén? Fene si so kolindangi tukén! 10 Kété sem tárfár sitéfén yéné mband maratan, wénde ámbiro sitéfén yau fifiofnar yém. 11 Wénde njam yénd mafina wém yéné sitéfén árké norayat, fá efe yéné sitéfén norayat, fá so kamndangi dábi tayot, a yénd kamndan dábi yéfefaf. 12 Táf fene ndimbal ménde njam némé Ngánján Mbérmbéroene fokot, fém táf yafarenati minde tukén yéné fokot árké ménáyam so yanote Yesuene mérén.
bt: Think about lifeless musical instruments as the flute or the harp. How will anyone know [what] sound they play, unless they make their soundes responsibly? 8 Also if the one who plays the trumpet does not sound a clearly, who will prepare for battle? 9 In the same way, how will anyone understand what you.p are talking about if your talk given in tongues is not clear? Your talks will disappear to up! 10 There are many different languages in the world, yet none of them is without meaning. 11 But if I do not know the language which is spoken, those who use that language, will be foreigners to me and I will be a foreigner to them. 12 Since you.p want to have the gifts of God Spirit, you.p must seek the very high gift by which help to build up the Yesu's family(church).

nama: Féyo yéné téfnan yéné ár efe noraye kankaye so fété ngánjánafé noraye ménát yémon so yéfene némé fá noraye. 14 Ménamén njam yénd Ngánjánafé norayan kankaye, tane mbérmbér Ngánjánafé noraye, wénde tane mwighé siyém mbweyofnar yém. 15 Féyo yéné téfnan némé yéndon so yafrotan? Yénd so Ngánjánafé norayan tane mbérmbére ndenéyameyo yénd wéi so norayan tane mwighé. Yéndon so eufanan wagh tané mbérmbére, ndenéyameyo yéndon wéi so eufanan wagh tane mwighaye. 16 Njam fémon yaramete kánjo Ngánjáne mbérmbérero, wét ndernáye so matyamofnar ár áumengéghan korayangé ndené "Efalo" fene kánjo ramégh Ngánjánefaf táf njam yau mat yém némé fém korayé mbérmbére. 17 Táf fene kánjo ramégh Ngánjánefaf minde merro yém, ámb árét yaufifi wáutárégh yém.
bt: Then in this reason that person who speaks in tongues must talk with God that he can explain what they say. 14 For if I talk with God in tongues, my spirit talks with God, but my mind is unfruitful. 15 Then in this reason what should I do? I will talk with God with my spirit, and I will also talk with God with my mind. I will sing with my spirit, and I will also sing with my mind. 16 When you.s give thanks to God in spirit only, how can without.knowledge people in the meeting say “Amen” to your.s giving thanks to God if he doesn't know what you.s are saying with spirit? 17 Even if your giving thanks to God is very good, to other people are not helped at all.

nama: Yéndon kánjo Ngánjáne yarametan némé yénd norayan kankaye yáinde fémofem méinyotio némé ewafrotati. 19 Wénde yéndon so tawaferén Yesuene mérén wáwefnoghan yénd norayan 5 si ménát eném so tanangi kwayaghaye ménát ámb ár watameghét, yau tárfár 10,000 si kanké sie árkéméné ámb árém yau yérsat kwayaghé.
bt: I give thanks to God what I speak in tongues surpasses what all of you.p do. 19 But I choose in Yesu's family(church) worship I speak five talks that can be understood clearly to teach other people, don't speak ten thousand of talks in tongues which other people cannot understand.

nama: Fém yau so em ndenanit ambumtértér féfene mwighayan. Aem ndenanit ambumtértér yaufimén, wénde. anoghkaf ár aem káfeté mwighamén. 21 Buk si noraye, "Yuré norayai, Ámb árfene kanké sie yénd so norayan tane árfefaf, Yénd so norayan dábi árfene mbírmbíre, wénde nde yémofem yénd yau so wiyáretat."
bt: My friends, do not be like children in your thinking. Be like children about evil, but be grown up about wisdom. 21 In the Scriptures says, “The Lord says, by other people's tongues I will speak to my people. and I will speak through lips of foreigners, but they will not listen to me.”

nama: Féyo fok kankaye si orayégh siyém efalo yátoghét yém efalo yátoghofnar árfet, yau efalo yátogh árfet, fok Ngánjánene si wafroghmén siyém efalo yátoghét yém efalo yátogh árfet, yau efalo yátoghofnar árfet.
bt: So the gift of speaking in tongues is proof for truly without.approving people(unbelievers), not for truly approving people(believers), while the gift of proclaiming God's talk is proof for truly approving people, not for truly without.approving people.

nama: Yénémaménnde, táf Yesuene mérén méinyotio njam kafángongi a méinyotio ár kufngongi orayégh kankaye a ámb ár efe fokofnar em o ámb efalo yátoghofnar ár kénlawangi, fá so korayangi nde fém em onon. 24 Wénde táf méinyotio árém so Ngánjánene si so nafrotat njam ámb efalo yátoghofnar ár o fokofnar ár kénlawangi, fá so mé kawayamngongi yéfenjo yaufiyammén ménáyan némé fá sái náyáretat. 25 Njam fá náyáretat, yéfene kwankwane mwighé so kénakmangi safét, a yémofem so tarámnjongi a táwefnongi Ngánjáne, yéné siafé, "Efalo Ngánján siyém yéna yém féfe kénjún!" Wáwefnogh nák Yesuene mérén kénjún
bt: Therefore, if the whole Yesu's family(church) meets together and every people starts speaking in tongues and if some people who are without.gift or truly without.approving people come in, they say that you.p are all crazy. 24 But if every people is proclaiming God's talk when some truly without.approving people or without.gift people come in, they will recognise of their sin by what they hear. 25 As they listen, their secret thoughts will be brought clearly, and they will bow down and worship God, with this talk, “God is truly here among you.p!” Worshipping rule in the Yesu's family(church)

nama: Tane fútaréf, yéné fiyém némé yéndon norayan. Njam fém nafángotati ménát wáwefnoghét, ámbiro ároene wagh so yém, ámb ároene watameghét, ámb ároene so yém uyafoghét kétándmé Ngánjáneta, ámb ároene so yém kanké simén, a mé so yém ámb ároene waferéghét némé sái noraya. Méinyotio yam so yém wáutárégh Yesuene mérnot. 27 Njam ámb ár kufngongé kankaye siot, sómbio o nambio ámbiroámbiroero so norayat, a ámb árom fété so yéfene yéné si. 28 Wénde njam yau kété yém emon so kanké si yéfene, féyo fá efe noraye kanké si fété mé kékmbongé Yesuene mérnan a noraya mé yéfefetio a Ngánjánefaf. 29 Taumiyénd sómbio o nambio Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár orayéghét, njamandio ámb árém ewafénmetat némé si fá norayat. 30 Wénde njam ámb ár kété yémor áumengégh sifayan si Ngánjánam taramangé yéné ámbiro efe noraye so kékmbongé. 31 Méinyotio fémofem Ngánjánene si so nafrotati, ámbiroámbiroero, ménát méinyotio ár mat so ewatametat a wáutáréghafé so yém yéfeyot. 32 Ngánjánene si wafroghmén árfene mbérmbér so yém Ngánjánene mat yátogh ároene limánégh mbanmbanan, 33 ménamén Ngánján yau ménde yém yénd sái yisramboghe yéném wénde afrengéghan a krufrátan.
bt: My friends, this is what I say. When you.p meet for worship, one person has a hymn, another a teaching, another a revelation from God, another a talk in tongues, and still another people's explanation of what say. Everything must be helpful to the Yesu's family(church). 27 If someone is going to speak in tongues, two or three only one after the other should speak, and someone must explain that talk. 28 But if no one is there who can explain, then the one who speaks in tongues must be stopped in the Yesu's family(churches) and speak only to himself and to God. 29 Allow two or three who make known God's talk(prophests) to speak, while the other people are to judge what they say. 30 But if someone sitting in the meeting receives a talk from God, the one who is speaking should stop. 31 All of you.p may proclaim God's talk, one by one, so that every people will learn and be helped. 32 The spirit of who proclaim God's talk should be under the speaker's control, 33 because God does not want us to be in disorder but in harmony and peace.

nama: amaf métar so ekmbotat Yesuene mérén áumengégh kénjúmé. Fá yau eumyotat orayéghét, ndené fété Ju árfene nák snoraye, yémofem yau so ersat sifayé siot. 35 Táf fá njam ménde em ámoghét matot, yémofem so emotat yéfenjaréf kété méngon. Yéné siyém wáiwáiafé yém amafoe sái noraya Yesuene mérén áumengégh kénjún.
bt: the women should keep quiet in the meetings. They are not allowed to speak, as the Ju people's law says, they must not be in charge. 35 If they want to know the questions, they should ask their husbands at home. It is a shameful thing for a woman to speak in a Yesu's family(church) meeting.

nama: O fém nemnati ndené Ngánjánene si yénmormén féfe tambén? O fémro fété em endefefafmé yénmormén? 37 Táf ámb ár njam nemna nde fá siyém Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár o Ngánján Mbérmbéroene fokafé, fá fété kawayamngongé ndené némé yéndon yafarotan féfefaf siyém Yurane si muyé. 38 Wénde njam fá yau ménde em sái yumyoi yéné si, Ngánjánam yau so eumyoi fá.
bt: Or do you.p think that God's talk came from you.p? Or are you the only ones to whom it came? 37 If anyone thinks he is a man to know God's knowledge(prophet) or has a gift by God Spirit, he must realize that what I am writing to you.p is the Lord's command. 38 But if they don't want to accept this talk, God will not accept them.

nama: Féyo, tane fútaréf, yafarenati Ngánjánene si wafroghét, wénde yau kékmbongi kanké orayégh. 40 Méinyotio yam miya ewafrotati merkéraye a mbarkándero.
bt: So, my friends, seek to proclaim God's talk, but do not stop the speaking in tongues. 40 Everything must be done in a proper and straightly.

nama: Sénonjo, tane fútaréf, yénd ménde wém mwighat fém so ewangotan mer simén árké yéndon yaitotamén féfeyot, árké fémofem yáneyénd, a árké tukén féfene ndimbal mwighé a yiyang ndérndérafé. 2 Yéné fiyém mer si yéndon yaitotamén féfeyot. Fém nawanjaryénd mer sie njam fém mé naweretati taikrataye yéné sian-njam yéné yau em, féfene efalo yátogh so yém fifiofnar.
bt: Now, my friends, I want to remind you.p of the good talk which I preached to you.p, which you.p received, and on which your big mind(faith) stands firm. 2 This is the good talk that I preached to you.p. You.p are saved by the good talk if you.p hold firmly in this talk, if it is not, your.p truly approveing(faith) is without.meaning.

nama: Yéndon yafnamotan féfefaf némé yéndon yumyoyén, árké yém minde ndimbal yam: ndené Keriso kér yémormén téfene yaufiyammén, ndené fété Buk si norayé. 4 Fá té yiyatotaménd a té nanoyéng ghérsat nambio efogh kénjún, ndené fété Buk si norayé. 5 Fá nufaryéng Fitafaf a kétán méinyotio 12 Yesuene muyafé árfefaf. 6 Yéné soramé, fá nufaryéng tárfár árfefaf ndené 500 efalo yátogh ár, tárfáryo ghérsé mé em, ndemamén ámb kér emormén. 7 Yéné soramé, fá nufaryéng Jemsefaf, a wéi yénamayo méinyotio Yesuene muyafé árfefaf.
bt: I passed on to you.p what I received, which is very big thing: that Keriso died for our sins, as the Scripture says. 4 He was buried and was raised to life on the third day, as the Scripture says. 5 He appeared to Fitè and then to all 12 men with Yesu's authority(apostles). 6 After that, he appeared to many people over 5 hundred of his followers, many of them are still alive, although some have died. 7 After that, he appeared to Jems, and later to all the men with Yesu's authority(apostles).

nama: Soraméfifi fá nufaryéng ndenáyo tafaf, ámbrérero yénd wém ndené fété ámb ár endene áráfégh yau mer yémormén. 9 Yénd némbne wém méinyotio Yesuene muyafé árfeta, a féyotarofnar wém sái wémotai Yesuene muyáfé ár, ménamén yéndon máméghéraye tésmetawén Ngánjánene Yesuene mérén. 10 Wénde Ngánjánene ngáráme yénd yénéf wém némé yénd wém, a ngárám yémon ta waramayéng yau témorwén fifiofnar. Wénde tane wafroghyamam ewainde ámb Yesuene muyafé árfene wafroghyam, wénde yénd yau násáfotan yéné ásáfogh yénd yéndio. Ngánjánam fáyo yéramete ngárám tafaf a wéramete yáne muyé a féyo yéndon násáfotan yéné ásáfogh. 11 Féyo kétándmé ta tambén o yéfe tambén yénmormén, yéné féyém némé yéndfem méinyotio yaitotam a yéné féyém némé fémofem efalo yiyátotati. Téfene anogh
bt: Last of all he appeared also to me, even though I am like someone whose birth was not good. 9 I am the least of the men with Yesu's authority(apostles) and am able to be called a man with Yesu's authority(apostle), because I persecuted God's Yesu's family(church). 10 But by God's grace I am what I am, and the grace that he gave me was not without.meaning. But my doing thing surpasses doing things of the other men with Yesu's authority(apostles), but I am not doing that work by myself. God himself gave grace to me and gave me his strength, so I am doing that work. 11 So whether it came from me or from them, this is what we all preach and this is what you.p truly approve(believe). Our resurrection

nama: Wénde njam ndené nafrotat ndené Keriso té nanoyéng kétándmé kérta, ndernáye wét fém ámb norayati ndené kér ár yau so nanotat ghérsat? 13 Táf efalo njam yém, yéné fifi ndené yém Yesu yau nanoyéng. 14 A njam yau Keriso kanongai kétándmé kérta, féyo téfene si waitogh fifiofnar yém a ndenéyameyo féfene ndimbal mwighé. 15 A yéné yém tukén ndené, yénd nauyafotam ndené nuindam Ngánjánenemén, ménamén yénd norayam ndené Ngánjánam yanoi Keriso kérta. Wénde njam yéné efalo témorwén ndené kér ár yau nanoyénd ghérsat, táf ndené yémon yau yanoi Keriso. 16 Njam yau kér ár nanotat kérta, féyo Keriso yau kanongai kérta. 17 Njam Keriso yau kanongai, féyo féfene ndimbal mwighé sof yém fifiofnar yém a fém wéi sem mé orsoghan féfenjo yaufiyan em. 18 A ár efe efalo yiyátotat Keriso a efe té kémégh nékakmetaménd ndenéyameyo orsoghan em. 19 Njam téfene méghmégh Keriso kénjún mer yém mé yéné ghérsatro, yéndfem so minde ghérghérsisé tawinjongém yémofem efe méinyotio mband maratan em.
bt: But if it is preached that Keriso has been raised from death, how can some of you.p say that dead men will not be raised to life? 13 If that is true, it means that Keriso was not raised. 14 And if Keriso has not been raised from death, then our preaching is useless and so is your big mind(faith). 15 And above that is this, we are shown to be lying about God, because we said that God raised Keriso from death. But if it is true that dead men are not raised to life, then he did not raise Keriso. 16 If dead men are not raised, then Keriso hasn't been raised from death. 17 If Keriso has not been raised, then your big mind(faith) is without.meaning and you.p are still lost in your sins. 18 And the men who truly approving(believe) Kesriso and who have died are also lost. 19 If our hope in Keriso is only for this life, we are the more to be pitied from all the people in the world.

nama: Wénde efalo si siyém ndené Keriso té nanoyéng kétándmé kérta. Keriso yém fronde ár féyo fá efe kémégh té nakmetaménd, fá ndenéyameyo so nanotat kérta. 21 Ndené kér yénmormén ámbiro ár kénjúmé, ndenéyameyo anogh kétándmé kérta so yéném ámbiro ár kénjúmé. 22 Ndené fété méinyotio ár kér kés em Edémene kénjún, ndené endeyo méinyotio ár so nanotat ghérsat Keriso kénjún. 23 Wénde ámbiroámbiro ár so nanotat ndenéyameyo Keriso fronde, féyo yáne angoghan fá efe yáne em. 24 Féyo yatogh so yéném, Kerisom so tawaindangé méinyotio mbérmbére limánégh ár, si muyé, a wafté, a so tafnamongé limánégh kétán Afé Ngánjánefaf. 25 Kerisom so yalimnde kétánotio Ngánjánam tawaindangé méinyotio mék téfnár a fá tékmangé yáne kafkaf mbanmbanan. 26 Soramé mék téfnár waingéghét siyém kér. 27 Buk si noraye, "Ngánjánam ekmete méinyotio rokar yáne kafkaf mbanmbanan." Mé safénro yéné si kés em ndené, "méinyotio rokar" yau kété yafnyang Ngánján fáyo, emon ekmete méinyotio rokar Kerisoene mbanmbanan. 28 Wénde njam méinyotio rokar té ekmayéng Kerisoene limánégh mbanmbanan, féyo fá fáyo, Ambum, kakmangé Ngánjánene mbanmbanan, emo ekmete méinyotio rokar yáne mbanmbanan, ménát Ngánján so yém méinyotio kénjún.
bt: But the true talk is that Keriso has been raised from death. Keriso is the first man of who have died, and are also raised from death. 21 For just as death came through a man, in the same way the rising from death comes through a man. 22 Just as everyone die in Edèm, in the same way everyone will be raised to life in Keriso. 23 But each one will be raised in same way: Keriso, first, then, at the time of his coming, those who belong to him. 24 Then the end will come, Keriso will overcome all spiritual rulers, authorities, and powers, and will hand over the kingdom to God the Father. 25 Keriso must rule until God defeats all enemies and puts them under his feet. 26 The last enemy to be defeated will be death. 27 The scripture says, “God put all things under his feet.” It is clear that the talks “all things” do not include God himself, who puts all things under Keriso. 28 But when all things have been placed under Keriso's ruling, then he himself, the Son, will place himself under God, who placed all things under him, so that God may be in all.

nama: Némamén wét yéné ár efe ewalitote kér árfet? Némé yéfene méghmégh yém ufroghét? Njam yéné efalo yém, ndené fété ámb norayat, kér ár yau so nanotat ghérsat, némamén yéné ár ewalitotaménd kér árfet? 30 A téfeyot siyém, némamén yéndfem kérté yérsam méinyotio efoghfénefénen? 31 Tane fútaréf, yéndon kér yafángotan méinyotio efghon! Yéné tuktuk tane swém féfene kénjún siyém téfene ghérsé Keriso kénjún, Yesu téfene Yuré, ndernáye yénd mat yényátote. 32 Njam yénd té nasémwam "munjan tékrat wérárafé" yéna Efesos kénjún áfrátaye kétándmé árfene mwighata, némé mer yéndon so tanan? Wénde njam kér ár yau nanotat ghérsat, féyo, ndené si kés yiyota, "Kamyongém njamke a nu nénet, ménát yénd kér so yéném káye."
bt: What about those people who are baptized for the dead? What do they hope to accomplish? If it is true, as some say, dead men are not raised to life, why are those people being baptized for the dead? 30 And as for us, why would we carry the problem every hour? 31 My friends, I face death every day! The pride I have in you is our life in Keriso Yesu our Lord, as we know. 32 If I have fought “wild beasts” here in Ephesus simply from human motives, what have I gained? But if dead men are not raised to life, then, as the saying goes, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we will die.”

nama: Yau nawawérsotati fém: "Mámáfi mérnam ewaghnat mer wafroghyam." 34 Féfenjo mbarkánd mwighét kénangongi a kékmbongi féfenjo yaufita. Yénd norayan yéné ndené fém wáiwái aem ménamén ámb fém mafina em Ngánján. Anoghmén fifi
bt: Do not be fooled. “Bad groups ruin good character.” 34 Come back to your straight minds and stop your sinful ways. I say this to your shame that some of you.p do not know God. The resurrection body

nama: Ámb árom so kémotangé, "Ndernáye kér ár so nanotat ghérsat? Némé sáf fifi so yéfene ewam?" 36 Fém onon! Njam fémon tiang wén mbwe mband kénjún, yau mé so káfyarngé mbém ghérsat, táfandro kér njam tramngongé. 37 A némé fémon tiang siyém mbwe, némé sái kénafyarngé siyém fénataye kun o ámb mbwe. 38 Ngánján nuséne mbwe fifiyafé ndernáye fá ménde yém, a ámbiroámbiro mbweyot yémon eramete fénatayo fifi.
bt: Someone will ask, “How can dead men be raised to life? What kind of body will they have?” 36 You fool! When you.p plant a seed in the ground, it does not sprout to life unless it dies. 37 And what you.p plant tree is a seed, what will be grown other plants like as corn or of other seeds. 38 God provides that seed with the body as he wants, and he gives each seed its own different body.

nama: Méinyotio fifi sem yau ámbiro sáfot em. Árfene sewam ámb sáf fifi, wérárafene fénatayo, menafene fénatayo a wagifafene fénatayo.
bt: All bodies is not all the same kind of body. Human beings have one kind of body, animals different, birds different, and fish different.

nama: A kété em njafarmén fifi a mbandmén fifi. Merafé yam njafarmén fifi sem fénatayo a kétándmé mbandmén fifi sem fénatayo merafé yam. 41 Efogh siyém yánjo merafé yam, ghérare fénatayo merafé yam, a kénd sem fénatayo merafé yam.Kété em fénaté sáfot merafé yam kéndan
bt: And there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The beauty that belongs to heavenly bodies is different from the beauty that belongs to earthly bodies. 41 The sun has its own beauty, the moon different beauty, and the stars a different beauty. Stars are different kinds of beauty.

nama: Ndené so em njam kér ár kanongé ghérsat. Njam fifi eyatotat, táf siyém kérwén, wénde njam kérta kanongé táf sem kérofnar fifi. 43 Njam fifi tiyatongi táf siyém merofnar a njangénnjangén, wénde njam kérta kanongé táf siyém merafé yam a muyafé. 44 Njam fifi tiyatongi mbandan táf siyém mbandmén fifi, wénde njam anoghan yém, táf siyém mbérmbérmén fifi. Kété siyém mbandmén fifi féyo kété wéi so yém mbérmbérmén fifi. 45 Yénémamén Buk si noraye, "Fronde ár Edém, Ngánjánam yafrotaméng ghérsé ár." Wénde soramé Edém siyém ghérsé ramégh Mbérmbér. 46 Mbérmbérmén ár yau fronde yéném, wénde fifimén, a soramé mbérmbérmén ár. 47 Fronde Edém, yafrotaméng mbandmén, yénmormén kétándmé mband maratta, yénamayo Edém yénmormén kétándmé njafarta. 48 Fá efe yéné mband maratmén yém ndenanit ámbiro efe yafrotaméng mband maratta, a fá efe njafarmén ndenanit yém ámbiro efe yénmormén kétán njafarta. 49 Sénonjo ndené fété yénd kés yéném fifi ndenanit mbandmén árnit, féyo yénd ámb káye téfene fifi so em ndené njafarmén ár kés yém.
bt: This is how it will be when dead men are raised to life. When the body is buried, it is mortal, but when raised, it will be immortal. 43 When buried, it is ugly and weak, but when raised, it will be beautiful and strong. 44 When buried in the ground it is a earthly body, but when raised, it will be a spiritual body. There is a earthly body, so there is also a spiritual body. 45 So the scripture says, “The first man, Edèm, God created a living being.” But the last Edèm is a life-giving Spirit. 46 It is not the spiritual that comes first, but the physical, and then the spiritual. 47 The first Edèm, made of soil, came from the earth; the second Edèm came from heaven. 48 Those who belong to the earth are like the one who was made of earth, and those who are of heaven are like the one who came from heaven. 49 Now just as we are body like the earthly man, so we will someday be like the body like heavenly man is.

nama: Tane fútáréf, némé yéndon norayan siyém némé yafrotam fifie a férke yau so tanangé Ngánján limánégh, a némé kérafé fifi yau so tanangé kérofnar yam. 51 Káyárangi yéné kwankwane si: Yénd yau so méinyotio kér yéném, wénde yénd so kafurnangém. 52 Yéné nufrotam wékeye ndené ghékéf nau taumbérto a tasawér. Féyo sufér njam kutnangé, kér ár so nanotat, yaufifi so wéikor kér em, a yénd méinyotio so kafurnangém. 53 Némé kérafé yém so kafurnangé ménát kérofnarot. Némé kérafé yém so kafurnangé kérofnarot. 54 Féyo njam yéné yamam nufrota, kérafé fifi so kafurnangé kétán kérofnare fifiot, táf so Buk si efalo káyátongé ndené: "Kér té yiror, waingégh té yifogh!"
bt: My friends, what I am saying is that what is made by body and blood cannot receive the kingdom of God, and these dying bodies cannot receive what will last forever. 51 Listen this secret talk: We will not all die, but we will all be changed. 52 It will happen quickly at as the blinking of an eye. Then the trumpet will sound, dead men will be raised, never to die again, and we will be changed. 53 What is mortal must be changed into the immortal. What will die must be changed into the without.dying. 54 So when this thing happens, mortal bodies has been changed into the immortal, then the scripture will come true: “Death is destroyed, victory is complete!”

nama: "Kér! Fene waingégh ánde yém? Kér! Fene wafté ánde yém ngiot?"
bt: “Death! Where is your victory? Death! Where is your power to kill?”

nama: Kéram yérse yánjo wafté ngiot kétándmé yaufita, a yaufiyam yérse yánjo wafté kétándmé Ngánjánene nákta. 57 Wénde Ngánjáne kánjo yarametat emo yénmaramet téfe waingégh muyé téfene Yuré Yesu Keriso kénjúmé!
bt: Death carry his power to kill from sin, and sin carry his power from the law of God. 57 But give thanks to God who gives us the victory through our Lord Yesu Keriso.

nama: Yénémaménnde, tane tikfafé fútaréf, kokayongi taikrataye a ndernder. Mia em túmén ásáfoghan Yurai, ménamén fém mat em ndené Yurane kénjún fifiofnar ásáfogh yau so násáfotati.
bt: Therefore, my friends, stand strongly and immovable. Always give yourselves to the work of the Lord, because you.p know that nothing you do in the Lord is not without.meaning.

nama: Sénonjo, yénd so norayan féfe ménamén mani womengét Ngánjánene árét wáutárghét Judia kénjún. Fémofem yafrotati némé yéndon engémndayan Yesuene mérén Galasia kénjún wafroghét. 2 Ámbiroámbrio Sandeyan fémofem so ekmetati téndon ámb mani, kétándmé némé fémofem ewinjotati, a eumendati ménát yau so mani womengégh yangém táf njam wéném féfefaf. 3 Soramé yénd so wéném, yéndon so tangwaraman mat yátogh férén yéfeyot emofem té yiyátongi, a engwatembetan ménát fémofem so engérsati féfenjo ramégh rokar kétán Jerusalemét. 4 Táf mer njam yém tayot álighét, yémafé yénd so kuwanongén. Folene mwighé farorégh
bt: I am going to talk to you.p about your collecting money to help God's people in Judea. You.p must do what I told the Yesu's families(churches) in Galasia to do. 2 Each Sunday you.p must put aside some money, from what you.p have earned, and collect it so that there will no need to collect money when I come. 3 After I come, I shall give letters to know to those you.p have approved, and send them that you will take your gift to Jerusalem. 4 If it seems good for me to go, they can go along with me. Fol's plans

nama: Yéndo so wéném féfefaf soramé yénd so wéngém yénamé Makedonia, yéné soramé yénd so wéngém yénamé Makedonia. 6 Yénd so ngámnjon ámb efogh fémofé o tengwifon yunjéf fémofé, féyo fémofem fété so kwáutánati ménát tane áligh wasamngon, árkémé njam yénd nguwanon. 7 Yénd ménde wém féfe fandaghét mindefifi yau sái mé tangwaindangén. Yénd nemnan tekeyo ámnjoghét fémofé, táf Yuram njam kumyongé.
bt: I shall come to you.p after I have gone through Makedonia, after that I will go through Makedonia. 6 I will spend some time with you.p, or spend the winter, and then you.p can help me to continue my trip, wherever I go. 7 I want to see you.p very more, not only passing you.p. I think to spend a long time with you.p, if the Lord allows.

nama: Yénd so wémor yénan Efesos kénjún kétánotio Fentikos efogh kawerangé. 9 Yéné siyém ndimbal sésafne té nasawér tayot ásáfoghét, a karogh ár wéi sem tárfár em.
bt: I will stay here in Efesos until the day of Fentecost comes. 9 There is a big door has open to me for working, and there are many opponents.

nama: Njam Timoti yéném, fia nemne ndené té wumyongi féfe kénjút, ménamén fá siyém Yurai násáfote, ndené fété yénd swém. 11 Árké árom yau so yékarote fá, wénde fémofem mé ténmáutánangi yáne álighan krufráte, ménát fá so nénangote tafaf. Ménamén yénd nawanjetan yáyot ámb Yesuene efalo yátogh árfé.
bt: If Timoti comes, be think that he makes him welcome among you, because he is working for the Lord, just as I am. 11 No one should despise him, but you.p must help him continue his trip in peace, so that he will come back to me. Because I am waiting for him back with the other truly approving people(believers).

nama: Sénonjo fútar Afolosenemén: yéndo taikratye yawéngsotan fá féfe yafoghét ámb efalo yátogh árfé. Fá méndefnar tamorwén álighét sénonjo, wénde fá so yéngém njam kété yém féneféne. Soramamén si
bt: Now about friend Afolos: I strongly urged him to go to you.p with the other friends. He was quite unwilling to go now, but he will go when he has the opportunity. Final words

nama: Mwighé sauréghkaf aem, kokayongi taikrataye ndimbal mwighé kénjún, miya ndernder ewakai, miya táikér em. 14 Ewafrotati méinyotio féfenjo ásáfogh uwégh kénjún.
bt: Be alert, stand strongly in the big mind(faith), stand not shaking, be strong. 14 Do all your work in love.

nama: Fém mat em Stefanas a yáne mérén. Fá em fronde efalo yátogh ár Akaya yumén a naramayénd Ngánjánene árét ásáfoghét. Yéndon ewamnendan fém, tane fútaréf 16 ménát sái namyotati ndené árfefaf a méinyotio árfefaf efe nufeindat yéné ásáfoghét a násáfotat yénayan.
bt: My friends, you know about Stefanas and his family. They are the first truly approving man(believer) in Akaya region and have given themselves to the work for God's people. I beg you.p, 16 to submit to such people and to every people who joins in this work and work in this.

nama: Yénd áuwéghan wém yéfene wáfareghmén Stefanas, Fotunatus, a Akaikus, ménamén yémofem té ténmafrotat yéné wafrogh féfene yukéra. 18 Yémofem tane mwighé ásneghét yanoyénd a féfene ndenáyo. Fémofem sái tawayamngongi ndené ár.
bt: I am happy about the coming of Stefanas, Fotunatus, and Akaikus, because they have made up for your absence. 18 They have cheered me up a yours also. You must know such men.

nama: Yéné Yesuene mérén Esia yuwan enwawatembangi féfe yéfene áuwégh. Akwila a Frisila a Yesuene mérén árké nafángotat yéfenjo méngon engwawatembangi efalo yátogh árfene áuwégh. 20 Méinyotio fútarém yéné sem engwawatembangi áuwégh. Nafángotati fútarafé ásneghafé.
bt: This Yesu's families(churches) in the province of Esia send you.p their greetings. Aquila and Frisila and the Yesu's family(church) that meets in their house send the greetings of truly approving man(believers). 20 All the friends here send greetings. Meet friends with kissing.

nama: Tanjo ngángaye yéndon yéné yafarotan: Áuwégh Foleta.
bt: With my own hand I write this: Greetings from Fol.

nama: Emon njam yau uwetat Yuré so yém wafalngogh yáne! Marana ta, yáne fifi, téfene Yuré, Kénaf!
bt: Whoever does not love the Lord will be curse on him! Marana ta, its meaning, our Lord, come!

nama: Yuré Yesuene ngárám so yém féfefaf.
bt: The grace of the Lord Yesu will be with you.p.

nama: Tane uwégh so yém féfefaf méinyotio Keriso Yesu kénjún.
bt: My love will be with you.p all in Keriso Yesu.

nama: Yéné férén siyém Foleta, Keriso Yesuene muyafé ár Ngánjánene méndeyan, a kétándmé téfene fútar Timoti, Yéndon yéfarotan kétán Ngánjánene Yesuene mérnot Korint kénjún, a kétán Ngánjánene árfefaf Akaya yuwomé.
bt: This letter is from Fol, a man with Yesu's authority(apostle) of Keriso Yesu by God's will, and from our friendr Timoti. I am writing to the Yesu's family(church) of God in Korint, and to all God's people throughout Akaya region.

nama: So Ngánján téfene Afé a Yuré Yesu Keriso yéramet féfe ngárám a krufrát. Folém yaramete kánjo Ngánjáne
bt: May God our Father and the Lord Yesu Keriso give you.p grace and peace. Fol gives thanks to God

nama: Áwégh Ngánjáneot a Afé Yuré Yesu Kerisoene, ghérghérsisé kér ro Afé, Ngánján ende tambén méinyotio áwéghafé a muyé yéném. 4 Yémon yénmáutáne téfe méinyotio téfene kérté kénjúmé, ménát yéndfem so ewáutánam ámb ár endefene fénatayero kérté ewam, wáutárégh ndenéyameyo yéndfem árké ewanayém kétándmé Ngánjáneta. 5 Ndené fété yénd yéném Kerisoene tárfár tékratan, féyo wéi Keriso kénjúmé yénd yéném Ngánjánene ndimbal wáutáréghan. 6 Njam yénd tékratan yéném, yéné siyém ménát féfe wáutárghét a wanjarghét. Njam yénd yénmáutáne, wéi fém so ewáutáne a muyé so tawaramangé awamaroghét métare ndené sáfotyo tékrat yénd kawamarotam. 7 Féyo téfene méghmégh féfe kénjún yau násambnete, ménamén yénd mat yéném ndené fété fém ewafnyang téfene tékratan, fém wéi ewafnyang téfene wáutáréghan Ngánjáneta.
bt: Praise to God and Father of our Lord Yesu Keriso, the merciful Father and the God of all comfort. 4 He helps us in all our troubles, so that we can help other people who have various troubles with the same help that we have received from God. 5 Just as we are in Keriso's many sufferings, so also through Keriso we are in God's big help. 6 If we are in suffering, it is for your.p help and salvation. If we are helped, you too are helped and given the strength to endure with patience the same sufferings that we endure. 7 So our hope in you.p is never shaken, because we know that just as you.p share in our sufferings, you also share in our help from God.

nama: Fútaréf, yéndfem mat eyátotam fém kértamén yénd sténmorwén Esia yuwan. Yéné kérté téfayongérwén téfefaf tamorwén ndimbalfifi a yénd mwighayan nuyángém téfene ghérsamén.{i{ ...f* {i{1.8 yénd mwighayan nuyángém téfene ghérsamén. need to BT
bt: Friends, we want to know you.p of the trouble we had in the province of Esia. The troubles laid upon us were so big heavy and 9 We thought that we were almost died. But this happened so that we should not rely ourselves, but only on God, who raises dead men. 10 He save us from very bad death, and will save us. We have placed our hope in him that he will save us again. 11 and you.p are continally helping us by means of talking with God for us. It will be answered that the many talking with God for us, and God will bless us and many people will raise their voices to God in thanksgiving for us. Fol's decision again

nama: Yénd nawarsam ndérnáye yénd yénmor téfene ghérsé yéné mband maratan ndené fété téfene mwighayam yufote.Téfene fútaryam fémafé té yafrotam Ngánjánene njúnnjúnofnar a unyéghofnar yamam. Yénd té yafrotam ménaye Ngánjánene ngáráme a yau árfene káfeté mwighaye. 13 Yéndfem yau yéfarotam féfeyot ámb árkéméné fémofem yau so yaftati o mat yiyátotati. A yénd ndené nemnan, 14 ndenéyameyo fém té mat náyátongi ámb, fém so kor mat fifi káyátongi ánde so nawarsati téfenemén ndené yénd nawarsam féfenemén yéné efghot Yuré Yesuene.
bt: We are boasting how we live our life in this world as our mind shows. Our friendships with you have been done by God's holiness and sincerity. We have done by God's grace and not by human wisdom. 13 For we do not write you anything you.p cannot read or understand. And I think that, 14 as you.p have understood us some, you.p will come to understand fully that you.p can boast of us just as we will boast of you.p in the day of the Lord Yesu.

nama: Yénd mwighé taikér kwémorwén méinyotio yénémamén ndené yéndon mwighé yafrotamén fronde ménát féfe yafoghét, ménát fém so frengéghan em sómbio mbinotmé. 16 Yénd ménde kwémorwén féfefaf sái tayafongayénd tane álighan Makedoniat a wéi sái kénangon féfefaf kétamé Makedoniata, a fémofem so kwéngwatembangi yénd kétán Judiat. 17 Yénd yéné mwighé yau yéfarotan amyoghofnar. Yéndon yau tane mwighé yéfarotan nité yamta, ndené fété ámb ár efe mafina em Ngánján. Yéndo yau norayan, 'A,' a féyo yénd norayan tekofnare "Yau'. 18 Ngánján túmén némé fá noraye yémon so yafrote. Yénémamén, tane unyéghofnar si féfefaf yau témorwén "A" a "Yau." 19 Timoti, Sailes a yéndio yéndfem féu táitotawém féfefafmé Yesu Keriso Ngánjánene Ambumoenemén, fá yéné yau fiyém "A" a "Yau." Wénde fá siyém Ngánjánene "A." 20 Méné nderé tárfár unyéghofnar si Ngánjánam ewafrote, fá sem mé "A" yáne fifi siyém ndené Yesu Keriso kénjúmé efe yém téfene "Efalo" yénémamén yénd norayam kétán Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghat. 21 Ngánjánam emo yényátoi méinyotio fém a yénd sái yénmakai taikrataye Keriso kénjún. Yémo yénfagharyéng yénd, 22 té yéntenai yénd, a yényawai yáne Mbérmbér téfene tikéfan kétán féyotaroe némé so yéném.
bt: I was sure of all this that I made plans at first to visit you, so that you,p might be blessed twice. 16 I wanted to visit you on my way to Makedonia and to come back to you.p from Makedonia, and you send me to Judia. 17 I did not decide this carelessly. I do not make my plans from selfish things, like someone who does not know God. I do not say "Yes," and then I immediately say "No." 18 God always does what he says he will do. In the same way, my without.lying talk(promise) to you was not “Yes” and a “No.” 19 Timoti, Sailes and myself we preached among you.p Yesu Keriso, the Son of God, he is not one who is “Yes” and “No.” But he is God's “Yes.” 20 For no matter how many without.lying talk(promise) God has made, they are “Yes,” this is why through Yesu Keriso our “Amen” is said to the big light(glory) of God. 21 God who makes both us and you stand strongly in Keriso. He anointed us, 22 set his seal of ownership on us(note:owner put the seal on the door of yam storage), and put his Spirit in our hearts as the guaranteeing what is to come.

nama: Yéndon yémotan Ngánján ndené fété tane ghékfe fandagh ár. Ngánján mat yém tane tikéf. Yéné téfnan yéndon ewarawenayén fém mwighé yafrotamén yau álighét Korintét. 24 Yéndfem yau ewafaretam fémofem némé so efalo yiyátotati. Yénd mat yéném fém taikrataye ewakai ndimbal mwighayan. Wénde yénd násáfotam fémafé féfene áuwéghét.
bt: I call God as my watching man. God knows my heart. In this reason I spare/guide you that I decided not to go to Korint. 24 We are not trying to order to you what you must truly approve(believe). We know that you stand strongly in the big mind(faith). But we are working with you for your happiness.

nama: Féyo yéndon mwighé yéfarotan yau álighét féfefaf wéikor ménamén áuwéghofnar mété táyátongayén. 2 Yéndon njam eyátotan áuwéghofnar, efe so wiyátote yénd áuwéghafé? Efaloe yau yém yéné ár yéndon efe eyáton áuwéghofnar. 3 Yéné téfnan yéndon yéfarotamén tane férén féfefaf. Yénd yau ménde wém sái wéném féfefaf a táyáton áuwéghofnar ménaye ámb árém sái yénd áuwéghafé kwiyátongi. Yénd mwighé taikér wém ndené njam yénd áuwéghafé wém, féyo fém wéi méinyotio áuwéghan em. 4 Yéndon yéfarotan féfefaf ndimbal kértafé a tikéf ndémkefé a tárfár yengamafé. Tane mwighé yam témorwén yau ménát áuwéghofnar táyátongayén, wénde ménát mat so káyátongayénd nderé minde yéndon fém euwetan méinyotio. wawalindangégh emon néméinde kérta
bt: So I decided not to come to you again to make you without.joy. 2 If I make you without.joy, who will make me joy? Certainly not someone I have grieved? 3 That is why I wrote my letter to you.
