I did not want to come to you and be made without.joy by soem people who should make me joy. I am convinced that when I am joy, then all of you are joy too. 4 I wrote you.p with a big trouble and painful heart and with many tears. My thinking thing was not to make you.p without.joy, but to make you.p know how much I love you.p all. Forgiveness for the Offender

nama: Njam árké árom té kénméinde áuwéghofnarot, yau tafrote tafaf, wénde méinyotio féfefaf. Yénd norayayén yéné ménamén yau ménde wém sái minde nékw wém yáfaf. 6 Yéné kérté yafayoyénd yáfaf tárfár árém féyotaro yéné fyémormén. 7 Féyo sénonjo fémo fiya tawalindangi a yawéngsotati, ménát yau so áuwéghofnar káyátongé a kékmbongé féyotio. 8 A yéndon ewamnendan fém mat tiyátongi ndené féfene uwégh mé yém yáyot. 9 Yéndon yafarotamén féfe yéné férén ménamén yénd ménde wém ménát sái tawinjon nderé mer fém tawakai yéné ufogh yaman a túmén afrengéghkaf em áyárghét tane watamegh sifaf. 10 Njam fémofem tawalindangi ároene yaufiyam némé yémofem té tanwafrotat, yéndon wéi tawalindangen. Njam yéndon tawalindangen, kété njam ámb em walindangégh, yéndon yafrotan Kerisoene sáfrére ménamén féfenemén, 11 ménát yéndfem yau so sifayé yarametam Sáiférefaf. Ménamén yénd mat yéném némé Sáiféroene mwighé a wafrogh yém ndené. Folene mwighé wawérsogh Troasén
bt: If anyone has made somebody without.joy, he has not done it to me, but to all of you. I say this because I do not want to be too much angry on him. 6 The punishment inflicted on him by many people is enogh. 7 So now you should forget him and encourage him, not to make him without.joy and to give up totally. 8 And I beg you.p to make him know that your.p love is still to him. 9 I wrote you.p this letter because I wanted to find out how well you would stand the test and you are always ready to obey my teachings. 10 When you forget man's sin for what they have done, I forget them too. When I wash, if I need to forgive anything, I do it in the sight of Keriso because of you, 11 so that we will not give the space to Sáifér. Because we know what his thinking and action are like. Fol's worry in Troas

nama: Njam yénd néngufaryén Troasét mer si waitoghét Kerisoenemén, yéndon yinjoyén ndené Yuram yésauwéryéng end ménát kété ásáfoghét. 13 Wénde kénjkam mwighé wawérsoghan kwémorwén, ménamén yéndon yau yinjoyén tane fútar Taitus. Féyo yénd norayayén yawo árfefaf kété a néngwanoyén Makedonia yuwot. Waingégh Keriso kénjúmé
bt: When I arrived at Troas to preach the good talk about Keriso, I found that the Lord had opened the way for the work there. 13 But I was deeply worried, because I could not find our friend Taitus. So I said good-bye to the people there and went to Makedonia. Victory through Keriso

nama: Wénde kánjo Ngánjáne, emo túmén yénlimnde yénd waingéghét Keriso kénjún. Ngánjánam yénd yényátote wafaranéghét yáne matyam méinyotio yuwomé ndenanit méndefé wimafé wékwér. 15 Yénd yéném Kerisoene méndefé wimafé wékwér Ngánjanene sáfrére, yémafé efe té ewanjaryéng a efe olingéghkaf tamorwén. 16 Yénd yéném méndefé wimafé wékwér yéfeyot efe olingéghkaf tamorwén a méndefé wimafé ghékér yéfefaf efe té ewanjaryéng. Efe so féyotaro yém yéné ásáfoghét? 17 Yénd yau yéném ndené tárfár ár kés yém, emofem Ngánjánene si yaitotat ménát yéfendio merot. Yénd nafrotam Ngánjanene si efalo yátogh kér ro a ndené fété Ngánjánene ásáfogh ár.
bt: But thanks be to God, who always leads us in victory in Keriso. God uses us to spread his knowedge of him everywhere like a sweet-smellig liquid. 15 We are Keriso's sweet smelling liquid before God, which spreads among those who are being saved and those who are being lost. 16 We are an sweet smelling liquid to whom are being lost and an sweet smelling liquid to whom are being saved. Who is equal to this work? 17 We are not like many people, who preach the talk of God for their benefit. We preach the word of God with sincerity and as without.payment working men(servants) of God.

nama: Yéné kém ndené yém yénd kar nawarmbotam téfenjomén? Fété nérete ndenáye ámb árnit, yénd ménde yéném yátogh férén féfefaf o féfeta? Yau. 2 Fém fémyo em téfene férén, té efarotamém téfene tikfan ménát méinyotio ár matot a wafghét. 3 Yéné siyém mé safénro ndené Kerisom yémonyo yéfarotam yéné férén a yatambangém yéndfemyo. Yéné férén siyém, yau kayogh wékwre wénde akegh Ngánjánene Mbérmbére, yau wím tukén wénde árfene tikfan.
bt: Does this sound as if we were again boasting about ourselves? Could it be like some other people, we need approving letters to you.p or from you.p? No. 2 You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts for every people to know and read. 3 It is clear that Keriso himself wrote this letter and sent it by us. This letter is, not with acid for writing(ink) but with the Spirit of the living God, and not on stone but on hearts.

nama: Yénd norayam ménamén sotafé yéném Ngánján kénjún Keriso kénjúmé. 5 Téfe kénjún némndafi yau em amyoghét féyotaro yéném wafroghét yéné ásáfoghét. Yéné wafté téfefaf siéném kétándmé mé Ngánjáneta. 6 Fá yém emo téfe yénaramayéng ásáfoghét totér árnyéghmén, árké yém Ngánján Mbérmbére, yau yém faroghe náke ménamén faroghe nákam yénérse kér, wénde Ngánján Mbérmbéram yénmaramete ghérsé.
bt: We say because we are strong(confindent) in God through Keriso. 5 There is nothing in us that allows us that we are capable of doing this work. The power we have comes only from God. 6 He is who made us to work for the new covenant, which is by God Spirit, not by a written law because the written law brings death, but God Spirit gives life.

nama: Yéné nák témorwén faroghkaf férén wíman, a Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghé nufaryéng njam yéramayéng. Ndemamén Mosesene sáfan kwayaghé kolindau, minde muyafé Israil ár féyotarofnar tamorwén ghékfe fandaghét yáfaf. Njam nákam, árkaménáyam yénérse kér yém wafareghan, yénmormén ndimbal kwayaghafé, 8 nderé ndimbal kwayaghé yém Ngánján Mbérmbéroene ásáfoghfaf! 9 Njam yéné ásáfogham árké yénérse kértafé témorwén ndimbal kwayaghé, nderé ndimbal kwayaghé ásáfogh yém árkéménayam yénérse mbarkánd yam! 10 Némé témorwén ndimbal kwayaghé sénonjo yau ndimbal kwayaghé yém njam yétémndam totér ndimbal kwayaghafé. 11 A njam té témorwén ndimbal kwayaghé árké yémormén tekofnaye, nderé ndimbal kwayaghé yém árkéméné nakete tuot!
bt: The law was carved letters on stone, and God's big light(glory) appeared when it was given. Even though the light on Moses' face was fading, it was so strong that Israil people could not watch with eyes to him. If the law, which brings death by force, came with big light(glory), 8 how the big light(glory) is to the work of God Spirit? 9 If this work that brought problem(condemnation) have big light(was glorious), how big light(glorious) is the work that brings straightness! 10 What was big light(glorious) has no big light(glory) now when we compare with new big light(glory). 11 And if there were big light(glory) which are without.long, how big light(glory) is which reamins forver!

nama: Ménamén téfene yém yéné méghmégh, yénd siéném mbénmbénate norayam. 13 Yénd yau yéném ndené Moses, emon so tafarngai sálámeye yáne sáf ménát Israil árém yau so tefandawét kwayaghé kawaindau a kolindau. 14 Yéfene mwighé ewam déudéu, sénonjo tawétio yéné efghon, yéfene mwighé sem wafaréghkaf yéné sálámeyeyo yémofem njam yaftat tándáwár árnyégh buk. Yéné sáláme so kitérmbongé ménáyero efalo yátoghe Keriso. 15 Ndené sénonjo, njam yémofem yaftat Mosesene nák, sálámeyam mé ewafne yéfene mwighé. 16 Wénde njam árké ár kawengongé Yurafaf, yéné sáláme táf kafnarngé. 17 Yuré siyém Mbérmbér, a ánde yém Yurane Mbérmbér, kété yém yuwek. 18 Méinyotio yénd, efe yéném so sáf awafaréghofnar yélalam Yurane ndimbal kwayaghé, a so ofnamoghe yáné sáffaf armboghanro ndimbal kwayághafé, árké yéném Yurata, efe yém Mbérmbér.
bt: Because we have this hope, we are very bold in our speech. 13 We are not like Moses, who had to put a cloth over his face so that the people of Israil would not see the light passed away and disappeared. 14 Their minds were doubt, and to this very day, their minds are covered with the same cloth when they read the old covenant. The cloth is removed only by believing in Keriso. 15 Even today, whenever they read the law of Moses, the cloth still covers their minds. 16 But whenever anyone turns to the Lord, this cloth is taken away. 17 The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom. 18 And we all, who with unveiled faces contemplate the Lordʼs big light(glory), are being transformed into his image with ever-increasing big light(glory), which comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.

nama: Ngánjánam té yénmaramai ásáfogh yánjo ghérghérsisé kénjúmé wafroghét, féyo yénd yau so nawewonam. 2 Yéndfem ekmayém téndot méinyotio kwankwane a wáiwáiafé wafroghyam. Yéndfem yau kawaindam, o yau Ngánjánene si unyéghe táitotam. Efalo si kwayaghé kénjún yénd yénmor Ngánjanene ghékfan a narametam yéndio méinyotio árfene mer mwighat. 3 Njam mer si yéndfem árké táitotam ténoghan yém, ténoghe yém mé yéfeta efe olinghéghkaf em. 4 Sáifér, efe yém yéné mbandmén yaufi ngánjánam, mwighé kwéfkwéf eyátotate efalo yátoghofnar árfene, ménát yémofem yau so yéfandat mer sioene kwayaghé árké yéramete Kerisoene ndimbal kwayaghé, efe yém Ngánjánene mbanmban. 5 Yénd yau nafrotam téfenjomén, wénde yénd nafrotam Yesu Kerisoenemén ndené fété Yuré, a yéndio ndené fété féfene faiofnar ásáfogh ár Yesuene téfnan. 6 Ngánján, emo emndai, "Kwéfitata kwayaghé so nálálat, " yéné féyém yénayo Ngánján emon yafrote yánjo kwayaghé nálálat téfene tikéfan, ménát sái yénnérse téfe matyam Ngánjanene ndimbal kwayaghé nálálat Kerisoene sáfan.
bt: God has given us this work to do in his mercy, and so we do not become discouraged. 2 We put aside all secret and shameful deeds. We do not deceit, nor do we preach falsefy the talk of God. In the full light of truth we live in God's sight and try to commend ourselves to every people's good mind. 3 If the good talk we preach is hidden, it is hidden only from those who are being lost. 4 Sáifér, who is the god of this age has blinded the minds of people who do not truly approve, so that they cannot see the light of the good talk that displays the big light(glory) of Keriso, who is the image of God. 5 We don't preach about ourselves, but we preach Yesu Keriso as Lord, and ourselves as your without.payment working men(servants) for Yesu' sake. 6 The God, who said, “Out of darkness the light shall shine!” is the same God who made his light shine in our hearts, to bring us the knowledge of God's big light(glory) shining in the face of Keriso.

nama: Wénde yénd efe yéném yéné mbérmbére ngonjél ngénd sinan, ménát so nauyafote ndené yéné minde tukén wafté siyém Ngánjánefaf yém, yau téfefaf yém. 8 Yénd túmén kértayan yéném, wénde yau kirorngém. Ámb káye mwighé déudéu yéném, wénde yau wirorghé. 9 Kété em tárfár mék téfnár, wénde yénd yau fútarofnaye yéném, a mámáfi yéném ámb efghon, wénde yénd yau wasérmboghkaf yéném. 10 Méinyotio efghon yéndfem yérsam téfenjo fifie Yesuene kér, ménát ndené Yesuene ghérsé so yéfandat téfene fifi kénjún. 11 Téfene ghérsé sem yénd túmén kéramé yényote Yesuene téfnan, ménát ndené Yesuene ghérsé so yéfandat téfene kérmén fifiyan. 12 Yéné fifi ndené yém kér siyém ásáfoghan yém téfefaf, wénde ghérsé siyém ásáfoghan yém féfefaf.
bt: But we who have this spiritual treasure in clay pots, in order to show that the very big power belongs to God, not to us. 8 We are often in trouble, but not crushed. Sometimes we are in doubt, but are not never collapsed. 9 There are many enemies, but we are never without a friend, and we become bad at times, but we are not destroyed. 10 In all days we carry in our mortal bodies the death of Yesu, so that Yesu's life also may be seen in our bodies. 11 Our lives are we are always thorugh death for Yesu' sake, so that Yesu's life may be seen in our mortal body. 12 This means that death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.

nama: Buk si noraye, "Yénd norayan ménamén yéndo efalo yiyátotan." Ndené ndimbal mwighé mbérmbér kénjúmayo yénd ndenéyameyo norayam ménamén yéndfem efalo yiyátotam. 14 Yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánján, emo yanoyéng Yuré Yesu kérta ghérsat, so ndenéyameyo ténmanongé yénd Yesufé a téngmangéngé yénd mérne fémafé yánjo kénjút. 15 Yéné méinyotio siyém féfene téfnan, a ndené fété Ngánjánene ngárámam so engwarafne tárfár ár, yémofem so erametat kétán ndimbal kwayaghé Ngánjánefaf tárfár kánjo ramégh orayégh. Ámnjogh ndimbal mwighaye
bt: The scripture says, “I spoke because I truly approved(believed).” In the same spirit of big mind(faith) we also speak because we truly approve(believe). 14 We know that God, who raised the Lord Yesu to life, will also raise us up with Yesu and take us together with you into him. 15 All this is for your sake, and as God's grace reaches many people, they will offer to the big light(glory) of God many talking with giving thanks. Living by faith

nama: Yéné téfnan yénd yaufifi kuyangém mwighayan. Ménamén téfene fifi muyafnar néngayatote, wénde mbérmbére kénjún ár siyém túmén wéikor totér yiyátote. 17 A yéné némbne watmé kértayan yénd snawambetam so yénérse ndimbal a tuot ndimbal kwayaghé. Yéné yam ndené yénd so ewanangém ewaindat méinyotio yéné kérté. 18 Féyo yéndfem ghékéf eurawetam yau némé efandam, wénde némé yau efandam ménamén némé efandam tekofnar em, wénde némé yau efandam naketat tuot.
bt: For this reason we never deiscourage. Even though our body become without.power, but our spiritual inside man always become new. 17 And this small and temporary trouble we suffer will bring us a big and eternal big light(glory). The good things we will receive surpass all of these trouble. 18 So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, because what we see are temporary, but what we cannot see last forever.

nama: Yénd mat yéném ndené njam yéné farai méngo yénd syénmor téfenjo fifie yéné mband maratan nirona, Ngánjánefaf méngo em njafaran téfeyot ámnjoghét, méngo yémonyo árké ewafrotam, árkéméné so naketat tuot. 2 Féyo yéné téfnan yénd ndémkefé yéném, yénémamén téfene ménde ndimbal yém ménát téfene méngo árké yém njafaran sái kafayongé téfefaf. 3 Sáláme wafarégh téfe so yém ménát yau so ténmingé firo. 4 Njamandio yénd yénmor yéné mbandmén farai méngo kénjún, yéndfem yafrotam kénjkam ke ndémkeyan máméghéraye, yau ndené yém yéndfem sái tawitérmbongém téfene mbandmén fifi, wénde ndené yénd ménde yéném yéné njafarmén fifi sái kafayongé téfefaf, ménát ndené némé mbandmén fifi yém so tengongé ghérsayam. 5 Ngánjánro yém emo yénd yénfrende yéné awengoghét, a yémon yénmaramayéng téfe yáne Mbérmbér amnyoghét kétán némé so yéném.
bt: We know that when this canvas house(tent) we live in our body here on earth is destroyed, there are house in God in heaven for us to live, a home he himself has made, which will last forever. 2 So in this reason we are painful, so big is our desire that our home which is in heaven should be put on over us. 3 We are wearing cloth will be that we shall not be naked. 4 While we live in this earthly tent, we made deep painful sound with oppression, it is not that we are going to get rid of our earthly body, but that we want to have the heavenly body put on over us, so that what is earthly body will be changed by life. 5 Only God is the one who has prepared us for this change, and he gave us his Spirit as guaranteeing what is to come.

nama: Féyo yénd túmén yéném wawéngsoghan remngoghafé. Yénd mat yéném ndené nderé teke yénd yéna yénmorang méngon fifie yénd ukér yéném Yurane méngota. 7 Efaloe, yénd yéna yénmor ndimbal mwighayero, yau ménaye ghékéf fandaghe. 8 Yénd yéném wawéngsoghan remngoghafé, a nemnam sái nguwanongém fifita a yéném méngon Yurafé. 9 Féyo mété yénd yéném yéna fifie o yukér yéném yéné fifie, yéndfem áuwéghét so yéméindam fá. 10 Yénd so méinyotio yénmakai Kerisoene moghan yáne sáfrére, ámbiroámbiroam so ewinjote némé yémo tawafrote, mer o mámáfi, yéné fifie mband maratan. Ngánjánafé fútar Keriso kénjúmé
bt: So we are always full of courage. We know that as long as we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord's home. 7 Truly, we live here by big mind(faith), not by sight. 8 We are full of courage, and would think of being away from the body and will be at home with the Lord. 9 So whether we are here in the body or are away from this body, we make him please. 10 All of us must stand the judgment of Keriso before him, [so that] each of us may receive what he did, good or bad in this earthly body. Friendship with God through Keriso

nama: Yénd mat yéném fifi némé yém Yuré férfér, a mwighat eufanam ámb ár. Ngánján mat yém téfenemén ndené efe yénd fifi yéném, a yénd nemnan ndené fém mat em féfenjo tikéf kénjún tanemén safénro. 12 Yénd yau narametam yéndio wéikor féfefaf, wénde yéndfem ámb mer téfén ewarametam féfeyot ménát fém so em áuwéghan téfefaf, ménát fémofem so ewawangotati yéfeyot endefene áuwégh yém némé ghékfe yéfande, yau yéné némé tikéf kénjún em. 13 Táf yénd njam onon yéném, yéné siyém mé Ngánjanene téfnanro. O táf yénd mbarkánd mwighayan njam yéném, mé féfene téfnanro. 14 Kerisoene uwégham yénd yénlimnde, féyo yénd mat yéném ndené ámbiro ár kér yémormén méinyotio árét, yéné fifi ndené yém méinyotio ár kér emormén. 15 Fá kér yémormén méinyotio árét, ménát fá efe emor yau so emor yéfendio, wénde yáyot efe kér yémormén a yénwanoi kérta yéfene téfnan.
bt: We know what it means to fear the Lord, and so we try to persuade other people. God knows us who we are, and I think that you know in your.p hearts me clearly. 12 We are not trying again to recommend ourselves to you, but we are trying to give you a good reason to please us, so that you.p will be able to answer those who please what he see, not about what are inside. 13 If we are crazy, it is only for God. Or if we are in our straight mind, it is only for you.p. 14 Kerisoʼs love leads us, so we knew that one man died for everyone, this means everyone died. 15 He died for everyone, so that those who live should no longer live for themselves, but for him who died and was raised from dead for their sake.

nama: Féyo yénandmé sénonjo yéndfem efe yau efalo eyatotam mband matyame. Táf yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Keriso yéné ende, yéndfem árké yau mé ewafrotam. 17 Njam árké ár kufnangé Kerisoefaf fá so yém totér ár. Tándáwár ár kitérmbongé a totér ár kamndangé. 18 Yéné méinyotio wafroghyam siyém Ngánjánene, emo Keriso kénjúmé yénd yénmengote kétándmé mék téfnárta yánjo fútarot a yémo yénmaramayéng ásáfogh ámb ár sái ewangotam yáne fútarot ndenáyo. 19 Téfene mer si ndené yém Ngánjánam táyátotawéng méinyotio ár yáne fútarot Keriso kénjúmé. Ngánjánam yau ewawerayéng yéfene yaufi, a yémon té yénmaramayéng téfe mer si árké yénmaufrote ndernáye yémon táyátongé fá yáne fútarot.
bt: So from now on we do not trust by worldly knowledges. If we trust Keriso in this way, we do not do so anymore. 17 If anyone who is joined to Keriso is a new man. The old man is disappeared and the become new man. 18 All this is done by God, who through Keriso changed us from enemies into his friends and gave us the work of making other people his friends also. 19 Our good talk is that God was making all people his friends through Keriso. God did not keep an account of their sins, and he has given us good talk which tells us how he makes them his friends.

nama: Féyo yénd norayam Kerisoe ndené fété Ngánjánam yéramete si téfe kénjúmé. Yénd noumnendam Kerisoene téfnan, tumyo Ngánján fém so kémengongé kétándmé mék téfnárta kétán yáne fútarot! 21 Keriso témorwén yaufiofnar, wénde mé téfe téfnan Ngánjánam yiyátoyéng fá téfene yaufi ménát fété yénd mérne yéném yémafé ménát yéndfem so yafalotam Ngánjánene mbarkánd yam.
bt: So we are speaking for Keriso, as God himself were making talk through us. We beg on Keriso's behalf, let God change you from enemies into his friends! 21 Keriso was without sin, but for our sake God made him share our sin so that are together with him that we might share the straightness of God.

nama: Féyo, téfene ásáfogh kénjún mérne Ngánjánafé, yéndfem ewamnendam fém emofem té yanayénd Ngánjánene ngárám yau ménát sái nawaghna. 2 Káyárangi némé Ngánján noraye: "Njam kwéf nawerai tayot uyafoghét tane ménde, yéndon eyárayén fém. Njam efogh yéném tayot féfe wanjarghét, yéndon té ewáutánamén féfe." Káyárangi! Yéné fiyém efogh Ngánjánene ménde umyoghét. Sénonjo fiyém efogh wanjarghét.
bt: So, in our work together with God, we beg you.p who have received God's grace not to let it be wasted. 2 Hear what God says: “When the time came for me to show you favor, I heard you. When the day came for me to save you, I helped you.” Listen! This is the time to receive God's favor. Today is the day to be saved!

nama: Yénd yau ménde yéném ámb árém sái yéfandat téfén téfene ásáfoghan, ménát téfene ásáfogh yau so yém nétér ámb ároene endén. 4 Wéndende méinyotio rokar kénjún yéndfem so yuyafotam ndené yénd yéném Ngánjanene ásáfogh ár ménaye métare eumyogh kérté, táikér yam, a mámé ndémkefé yam. 5 Yénd té ténsmetawét, kwéfkwéf méngon ténlawangayénd a ténéfmotawét; yénd ásáfoghanro ténmorwén a kuwanongayém kéméghofnaye o njamkefnaye. 6 A njúnnjúnofnar yam, matyam, métar, a meryam yénd té kauyafotam yéndio ndené fété Ngánjánene ásáfogh ár Ngánján Mbérmbére a ménaye téfene efalo uwégh. 7 Yénd yafrote efalo si afroghe. Ngánjánene waftayam násáfote téfene kénjún. Téfene fimb siyém mbarkánd yam, ménát ásméghét a téfendio awamaroghét. 8 Yénd yénmutemndat a wáiwáiot yénkametat, Yénd yénmasalitat a yénmuyotat. Yénd yénfandat ndené unyégh ár kar yéném, wénde efalo si yaitotam. 9 Méinyotio mat em téfenemén wénde yénfandat ndené mafina kar em, ndené kar kér yénmormén, wénde yénd mé ghérsé yéném. Ndemamén kértayan yénkmetat, wénde yau ténsmetat krot. 10 Ndemamén ghérghérsisat yénkmetat, wénde yénd túmén náuwetam. Yénd yauyaunit yéném, wénde yéndfem eyátotam tárfár ár wemár. Yénd rokarofnarnit yéném, wénde méinyotio rokar ewinjotam.
bt: We do not want anyone to find fault in our work, so that our work will not put obstacles in anyone's way. 4 Instead, in everything we show that we are God's without.payment working men(servants) by patiently enduring troubles, hardships, and difficulties. 5 We have been beaten, darkness houseed, and mobbed; we have been overworked and have gone without sleep or food. 6 By our without.dust way, knowledge, patience, and kindness we have shown ourselves as God's without.payment working men(servants) by the God Spirit, by our true love. 7 We faithfully preach the true talk. God's power is working in us. Our weapon is rightouesness, to fight and to defend ourselves. 8 We are honored and disgraced. We are insulted and praised. We are seen as liars, but we speak the truth. 9 All know about us but they see us like as they don't know, like as we were dead, but we still live on. Although they put us pain, but they could not kill. 10 Although they put us sadness, but we are always glad. We are like be poor, but we make many people rich. We are like without.thing, but we really possess everything.

nama: Téfene fútaréf Korint kénjún! Yénd norayam efalo siro féfefaf! Téfene tikéf té esawerngém marat féfeyot. 12 Yénd yau yénéf yéném emofem téfene tikéf emnam féfeyot, wénde fém yéné féyém emofem tikéf emnat téfeyot. 13 Yénd norayan sénonjo ndené fété fém em tane ambumtértér. Téfe ténmauyafongi ndenáyo emarégh téfene siyém féfeyot. Tasawérngi féfene tikéf marat! Yénd siéném akegh Ngánjánene wáwefnogh méngo
bt: Our friends in Korint! We have spoken only true talk to you.p. We have opened our hearts wide to you. 12 It is not we who have closed our hearts to you.p, but it is you.p who have closed your hearts to us. 13 I speak now as though you.p were my children. Show us the same feelings that we have for you. Open your hearts wide! We are the worshipping house of the living God

nama: Yau kaféghangi ásáfoghét efalo yátoghofnar árfé, yau so nufrote. Ndernáye so mbarkánd a wafarsogh so awarnyéghan ere? Ndernáye so kwayaghé a kwéfité ere ámbiro? 15 Ndernáye so Keriso a Sáifér mwighé tafghend? Némé so efalo yátogh árom a efalo yátoghofnar árfé ewafrot? 16 Ndernáye wét Ngánján wáwefnogh méngo káyátend mewangafé? Ménamén yénd siéném wáwefnogh méngo akegh Ngánjánene! Ndené fété Ngánján fáyo kés norayai, "Yéndon so erametan tane méngo tane árfefaf a wémor yéfe kénjún. Yénd so kamndan yéfene Ngánján, a fá so kamndangi tane ár."
bt: Do not work together with truly without.approving people, it cannot be done. How can straight and wrong be in partnership? How can light and darkness are one? 15 How can Keriso and the Sáifèr agree? What does a truly approving man(believer) work together with a truly approving man? 16 How can God's worshipping house stand together with idols? Because we are the worshipping house of the living God! As God himself has said, “I will make my home with my people and live among them. I will be their God, and they shall be my people.”

nama: A féyo Yuré Méinyotio Wafté Afoghare noraye, "Fémofem so tafrangongi fá a kafalongi fémyo kétándmé yéfe tambén. Yau eyanjati némé em mámáfi, a yéndon so taumyon fém.
bt: And so the Lord All Mighty says, “You must leave them and separate yourselves from them. Have nothing to do with what is bad, and I will accept you.p.

nama: Yénd so wém Féferafé, a fém so kamndangi tane ambum a mérésmérés."
bt: I will be a your Father, and you.p will be my sons and daughters.”

nama: Tane tikfafé fútaréf, ménamén yéné unyéghofnar si eramai téfefaf, yéndio nawainam kétándmé méinyotio yam némé ewafrotam fifi a mbérmbér njúnnjúnafé, a so yéném njúnnjúnofnar ménáyan sái yénmor Ngánján férfér kénjún. Folene áuwégh
bt: My dear friends, because these without.lying talks(promises) have been given us, let us wash ourselves from everything that makes body or soul with.dust, and let us be completely without.dust(holy) by living in fear of God. Folʼs Joy

nama: Wafrogh ámb sifayé téfe féfene tikéf kénjún. Yéndfem yau taghnam ámb ár, yau tasérmbotam ámb ár, o yau kemnam mer résaghét ámb ároeta, 3 Yénd yau norayan yéné féfe waghnaghét. Yénd té néngorayangén fronde ndené fém sem téfene tikfan, a yénd téu yénmor o kér yéném mérne fémafé. 4 Yénd swém féyotaro wém féfenemén. Yéndon yérsan ndimbal tuktuk féfenemén! Méinyotio téfene kérté kénjún, yénd mé wém áuwéghafé a yénd nérsan áuwéghafé.
bt: Make room for us in your hearts. We have wronged no one, we have corrupted no one, nor tried to take advantage of anyone. 3 I do not say this to condemn you. I have said before that you are in our hearts, and we would live or die with you. 4 I am so sure of you. I take such pride in you! In all our troubles, I am still full of happiness and I am running over with joy.

nama: Njam nufaryém Makedonia, yéndfem yau ewinjoyém enagh. Yénd ténmorwén kértayan méinyotio sifayamé, kété tamorwén amrorégh ámb árfé, a férfér téfene tikfan. 6 Wénde Ngánján, emo yénmamarotayéng njam tikfan nuyáyém yuwot, yénmamarotai yénd ménaye Taitusene ufarghe, 7 yau yáne ufarghero wénde yáne afrogh ndernáye yamarotayénd fá. Yémon yénmaufroi téfe ndernáye kawanjetawét ta fandaghét, nderé ghérghérsisé fém tamorwén, a nderé mere yénd fémofem wáutánaménd; yénémamén wéméindayéng yénd minde áuwéghét.
bt: When we arrived in Makedonia, we had no rest. We have troubles everywhere, there are quarrels with other people, fears in our hearts. 6 But God, who comforts when we are downhearted, comforted us by the coming of Taitus, 7 not only by his coming but also his report of how you encouraged him. He told us how much you.p wait for seeing me, how sorry you have, how good you.p help me, so I am so happy.

nama: Táf ndené tane férnam taméinde fém áuwéghofnarot, yénd yau ghérghérsisé wém tané wafaroghmén. Yénd so ghérghérsisé kwémorwén yénémamén njam yéndon tinjongayén ndené yéné férnam eméinde fém áuwéghofnarot mé némbne efoghfénefénetro. 9 Wénde sénonjo yénd áuwéghan wém, yau ménamén yéndon taméindan áuwéghofnarot, wénde ménamén féfene áuwéghofnar yamam eméinda fém féfene yam wengoghét. Yéné áuwéghofnar yam yafrotam Ngánjánam, a féyo yéndfem yau yéfunayém féfefaf tékrat. 10 Yéné áuwéghofnar yam Ngánjánam yafrotam yéné ménaye yénérsam awengogh tikfot árké ménáyam yénlimnde wanjarghét, a kété ámb kenagh yau yém! Wénde mband yamoene ghérghérsisayam yéfune kér. 11 Téfandangi némé áuwéghofnar yaman Ngánjánam yafrotam téfune féfefaf. Nderé minde fém té eyátoi, nderé sotafé mwighayan fém eyátoi féfene téfnofnar! Nderé nékw yaufiyamfaf, nderé sélngwél yam, nderé emarégh, nderé aramégh, némé afrengégh yém ménát tékrat kéméghét yaufi yafrogh yam! Fém té kauyafotati fémyo ndené njúnjúnofnar ár em méinyotio yaman.
bt: For even if my letter made you without.joy, I am not sorry about my writing. I could have been sorry when I saw that this letter made you.p without.joy for a while. 9 But now I am happy, not because I made you.p without.joy, but because your withoout.joy thing made you.p change your.p ways. That without.joy thing was worked by God, and so we do not caused you.p problem. 10 This without.joy thing that is worked by God brings a change of heart that leads us to salvation, and there is no regret in that! But worldly sadness causes death. 11 See what God did with without.joy thing of yours. How earnest it has made you.p, how strong mind to make you innocence! Such anger to sin, such surprising thing, such feelings, such devotion, such readiness to punish wrongdoing! You.p have shown yourselves to be without.dust in all things.

nama: Féyo yéndon yéfarotamén yéné férén féfe, yéné yau témorwén yéné téfnan emo tafrote mámáfi o emo yafrotam mámáfi. Wénde yéndon efarotamén safén yékmayén féfe, Ngánjánene sáfrére, ndernáye kénjkam féfene aramégh yém téfeyot fifi. 13 Yéné téfnan yénd yénmawéngsotayéng.
bt: So I wrote that letter to you.p, it was not because of the one who did wrong or the one who was wronged. Instead, I wrote it to make clear to you.p in God's sight, how deep your.p devotion to us really is. 13 That is why we were encouraged.

nama: Yénd tuktukan wém féfenemén yáfaf, a fémofem yau yénd áuwéghofnar wiyátoyénd. Wénde ndené fété méinyotio yammén yénd túmén kénorayan féfefaf siyém efalo, tane tuktuk féfenemén Taitusefaf siyém efalo náyáto. 15 A yáne uwégh féfe kanotawéng muyafé njam yémo tawafarotau ndenáye fém afrengéghkaf tamorwén yáne wawanjarégh si wafroghét, ndernáye fémofem yumyoyénd fá férférafé a mwighé uwérsoghafé. 16 Yénd áuwéghan wém ménamén yénd efalo tiyátotan féfe fémofem sái yafrotati némé mbarkand yém.
bt: I am in boasting of you.p to him, and you.p have not made me without.joy. But just as everything we always said to you was true, our boasting about you.p to Titus has proved to be true. 15 And his love for you.p grow strong when he remembers how you.p were ready to do his encouring talk, how you.p received him with fear and trembling. 16 I am happy because I truly approve you.p to do what is straight.

nama: Téfene fútaréf, yénd ménde yéném fém sái kamndangi némé Ngánjánene ngárámam té tafrote Makedonia Yesuene mérnamé. 2 Fá té taufotat tárfár kérté yamam fá yéné kénjúmé tayota, wénde yéfene áuwégh mé tamorwén minde ndimbalfifi fá tamorwén tukén marataye yéfene ramégh kénjún, ndemamén fá tamorwén minde yauyau. 3 Yéndon mat eyátotan ndené yémofem eramayénd minde tukén yéfene mwighé féyotaroyan, a minde tukén yafrotaménd raméghan. Yéfenjo méndeye tarametawét. 4 Fá komnendawét téfefaf ménjtaye ménát wáutárghét Ngánjánene árét Judia kénjún. 5 Yéné témorwén minde tukén ndené yénd némat kemnawém! Fronde fá naramayénd fáyo Yurafaf, a féyo Ngánjánene méndeyan fá ndenéyameyo naramayénd fáyo téfefaf. 6 Féyo yéndfem yamnendayém Taitus, emo yérnayéng yéné ásáfogh, so tasamngongai a ewáutána fém wifoghét yéné uwégh ásáfoghan. 7 Fém em mámé wemár méinyotio rokar kénjún némé féfene ewam. Ndimbal mwighé kénjún, si waitoghan, a matyaman, féfene ménde wáutárghét a féfene uwégh téfeyot. A yénd ménde yéném fém sái em marataye wéi yéné uwégh ásáfoghan.
bt: Our friends, we want you.p to know what God's grace has done in the Yesu's families(churches) in Makedonia. 2 They have been severely tested by many troubles they went through, but their joy was so big that they were extremely generous in their gift, even though they are very poor. 3 I know that they gave as much as they could, and even more than they could give. They gave them their own will. 4 They begged us repeatly helping God's people in Judia. 5 This was more than what we have thought! Firstly they gave themselves to the Lord, and then by God's will they also gave themselves to us. 6 So we urged Titus, who began this work, to continue it and help you.p complete this work of love. 7 You.p are very rich in all you have. In big mind(faith), in speaking, and in knowledge, in your.p willingness to help and in your.p love for us. And we want you.p to be generous also in this work of love.

nama: Yéndo yau ámb nák ekmetan. Wénde yéndon ewauyafotan nderé ménde em ámb efalo yátogh ár sái wárametawét yéfene mani. Yéné ende, yéndon ménde wém yéronjan féfene uwégh fifi némé yém. 9 Fém mat té em téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisoene ngárám. Nderé wemár fá témorwén, wénde fá nénamndai yauyau féfe téfnan, ménát fém sái téméindangé wemár yáne yauyaue.
bt: I am not laying down any rules. But I show how eager other truly approving people(believer) want to give their money. In that way, I want to see what your.p love is real. 9 You.p know the grace of our Lord Yesu Keriso. How rich as he was, but he made himself poor for your sake, in order to make you.p rich by means of his poverty.

nama: Yéndon so tawaraman tanjo mwighé yéné yammén némé yém mer féfeyot. Kétándmé yunjfan fém tamormén fronde ménde wáutárghét a yafrotaménd. 11 Sénonjo fémofem táf so tifongi némé fémofem té yérnyayénd a fém wéi kemnawét wáutárghét, féyo sénonjo yéfandam a tifongi yéné ásáfogh ndené fété fémofem mwighé kés téfaronati a tafngongi yénaye némé té ewam sénonjo. 12 Táf fém njam ménde em ramghét, Ngánjánam so taumyongé féfene ramégh yénayan féfene némé ewam ramghét, yau yénayan némé yau ewam.
bt: I give my thought about what is best for you in this matter. Last year you.p were the first who want to help and you.p did. 11 Now you.p should finish what you started and you also want to help, so now we see and you finish this work like as you decided and started it by what now have. 12 If you.p want to give, God will accept your.p gift on the basis of what you have to give, not on what you don't have.

nama: Téfene ménde yau ndenáye yém ámb ár sái em meran a fém so yérsat ndimbal kérté, wénde so em ámbrérero. 14 Yéné efghon njam fém rokarot néronjati a yéfene ewam tárfár, yémofem so ewáutánat fém. Yéné ende sómbio so nawáutárét ámbrérero. 15 Ndené fété Buk si noraye, "Ár emo eumendam tárfár yau tamorwén tárfárfifi, a ár emo eumendam némbnar yau tamorwén némbnarfifi." Taitus a yáne fútaréf
bt: Our desire is not that other people might be good and you must carry great heavies, but there might be equality. 14 At this time when you.p are in need and they have plenty, they will help you.p. In this way both will help equally. 15 As the scripture says, “The one who gathered muny did not have too many, and the one who gathered little did not have too little.” Taitus and his friends

nama: Yéndfem kánjo yarametam Ngánjáne emo Taituse yélawai tikéfan yéné mwighéyo árké mwighé témorwén tafaf féfe wáutárghét! 17 Yau yénaro yémo yumyoi téfene ómnengégh; fá ménde témorwén wáutárghét, fá kemnau álighét féfefaf yánjo méndeye. 18 Yéndfem yéngwatembetam fútar efe minde tukén yutémndat méinyotio Yesuene mérnamé yáne mer si afroghmén. 19 A yénamé ndené, fá té yafaryénd méinyotio Yesuene mérénam ménát yéndfé arengét ndené fété yéné uwégh ásáfogh Yurane ndimbal kwayaghé téfnan a yéndfem yuyafotam ndené yénd ménde yéném sái ewáutánam.
bt: We give thanks God who put into the heart of Taitus same thought which I have for you.p to help! 17 Not only did he welcome our request; he was so eager to help that he decided to go to you on his will. 18 We are sending the friend who is highly respected through all the Yesu's families(churches) for his preaching the good talk. 19 And besides that, he has been chosen by the Yesu's families(churches) to travel with us as we carry out this work of love for the sake of the Lord's big light(glory) and we show that we want to help.

nama: Yénd wéi nawarawenam yau táf nawatarna ménamén yéné marataye raméghmén. 21 Yénd nawarawenam sái yafrotam némé mbarkánd yém, yau mé Yurane ghékfanro, wénde ámb árfene ghékfan wéi.
bt: We are very careful not to stir up any complaints about this generous gift. 21 We are careful to do what is straight, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of other people.

nama: Féyo yéndfem yéngwatembetam téfene fútar yémafé. Yéndfem té tufotam fá tárfár mbinotmé a téfandam mer méndefé wáutárghét. A sénonjo ndené fá minde efalo tiyátoi fém, fá ménde yém wáutarghét mindefifi. 23 Ndené Taitus siyém, fá yém tane awarnyégh ár a ásáfogh fútar yéndfé ménát féfe wáutárghét, ndenáyo ámb fútaréf kés efe eyota yémafé, yéné tárfár Yesuene mérén yétkwénan a ndimbal kwayaghé yangotat Kerisoefaf. 24 Euyafotati féfene uwégh yéfeyot, ménát ndené méinyotio Yesuene mérnamé mat so em yénémamén a mat so kamndangi ndené yénd mbarkánd yéném tuktukan féfenemén.
bt: So we are sending our friend with them. We have tested him many times and found him with good willing to help. And now that he trust you very much, he wants to help even more. 23 As for Titus, he is my partner and worker friend with me to help you, as for the other friends who are going with him, in the name of many Yesu's families(churches) and bring big light(glory) to Keriso. 24 Show your.p love to them, so that all the Yesu's families(churches) will know it and know that we are straight in boasting about you.

nama: Kété siyém yau ménde yém tayot faroghét féfeyot ménát wáutárghét sái téngwatemban Ngánjánene árfefaf Judia kénjún. 2 Yénd mat wém ndené fém ménde em wáutárghét, a yénd tuktukan wém féfenemén yéné árfefaf Makedonia yuwon. Yénd norayan, "Efalo yátogh ár Akaya yuwon afrengéghkaf tamorwén wáutárghét kétándmayo ámb yunjéf." Féfene méndeyam té talimnde tárfár ár wáutárghét. 3 Sénonjo yéndon engwatembetan yéné efalo yátogh ár, ménát téfene tuktuk féfenemén yéné yammén yau so kawengongé fifiofnarot, wénde ndené yénd kés norayan, fém so nafrendati wáutárghét. 4 Njam ámb efalo yátogh ár kétándmamén Makedonia sái yéngém yéndfé a tawinjongém ndené fém yau afrengéghkaf em, yénd so yéném wáiwáiafé ménamén yéndfem efalo tiyátotam. A yénd nemnan ndené fém so em wáiwáiafé wéikor. 5 Yénémamén yénd kémnawén wawéngsoghét yéné efalo yátogh árfefaf ménát álighét ta frondeta a afrengéghét raméghmén árkémamén fémofem unyéghofnar si yafrotaménd. Féyo so frengéghkaf yém njam yénd kufarén, a tufarotangé ndené fém ménde em ramghét, yau fémofem so.
bt: There is really no need for me to write you.p about the help being sent to God's people in Judia. 2 I know that you.p are willing to help, and I have boasted of you.p to the people in Makedonia region. I said, “The truly approving people(believers) in Akaya region have been ready to help since last year.” Your.p eagerness has led many people to help. 3 Now I am sending these truly approving people(believers), so that our boasting about you.p in this thing may not turn out to be without.meaning, but just as I said, you.p will be ready to help. 4 If some truly approving people from Makedonia should come with me and find out that you.p are not ready, we would be shamed because we trusted you. And I think that you would be shamed as well. 5 So I thought it was necessary to urge these truly approving people(believers) to go to you ahead of me and get ready the gift which you did without.lying talk(promise) to make. Then it will be ready when I arrive, and it will show that you give because you want to, not because you have to.

nama: Kawafarotangi yéné ár emo nambio mbwe tayangé so etane nambioro njamke, wénde yéné ár emo tayangé tárfár so etane tárfár. 7 Fémofem ámbiroámbiroero so fété erametati, ndené fémofem mwighé kés táitotati, yau akenagh mwighafé o wafrogh yam mwighaménro, ménamén Ngánjánene uwégh yém yéné ároe emo yéramete áuwéghafé. 8 A Ngánjánam so fété tawaramangé minde tukén féfenjo méndeyan, ménát ndené féfene mé so ewam túmén tárfáryo méinyotio mer ásáfoghét. 9 Ndené Buk si noraye, "Yémon ewaramete marataye yauyau árét. Yáne mbarkánd yam nakete tuot."
bt: Remember that the person who plants few seeds will have a small crop, but he one who plants many seeds will have a many crop. 7 Each of you.p should give, as you have decided, not with dislike mind or with duty mind, because God's love is to the one who gives gladly. 8 And God is able to give you.p more than you need, so that you will always have all you need for yourselves and more than enough for every good work. 9 As the scripture says, “He gives generously to the needy. His straight thing lasts forever.”

nama: A Ngánján, emon ewaramete mbwe yégh téfnárai a njamke nénet, yémon so ndenáyo ewaramete féfeyot méinyotio mbwe fém ménde kés em a so eméinde anoghét a funaghét minde tárfár kétándmé féfene marataye raméghta. 11 Yémon so fém eyátote wemár féyotaro ménát marataye raméghan em méinyotio efghon, ménát tárfár árém so kánjo yarametat Ngánjáne ménamén féfene raméghmén árké téfe tambénta ewinjongi. 12 Yéné ásáfoghmén fémofem árké tafrotati yau Ngánjánene árfene ménde wifoghétro, wénde wéi téfunati ramégh tárfár yame kánjo Ngánjáne. 13 A ménamén fém kayaufotati fémyo yéfefaf, féfene ásáfogham eméindai tárfár árét ndimbal kwayaghé so yawangotat Ngánjáne ménamén féfene wafroghyam Kerisoene mer simén, árkémamén norayati, a féfene marataye ramégh yémafé a méinyotio árfé. 14 A féyo kénjkam uwéghafé fá so Ngánjánafé norayat féfeyot ménamén yéné warafaréghofnar ngárám Ngánjánam ewaramai féfe. 15 Yéndfem kánjo yaramétam Ngánjáne ménamén yéné warafaréghofnar raméghmén!
bt: And God, who gives seed for the sower and bread to eat, he will also give you.p with all the seed you.p need and will make it grow and produce a very much from your giving widely. 11 He will make you rich enough to be giving widely at all times, so that many people will give thanks to God for your.p gifts which they receive from us. 12 This work you.p do, not only meets the needs of God's people, but also produces an outpouring in many way of thanks to God. 13 And because you showed yourselves to them, your work cause many people will give big light(glory) to God for your doing to the good talk of Keriso, which you say, and for your giving widely with them and every people. 14 And so with deep love they will talk with God for you.p because of this without.counting grace God has given you.p. 15 Let us give thanks to God for his without.counting gift!

nama: Yéndo, Folém yérametan ómnengégh féfefaf efe métar wém njam yénd wém fémafé, wénde muyafé wém féfeyot njam yénd wém yukér. Ménaye Kerisoene métare a meryame 2 yéndon ewamnendan fémofem yau wéméindati muyat njam yénd wéném. Ménamén mat wém ndené yéndon so yafrotan muyafé yéfefaf efe norayat ndené yénd násáfotam kétándmé mband maratoene mwighata. 3 Efalo yéné yém ndené yénd mband maratan yénmor, wénde yéndfem yau yafrotam ásmégh kétándmé mband maratoene mwighata. 4 Yéné fimb yéndfem seweretam téfene ásmégh kénjún yau yéné em mband maratoene fimb, wénde Ngánjanene waftafé fimb, árkéméné yéndfem eweretam ménát wirorghét táikér awémarogh sifayé. Yéndfem ewironam unyégh amrorégh. 5 Yéndfem ewewonam yuwot méinyotio tuktuk yam árké nanote Ngánjánene matyam ngiot a yéndfem méinyotio mwighé ewangotam kétán Kerisoefaf. 6 A yéné soramé fém njam káyátongi efaloe, yénd so afrengéghkaf yéném tékratan kémghét fá efe náyáretat.
bt: I, Fol, make an appeal to you.p who is mild when I am with you.p, but am strong to you.p when I am away. By the gentleness and kindness of Keriso 2 I beg you.p not to cause me to be strong when I come. Because I know that I can do strongly to those who say that we work from worldly minds. 3 It is true that we live in the world, but we do not fight from worldly minds. 4 The spears we use in our fight are not the world's weapons, but God's powerful spears, which we use to destroy strong defending place. We destroy false arguments. 5 We pull down every proud thing which is raised against the knowledge of God and we turn every thought to Keriso. 6 And after you.p have proved truly, we will be ready to punish to them who are disobeying.

nama: Fém néretat faru rokaran. Efe naramangi fáyo kétán Kerisoefaf? Mer, fá so kemnangi wéikor yéfenjomén, ménamén yénd Kerisoefaf yéném ndené fété fá kés eném. 8 Yénd yau wáiwái wém, njam yénd tuktukan wém ámbmén mindefifi ménamén muyé árké Yuram yénmaramayéng téfe. Wénde téfene muyé eramai féfe tukét, yau tirarai féfe yuwot. 9 Yau nemnati ndené yéndo kar fém férférafé eyáton tane férne. 10 Ámb ár so korayangé, "Folene férén sem kértaye a muyafé, wénde njam fá yém yéna yéndfé fifie, fá siyém njangénnjangén a yáne si sem fifiofnar!" 11 Ndené ár so mat káyátongé ámb yau fénaté yénayan yém némé yéndfem yéfarotam téfenjo férnan njam yénd yukér yéném a némé yéndfem so ewafrotam njam yénd yéném kété fémofé.
bt: You.p are looking at the outside of things. Who gave themselves to Keriso? Well, let them think again about themselves, because we belong to Keriso just as they do. 8 I am not ashamed, if I have boasted too much about the authority that the Lord has given us. But our authority builds you.p up, doesn't tear you.p down. 9 Don't think that I am trying to frighten you.p with my letters. 10 Someone will say, “Fol's letters are heavy and strong, but when he is with us by body, he is weak and his talks are without.meaning!” 11 Such a person must understand no difference between what we write in our letters when we are away and what we will do when we are there with you.

nama: Yénd yau nátémndam o naufotam yéndio yémafé efe nawarsat fáyo. Nderé matyamofnar em! Yémofem ewafarotat yéfenjo matyam aufoghét, a fá nawafénmetat fáyo yéfenjo matyame! 13 Wénde ndené yénd kés yéném, yénd yau so nawauyotam minde ámb sifayat, wénde so yém ásáfogh fúan Ngánjánam árké té yékmayéng téfeyot, a yénayan yafein téfene ásáfogh féfe kénjún. 14 A táf fém em yéné fúan, yénd yau néngférmetam yéné fú njam yénd yénmormén féfefaf, njam yéndfem yénérsam mer si Kerisoenemén. 15 Féyo yénd yau nawauyotam ásáfogh ndené ámb árém té tafrotat fú Ngánjánam árké té yékmai. Wénde yénd nemnam nde féfene ndimbal mwighé mé so nanote a yéndfem mé so yafrotam minde ndimbal ásáfogh féfe kénjún, túmén yéné fúan Ngánjánam árké té yénmakmayéng. 16 Féyo yéndfem fété so yaitotam mer si ámb ndimbal yuwomé néférmai fém. Yénd yau ménde wém sái nawauyotam ásáfoghmén árké té násáfotamém tándái ámb árfene yuwan.
bt: We would not dare to classify or compare ourselves with those who estimate themselves highly. How without.knowledge they are! They make up their own knowledge to measure themselves by, and they judge themselves by their own knowledge! 13 But as for us, we are not boast beyond into other place, but it will be within the boundary of work which God has set for us, and this includes our work among you.p. 14 And since you are within those marks, we were not crossing over this marks when we came to you.p, when we bring the good talk about Keriso. 15 So we do not boast about the work that other people have done beyond the boundary God set for us. Instead, we think that your big mind(faith) may grow and that we may be able to do a much big work among you.p, always within the boundary that God has set. 16 Then we can preach the good talk in other countries passover you(beyond you). We do not want to boast about work which we had done in someone else's field.

nama: Wénde ndené Buk si noraye, "Efe ménde yém tuktukot so tuktuk yéfande némé Ngánjánam ewafrote." 18 Efe efalo mer yém? Yéné yau yém ár efe nemna merro fáyo. Yéné yém ár efe Yuré nemne merro yánemén.
bt: But as the scripture says, “Whoever wants to boast must boast what the Lord has done.” 18 Who is really good? It is not the person who thinks good himself. It is the person that the Lord thinks good about him.

nama: Yénd nemnan fémofem so wamarotati yénd, táf njam yénd némbnare onon wém. Néméwét, yénd kwamarotangi! 2 Yénd naweramnjan féfeyot ngánjánene waramnjaye. Fém ndené em njúnnjúnofnar yuwagia mérésnit endefaf yénd unyéghofnar si norayayén awerghét kétán ámbiro ároefafetro, Kerisoefaf. 3 Yénd férfér wém ménamén féfene mwighé so em nawaghnat a fémofem so efrangotati féfenjo afoghkaf a njúnnjúnofnar aramégh yam kétán Kerisoefaf yéné end kénjúmé If té yarsotam Sáifram unyéghe. 4 Fémofem áuwéghafé tafángongi yéné ár efe yéném féfefaf a nafrotat fénaté Yesuenemén, yau yéné Yesu, endenemén yéndfem nafrotam. Fémofem yumyoyénd fénaté mbérmbér a mer si méinyo fénatayo kétándmé Ngánján Mbérmbér, a mer si fémofem árké té yumyoyénd téfeta!
bt: I think you.p will tolerate me, when I am a little foolishness. Yes, toleate me! 2 I am jealous for you.p with a godly jealousy. You are like a without.dust(holy) virgin to him who I said without.lying talk(promise) you to engage to one husband, to Keriso. 3 I am afraid that your minds may somehow be led astray from your sincere and without.dust devotion to Keriso just as If was deceived by the Sáifér's lying. 4 You.p gladly tolerate anyone who comes to you.p and preaches a different Yesu, not the one we preached. You.p accept a different spirit and a good talk completely different from the Spirit, and the good talk you received from us!

nama: Yénd yau nemnan ndené yénd wém soramé yéfefaf efe emndetat "minde waftafé Yesuene muyafé ár!" 6 Yénd yau watametaménd siot, wénde táfaf yém matyam. Yéndfem té safén féfe tawakmetam féfe méinyotio efghon a méinyotio ende. 7 Yéndon yau faiot tawamenendan fém njam yéndon táitotan mer si Ngánjánene féfeyot. Yénd yuwan kawewonan ménát fém so eyátotan minde mer. Yéné mámáfi témorwén tayot? 8 Njamandio yénd kásáfotawén féfe kénjúmé, yénd fai kafrotawét ámb Yesuene mérnam. Yéndon oro tafrotawén yéfefaf, ménát siot, ménát féfe wáutárghét. 9 A yéné kwéfon yénd njam kwémorwén fémofé yéndon yau mámé mwighat taméindawén fém wáutárghét njam yénd maniot káronjawén. Efalo yátogh ár efe enmormén kétándmé Makedoniata yémofem enérsaménd tayot méinyotio rokar yénd némat ménde kwémorwén. Ndené frondeyan a soramé yénd yau so kérté wém féfeyot. 10 Ndené fété Kerisoene efalo si tane kénjún, yénd unyéghofnar si norayan ndené yéné tuktuk tane sian yau so métare yém yénamé méinyotio Akaya yuwomé. 11 Yénd norayan tane uwégh kar yau yém féfeyot? Yau, Ngánján mat yém tane uwégh féfeyot!
bt: I do not think I am last to those who told “very strong men with Yesu's authority(apostles).” 6 I may be untrained as a speaker, but I have knowledge. We have made this clear to you.p all the time and in every ways. 7 I did not charge you when I preached the good talk of God to you. I humbled myself in order to make you more good. Was that wrong of me? 8 While I was working among you, I was paid by other Yesu's families(churches). I was robbing them, so to speak, in order to help you. 9 And during the time I was with you.p I did not bother you.p for help when I needed money. The truly approving people(believers) who came from Macedonia brought me everything I needed. As in the past, so in the future I will never be a burden to you.p! 10 By Keriso's true talk in me, I say without.lying talk(promise) that this boast in my talk will not be silenced anywhere in all of Akaya region. 11 Do I say this because I am not loving you? No, God knows I love you!

nama: Yénd mé so wafroghan wéngém némé yéndon yafrotan sénonjo, ménát ndené so engénfetat ámb unyégh Yesuene muyafé árfene ásáfogh árkéméné fá tuktukan norayat nde fá násáfotat ndenéyameyo yéndfem kés ewafrotam. 13 Yéné ár sem unyégh Yesuene muyafé ár em, unyégh ásáfogh ár, efe nuindat yéfenjo ásáfoghmén a nafurnat ménát so néretat ndené Kerisoene muyafé ár kar em. 14 Yau néworsota, ménamén Sáifér fáyo so nafurne ndenanit Kwayaghane njafar si résagh ár kar yém fá. 15 Yau sélngwéle yéné yammén em njam yáne faiofnar ásáfogh ár ndenáyo nafurnat nde mbarkánd yamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár kar em. Yéfene yatogh siyém ndené so yém ndernáye yéfene wafroghyam ewam. Folene tékrat ndené fété Yesuene muyafé ár
bt: I will go on doing what I am doing now, in order to keep those other men with Yesu's authority(apostles) from having any reason for boasting and saying that they work in the same way that we do. 13 These men are false men with Yesu's authority(apostles), deceitful workers, who lie about their work and disguise themselves to look like real men with Yesu's authority(apostles) of Keriso. 14 No wonder, because Sáifér can disguise himself like a man carrying heavenly message(angel) of light. 15 It is not surprising if his without.payment working men(servants) also disguise as without.payment working men(servants) of straightness. Their end will be what their actions deserve. Fol's sufferings as man with Yesu's authority(apostle)

nama: Yénd kor norayan, yau ámb ár so nemne yénd kar wém onon. Wénde njam téu warawenati, mé kumyongi onon árnit, tane némbnar tuktuk téfnan. 17 Némé norayan sénonjo siyém yau yéné némat Yuré ménde yém tafaf siot, wénde ndenanit onon árnit. 18 A ménamén tárfár ár efe em tuktukan mband maratoene yam yafroghan, yéndon so yafrotan ndenéyameyo. 19 Fém fémyo sem káfeté mwighafé, féyo fémofem eumyotati onon ár áuwéghafé! 20 Fémofem yénmiyotati ámb ár emo ewafarete fém o yérse mer féfenemén o tarnyangé fém o yuwot efanda fém o tasmangé sáfan. 21 Yénd wém wáiwái yénd so korayangén yénd ténmorwén njangénnjangén yéné yam wafroghét!
bt: I repeat, no one should think that I am a fool. But if you do, then allow me like a foolish man in the reason my little boasting. 17 What I am talking now is not talking as the Lord would, but as a fool. 18 And because many men are boasting in the way the world does, I too will do. 19 You.p yourselves are wise, so you.p gladly accept fools! 20 You.p accept anyone who orders you around or takes good of you or traps you or looks down on you or slaps you in the face. 21 I am ashamed to say that we were too weak to do those things!

nama: Fá em Ibru ár? Yénd fété wém. Fá em Israil ár? Yénd fété wém. Fá em Ebaamene ákiambum? Yénd fété wém. 23 Fá fété em Kerisoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár? Yénd norayan mwighafnar árnit. Yénd fété wém tukényo. Yénd té kásáfotan mindefifi, yénd té kwélawangérwén kwéfkwéf méngon tárfár mbinotmé, yénd té wéfmotaménd minde tukén, a yénd té kwémorwén kér kaka túmén. 24 Faif mbinotmé 39ot wéfmotaménd Ju árém. 25 Nambinotmé yénd wéfmotaménd wéne Rom árém, a ámbirotaro wíme wéfiyátotaménd a nambinotmé yénd té wémormén bout olitogh kénjúmé, a ámbiro sémbár a efogh kwémorwén arakafan. 26 Tane tárfár arengégh kénjún yénd té kérté kéféramé kwémorwén a oro téfnárafeta, kértayan tanjo árta a Téndo árta. Kété kérté témorwén ndimbal yuwomé, kérté fanfne yuwomé, kérté tukén árakafamé, kérté kétándmé unyégh efalo yátogh árfeta. 27 Yénd násáfotan mindefifi a témbé, túmén yénd kwémorwén kéméghofnar. Yénd kwémorwén nu a njamke siogh a túmén tane njamke yau kor kwamorwén. Yénd minde krufér a firo kwémorwén. 28 A yau so eufanan ámb yam, méinyotio efogh yénd wém kérté yam wétumndat tane méghmégh siyém méinyotio Yesuene mérén árfenemén. 29 Njam ámb ár yém njangénnjangén, yénd ndenáyo wém njangénnjangén. Njam ámb ár yaufiyam kénjún yém, yénd tikfan kuyan.
bt: Are they Ibru? So am I. Are they Israil people? So am I. Are they Ebraamʼs descendants? So am I. 23 Are they without.payment working men(servants) of Keriso? I talk like without.mind people. I am more. I have worked hard, I have been put in darkness house many times, been beaten very much, and been near darth often. 24 Five times I was given 39 smashing by the Ju people. 25 Three times I was beaten with sticks, once I was stoned and three times I was shipwrecked, I spent a night and a day in the sea. 26 In my many travels I have been in danger from floods and from stealing men, in danger from my own people and from Side people. There have been dangers in the cities, dangers in the deserts, dangers on the high seas, and dangers from false truly approves(believes). 27 I worked hard and long, often I have gone without sleep. I have been hungry and thirsty and I have often been without enough food. I have been very cold and naked. 28 And not to mention other things, every day I am under the pressure of my concern for all people in the Yesu's families(churches). 29 When someone is weak, then I feel weak too. When someone is led into sin, I am fell down into pain.

nama: Njam yénd tuktukan wém, yénd so tuktukan wém rokarmén árkéménayam yufane tane njangénnjangén. 31 Ngánján a Yuré Yesuene Afé, efe so yuwetam tuot, fá mat yém yénd nafrotan efalo. 32 Njam yénd kwémorwén Damaskos kénjún, ngafana Warsagh ár Aretasene mbanmbanan yémon ewakayoi Damaskosmén wanjengégh ár ndimbal tesen sésafneyan ménát ta werghét. 33 Wénde yénd kwéwonawét mbéf yafe yuwot wím warengégh áráke fénemé a nawanjaryén yá tambén.
bt: If I boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness. 31 The God and Father of the Lord Yesu, who is to be praised forever, knows that I am saying true. 32 When I was in Damaskos, the governor under King Aretas placed guards at the city gates of Damaskos to arrest me. 33 But I was lowered in a basket through the hole of the stone wall and escaped from him.

nama: Yénd so tuktukan wém. Ndemamén némndafi yau yém winjoghét, yénd so norayan ghékfe waingéghmén a uyafoghmén árké waramayéng Yuram. 2 Yénd mat wém ámb efalo yátogh ár efe tamorwén 14 yunjéf frondeyo uyoyéng minde tukét njafarot. Yénd mat yau wém yéné fété nufrotam o mé ghékfe waingégh yémormén. Mé Ngánjánro mat yém. 3 Yénd mat wém yéné ár, yémormén fifie o fénataye kétamé fifita yénd yau mat wém, wénde mé Ngánjánro mat yém. 4 Fá yémormén yuyoi áuwégh méngotuot a kété si náyárai yéné rokarmén árkéméné sie yau so tékém, yéné rokarmén ároene mbírmbíram yau so noraye. 5 Féyo yénd so tuktukan wém yéné ároenemén, wénde yénd yau so tuktukan wém tanjomén, wénde yéné rokarménro árkéménayam uyafote yénd wém njangénnjangén. 6 Efaloe, yénd njam kemnawén yéndo awarmboghét tanemén, yénd yau so norayan ndenanit onon árnit, ménamén yénd so norayan. Wénde yénd nawewonan yéndio yau nawarmbotan. Yénd nemnan ndené mété táf ár nemnat tane simén a tane efalo wafroghmén a fá so norayat ménamén tane ámb wafroghyammén yéné árkéméné yau yémofem tafandat a yémofem so wuyotat a tane yétkwén so tukén yém fifiofnar.
bt: I have to boast. Even though there is nothing to be gained, I will talk about eye passoverings(visions) and revelations which were given me by the Lord. 2 I know a certain truly approving man(believer) man who 14 years ago was caught up to the very high heaven. I do not know whether this actually happened or whether he had a eye passovering. Only God knows. 3 And I know that this man, whether in the body or apart from the body I do not know, but God knows. 4 He was caught up to happy village and heard these things which cannot be put into talks, these things that man's lips may not speak. 5 So I will boast about this man, but I will not boast about myself, but only these things that show I am weak. 6 Truly, if I was thinking to boast about me, I will not speak like a crazy man, because I will speak the truth. But I put down myself and I don't praise myself. I am thinking that perhaps men won't think of my talk and my real behavior and they will talk also about some of my deeds that they didn't see and they will praise me and my name will go up high for without.meaning.

nama: Wénde ménát sái kékmbon tuktukta ménamén tárfár mer rokar yéndon tafandawén, yénd kwémorwén waramayénd fifiyan ndémkefé wét, árké nufrote ndené Sáiféroene si résagh ár ménát ta ngiot a sái kwékmbongi kétándmé tuktukta. 8 Nambinotmé yéndon Ngánjánafé koraiwam Yurafaf yénémamén a yómnendayén fá ménát sái titérmbongai. 9 Wénde yáne wangogh yémormén, "Tane ngárám siyém féyotaro yém feyot, méné tane wafté siyém ndimbalfifi njam fém ném njangénnjangén." Yénd swém mindefifi náuwétan, sái wém tuktukan tane njangénnjangénmén, ménát yéndon so yayáretan Kerisoene wafté so násáfote tafaf. 10 Yénd náuwetan njangénnjangénmén, wasálighmén, táikér yammén, ngimén, náuwéghmén Kerisoene téfnan. Njam yénd wém njangénnjangén, féyo yénd muyafé wém. Folene emarégh efalo yátogh árfeyot Korintén
bt: But to stop from pride because of the many good things I saw, I was given a painful thorn in the body, which acts as Sáifér's messenger to beat me and stop me from being proud. 8 Three times I talked with God to the Lord about this and asked him to take it away. 9 But his answer was, “My grace is enough for you, for my power is very big when you are weak.” I am very happy, to be proud of my weaknesses, so that I will seek Keriso's power will work to me. 10 I am happy with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and difficulties for Keriso's sake. For when I am weak, then I am strong. Fol's feeling for the truly approving people(believers) in Korint

nama: Yénd nimndan ndenanit onon ár, wénde fémofem té kwénméindati yénd wafroghét. Fém sof em efe so nafrotati meryam tanemén. Yénd wém fifiofnar, wénde yénd nátémndan yémafé "efalo Yesuene muyafé árfé." 12 Tárfár sélngwélafé yam a sunsunafé yam, yéné yamam efalo yiyátote ndené yénd wém Yesuene muyafé ár yéndon tawafrotan féfene kénjún ndimbal métar yamafé. 13 Némé yéndo tafrotan ámb Yesuene mérnat ndené yéndon yau tafrotan féfeyot, yéndon yau kértan ekman fém mani wáutárghét? Kawayangi tane ménamén féfefaf tafrotan yaufi!
bt: I am acting like a fool, but you.p have made me do it. You.p are the ones who ought to say good thing of me. I am without.meaning, but I can match with them “true men with Yesu's authority(apostles).” 12 The many surprsing things(signs) and wonderous things(wonders), these things prove that I am a man with Yesu's authority(apostle) were did among you.p with much patience. 13 What I do for the other Yesu's families(churches) that I have not done for you.p, I did not put to you.p for financial help. Forgive me for doing you wrong!

nama: Sénonjo yéné siyém nambiomén yénd wéikor kénangotan féfe yafoghét, a yénd yau so nómnendan ámb féfefaf, ménamén yénd ménde wém féfefaf, yau féfene mani. Ambumtértéram yau so nusénat yéfenjo afafet a amafet, wénde amaf a afafem so nusénat yéfenjo ambumtértérat. 15 Yénd so náuwetan sái ewitan némé méinyotio tane em, a yéndio karaman ndenáyo, ménát sái ewáutánan féfeyot. So féfene uwégh tayot yuwon so yém ménamén tane uwégh so yém mindefifi?
bt: This is now the third time that I am going to visit you.p, and I will not ake anything to you.p, because I want you.p, not your money. Children should not have to provide for their parents, but parents should provide for their children. 15 I will be glad to spend all I have, and to give myself as well, in order to help you.p. Will you love me less because I love you so much?

nama: Fém so kuwangongi ndené yénd yau kwémorwén kérté féfeyot. Wénde ámb árom so korayangé ndené yénd kwémorwén unyégh ár, a ernyayén fém unyégh sie. 17 Yéndon té térsan mer rokar féfe tambén yéné si ghany téfnárafeta yéndon árké té engwafaretamén? 18 Yéndon yamnendayén Taitus álighét, a yéndon yafaretayén ámb efalo yátogh ár yémafé. Fém nemnati Taitusém yanai mer rokar féfenemén? Fá a yénd ámbiro mwighaye ewafrowam ámbrérero!
bt: You will agree that I was not a burden to you. But someone will say that I was lying man, and trapped you with lying talk. 17 Did I take good things from you.p through any of the messengers I sent? 18 I begged Titus to go, and I sent the other truly approving man(believer) with him. Do you think that Titus took good thing of you? He and I do same minds in the same way!

nama: Fém nemnati ndené méinyotio yénd mé nawamarotam yéndio féfe sáfrére. Yau! Yénd té kénorayam Ngánjánene sáfrére ndené fété efe em Kerisoene kénjún. Tikéfafé fútaréf, méinyotio yam yéndfem ewafrotam yéné kufrote féfe wáutárghét. 20 Yénd férfér wém njam yénd kufarén yéndo so tawinjon fém fénatayo kétándmé némat yénd ménde wém fém fiya tam a fémofem so kwinjongi yénd fénatayo kétándmé némat fém ménde em yénd fiya kwém. Yénd férfér wém ndené kété em amrorégh, waramnjagh, nékw, a awamanjarégh, yau si orayégh, warunyégh, tukal a isrambogh. 21 Yénd férfér wém ndené ámb káye yénd njam wéngém tane Ngánjánam so kwéworngé yénd féfene sáfrére, a yénd so ye nendan tárfár árfenemén efe yaufiyaman emormén tándái a yau nawengoyénd yémofem árké ewafrotaménd yéfenjo yaufi wal mwighé a yaufi walyam.
bt: You.p think that all along we have been trying to defend ourselves before you. No! We have been speaking in the sight of God as those in Keriso. Heartful friends, everything we do is done to help you.p. 20 I am afraid that when I come I will find you.p different from what I want you.p to be and you will find me different from what you would like me to be. I am afraid that I will find quarreling and jealousy, anger and selfishness, insults and gossip, pride and disorder. 21 I am afraid that the next time when I go my God will put me down in your.p presence, and I shall weep over many people who sinned in the past and have not turn away of the immoral things they have done their bad sexual thinking and and their sexual sins.

nama: Sénonjo siyém nambinotmé yénd wéikor kénangotan féfe yafoghét. Buk si noraye, "Némé awánangogh sái em so yém yénayan sómbio o nambio ghékéf fandagh árfene yátoghe." 2 Yéndon té mat engiyátoyén njam yénd fémafé témorwén yénamayo efghon. Sénonjo yéndon wéikor yéfertan njam yukér wém. Tane angoghan yéndo yau so ewarawenan fá emofem yaufiyam ewafrotaménd fronde o ámb kés em. 3 Yéndon so tawaraman féfe méinyotio téfén fém kés ménde em ndené Keriso sái noraya tane kénjúmé. Fá njam násáfota féfefaf, fá yau yém njangénnjangén, wénde tuyafongé yáne wafté féfe kénjún. 4 Ndemanén fá yékmayénd kéran wafatarégh wénan njangénnjangéne, wénde fá sénonjo ghérsé yém ménaye Ngánjánene waftaye. Keriso kénjún yénd yémafé ndenéyameyo njangénnjangén yéném, wénde téfene awarnyégh kénjún fémafé yéndfem yéfalotam Ngánjánene wafté yáne ghérsé kénjún.
bt: This is now the third time that I am coming to visit you.p. The scripture says, “What accusation are must be received by the approval of two or more watching men.” 2 I have already known you.p when I was with you.p the second time. I now repeat it while I am absent. On my return I will not spare those who sinned earlier or any of the other people. 3 I will give you.p all proof you.p want that Keriso speaks through me. When he works to you.p, he is not weak, but he shows his power among you.p. 4 Even he was put to death on the cross in weakness, he now lives by God's power. In Keriso we also are weak, but in our relations with you.p we shall share God's power in his life.

nama: Kakmangi fémyo ufoghan a namotati fémyo, ménát winjoghét némé fém emor ndimbal mwighayan o yau. Efaloe fém mat em ndené Keriso Yesu siyém féfe kénjún yém? Táfandro fém njam kuyangi. 6 Yéndon efalo eyátotan fém mat em ndené yénd yau uyégh ár yéném. 7 Yénd Ngánjánafé noraiwém ndené fém yau so ewafrotati yaufi. Yéné yém yau ménaye yuyafotam kar yénd yéném féyotaro, wéndende fémofem so ewafrotati némé mbarkánd yam em, ndemamén yénd so yéném uyégh ár. 8 Yéndfem yau so ámb yam ewafrotam fénataye efalofaf wénde efaloyotro so. 9 Yénd náuwetam njam yénd njangénnjangén yéném wénde fém sem muyafé. A yénd wéi Ngánjánafé noraiwém ndené fém so féwénféwénofnar em. 10 Yéné téfnan yéndon yéfarotan yéné férén njamandio yénd yukér wém féfeta. Yéné yém ménát njam yénd kufarén yéndo yau so máméghéraye tawafngon fém yéné waftaye árké Yuram té waramayéng ta féfe wanoghét, yau ménát sái ewironan fém yuwot.
bt: Put yourselves to the test and judge yourselves, to find out whether you.p are living in big mind(faith) or not. Surely you.p know that Keriso Yesu is in you.p? Unless you.p have completely failed. 6 I trust you.p will know that we are not failing men. 7 We talk with God that you.p will not do wrong. It is not to show that we are enough, but so that you.p may do what is straight thing, even though we may seem to be failing men. 8 We cannot do any thing against the truth, but only for truth. 9 We are glad when we are weak but you.p are strong. And we also talk with God that you.p will become without.flaw(perfect). 10 That is why I write this letter while I am away from you. It is that when I arrive I will not have to deal badly with you in using the power that the Lord has given me to build you up, not to tear you down.

nama: Ambinomén, tane fútaréf, yawo! Náuwetati! Nanotati sotafé. Náyáretati tane wawanjagh si. Miya em ámbiro mwighayan a krufrát emor. A Ngánjánene uwégh a krufrát so mé yém féfefaf.
bt: Finally my friends, good-bye! Rejoice! Grow to maturity. Listen to my enocouraging talk. Agree with one another and live in peace. And the God of love and peace will be with you.p.

nama: Áuwéghafé nafángotati féfe kénjún ásneghafé krufráte.
bt: Greet one another with the kiss of peace.

nama: Méinyotio Ngánjánene árém yéna enwawatembangi yéfenjo áuwégh.
bt: All Godʼs people here send their greetings.

nama: Yuré Yesu Kerisoene ngárám, Ngánjánene uwégh a Ngánján Mbérmbéroene awarnyégh mé so yém féfefaf méinyotio.
bt: May the grace of the Lord Yesu Keriso, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the God Spirit be with you.p all.

nama: Yéné férén siyém ta tambén, Fol. Yénd wémotai ménát Yesuene muyafé árot. Yéné ásáfogh siyém yau yénmormén kétándmé ár mérénta o ároene si muyata, wénde kétamé Yesu Keriso a Afé Ngánján, emo Yesu yanoi kérta. 2 Yénd a tane fútar yénmatembangém yéné férén féfefaf, Yesuene mérén Galasia yuwomé.
bt: This letter is from me, Fol. I was called to be(lit. he called me)for the man who has Yesu's authority. This work does not come from people group or man's authority, but from Yesu Keriso and the Father God, who raised Yesu from the death. 2 I and my friends send this letter to the you.p, Yesu's family(church) through Galatia region.

nama: Afé Ngánján a Yuré Yesu Kerisom so tawaramangé féfe ngárám a krufrát. 4 Keriso fáyo naramai téfene yaufiyammén. Yémo yéné yafrote ménát yénd sái ténmanjarngé kétándmé sénonjo yaufi ár satata. Yéné fiyém némé téfene Afé Ngánján ménde témorwén yémo yafrotam. 5 Ngánjáne so yém minde ndimbal yétkwén tuot! Efalo. Yéna yém mé ámbiro mer si, yau ámb
bt: The Father God and the Lord Yesu Keriso will give you.p grace and peace. 4 Keriso gave himself for our sins. He did that to save us from the present evil people section(generation). This is what our Father God wanted he did. 5 To God will be very big name forever! Amen. Here is only one good talk, not other

nama: Ngánjánam ewaferyéng fém ménát Kerisoene ngárám kénjún so emor. Wénde yénd áuwéghofnar namndayén féfenemén ménamén fémofem wékeyero yéngkenati a fémofem yumyotati ámb fénataye mer si. 7 Efaloe, yéna yau fénataye mer si yém, wénde yénd norayan ndené ménamén yéna em ámb ár emofem eméindat fém warsoghét a yéta erametat ménát wengoghét Kerisoene mer sita. 8 Wénde njam té yéndfem o njafar si résagh árom té táitote mer si árké yém fénataye kétándmé yéndfem kés táitotam mer si fronde, fá so tawafalngongé! 9 Yénd té kénorayam fronde, wénde sénonjo wéikor yénd norayan: Emo njam yáitota mer si féfefaf, wénde yéné yém fénatayo kétándmé yéndon kés ewaramayén féfe, fá so tawafalngongé!
bt: God chose you.p in order to live in the grace of Keriso. But I became without.joy of you because so quickly you.p are quickly rejecting him and you accepted a different good talk. 7 Actually, there is no different good talk, but I say this because some people cause you confusion and trying to make to turn the good talk of Keriso. 8 But even if we or a man carrying heavenly message(angel) will preach to you a good talk which is different from we preached to you.p before, he will be cursed! 9 We have said it before, but now I say it again: If someone preaches a good talk to you.p, but it is different from that I gave you, he will be cursed!

nama: Yénd njam norayan yéné yammén, yéndon yau áuwéghét eméindan ár, wénde Ngánjánro yéndo áuwéhgét yéméindan. Njam yéndon ár áuwéghétro eméindan, féyo yénd yau so wém Kerisoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. Ndernáye Fol namndai Yesuene muyafé ár
bt: When I say these things, I am not trying to make people happy, but only God I am trying to please. If I only wanted to try to make people happy, then I will not be without.payment working man(servant) of Keriso. How Fol became a Yesu strong man

nama: Tane fútaréf, yénd ménde wém fém sái mat em ndené yéné mer si árké yéndo yaitotan yau ár wafroghmén yém. 12 Yéndon yau yanayén árta, o emo yau yénd watametam. Yesu Keriso fáyo nauyafoi tafaf.
bt: My friends, I want you.p to know that this good talk which I preached is not made by man. 12 I did not receive it from man, or none taught me. Yesu Keriso himself showed it to me.

nama: Fém mat em ndené yéndon némé tawafrotawén fronde, njam yéndon tawaitotawén Ju árfene wafrogh yam. Yéndon máméghéraye tawasérmbotawén Yesuene mérén a tarametawén wirorghét. 14 Yénd táf minde kásáfotawén tawaitotawén tane ákiafene wafroghyam. Ménayan tawaitotawén Ju árfene wafrogh yam, yéndo tawaindawén tane réremén ár efe tamorwén Ju ár a yéndo tawaitotawén minde taikre. 15 Wénde Ngánjánam yénd waferyéng amane fút kénjún a wémotai yénd yáne ngáráme. 16 Ngánjánam mwighé yéfaronam ménát safén yufaryéng yáne Ambum tafaf, ménát yéndon so yaitotan mer si yánemén kétán Téndo árfet. Njam Ngánjánam waferyéng ménát yéné ásáfoghét, yénd yau yénémamén korayawén ámb árfé. 17 Yénd yau wéngmormén Jerusalemét ménát sái tanfandawén efe fronde náyátoyénd Yesuene muyafé ár yénd soramé. Wénde yénd wékeye wéngmormén Arabia yuwot, a soramé nénangoyén Damaskos tesenot.
bt: You.d know what I have been doing at first, when I followed the Ju people's way of doing. I treated badly Yesu's family(church) and wanted to destroy them. 14 I used to work hard to follow my ancestor's initiative. Regarding following Ju people's doing, I surpassed my ages people who are Ju people and I followed them very strictly. 15 But God chose me in my mother's womb and called me by his grace. 16 God has made mind up to reveal clearly his Son to me, so that I will preach the good talk about him to the Side people. When God chose me this work I have not talk about this with other people. 17 I did not go to Jerusalem to visit those who have became men with Yesu's authority(apostles) I was later(before me). But I quickly went into Arabia region, and later I went back to Damaskos town.

nama: Nambio yunjéf soramé yénd wéngmormén Jerusalemét ménát mat so káyáteyayém Fitafé, a kété yénmorarmén Fitafé sómbio Safat. 19 Wénde njam yénd kété kwémorwén, yéndo yau yinjoyén ámb Yesuene muyafé ár, wénde Jemsro, efe yém Yurane néngén. 20 Ngánján mat yém ndené némé yéndon yéfarotan féfefaf siyém efalofifi yém. Yénd yau nuindan féfefaf!
bt: Three years later I went to Jerusalem to get to know Fité, and there we(two of us) stayed with Fitè for two weeks. 19 But when I was there, I even did not see any men with Yesu's authority(apostles), but only Jems, who is the Lord's younger brother. 20 God knows that what I am writing to you is very true. I am not lying to you.p.

nama: Soraméfifi, yénd wiyotam kétán Siria a Silisia marat yuwot. 22 Yéné kwéfon ár Yesuene mérén kénjún kété Judian emo yau wéfángoi o yinjoi tane sáf. 23 Wénde fá mé siro káyáretawét tanemén: "Yéné ár Fol, emo yénd túmén ténmasérmbotau sénonjo si yaitote ménamén ndimbal mwighamén yéné árké fronde wirorghét térametau!" 24 Féyo yémofem tuyotawét Ngánján tanemén.
bt: Later, I went to Siria and Silisia wide place(provinces). 22 On that time a man in Yesu's family(church) in Judia has not met me or seen my face. 23 But they.p were hearing only talk about me: "This man, Fol, who used to hit us is now preaching about the big mind(faith) which at first he tried to destroy!' 24 Then they were praising God because of me.

nama: 14 yunjéf soramé, yénd néwanoyém wéikor Jerusalemét Banabasfé, a Taitusfé mérne téngmorwén. 2 Yénd néwanoyén ménamén Ngánjánam wauyafoi ta ndené so kuwano. Njam yénd kété kwémorwén yéndfem áumengégh tanangayém Yesuene mérén sénko árfé a tawawafenawén yéné mer sioene fifi árké yéndon táitotawén Téndo árfet, ménat sái kwiyátongayénd. Yénd yau ménde wém tane ásáfogh sái em frondemén a sénonjomén fifiofnar. 3 Taitusfé yénd ténérwén, a ndemamén fá Grik ár témorwén, emo yau táfáretau ghéngan néfghét. 4 Yénd korayan féfefaf ménamén ámb unyégh ár kwankwane nufnyayénd téfene mérén kénjút. Yémofem tafénmetawét téfene yuwek ghérsamén árké yém téfefaf Yesu Keriso kénjún a yénd yénméindayénd sái faiofnar ásáfogh ár yéném yéfene nákfaf. 5 Wénde yénd yau namyoyém yéfefaf efoghféneféneyan, ménát sái mé yém mer sioene efalo féfefaf.
bt: 14 years later, I came back again to Jerusalem with Banabas, and Taitus with me together. 2 I went because God showed me that I should go. When I was there I met with Yesu family leaders and I explained this good talk's meaning which I have being preaching to the Side people for their agreement of me. I did not want my work from before and now to be without.meaning. 3 Taitus was with me, and even though he was Greek man, no one forced him to cut skin, 4 I said this to you.p because lying people joined scretly to our group. They examinde our free life which is in us Keriso Yesu and cause us to be without.payment working people to their rules. 5 But we did not obey to them for a minute, so that the truth of the good talk will remain to you.p.

nama: Ámb ár, fá nemnat ndené limánégh ár kar em, yau némndafi yéfghayénd tane sifaf. Yéné yau nérete kértaye tafaf némé fá em ndimbalfifi o yau, ménamén Ngánjánam yau yénmaufénmete farutaye. 7 Wéndende, fá yayamngoyénd ndené Ngánjánam ásáfogh waramai mer si waitoghét Téndo árét, ndené fété Fitai kés ásáfogh yaramai mer si waitoghét Ju árét. 8 Ngánján kásáfotau Fitane kénjúmé sái tafrota yáne ásáfogh ndené Yesuene muyafé ár Ju árét, a Ngánján wéi kásáfotau ta kénjúmé sái tafrota tane ásáfogh ndené Yesuene muyafé ár Téndo árét. 9 Jems, Fité, a Jon, efe mat eyátoyénd limánégh árot, ewayamngoyénd ndené Ngánjánam waramai yéné fénaté ásáfogh, féyo yémofem yényátoyénd fútar ngángé raméghe. Yéndfem mwighé ámbiro yiyátoyém ndené Banabas a yénd so násáfowam Téndo árfefafmé a fá so násáfotat Ju árfefafmé. 10 Ámbiro yéfene ómnengégh témorwén ndené yéndfem so ewáutánam áronjagh ár yéfene mérén kénjún, árké yam fifiot yénd ménde kwémorwén wafroghét. Folém sote yénfai Fité
bt: Other people, they think that they are leaders, did not contribute anything to my talk. This does not look burden to me whether they are big or no, because God does not examine us with outside. 7 Instead, they recognized that God gave me the work to preach the good talk to the Side people, just as he gave the work to Fitè to preach the good talk to the Ju people. 8 God worked through Fitè to do his work as a Yesu strong man to the Ju people, and he also worked through me to make my work a Yesu strong man to the Side people. 9 Jems, Fitè, and Jon, who were known as the leading people, they knew that God gave me this different work, then they made us friends by shaking hands. We all made one agreement that Banabas and I will work through the Side people and they will work among the Ju people. 10 Their one asking was that we should help poor people in their group, which sort of work I wanted to do. Fol rebuked Fitè

nama: Fité njam yénmormén Antiokét, yéndon sote yénfayén ár néndkéfan, ménamén yémon yaufi yafrotam safénro. 12 Njam fá fronde nufaryéng Antiokén, Fitam té njamke enetam Téndo efalo yátogh árfé. Féyo Jemsém ewáfáretam ámb Ju efalo yátogh ár Jerusalemta kétán Antiok.
