You have life with God because I told you the good news about Yesu. 20 So, my friend, do this favor to me for the Lord's sake, as a friend in Keriso, help me to feel happy! 21 As I am writing this letter, I totally know you.s will do what I ask. Truly I know that the good actions you.s will do to him will surpass what I tell you.s. 22 One more thing: prepare another house for me, because my hope that I am thinking God will hear your.p talking with God in order that give me back to you.p.

nama: Áuwégh si féfeyot kétamé Efafras, yéndfé emafé yényaweyayénd kwéfkwéf méngon Keriso Yesuene téfnan, 24 a kétamé Mak, Aristakus, Demas a Luk, tane mérne ásáfogh ár.
bt: Greeting talk to you.p from Efafras, who is in darkness house with me for the sake of Keriso Yesu, 24 and from Mak, Aristakus, Demas and Luk, my fellow workers.

nama: Ngárám so yém téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisoene féfefaf méinyotio.
bt: May the grace of our Lord Yesu Keriso be with you.p all.

nama: Tándái Ngánján korayau téfene ákiafefaf yéfe kénjúmé Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár tárfár mbinotma a tárfár ende, 2 wénde sénonjo kerker efghon fá té noraye téfefaf yánjo Ambum kénjúmé. Ngánjánam yuyafoi yánjo Ambum so tawanangé méinyotio rokar, a Ngánjánam wéi yafrotam mband marat yánjo Ambum kénjúmé. 3 Ambumom yufarote Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghé a yém ndené sáfotio Ngánján, yémo yerete méinyotio mband marat yánjo waftafé sie. Soramé yémo yénmawafrotam yaufiyam wányányéghét, fá námnjoi Ngánjánene tambén ngángata njafaran. 4 Yéné kénjúmé yémo yénmauyafote yéné, Ambumom minde ndimbale ewainde njafar si résagh ár. Ndené yameyo yétkwén Ngánjánam yánjo Ambumoe siyaramai siyém merfifi, yéné yétkwénam ewainde njafar si résagh árfene yétkwén.
bt: In the past God spoke to our ancestors through them the God's people who make know(prophets) many times and in many ways, 2 but today in last days he has spoken to us through his Son. God appointed his Son receive will all things, and he also made the world through his Son. 3 The Son reflects Godʼs big light(glory) and he is the exact likeness of God, he is holding all world by his powerful talk. After he had done cleaning for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God in heaven. 4 Through that it shows us this, the Son very greatly surpasses the man carrying heavenly message. In the same way the name God gave his Son is very good, that name surpasses the names of the man carrying heavenly message.

nama: Ngánjánam yau yémndai ámb njafar si résagh ár, "Fém ném tane Ambum; sénonjo yénd té nénamndan fene Afé"? o wéikor, "Yénd so wém yáne Afé, a fá so yém tane Ambum"?
bt: God never said this to any man carrying heavenly message, “You are my Son; today I have become your.p Father”? or again “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son”?

nama: A wéikor, táf Ngánjánam njam ténramangé yánjo fronde sénko Ambum yénan mbandot, fá noraye, "Méinyotio Ngánjánene njafar si résagh árém so yáwefnotat!"
bt: And again, when God brings his own firstborn Son into the world, he says, “Let all Godʼs men carrying heavenly message(angels) worship him.”

nama: Ngánján noraye njafar si résagh árfenemén ndené yéné, "Yémo eyátote yánjo njafar si résagh ár ndené mbormbornit, a yánjo faiofnar ásáfogh ár fés kánkékánkénit, "
bt: God says about the men carrying heavenly message(angels) like this, “He makes his own the men carrying heavenly message(angels) like winds, and his without.payment workers(servants) like flames of fire.”

nama: Wénde Ambummén fá noraye, "Fene limánégh ámnjogh sifayé, o Ngánján, so nakete tuot. a fémon so yélimnde Ngánján limánégh mbarkánd wétuafé
bt: But about the Son he says, “Your.s respectful sitting chair(throne), O God, will last forever; and you.s will be led God's kingdom with a walking stick of justice.

nama: Fémo té yuwetam mbarkánd yam a méndméndat yéfandam yaufi yam, yénémaménnde, O Ngánján, fene Ngánjánam, té néfagharyéng fém, a yéméindai fene yékwén nénamndai ndimbal a fémo yayarai ndimbal áuwégh. Fene yétkwénam ewainde méinyotio fene fútarfene.
bt: You.s have loved straightness and hated wickedness, therefore, o God, your.s God, poured oil in you.s and caused your name to become great and you.s feel grat happines. by anointing you.s with the oil of joy.”

nama: Fá wéi noraye, "Ufroghan, Yuré, fémo yékmai mband maratoene téfén a njafar em fene ngángé ásáfoghmén.
bt: He also says, “In the creation, Lord, you.s laid the foundations of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your.s hands.

nama: Njafar a mband so kolindangi, wénde fém so nakete, njafar a mband so méinyotio kényáyátongi tándáwár sálámenit.
bt: The heaven and the earth will perish, but you.s will remain, the heaven and the earth will all become like old clothes.

nama: Fémo so ekéne fá ndenanit ángégh, sálámenit fá so tawengo. Wénde fém ném ndenaro, a fene yunjéf yau so kefongi."
bt: You.s will roll them like a rope, like clothes you.s will change them. But you.s are the same, and your.s years will not end.”

nama: Yau Ngánján ámb norayai njafar si résagh árfefaf, wénde yánjo Ambumoefaf, "Kámnjo tane tambén ngángata kétánotio yéndon tákman fene mék téfnár fene kafkaf mbanmbanan"
bt: God never said to any of the men carrying heavenly message(angels), but to his Son, “Sit at my right hand until I put your.s enemies under your.s feet”

nama: Wénde méinyotio njafar si résagh ár sem mé faiofnar ásáfogh ár. Fá sem mbérmbér eném kétamé Ngánjáne tambén ménát wáutárghét yéfeyot emofem so tawanangi wanjarégh.
bt: But all the men carrying heavenly message(angels) are only without.payment workers. They are spirits came from God to help for those who will receive salvation.

nama: Yénémaménnde, yénd so náyáretam merkéráye kétán efalofaf yéndfem té náyárayém, ménát yénd yau sái ténférnangé nayu. 2 Si Ngánjánam ténmarametau njafar si résagh ár kénjúmé káyátotawét túmén efalo, a ár tamorwén tékrátan ménamén táráfnawét nák a méinyotio áyáréghofnar yaman. 3 Némayam yénméinde yénd mwighat ndené yénd so kengongém táf njam yéndfem yékenam yéné ndimbal wanjarégh? Yuré Yesumyo té fronde mat táyátotau yéné wanjaréghmén, fá emofem tiyáretawét efalo yiyátoyénd téfefaf yéné siyém efalo yém. 4 A Ngánjánam wéi efalo tiyátotau si ménaye ufarogh yame, sunsune yame a fénaté sélngwélafé yame a ménaye tawarametau fok Ngánján Mbérmbéram mé yáne méndeyanro. Emo yénlimnde yénd wanjaréghfaf
bt: Therefore, we must listen well to the truth we have heard, so that we do not drift far. 2 The talk God gave us through the men carrying heavenly message(angels) has always proved true, and people were in pain because they were breaking the law and all disobedient action. 3 What makes us think that we can run away if we ignore this big salvation? The Lord himself first announced this salvation, and those who heard him proved to us that it is true. 4 And God also really proved talk by showing things, surprising things and various special workings(miracles) and by giving gifts of the God Spirit by his own will. The one who leads us to salvation

nama: Ngánjánam yau ekmai njafar si résagh ár ménát sái yélimndat soramé yu árké yéném-árké mband maratmén yénd norayam. 6 Wénde ámb sifayan Buk si kénjún noraye: "O Ngánján, némé ár em fém snemne yéfenemén, o ndené ároene ambum fémo sái yarawena?
bt: God has not placed the men carrying heavenly message(angels) as rulers over the new world to come—the world of which we speak. 6 But in some place in the Scriptures say: “O God, what are men you.s are thinking of them, or a son of man that you.s care for him?

nama: Fémo yékmai fá njafar si résagh árfene yuwan tekofnarot. A fémo utémnde a yaramai yá ndimbal kwayaghaye.
bt: You.s put him under the men carrying heavenly message(angels) for a while. And you.s honoured him and gave him with big light(glory)

nama: Fémo ekmai méinyotio rokar yáne wafté mbanmbanan."
bt: You.s put everything under his power.”

nama: Wénde yéndfem yéfandam Yesu. Ngánjánam tekofnarot yiyátoi Yesu yuwan njafar si résagh árfeta. Sénonjo yéfandam ndené Ngánjánam té yaram Yesue ndimbal kwayaghé a utmanégh ménamén yánjo kéran tékrát yéfandam, ménát Ngánjánene ngáráme yémo so tufongai kér méinyotio árét.
bt: But we see Yesu. God mad Yesu lower from the men carrying heavenly message(angels) for a while. And now we see that God gave to Yesu big light(glory) and honor because of his death he suffered, so that through God's grace he should taste death for every people.

nama: Yéné mé mbarkánd témorwén ndené Ngánján, endeyot a emo yafrotam méinyotio rokar, so enérse yánjo tárfár ambum ndimbal kwayaghat Yesuene tékrát kénjúmé. Ngánjánam yiyátoi Yesu féwénféwénofnar limánégh ár, efe féyotaro yém sái enérsa fá wanjaréghét.
bt: It was only straight that God, for whom and who made everything, should bring his many children into big light(glory) through the suffering of Yesu. God made Yesu a without.flaw leading man, who is enough to bring them into salvation.

nama: Sénonjo Yesu a fá efe eyátote njúnnjúnofnar ewam ámbiro Afé. Yénémamén Yesu yau yém wáiwáiyafé sái emndeta fá yáne fútar. 12 Fá norayai Ngánjánefaf, "Yéndon so marat tiyáton fene mer yétkwén yéfeyot tane árét; Yéndon so némuyotan fém fene méinyotio ár kénjúmé.
bt: Now Yesu and those whom he makes without.dust(holy) have the one Father. That's why Yesu is not ashamed to call them his friends. 12 He says to God, “I will make wide your.s good name to them my people; I will praise you.s among all your.s people.”

nama: Fá wéi norayai, "Yéndon so efalo yiyátotan fá." A yéné sieyo fá norayai, "Yéna yénd wém mérne yémafé ambumtértér Ngánjánam árké waramai ta."
bt: He also says, “I will trust him.” And he says with this talk, “Here I am with the children which God has given me.”

nama: Ménamén ambumtértér sem ár férkafé a fifiyafé, Yesu wéi nénamndai férkafé a fifiyafé. Yémo yéné yafrotam ménát yáne kér kénjúmé yémo so tasérmbotau Sáifér, efe yém kroene waftafaf, 15 mé yéné endero so yémon tautorngé fá efe té tamorwén faiofnar ásáfogh ár méinyotio yéfene ghérsayan ménamén yéfene kér férférmén. 16 Yénd méinyotio mat yéném ndené Yesu yénmormén wáutárghét Ebraamene ákiambumat, yau ménát wáutárghét njafar si résagh árfet. 17 Yéné téfnan Yesu nénamndai yáne fútarnit méinyotio ende, ménát fá so kénamndangai yéfene ndimbal mwighé kér ro a ghérghérsisafé Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár Ngánjánrére, a yémo so yafrote ghéngén árfene yaufimén. 18 Ménamén yémonyo tékrát yéfandam njam fá yufotaménd, fá féyotaro yém wáutárghét yéfe efe em ufoghan.
bt: Because the children are people with blood and flesh, Yesu too became with blood and flesh. He did this so that through his death he might destroy Sáifèr, who has the power over death, 15 only in this way he could take out those who were without.payment working men(slaves) in all their lives because of their fear of death. 16 We all know that Yesu came to help the descendants of Ebraam, not to help the men carrying heavenly message(angels). 17 In this reason Yesu became like his friends in all the ways, so that he will be their faithful and merciful High burning sacrifice man(priest) in front of God, and he might did sacrifice for the people's sins. 18 Because he himself saw the pain when he was tempted, he is able to help those who are in temptation.

nama: Yénémaménnde, njúnnjúnofnar fútaréf, efe em Ngánjánene a njafaroene, nemnati yáne Yesu, efe Ngánjánam yénmafaretam sái témorwén Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár yéné ndimbal mwighayan yéndfem árké yéngwaitotam. 2 Yesu témorwén ndimbal mwighé kér ro Ngánjánefaf, emo yuyafoi fá, ndené fété Moses témorwén ndimbal mwighé kér ro yánjo ásáfoghan Ngánjánene méngon. 3 Méngo ramégh ároene yétkwénam yáinde méngoene yétkwén, ndené ende Ngánjánam yuyafoi Yesu témorwén merfifi, yémo yaramai ndimbal yétkwén árkéménayam yáinde yéné yétkwén Mosesene árké témorwén. 4 Méinyotio méngo sem ár té yém méngo ramégh ár, wénde Ngánján yéné yém méinyotio rokar emo eramete. 5 Moses témorwén ndimbal mwighé kér ro ndené fété faiofnar ásáfogh ár Ngánjánene méngon, a fá kafrotau némé Ngánján so korayau soramé yuwon. 6 Wénde Keriso siyém ndimbal mwighé kér ro ndené fété Ambum kété Ngánjánene méngon, a yénd yéném yáne méngo, táf yénd njam naweretam kétán téfene mwighé táikér a téfene méghmégh Keriso kénjún. Enagh yéfeyot Ngánjánene ár
bt: Therefore, without.dust(holy) friends, who belong to God and heaven, think of Yesu, whom God sent to be the High burning sacrifice man(High priest) in this big mind(faith) which we proclaim. 2 Yesu was faithful to God, who chose him, just as Moses was faithful in his work in God's house. 3 The name of a man who builds a house surpasses the name of the house itself, in the same way God saw that Yesu was very good, so he gave him a big name that surpasses the name Moses had. 4 Every house has a builder, but God is the one who has built all theings. 5 Moses was faithful as a without.payment worker in God's house, and he spoke of what God would say in the future. 6 But Keriso is faithful as the Son there in God's house, and we are his house, if we hold firmly to our mind courage and our hope in Keriso. Rest for God's people

nama: Yénémamén Ngánján Mbérmbér noraye, "Sénonjo fém njam náyáretati yáne kém,
bt: So the God Spirit says, “Today when you.p hear his voice,

nama: yau eyátotati táikér féfenjo tikéf yá tambén, ndené Israil skufrotawét njam yau káyáretawét, njam yémofem tufotawét Ngánjánene métar kété fanfne yuwon.
bt: do not make harden your.p heart against him, as Israil did when they did not listen, when they test God's patience there in the clear place(desert),

nama: Ánde féfene ákiam kwufotawét yénd ndemamén yémofem téfandawét némé yéndon tafrotawén yéné 40 yunjfan.
bt: Where your.p ancestors tested me even though they saw what I did for 40 years.

nama: Yéné téfnan yénd kwémorwén nékw yéné ár sátafaf; a yénd korayawén, 'Yéfene tikéf sem túmén nayu, a fá yau mat náyátongi tane end.'
bt: In this reason I was angry with that generation; and I said, ‘Their hearts are always far away, and they did not know my ways.'

nama: Tane nékwan yéndo tafrotan unyéghofnar si, "Fá yau so kényawangi tane enagh sifayat."
bt: In my anger I made a without.lying talk(promise), "They will never enter my resting place.”

nama: Tane fútaréf, ndené yau féfefaf so yém yaufi kér ro a efalo yátoghofnar tikéf árkéménayam kémengongé kétamé ghérsé Ngánjáneta. 13 Fémofem a mat eyátotat méinyotio ár túmén efghon, ndené fété kés yémndetat, "Sénonjo, " ménát yau so ewawindat yaufiyamam a táikér eyátotati Ngánjáneta. 14 Yénd té nénamndayém sái yénmafnyang Keriso kénjún táf yénd njam naweretam taikrataye kétánotio yatoghfaf mwighé táikér téfene árké témorwén fronde. 15 Wénde yau ewawalinda mat si: "Njam fémofem náyáretati Ngánjanene si sénonjo, yau táikér eyátotati féfene tikéf yá tambén, ndené féfene ákif stamorwén táf tékenawét."
bt: My friends, should be none of you.p has a sinful and unbelieving heart that turns away from the living God. 13 You.p must know all people every day, as it is called, "Today," so that sin decieve none of you.p and you.p hardened against God. 14 We have become to share in Keriso if we hold firmly to the end the confidence which we had at the beginning. 15 But never forget the warning talk: “When you.p hear God's talk today, do not harden your.p heart against him, as your. ancesters were when they rebelled him.”

nama: Efe fá tamorwén efe náyárayénd a nakenayénd? Fá méinyotio efe Mosesém eutoryéng kétamé Ijifta? 17 A endefefaf témorwén Ngánján nékw 40 yunjéf? Yéné árfefaf emofem yaufi tawafrotawét, efe kérwén kuitawét kété fanfne yuwan. 18 A endefefaf wét Ngánján korayau ndené fá yau so kényawangi yáne enagh sifayafaf? Ngánján korayau yéfefaf emofem yau tiyáretawét fá? 19 Yénémamén yéndfem yéfandam ndené fá yau eumyoi élaughét yáne enaghfaf ménamén yéfene efalo yátoghofnarmén.
bt: Who were they who heard and rebelled? All those who Moses led out of Ijift. 17 And with whom was God angry for forty years? With that people who did sin, who fell down dead there in the desert. 18 And to whom did God said that they would never enter his resting place? Was God speaking to those who disobeyed? 19 So we see that he did not allow them to enter his rest because of their unbelief.

nama: Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si élaughét yáne enaghfaf mé yakai, féyo yénd férférafé yéném ménamén fém ámb náláitati ménát sái yéfandati yéné yam. 2 Yéndfem té náyárayém mer si, ndenéyameyo yémofem kés náyárayénd. Yémofem yéné si náyárayénd, wénde yéné yam yau mer ewawafrotam, ménamén yémofem njam náyárayénd, yau yumyoyénd ndimbal mwighaye. 3 Sénonjo yénd emofem efalo yiyátongém nélawangém yéné enaghét, ndené fété Ngánján ndernáye norayai, "Féyo yénd nékw kwémorwén a yafrotamén unyéghofnar si: 'Fá yau so kélawangi tane enaghfaf.'"
bt: God's without.lying talk(promise) of entering his rest still stands, so we ought to have fear that some of you might fail to see this thing. 2 We have heard the good talk, just as they did. They heard the message, but it did them no good, because when they heard it, they did not accept it with big mind(faith). 3 Now we who truly approve(believe) enter that rest, just as God said, “So I was angry and made a without.lying talk(promise): ‘They will never enter my rest.’ ”

nama: Ámb sifayan fá té noraye ménamén 7 efoghmén Ngánjánene si kénjún: "Yéné 7 efghon Ngánján nenam kétamé méinyotio yánjo ásáfoghta." 5 A wéikor ámb si ménjan fá noraye, "Fá yau so kélawangi tane enaghfaf."
bt: In some place he has spoken about 7 day in God's talks: “On 7 day God rested from all his works.” 5 And again in other passage he says, “They will never enter my rest.”

nama: Féyo Ngánjánene enagh kété yém ménát ár sái kélawangi enaghét, wénde fá efe fronde náyárayénd mer si nuyayénd élaughét ménamén yémofem yau yiyárayénd Ngánján. 7 Yénémaménnde, Ngánjánam wéikor yiyátoi yéné efogh, yémndete "Sénonjo." Tárfár yunjéf soramé fá korayau Deifide kénjúmé, ndené Buk si kénjún té norayai, "Sénonjo njam fém náyáretati yáne si, yau táf táikér eyátotati féfenjo tikéf."
bt: So God's rest is there for people to enter, but those who first heard this good news failed to enter because they disobeyed God. 7 Therefore, God again set a this day, calling it “Today.” So many years after he spoke through Deifid, as in the Buk(scripture) already said: “Today when you.p hear his talk, do not harden your.p hearts.”

nama: Táf njam Josuam té ewaramai yéfe enagh, Ngánján yau so korayau soramé ménamén ámb efoghmén. 9 Yéna mé yém Safat enagh Ngánjánene árét, 10 árké ár efe nélawetat Ngánjánene enaghfaf so wéi nene yéfenjo ásáfoghta, ndené fété Ngánján kés nenam yánjo ásáfoghta. 11 Yénémaménnde, yénd so nufotam sái nélawetam yéné enagh sifayafaf, ménát efe yau so nuitat ndené fété fá kés kufrotawét ménamén yéfene áyáréghofnarmén.
bt: If Josua had given them rest, God would not speak later about another day. 9 There still remains a Safat-rest for the people of God, 10 which people who enter Godʼs rest also rest from their works, just as God rested from his works. 11 Therefore, let us try to enter that rest place, so that no one will fail as they did because of their without.hearing.

nama: Ngánjánene si siyém ghérsé yém a waftafé yém. Yáne séntayam yáinde witmafé sénté náifé. Yénayam yénfete ánde kénjú mwighé a mbérmbér nafángotat, a ánde budakéf a mér nafángotat. Yénayam moghan ekmete kénjú mwighé a ménde. 13 Yau méinyotio ufroghmén rokar átnoghe em kétamé Ngánjáne tambén. Méinyotio rokar siyém wafaréghofnar a safénro yáne ghékfan. Yéndfem so ewafenam yáfaf méinyotio yéndfem némé tanwafrotam téfene ghérsayan. Yesu yém minde Ndimbal limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár
bt: The talk of God is alive and powerful. Its sharpness surpasses sharpnesses the double-edged knife. It cuts all the way through, to where soul and spirit meet, to where joints and marrow come together. It judges the desires and thoughts of the heart. 13 Nothing in all creation is hidden from God. Everything is uncovered and clear before his eyes. We must explain to him all what we have done during our life. Yesu is the big High burning sacrifice man

nama: Yéné téfnan téfene yém minde Ndimbal limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár efe té yéngmormén njafarot, Yesu Ngánjánene Ambum, féyo yénd so naweretam taikrataye yáfaf a yau kékmbongém ndimbal mwighayan yáfaf. 15 Téfene Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár mat yém téfene njangén, yémo té tafángotau méinyotio sáfot ufogh téfefaf sem, wénde yau yaufi yafrotam. 16 Yénd so yéném táikér mwighafé kétán Ngánjánene ngárám limánégh ámnjogh sifayafaf. Kété yéndfem so tanangém ghérghérsisé a tinjongém ngárám sái yénmáutána yénd njam ménde yéném.
bt: In this reason we have a big High burning sacrifice man who has ascended into heaven, Yesu the Son of God, so let us hold firmly to him and never stop in big mind(faith) to him. 15 Our High burning sacrifice man know our weaknesses, he have faced all shape temptation we do, but he did not do sin. 16 Let us come strong mind to God's respectful sitting chair(throne) of grace. There we will receive mercy and find grace to help us when we need it.

nama: Méinyotio limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár ekafetat kétamé árta a tékafangi Ngánjánene ásáfoghét yéfene yétkwénan, ménát sái erametat yéfenjo ramégh a ghéngén yaufimén. 2 Yémo so ewafrote merafé yam yéfefaf efe em áyáréghofnar a nayu, ménamén fá té yénmormén ufogh yamamé. 3 Yéné téfnan yémo té eramai ghéngén yánjo yaufimén a ndenéyameyo árfene yaufimén. 4 Yau árké árém tawanangi yéfendio utmanégh, fá so fété témotangé Ngánjánam yéné ásáfoghmén, ndené fété Aron kés yémormén.
bt: They select every leading burning sacrifice man among the people and appoint to serve God on their name, to offer their gifts and sacrifices for sins. 2 He will do good things to those who are ignorant and far, because he had gone through test things. 3 In this reason he has offered sacrifices for his sins and also people's sins. 4 No one take honor for himself, God must call him for this work, just as Aron was.

nama: Ndené yameyo, Keriso yau kawarmbotau fáyo ménát sái kénamndangai limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár. Wénde Ngánjánam yémndai fá, "Fém ném tane Ambum, sénonjo efghon yénd té nénamndan fene Afé."
bt: In the same way, Keriso did not honor himself in order to become a leading burning sacrifice men. But God said to him, “You.s are my Son; today I have become your.p Father."

nama: Fá wéi noraye ámb sifayan, "Fém ném ghéngén wárogh ár tuot ndené Melkisedek."
bt: He also said in another place, “You.s are burning sacrifice man forever, like Melkisedek.”

nama: Yesuene ghérsayan yéné mband maratan, fá Ngánjánafé koraiwét a yamnendau ndimbal yefé a yengamafé fá, emo so tanjarngai fá kétamé kérta, a Ngánjánam yiyárai yáne Ngánjánafé orayégh ménamén yáne efalo ghérsé yáfaf. 8 Ndemamén Yesu témorwén Ngánjánene Ambum, fá kotametau áyárégh yam kétamé tékrát yamta. 9 Njam fá yiyátoi féwénféwénofnar, fá nénamndai téfén tuot ghérsé wanjarégh yéfeyot méinyotio emofem yiyáretat fá, 10 a Ngánjánam yékafai fá sái kénamndangai limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár, ndené Melkisedek. Mat si ménamén uyéghmén
bt: In Yesu's life on this earth, he talked with God and requested with big cries and tears to him, who could save him from death, and God heard his talking with God because of his true life to him. 8 Even though Yesu was God's Son, he learned obedience from sufferings. 9 When he became without.flaw(perfect), he became the source of eternal salvation for all those who obey him, 10 and God chose him to be leading burning sacrifice man, as Melkisedek. Warning against falling down

nama: Téfefaf yém tárfár si sái norayam Melkisedekenemén, wénde táikér yém waferghét féfefaf, ménamén fém mé nawanetati otameghét. 12 Kété té yénmormén efogh féyotaro féfeyot sái em watamegh ár, wénde fémofem mé eronjati ámb ár féfe fronde watameghét Ngánjánene simén. Fém ndenanit em fasri tértér emofem efamndat nonoro a yau so enetat táikér njamke. 13 Árké ár emo efamnde nono mé yém fasri, a mafina yém watamegh mbarkándmén o mámáfimén. 14 Wénde táikér njamke yém yéfeyot ndimbal árét, efe otamegh kénjúmé yém wayamngogh so yém yénayan mer a yaufi.
bt: We have many talk to say about Melkisedek, but it is hard to explain to you.p, because you.p are still delay to learn. 12 There has been enough time for you.p to be teaching men, but you.p still need someone to teach you.p the first of God's talk. You are like babies who drink only milk and cannot eat solid food. 13 Which people who drinks milk is still a baby, and doesn't know the teaching about straight and wrong things. 14 But solid food is for them adults, who through learning are able to distinguish between good and evil.

nama: Yénémaménnde yénd yau kétándro so túmén yéném afrátaye mat áyátogh Kerisoene si árkéméné yéndfem té yanayém rényéghan. Yénd méndefnar yéném sái térnyangém méinyotio wéikor téfén yaufiyam awengoghmén, a ekmetam téfene ndimbal mwighé Ngánjánefaf. 2 Yénd té mat náyátoyém nu walitoghmén, ngángé fayoghmén, kérta anoghmén, a tuot afaloghmén. 3 Néméwét, yéndfem so taurongém fronde watamegh tékafangém táikér watamegh, njam Ngánjánam tumyongé.
bt: So then we must not continue to remain only at the easy to understand talk of Keriso which we received at the beginning. We don't need to start all over again the foundation of turning away from sin, and putting our big mind(faith) to God. 2 We have already learned about about baptisms, the laying on of hands, the rising of the dead, and the eternal seperation. 3 Yes, we can leave first teaching and choose strong teaching if God allows.

nama: Yéné siyém féyotarofnar yém sái enwangotat yaufiyam awengoghfaf efe fronde té etremngoi kwayaghayam, emofem té ernoyénd njafarmén tambén, emofem té ersat Ngánján Mbérmbéran, 5 emofem té ernoyénd Ngánjánene sioene mer a wafté soramamén. 6 Wénde yéné soramé, yémofem euroyénd yéfene ndimbal mwighé. Yéné féyotarofnar yém yéfe wangoghét fá wéikor yaufita awengoghét, ménamén wéikor yémofem Ngánjánene Ambum yénamndat wafatarégh wénan a yékmetat wáiwáian marataye.
bt: This is impossible to bring back to repentance of sin those who light enlighted once, who have tasted the heavenly gift, who have shared in the God Spirit, 5 who have tasted the goodness of the talk of God and the powers of the coming age. 6 But after all that, they abandoned their big mind(faith). This is impossible to bring them back to turn away again, because they are again nailing the Son of God on the cross and putting him to public shame.

nama: Mband árkéménayam yénete nu afaméghmén táf njam nuite kétán a árkéménayam efune mbwe fifiotio yéfeyot endefeyot eitaménd, yéné siyém Ngánjánene frengégh yém. 8 Wénde mband árkéménayam yéfune wétwét a yus téfnat, siyém fifiofnar. Ngánjánam so tekofnar tafalngongé yéné mband. Soramé so nakérne fésén.
bt: Land which drinks in the rain when often falls on it and which produces a crop useful to those for whom it is farmed, this is God's blessing. 8 But land which produces thorns and weeds, it is without.meaning. God will very soon speak against that ground. Later it will burn on fire.

nama: Téfene tikfafé fútaréf, yéndfem ewaufrotam yéné ménamén yénd ménde yéném fém sái merero mé engém. Yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánjánam té ewanjaryéng fém a ndené yémo so mé engéfrende fém. 10 Ngánján túmén yém mbarkánd. Fá yau so tawalindangé ásáfogh fémofem árké tawafrotati a uwégh árké té tuyafotati yáyot yénaye tarawenati ámb efalo yátogh ár, ndené fémofem mé kés ewafrotati. 11 Téfene ndimbal ménde siyém fémofem mé so euwetati ámb ár yéné teke njamandio ghérsé mé em, ménát so nufrote ndené némat fém méghmégh em so efalo tiyátongi. 12 Yénd méndefnar yéném fém sái kénamndangi sákér, wénde sái ewafénmetati fá efe em ndimbal mwighafé a métar ghérsé kénjúmé ewanayénd Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si. Ngánjánene unyéghofnar siam yénérse méghmégh
bt: Our heartful friends, we are telling you this because we want you to continue well. We are know that God has saved you and that he will continue to bless you. 10 God is always straight. He will not forget the work which you.p did and the love which you.p have shown him by carrying for other approving truth people, as you.p still do. 11 Our big desire is you.p keep on loving other people as long as while life lasts, so that it make certain that what you.p hope will come true. 12 We do not want you.p to become lazy, but to imitate those who are through big mind(faith) and patience receive God's without.lying talk(promise). God's without.lying talk(promise) bring hope

nama: Njam Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si norayam Ebraamefaf, ménamén kété yau témorwén ámb ndimbalfifi Ngánján yáyot, yémo yánjo yétkwénan norayai táf yémo njam yafrotam unyéghofnar si. 14 Fá norayai, "Yéndo unyéghofnar si némndetan fém ndené yéndon so kénéfrendangén fém, a kémaraman tárfár ákiambum." 15 Féyo Ebraam kawanjetau métare, a yémo yanai némé Ngánjánam té unyéghofnar si norayai. 16 Njam árém unyéghofnar si ewafrotat, yémofem tanmongi ámb ár emo ewainde fá ménát so tawerangé fá kété. A unyéghofnar siam tafrendangé méinyotio amroréghta. 17 Ngánján ménde témorwén sái tuyafongai safét ndené yémo so tengongai yánjo mwighé. Fá ménde témorwén yáne ár mat so káyátongayénd ndené yémo so tafrendangai fá. Féyo njam yémo unyéghofnar si yafrotam, yémo wéi yánjo yétkwénan norayai ménát sái táikér tiyátongai. 18 Yénémamén Ngánjánam té yénmaramai téfe yéné sómbio yam, yáne unyéghofnar si a sotafé si. Yéné sómbio yam sere wengoghofnar ménamén yéné siyém féyotarofnar yém Ngánján sái nuinda. Yénémamén, yénd efe té nenngoyém yáfaf ménát wanjarghét so yém táikér mwighé sái yéndfem yéretam méghmégh árké té yéramai téfeyot. 19 Téfene yém yéné méghmégh ndené bout kémboghmén tot téfénnit téfene ghérsat. Yéné yém táikér a awafaroghét, a yéngém kétán njúnnjúnofnar satat wáwefnogh méngot ndimbal sáláme árké tamangérwén yéné kénjúmé. 20 Ménát téfe wáutárghét, Yesu fronde nélawai yéné satat. Fá té nénamndai limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár tuot, ndené Melkisedek.
bt: When God made his without.lying talk(promise) to Ebraam, because there was no one bigger than by God himself, he said by his name when he made his promise. 14 He said, “I say without.lying talk(promise) you.p that I will bless you.p, and give you.p many descendants.” 15 So Ebraam waited patiently, and he received what God said promising talk. 16 When people make a without.lying talk(promise), they call on someone who surpasses themselves to hold them to it. And the without.lying talk(promise) settles all arguments. 17 God wanted to show very clearly that he would never change his purpose. He wanted his people to know that he would certainly bless them. So when he made this without.lying talk(promise), he also used his own name to make it strong. 18 So God has given us these two things, his without.lying talk(promise) and his strong talk(oath). These two things are unchangeable because this is impossible for God to lie. Therefore, we who have fled to him for solvation can have strong mind(confidence) as we hold the hope which is offered to us. 19 We have this hope as steal for stopping boat(an anchor) for our lives. This is strong and trustworthy, and goes into the without.dust(holy) room in the worshipping house through the big cloth that hangs(curtain) . 20 In order to help us, Yesu entered first into that room. He has become a leading burning sacrifice man forever, as Melkisedek.

nama: Yéné Melkisedek témorwén warsagh ár Salem ndimbal tesenmén a wéi témorwén Minde Tukén Ngánjánene ghéngén wárogh ár. Njam Ebraam kénangotau méngotuot soramé ndimbal mékta njam nawaindai ásméghan ámb tárfár warsagh árfe ngita, Melkisedekém yéfángoi fá a yéfrendai. 2 Féyo Ebraamém enérsam ámbiro 10mén méinyotio yémon árké ewanai ásméghan a eramai Melkisedeke, yáne yétkwén fifi siyém, 'Warsagh ár mbarkándmén.' A fá témorwén Salemmén warsagh ár, yáne fifi siyém, "Warsagh ár krufrátmén." 3 Ámb yau yékai yáne yárefanemén o yátmanemén, o ámb yáne yákifenemén; yau yékai yáne áráféghmén a kérmén. Fá mé yém ghéngén wárogh ár tuot, ndenanit yém Ngánjánene Ambum.
bt: This Melkisedek was the ruling man(king) of the city of Salem and also burning sacrifice man of Most High God. When Ebraam was returning home after big fight when he won in fighting against many ruling men(kings) being killed, Melkisedek met him and blessed him. 2 Then Ebraam brought out of a tenth of everything which he had got from fighting and gave them to Melkisedek, his name meaning is “the ruling man(king) of straightness.” And he was the ruling man(king) of Salem, its meaning is "the ruling man(king) of peace." 3 Nothing is written about his father or mother, or any of his anscestes, no written of his birth or of his death. He remains burning sacrifice man forever, like the Son of God.

nama: Mato téfandangi nderé ndimbal yéné Melkisedek témorwén. Ndemamén Ebraam, Israil árfene ndimbal sénko ár, mat náyátoi nderé ndimbal Melkisedek témorwén yénayan siyaramai yá ámbiro 10mén némé yémo té enmanai ásméghan. 5 Sénonjo ghéngén wárogh ár, efe em Lefiene áki ambum, ewáfárete Mosesene nákam ménát sái ekafetat ámbiro 10mén mani kétamé méinyotio ár tambén, ndemamén fá wéi em Ebraamene ákiambum. 6 Wénde Melkisedek, efe yau témorwén Lefiene ákimén, enkafai ámbiro 1omén kétamé Ebraameta a Melkisedekém yafayoi frengégh yáyot Ebraame, fá emo té ewanai unyéghofnar si Ngánjánenemén. 7 A amogh siofnar, ár endefaf yém wafté ménát frengéghét siyém túmén ndimbal yém yáyot efe yéfrende. 8 Ghéngén wárogh ár emo ekafete ámbiro 10mén em ár efe kér so em, wénde Melkisedekém ewainde fá, ménamén téfe té yénmaufroyénd ndené fá ghérsé mé so yéngém. 9 Ámbiro mété korayangé ndené Lefi, emo ekafete ámbiro 10mén, fai tawafrotau ámbiro 10mén Ebraame kénjúmé. 10 Ménamén njam Melkisedekém yéfángoi Ebraam, Lefi táf mé témorwén áráféghofnar yáki kénjún. Yesu ndenanit Melkisedek
bt: Just think how big this Melkisedek was. Even Ebraam, the big head man of Israil people, recognized how big Mekisedek was by giving him a tenth of what he had taken in the fighting. 5 Now burning sacrifice men, who are descendants of Lefi, are commanded by the law of Moses to collect one tenth from all the people, even though they are also descendants of Ebraam. 6 But Melkisedek, who was not descendant of Lefi, collected one tenth from Ebraam and Melkisedek placed a blessing upon Ebraam, he who had received the without.lying talk(promise) from God. 7 And without question, the person who has the power to bless is always big to him who is blessed. 8 The burning sacrifice man(priest) efe collect one tenth are men who die, but Melkisedek surpasses them, because they told us that he is still lives. 9 One might say that Lefi, who collects one tenth, paid one tenth through Ebraam. 10 Because when Melkisedek met Ebraam, Lefi was still without.birth in his ancestor. Yesu Like Melkisedek

nama: Yéné témorwén téfén Lefiene ghéngén wárogh yaman ndené Ngánjánam nák ewaramai Israil árét. Sénonjo, njam Lefiene ghéngén wárogh árfene ásáfogh témorwén féwénféwénofnar, kété yau so témorwén ámb ménde fénataye sáfot ghéngén wárogh ár sái kufarngé, fá efe yém ghéngén wárogh ár ndené Melkisedek, yau Aronnit. 12 Njam ghéngén wárogh yam tengongai, nák wéi ndenáyo so tawengongai. 13 Yánemén yénd snorayam yém fénataye mérénmén, endefene ár yau násáfotat ghéngén wárogh kitarayan. 14 Yéndon némé yésérmbnan siyém, téfene Yuré yénmormén kétamé Judane mérénta, a Mosesém yau emndai Judane mérén sái kufnyangayénd ghéngén wárogh ásáfoghan. 15 Némé yénd té korayam té náyátote safénro sénonjo. Ámb sáfot ghéngén wárogh ár té nufar, efe yém ghéngén wárogh ár ndenanit Melkisedek. 16 Fá nénamndai ghéngén wárogh ár, yau ménamén fá témorwén Lefiene mérénmén, wénde yéné ghérsane wafté kénjúmé árkéméné yau so nirone. 17 Buk si noraye yánemén, "Fém ném ghéngén wárogh ár tuot, ndené fété Melkisedek."
bt: This was on the basis of the Lefi's burning sacrifice way that God gave the law to the people of Israil. Now, if the work of the Lefi's burning sacrifice men was without.flaw(perfect), there would be no need for a different kind of burning sacrifice man to appear, he who is burning sacrifice man like Melkisedek, not like Aron. 12 If burning sacrifice way is changed, the law also must be changed. 13 About him we are talking is from differen family, whose people do not serve at the sacrificing table . 14 What I mean is, our Lord came from Juda's family, and Moses never said Juda's family joined to the work of burning sacrifice. 15 What we have said becomes even clearer now. Another kind of priest has come, who is a priest like Melkisedek. 16 He became burning sacrifice man, not because he belong to Lefi family, but through the power of a life which never be destroyed. 17 The Buk(scripture)s say about him, “You.s are burning sacrifice man forever, just like Melkisedek.”

nama: Ngánjánam té ewitérmboi tándáwár nák ménamén ghéngén wároghmén, ménamén yéné nák namndai muyafnar, féyo yéné féyotarofnar témorwén téfe wáutárghét. 19 Nákam yau yiyátoi féwénféwénofnar. A sénonjo mer méghmégham té néusénam yéné kénjúmé árkéménáyan yénd yéném kaka Ngánjánefaf.
bt: God has destroyed an old law, because that law became powerless, so it was unable to help us. 19 The law never made without.flaw. And now a good hope has been provided through this which we come near to God.

nama: Kété wéi yém Ngánjánene sotafé si. Kété yau em ndené sotafé si njam ámb ár eyátoyéng ghéngén wárogh ár. 21 Wénde Yesu namndai ghéngén wárogh ár sotafé sie njam Ngánján noraye yáfaf, "Yuram té yafrotam sotafé si a yau so térafarngé yánjo unyéghofnar si: 'Fém ném ghéngén wárogh ár tuot.'"
bt: There is also God's strong talk(oath). There was no such strong talk(oath) when the other people become burning sacrifice men. 21 But Yesu became a burning sacrifice man by a strong talk(oath) when God said to him, “The Lord has made his strong talk(oath) and will not break his without.lying talk(promise): ‘You.s are burning sacrifice men forever.’ ”

nama: Ménamén Ngánjánene sotafé simén, Yesu yéné féyém emo yafrotam mer árnyégh sái kufrotau.
bt: Because of God's strong talk(oath), Yesu is the one who causes a better covenant to be fulfilled.

nama: Ámb fénataye siyém: kété tamorwén tárfár ghéngén wárogh ár, ménamén fá kér emormén a féyotarofnar tamorwén wasamngoghét yéfene ásáfogh. 24 Wénde Yesu mé yém ghéngén wárogh ár tuot, a yáne ghéngén wárogh yam yau so kefonga. 25 Yénémaménnde fá féyotaro yémormén, sénonjo a túmén, ménát wanjarghét méinyotio ár efe eném Ngánjánefaf yá kénjúmé. Fá ghérsé yém tuot ménát sái yém néndkéf ár yéfeyot.
bt: There is another difference: there were many burning sacrifice men, because they died and could not continue their work. 24 But Yesu still remains burning sacrifice man forever, and his burning sacrifice way will never end. 25 Therefore he is able, now and always, to save every people who come to God through him. He lives forever to be middleman for them.

nama: Yesu yéné féyém Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár yénd ende ménde yéném ménamén fá siyém njúnnjúnofnar a téfnofnar, yaufiyam yau. Fá sénonjo yémor fénataye kétamé yaufiyam árta a yuyoi tukét njafarot. 27 Fá yau yém ndené ámb limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár. Yémon yau so eramete ghéngén túmén efghon yánjo yaufiyamot fronde a féyo ámb árfene yaufiyammén. Yémon yéramai ámbiro ghéngén, ambinotaro a méinyotioat njam fáyo ghéngén naramai. 28 Mosesene nákam ekafete ár efe féwénféwénafé em sái tamorwén limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár. Wénde Ngánjánam yafrotam unyéghofnar si sotafé, árké yénmormén soramé nákta, yékafai Ambum, efe té yiyátoi féwénféwénofnar tuot.
bt: Yesu is the the High burning sacrifice man who we need because he is without.dust(holy) and blameless, no sin. He has now live different from sinners and raised above the heavens. 27 He is not like other leading burning sacrifice men. He does not need to offer sacrifices every day for his own sins first and then for the sins of the other people. He offered one sacrifice, once and for all, when he offered sacrifice himself. 28 The law of Moses appoints men who are with.flaw(unperfect) to be leading burning sacrifice men. But God made without.lying talk(promise) strongly, which came later than the law, appoints the Son, who has become without.flaw(perfect) forever.

nama: Yéné siyém si téfén: Téfene té yém Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár, efe námnjoi limánégh ámnjogh sifayan kété njafaran kétamé Ngánjánene tambén ngángata. 2 Fá násáfote ndené fété limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár Minde Njúnnjúnofnar sifayan, efalo farai méngon árkéméné yérametam Yuram, yau ár ngángayam.
bt: This is the foundation of the talk point: We have a High burning sacrifice man, who sits down in the table of high honor in there heaven at God's right hand. 2 He serves as leading burning sacrifice man in the Most Without.dust Place, in the real canves house which Lord built, not by human hands.

nama: Méinyotio limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár ekafetat sái erametat ramégh a ghéngén, a féyo téfene Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár, Yesum so yafrote ghéngén ramégh ndenáyo. 4 Táf Yesu njam yéna témorwén mband maratan, fá yau so témorwén ghéngén wárogh ár, ménamén té yéna em ghéngén wárogh ár emofem erametat ramégh nákmén árkéméné Ju árém so yaitotat. 5 Fá násáfotat wáwefnogh sifayan árkéménayam yafarnje, a mbanmban yém kétán efalofifi kés yém njafaran. Yéné siyém ndenáyo témorwén Mosesefaf. Njam Moses té káfrendau wáwefnogh farai méngo ramghét, Ngánjánam yémndai: "Kor a ném ménát fémo sái ewafrota méinyotio yénamé yéndon kés kémauyafotan fe yéna mband tunakfan." 6 Wénde efaloe yéné ásáfogham Ngánjánam té yaramayéng Yesue ásáfoghét yáinde yéné ásáfogh ewaramai ámb ghéngén wárogh árfet ásáfoghét, ndené fété totér árnyégham yáinde tándáwár árnyégh. Yesu siyém néndkéf ár totér árnyéghmén a yémon ewáutáne totér árnyégh táikér sái em. A njafarmén mer yamam ndené totér árnyégh noraye yényam so ténmaramangé téfe ewaindat mer yam tándáwár árnyégh norayai yéné yam so ténmaramangai téfe.
bt: Every leading burning sacrifice man is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices, and so our High burning sacrifice man, Yesu must also make an offerings. 4 If Yesu were on earth, he would not be burning sacrifice man, because there are burning sacrifice men who offer the gifts because of the laws which the Ju people must follow. 5 They work in worshipping place which is a copy, a shadow of its to real thing in heaven. This is the same as it was with Moses. When Moses was getting ready to build the worshipping canvas house, God told him: “Be sure so that you.s should make everything according to that I have shown you.s on the mountain.” 6 But in fact the work God gave Yesu to do surpasses the work he gave the other burning sacrifice men to do, just as the new covenant surpasses the old covenant. Yesu is a middleman(mediator) of new covenant and he helped the new covenant to be strong. And the good things of heaven that the new covenant says it will give us surpasses the good things the old covenant said it would give us.

nama: Njam fronde árnyégh témorwén téfnofnar, kété wéi yau so témorwén ámb ménde ménát yénamayomén árnyégh sái tasongai yáne sifayan. 8 Wénde Ngánjánam yémonyo yinjoi téfén yánjo árfefaf a norayai: "Yuré noraye, 'Efogh so yéném, njam yéndon so yérametan totér árnyégh yémafé Israil árfé a Judamén árfé.
bt: If the first covenant had been faultless, there would have been no need for a second covenant to replace its place. 8 But God himself saw fault with his people, and said, “The Lord says, 'The days are coming, when I will make a new covenant with the people of Israil and with the people of Juda.

nama: Yéné árnyégh yau so ndené yém yéndon sirametamén yéfene ákiafé njam yéndon tarsawén fá ngángaye a elimayén kétamé Ijif mbandta. Fá yau kawéretawét ndimbal mwighaye kétán tane árnyéghfaf, féyo yéndon ewawengoyén tane sém yéfeyot.
bt: This covenant will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors when I took them by the hand and led them out of Ijif. They didn't hold faithfully to my covenant so I turned my back on them.

nama: Wénde yéné siyém totér árnyégh yéndon so yafrotan yémafé Israil árfé yéné efghon, noraye Yuré: Yéndon so ekmetan tane nák yéfene mwighayan a efarotan yéfene tikfan. Yénd so wém yéfene Ngánján, a fá so em tane ár.
bt: But this is new covenant I will make with the people of Israil in that days, says the Lord: I will put my laws in their minds and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.

nama: A yémofem yau so ewatametat yéfe fútaréf, yau so yémofem ewatametat yéfenjo mérén, néngorayat, 'Fém fai mat tam Yuré.' Ménamén méinyotio ár kétamé némbnar kétán ndimbal so mat em tanemén.
bt: And they will not teach their friends, they will not teach their family, saying, ‘You.p should know the Lord.’ Because every people from small to big will know about me.

nama: Yénd so kwawalindangé yéfene yaufi, a yéndon yau so ewawafarotan yéfene yaufi."
bt: I will forget their sins and I will never remember their sins.”

nama: Njam Ngánján noraye totér árnyéghmén, Ngánjánam té yiyátoi frondemén árnyégh tándáwár, a némndafi árkéméné kamndangé tándáwár so tekofnaye kolindangé.
bt: When God speaks of a new covenant, God has made the first covenant old, and anything which is becoming old like that will not.far disappear.

nama: Kété fronde árnyéghan tamorwén náknák wáwefnoghmén a wéi fénatayero sifayé wáwefnoghmén yéna mband maratan. 2 Kété tárwén sómbio saté yéné farai méngon. Fronde satayan tamorwén lambefé fayoghmén, a séki, a féskaf njúnnjúnofnar flawé tafayongérwén sékian. Yénéf témndetawét njúnnjúnofnar sifayé. 3 A kété témorwén tamangérwén sáláme, a sáláme sémta témorwén yénamayo saté témndetawét Minde Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayé. 4 Yéné satayan témorwén gol wafroghmén ghéngén wárogh séki a wénmén boks tanmotawét árnyégh boks, árkéméné wamoghkaf témorwén gole méinyotio téndomé, kénjún árnyéghmén boks témorwén gol wafroghmén dis árké ménáyan tamorwén ámb Manna, Aronene wétu árkéménayam téfunau árau, a sómbio wímmén karéf efalo yátogh yammén ánde 10 nák efarotaménd yénayan. 5 Ndimbal kwayaghafé sómbio njafar si résagh ár tárwén yénamé boks wafarégh tukmé. Yéfene njamb tawarwén yénamé boks wafaréghmén tukmé, ghérghérsisamén sifayé. Wénde yéndfem yau so yafenam méinyotio yéné yammén sénonjo.
bt: There are rules for worship in the first covenant had rules and a also special place for worship here on earth. 2 There were two rooms in this canvas house. In first room there were the lampstand, and the table, and cooked without.dust(holy) bread put on the table. This was called the without.dust(holy) place. 3 And there were hanging cloth(curtain), and behind the curtain was the second room called the Most Without.dust Place. 4 In that room it were the gold-made incence burning table(altar) and wooden boks called covenat boks, which was covered with gold on all side, inside covenant ark were gold-made dish which were some Manna, Aron's staff that sprouted leaves, and the two stone tablets for covenant where 10 commandments were written on them. 5 The big light(glorious) two men carrying heavenly message(angels) were above the boks's cover. Their wings spread over the boks's cover, the place of atonement. But we cannot explain all of this things now.

nama: Njam méinyotio yam káfrendawét ndenáye, ghéngén wárogh ár túmén kényawangayénd fronde satat ménát tawafngongayénd yéfenjo ásáfogh. 7 Wénde fáro limánégh ghéngén wárogh árro téngmorwén kénjút kétán Minde Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayat, a mé ambinotaro ámbiro yunjfan. Túmén ménáyafé férkafé árkéméné yémo tawatetau Ngánjánefaf ménát yánjoyo yaufiyam wafarghét a yaufiyam ámb árfene árké tawafrotawét matofnar. 8 Yéné náknáke Ngánján Mbérmbéram tuyafotau ndenénde Minde Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayé yau tésawérngérwén árfeyot yéné teke fronde saté a méinyotio yam wafrogham árkéménayam tufarotau mé témorwén ásáfogh. 9 Yéné siyém waufarogh yam nuyafote kétán sénonjo efoghfaf em ndené ramégh a ghéngén árké tawatetawét yau féyotaro tamorwén sái tawaumbnau wáwefnogh árfene mwighé. 10 Yéné ramégh a ghéngén mé yénémaménro tamorwén njamke a nu fénataye umbaréghmén. Yéné náknák tamorwén mé fifiménro sái táitotawét kétán yéné efghot Ngánjánene totér endfaf. Kerisoene férék
bt: When everything prepared like this, burning sacrifice men always go into the first room to start their works. 7 But only the leading burning sacrifice man goes into the Most Without.dust Place, and he does so only once a year. He always takes with blood which he offers to God on behalf of himself and for the sins which other people have committed without knowing. 8 By these rules the God Spirit showed that the Most Without.dust Place has not been opened for people this first room and the entire doing work which represents was still working. 9 This is a showing thing pointing to the present time as gifts and sacrifices which are offered were not able to cleanse thinking of worshipper. 10 Those gifts and sacrifices were only about food and water for special washing. These rules were only for body to be followed until that time God's new way. The blood of Keriso

nama: Féyo Keriso té sénonjo nénamnde Limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár yéné méinyotio mer rokarfaf árké té taném, fá té nényawai yéné ndimbal a féwénféwénofnar wáwefnogh sifayé kété njafaran, yau yafrotam ár ngángaye a yau ámb yénamé yéné mband marat snufrotam. 12 Ambinotaro méinyotio efghot yémo yérsam férék kétán yéné Minde njúnnjúnofnar sifayat, wénde yau goutafene férék a kauafene férék, yémo yérsam yánjoyo férék, a yénaye yémo yatnai téfene wanjarégh tuot. 13 Tándáwár wafroghyam mbanmbanan, goutafene férék a ndimbal kauafene férék a kauafene tosé fakfakam so tawainau árfene fifi kétamé Yesuene mérén téménd njúnnjúnofnar tambén. 14 Mé kemnangi nderé mindefifi Kerisoene férékam so merfifi tayátongé téfene tikéf kétamé yam wafrogh árké yénlimndat kérfaf ménát yéndfem so yáwefnotam ghérsé Ngánjáne. Ménáye akegh Ngánján Mbérmbéroene waftaye, Keriso naramai fáyo Ngánjánefaf ndené fété féwénféwénofnar ghéngén téfene yaufiyammén.
bt: So Keriso has now become the leading burning sacrifice man over all this good things which have come, he has entered that big and without.flaw worship place there in heaven, not made by human hands and not part of this created world. 12 Once for all time he took blood to that Most Without.dust Place, but the blood of goats and cows, he took his own blood, and with it he secured our salvation forever. 13 Under the old doing, the blood of goats and adult cows and the ashes of a young cows could clean people's bodies from Yesu's family(church) ceremony(ritual) impurity. 14 Just think how much more the blood of Keriso will become good our hearts from deeds which lead to death so that we can worship the living God. By the power of the eternal God Spirit, Keriso offered himslef to God as a without.flaw sacrifice for our sins.

nama: Yénémamén Yesu yéné féyém emo so yéfrende totér árnyégh yémafé Ngánján a ár, ménát méinyotio efe eumyote so ersat tuot rokar Ngánjánam némat mat eyátoi fá ménamén Keriso kér yémormén ménát sái yuwek táyátongai kétamé yaufiyamoene faita yémofem árké tawafrotawét fronde árnyégh mbanmbanan.
bt: So Yesu is the one who will settle new covenant between God and people, so that all who are invited can receive the eternal things for what God did without.lying talk(promise) them because Keriso died to make free them from sins' payment which they did under the first covenant.

nama: Árém mwighé yéfaronam soramatau ndené, "Yénd njam kér wém emo so tawanangé tane rokar?" Féyo njam ámb yáne ambum ménde yém yárefane rokar résaghét, so fronde mat kamndangé yárefé kér té tém o yau. Yéné soramé yémo so tawanangé yárefane rokar. 17 Njam yéné ár efalo kér yémormén, yáne soramamén si so kamndangé fifiafé, wénde njam ár mé ghérsé yém, yáne soramamén si yau so fifiafé yém. 18 Ndené yameyo, Ngánjánene fronde árnyégh yáne árfé so fifiafé témorwén wérár férkan. 19 Njam Mosesém tawaufrotau árét méinyotio Ngánjánene nák, yémo tawanangai tosé kaumén férék a goutafene férék, ménáyafé nuafé, férkat ngu a wén kafeyafé, táyátotawét buk a méinyotio ár. 20 Fá norayai, "Yéné férkam efalo yiyátote árnyégh árkéméné Ngánjánam té yafrotam féfeyot yémafé." 21 Yéné yameyo, yémo táyátotau férke yénayafé wáwefnogh farai méngo a méinyotio kété ásáfoghmén rokar. 22 Mbarkándaye, Mosesene náke, matot méinyotio rokar stamorwén tangyátotau merfifi ménáyam férke yátogham, yau férék njam témorwén, kété yau wawalingégh so témorwén yaufiyammén.
bt: A man decided for later things that, "When I died, who will take my things?" So if one of his son want to get his father's things, he must first know his father died or not. After this he will take his father's things. 17 If the man has indeed died, then the final talk becomes meaningful, but if the man is still alive, the final talk is meaningful. 18 In the same God's first agreement with his people will be meaning with the blood of animal. 19 When Moses told to the people all the commandments of God, he took the blood of young cows and goats, together with water, red wool and tree stick, sprinkled the book and all the people. 20 He said, “This blood confirms the covenant which God had made with you.” 21 In the same way, he also sprinkled the blood on the worship canvas house(tabernacle) and over all working things. 22 In fact, by the law of Moses, almost everything was made good by sprinkling with blood, if there in no blood, there is no forgiveness of sins.

nama: Farai méngo a yáne rokar so káyátongi njúnnjúnofnar wérárafene férkan. Yéné tamorwén wafarnjagh yam kétán némé em njafaran. Wénde wérárafene férék yau féyotaro yém sái efrenda efalo yam njafaran. Mé yéné ramégh yamamro árké yáinde wérárafene férék so efrende yéné yam. 24 Keriso yau nényawai ár wafroghmén Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayat árkéménayam tafarnjau ámb efalofifimén. Fá nényawai njafarot fáro, sénonjo ménát sái yakai téfeyot Ngánjánene rére. 25 Mbandmén limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár kényawangai Minde Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayé yunjfero ménát stawatetawét wérárafene férék. Wénde njam Keriso yéngmormén njafarot fáyo awateghét Ngánjánefaf, yau tafrotau férghe. 26 Ndené wafroghét, fá so aférghe kafértau kéran, tárfár mbinotmé kétamayo mband ufrogh. Yau! Ndenaye yau yém. Sénonjo Keriso yénmormén ambinotaro yéné efghot kété kerkeran sái titérmbongai yaufiyamoene wafté tuot ménaye yáne ghéngén kére téfeyot. 27 Méinyotio ár so kér em ambinotmaro, a yéné soramé fá so kokayongi moghan. 28 Ndené yameyo Keriso kér yémormén ambinotaro ndené fété ghéngén ménát sái tawitau tárfár árfene yaufiyam. Fá so kufarngé wéikor, yau ménamén yaufiyammén, wénde ménát sái yénérse wanjarégh yéfeyot emofem yánjetat fá.
bt: The tabernacle and its things can make clean with animal blood. Those were copies of what is in heaven. But animal blood can not clean the true things in Heaven. Only an offering which surpasses animal blood will make clean those things. 24 Keriso did not enter a man-made Without.dust(Holy) Place which was a copy of the real one. He entered into heaven itself, now in order that he stands for us in the presence of God. 25 The earthly leading burning sacrifice man enters the Most Without.dust Place year after year to offer the blood of animal. But when Keriso went into heaven to offer himself to God, he did not do that repeatedly. 26 To do that, he would need to die repeatedly, many times since the creation of the world. No! It is not that way. Now he came once for that time at the end to destroy the power of sin forever by his sacrificial death for us. 27 Everyone must die once and after that they will stand in judgement. 28 In the same way Keriso died once as a sacrifice to take away the sins of many people. He will appear again, not for sin, but to bring salvation to those who are waiting for him.

nama: Tándáwár wafroghyam Mosesene nákan témorwén mé mbanmban kétán ámb yam sái témorwén, yau efalofifi meryammén Kerisom némé yafrotam téfeyot. Ghéngén wárogh tándáwár yam wafrogh mbanmbanan stamorwén férghe tafértawét yunjfero, wénde yéné yam yau táyátongai féwénféwénofnar yéfeyot efe tanmorwén wáwefnoghét. 2 Njam tándáwár nákam té táyátotawét féwénféwénofnar, ghéngén wárogh táf so kefongai, wáwefnogh ár táf so tamorwén njúnnjúnofnar ambinotmaro méinyotio efghon, a yéfene kérté mwighé so kolindangai. 3 Wénde yau yéné ndené yém, yéné yunjfero ghéngén wárogh yamam mwighat tawangotawét yéfenjo yaufimén. 4 Yau féyotaro yém ménát kauafene a goutafene férke sái ewita yaufiyam.
bt: The old system in the law of Moses was only a shodow of other things to come, not the reality of the good things what Keriso did for us. The sacrifices under the old system were repeated again and again, year after year, but these things were never able to make without.flaw for those who came to worship. 2 If the old rules could make them without.flaw, the burning sacrifice would have finished, for the worshippers would have been without.dust(holy) once for all time, and their feelings of guilty would have disappeared. 3 But it's not like that, those yearly burning sacrifice things reminded them of their sins. 4 It is not possible for the blood of cows and goats to take away sins.

nama: Yéné téfnan Keriso njam yénmormén mbandot, norayai Ngánjánefaf: "Fém méndefnar ném wérár ghéngén a ramégh yam, wénde fémo té waramai ta fifi ménát raméghét.
bt: In this reason when Keriso came into the world, he said to God: “You.s did not want animal sacrifices and giving things(offerings), but you.s have given me body to offer.

nama: Fém yau kénremngotau ménáyan njam wérár kaneghawét ghéngén kitarayan o ámb ramégh yam yaufiyammén.
bt: You.s were not pleased with animals burned on the sacrificing table(latar) or with other offering for sin.

nama: Féyo yénd norayayén, "Kéreta, yénd té kwéném sái yafrotan fene ménde, o Ngánján ndené fété kés yéfarotaménd tanemén kété Ngánjánene sian.
bt: Then I said, ‘Look, I have come to do your.s will, O God just as it is written about me there in the God's talk.

nama: Fronde fá norayai, "Fém yau ménde ném ramégh yam a wérár ghéngén wárogh. Fénataye sáfot ghéngén árkéménayam áuwéghofnarot néméinde." Fá norayai ndené ndemamén yéné méinyotio ghéngén ramégh yém Ngánjánefaf nák ende. 9 Féyo fá norayai, "Yénd swém, yénd té kwéném ménát wafroghét fene ménde." Yémo yitérmboi fronde árnyégh ménát yénamayomén sái tiyátongai fifiafé. 10 A yáne méndeyam, yénd té yényátoi njúnnjúnofnar yénamé Yesu Kerisoene fifi ghéngén kénjúmé ambinotmaro méinyotioat.
bt: First he said, "You.s don't want offering and the sacrifice of the animals. Any kind of sacrifice like that does not make you happy." He said this even though all these sacrifices are offered according to the law. 9 Then he said, “Here I am, I have come to do your.p will.” He cancels the first covenant in order to put the second into effect. 10 And by his will, he have made us without.dust(holy) through the sacrifice of the body of Yesu Keriso once for all.

nama: Méinyotio efghon, ghéngén wárogh árém yafrotat yéfenjo ásáfogh Ngánjánefaf. Yémofem yarametat ndenaro ghéngén ramégh férghe. Wénde yéné ghéngnam yau so ewite árfene yaufiyam. 12 Wénde Keriso naramai fáyo ndené fété yaufiyammén ghéngén árkéméné yém mer tuot, sénonjo fá yémor kété Ngánjánene tambénta. 13 A fá so yémor kété kétánotio yáne mék téfnár kor tákmangé yáne wafté mbanmbanan. 14 Ménaye yánjo ambinotmaro ghéngén yémo té tuot yényátoi féwénféwénofnar kétamé yaufiyamta yéné ár efe táyátongé njúnnjúnofnar.
bt: Every day, the priests do their work to serve God. They offer the same sacrifices to God again and again. But those sacrifices can never take away people's sins. 12 But Keriso gave himself as a sacrifice for sin that is good forever, now he is sitting God's right side. 13 And he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power. 14 By his only one sacrifice he has forever made those people without.flaw from sin those who he made holy.

nama: Ngánján Mbérmbér wéi noraye yénémamén. Ndené noraye:
bt: The God Spirit also says this. For he says:

nama: "Njam efogh kawerangé, yéndon so yafrotan árnyégh yémafé, yéndon so efarotan tanjo nák kété yéfene mwighayan a tikéfan
bt: “When time reaches, I will make an covenat with them. I will write my laws there on their minds and hearts.

nama: Féyo yénd so kwawalindangé ménamén yéfene yaufiyammén a yau so ewafarotan yéfene yaufi wafroghyam."
bt: Then I will forget about their sins and will not remember their evil doings."

nama: Njam yaufiyam té tawalindangé, kété wéi yau yém sái erametat ghéngén. Ke kétán awamaroghét
bt: And when he forgets sins, there is no more to offer sacrifice. A Call to persevere

nama: Tane fútaréf, Yesuene férkam téfe ténmaramangé táikér mwighé sái kényawangém Minde Njúnnjúnofnar Sifayé, 20 ménaye totér ende árké yénlimnde kétán ghérsat, a yéné endam yénngérse yénd yénamé tamangérwén marat sáláme, árké yém yáne fifi. 21 Téfene yém ndimbal limánégh ghéngén wárogh ár emo yélimnde Ngánjánene méngo. 22 Féyo yénd so yéném kaka Ngánjánefaf a yau so yéném férfér. Wénde yénd so yéném efaloe a yéndfem mé so efalo yiyátotam. Yémo njúnnjúnofnar yényátoi téfene yaufita. Téfene mwighayam yau kérté yémaramete téfene yaufiyammén. Yénd té nénamndayém njúnnjúnofnar. Yéné ndenanit yém yémo té eumbaryéng téfene fifi mer nue. 23 Yénd so naweretam taikrataye kétán méghméghfaf árké yéndfem efalo yiyátotam, ménamén yéndfem mwighafé so yéfandam Ngánján emo so ewérete yánjo unyéghofnar si. 24 Nemnam ámb ndernáye so ewawéngsotam ámb ár ménát yénguyafotam uwégh a meryam wafrogh. 25 A yénd yau so nakenam téfene mérne áumengéghta, ndené ámb ár kés néngufrotat, wénde yénd nawawéngsotam, ndené sénonjo efogh yáne angoghmén kaka nénamndete.
bt: My friends, the blood of Yesu gives us confidence to enter the Most Without.dust Place, 20 by a new way which leads us to life, and this way take us through the hanging wide cloth(curtain), which is his body. 21 We have a big leading burning sacrifice man who rules over God's house, 22 So we should come near to God and we should not be afraid. But we must be honest and we must continue to trust him. He has made us clean from the dirt of our sins. Our thoughts no longer give us trouble about our sins. We have become clean. It is like he has washed our bodies with clean water. 23 We must hold tightly to the hope which we truly approve(believe), because we can trust God who keep his without.lying talk(promise). 24 Let us think other how to encourage other people to show love and good deeds. 25 And let us no neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, as now the day of his coming back is becoming close.

nama: Njam yénd mé yéngém yaufiyaman soramé táf yéndfem té yanayém matyam efalomén, yau ghéngén yéné yaufiyam wighét nafrango. 27 Kété yém yénaro férférafé awanjengégh moghmén a ménamén nékwafé fésam árké so tawifongé Ngánjánafé mék téfnár. 28 Árké árom emo tékenangai Mosesene nák kér témorwén ghérghérsisafnar njam kafgheyayénd sómbio o nambio ghékéf fayogh ár. 29 Kemnangi nderé mindefifi yaufiyaufi kérté yam so yém yéfeyot emofem yékenat Ngánjánene Ambum a té yiyátotat férék árnyéghmén, árkéménayam yényátoi njúnnjúnofnar, ndené kar mé méné yém njúnnjúnafé, yéné árém té tasalitawét a wáiwáiot tékmétawét Ngánján Mbérmbér emo yénérse Ngánjánene ghérghérsisé kétán yánjo árét. 30 Yénd mat yéném yéné ár efe norayai, "Yéndon so yangotan fai, yéndon so fai tawawangon yéfeyot emofem tawafrotat yéné, " fá wéi norayai, "Yuram so moghan tákmangé yánjo ár." 31 Yéné siyém tékratafé yam yém sái kuye kétán akegh Ngánjánene ngángat.
bt: If we keep on sinning after we have received the knowledge of the truth, no sacrifice for washing this sins is left. 27 There is only a fearful expectation of judgment and of raging fire which will consume the enemies of God. 28 Anyone who rejected the law of Moses died without mercy when two or three watching men testify. 29 Think how much terrible the punishment will be for those who turned against the Son of God and have treated the blood of the covenant, which made us holy, as if it were meaning and dirty, such people have insulted and put the shame the God Spirit who brings God's mercy to his people. 30 We know this man who said, “I will give back payment(take revenge), I will repay those who do this,” and he also said, "The Lord will judge his people." 31 This is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

nama: Yau táf tawawalindangé tándái fém ndernáye tamorwén njam fémofem Ngánjánene kwayaghé yanayénd. Nawafarotati ndernáye fém mé tamorwén ndimbal mwighayan ndemamén tamorwén yaufiyaufi tékrátan. 33 Ámb efghon fém té marataye safén kénékmangayénd wasálighan a kéran, a ámb efghon fémofem tawáutánawét fá efe tékratan tamorwén ndenáyo. 34 Fémofem tékrát téfandawét yémafé efe tawitawét kétán kwéfkwéf méngot. Njam méinyotio féfene rokar tarsawét kétamé féfeta, fémofem taumyongayénd áuwéghafé. Fém mat tamorwén féfene em merfifi rokar nawanjetat féfeyot árkéméné so naketat tuot. 35 Féyo yau yitati yéné táikér mwighé. Nawafarotati ndimbal fai yéné yam yénérse féfeyot!
bt: Don't forgot how you ware in the past when you.p received God's light. Remember how you.p keep on staying in big mind(faith) even though it was terrble suffering. 33 Sometimes you.p were publicly exposed in insulting and death, and at other times you.p helped others who were suffering the same things. 34 You.p suffered along with those who threw you.p into the darkness house. When they took away all your.p things from you.p, you.p accepted with joy. You.p know you.p had better things waiting for you.p which will last forever. 35 So do not throw away this strong though. Remember big reward it brings you.p!

nama: Fém so em métar, ménát fémofem so mé yéngwafrotati Ngánjánene ménde. Féyo fémofem so tawanangi méinyotio némat yémo té néngorayai. 37 Mé tekofnaye, Fá efe yéném so yéném a yau so yale."
bt: You.p need to be patient, so that you.p will continue to do the will of God. Then you.p will receive all what he has said.

nama: Wénde tane mbarkánd ár so emor ndimbal mwighaye. A njam fá kangongi, yénd yau so náuwétan yéfefaf.
bt: “But my straight men will live by big mind(faith). And if they turn back, I will not be pleased with them.

nama: Wénde yénd yau ndené yéném efe nangotat férfére a Ngánjánam tékratan tankmangé. Wénde yénd yéném ár emofem mé efalo yiyátotat Ngánján, a féyo, téfene ghérsé so em wanjarégh Ngánjánafé.
bt: But we do not be like those who turn back in fear and God put in pain us. But we are people who continue to trust God, and so, our lives are safe with God.

nama: Ndimbal mwighé yém táikér mwighé ndené némat yénd méghmégh yéném yéné so nufrote, yénmaramete téfén rokarmén yéndfem árké yau efandam. 2 Ngánjánam yéramai yánjo efalo yátogh kétán árét tándái efghon ménamén yéfene ndimbal mwighamén.
bt: The big mind(faith) is confidence that what we hope for will happen, it gives us root/proof about things which we cannot see. 2 God gave his real approval to people in old days because of their big mind(faith).

nama: Ménaye ndimbal mwighaye yénd mat náyátotam ndené méinyotio mband marat kufrotau kété Ngánjánene sie, ménát némé yéndfem sénonjo efandam yau enmormén kétamé árké rokar té efandam.
bt: By big mind(faith) we understand that entire world was formed by God's talk, so that what we now see did not come from anything that we can be seen.

nama: A Abelém tafrotawéng ghéngén ramégh Ngánjáne fá efalo tiyátotau. Táfandio, yáne ghéngén ramégham yáindai yáné Keinene ghéngén ramégh a témorwén merfifi. Ngánján témorwén áuwéghafé yáfaf a yáne ramégh yammén a ndemamén Abel sénonjo yém kér téfnár, yáne ndimbal mwighé mé noraye téfefaf.
bt: And Abel was making an offering to God he truly approved(believed). At the same time, his offering sacrifice surpassed his older brother Kein's offering sacrifice and was very good. God was pleased with him and his gifts and even though Abel is now dead, his big mind(faith) still speaks to us.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Ngánjánam té yéngwanai Enok tukét kétán njafarot kérofnaye, sélngwéle fá nolindai ménamén Ngánjánam yéngwanai fá. Wénde soramé Ngánjánam fá yéngwanai tukét, Ngánján efalo yiyátoi ndené áuwégh témorwén Ngánjáneyot. 6 A ndimbal mwighafnaye féyotarofnar yém áuwéghét sái yéméinda Ngánján, ménamén efe ár njam yéném yáfaf táf efalo yiyátota ndené fá nakete a ndené yémo fai ewafrote fá emofem efaloe yéronjat fá.
bt: By big mind(faith) that he(God) took Enok up to heaven without dying, suddenly he disappeared because God took him. But before God took him up, God approved him as pleasing to God. 6 And without big mind(faith) is impossible to make please God, because anyone who comes to him must truly approve(believe) that he exists and that he does payments those who truly seek him.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Noam yérametam bout sái tawanjarngai yánjo mérén kétamé kéférta. Yémon tiyáretau Ngánján, emo tawéngsotau fá ménamén ámb yammén árké yau nufrotam tándái. Ménaye yánjo ndimbal mwighaye Ngánjánam yafalngoi méinyotio mband marat a nénamndai Noa mbarkánd Ngánjánene ghékfan.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Noa built a boat to save his family from the flood. He obeyed God, who warned him about something which had never happened in the past(before). By his big mind(faith) God condemned all the world and made Noa straight in God's sight.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Ebraam námyoi njam Ngánjánam yémotai fá ménát sái téfrangongai méngotu a kuwangongai ámb yuwot árké Ngánjánam so taramangai yá. Fá néwanoi mafinaye ándan fá tiotau. 9 Ménamén Ebraamene témorwén ndimbal mwighé, fá yémorangérmén ndené fété wan téfnár mbandan ándan Ngánjánam unyéghofnar norayai. Fá tékmangérwén farai méngon, ndené fété Aisek a Jeikof, endefet soramayo ewarameyai yénayo unyéghofnar si. 10 Ebraamém yafrotam yéné, ménamén fá kawanjetau kétán akegh ndimbal tesenot árké Ngánjánam yéfrendam a yérametam.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Ebraam obeyed when God called him to leave home and go to another land which God will give him. He went without.knowing where he was going. 9 Because Ebraam had big mind(faith), he lived as a stranger in the land where God said without.lying talk. He lived in canvas houses, as did Aisek and Jeikof, to whom were later given the same without.lying talk(promise). 10 Ebraam did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city which God had prepared and built.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Sara mérne yémafé Ebraamfé so féyotaro tawarwén ambum, ndemamén tárwén bulu a Sara témorwén mwinu. Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau ndené Ngánjánam so mé teretau yánjo unyéghofnar si. 12 A méinyotio ár marat nénáyátoi kétamé yéné ámbiro árta, Ebraam, efe témorwén bulufifi ménát sái tinjongai ámb ambum. Yáne mérén náyátoi tárfár ndené kénd njafaran a miri mband masoan, kété ánde ámb yau yém waféghét.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Sara together with Ebraam was able to have a child, even though they.d were too old and Sara was barren. Ebraam truly approved(believed) that God would keep his without.lying talk(promise). 12 And all people became wide from this one man, Ebraam, who was too old to have any child. His family became so many that like the stars in the sky and the sand on the seashore, there is no way to count them.

nama: Méinyotio yéné ndimbal mwighafé ár kér emormén ménáyofnar yau ewanayénd némat Ngánjánam té emndai unyéghofnar si, wénde yémofem yinjoyénd kétán nayuro a yumyoyénd Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si. Fá korayawét ndené, "Yénd ndenanit yéném wan téfnár a orsogh ár mband maratan." 14 Ár efe norayat ndené safén yékmetat ndené fá náronjat yéfenjo yuwot. 15 Njam fá mé kemnawét ménamén yumén yémofem árké efrangoyénd, fá so táf kangowét. 16 Wénde, fá káronjawét ménát merfifi yuwot, njafar méngotuot. Yénémamén Ngánján wéi yau wáiwái yém sái yémndetat yéfene Ngánján, ménamén yémo té yéfrendam ndimbal tesen yéfeyot.
bt: All these faithful people died without not taking what God had said without.lying talk(promise), but they saw it all from a distance and welcomed the without.lying talks(promises) of God. They agreed that they were strangers and lost men on earth. 14 People who say such things make it clear that they are looking for their country. 15 If they were thinking of the country which they had left, they could have gone back. 16 Instead, they were longing for a better country, a heavenly home. Therefore God is also not ashamed to be called their God, because he was prepared a city for them.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Ebraamém yéramai yáne ambum Aisek ménát ghéngénot njam Ngánjánam tufotau fá. Ebraamém té ewanai Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si, wénde fá témorwén afrengéghkaf ghéngénot yánjo ambumfifi, Aisek. 18 Ndemamén Ngánjánam yémndai fá, "Aiseke kénjúmé fene mérén so néngusamngote." 19 Ebraamém kemnau, "Aisek so kér témorwén, wénde Ngánjánam so tanongai." Féyo efalofifie yénd so norayam yéné siyém ufarogh yam yém némé efalo nufrotam. Yéné ndenanit témorwén Ebraamém kar yangoi Aisek kérta.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Ebraam offer his son Aisek as a sacrifice when God test him. Ebraam had received God's without.lying talks(promises), but he was ready to sacrifice his only son, Aisek. 18 Even though God said to him, “Through Aisek that your family will continue.” 19 Ebraam thought, “Aisak will die, but God will raise him back.” So truly we can say that is a picture of what really happened. It was like Ebraam receive Aisek back from death.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Aisekém efrendangeyai yánjo sómbio ambum, Jeikof a Esau, yáne témorwén táikér mwighé kétán némé Ngánjánam so tafrotau kété táf soramayan.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Aisek blessed his two sons, Jeikof and Esau, he had confidence in what God would do in the future.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Jeikof, njam fá témorwén bulu a nufngoi krot, efrendayéng fénatayero Josefene ambum. Fá namghai yánjo wétuan a Ngánjáne yáwefnoi.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Jeikof, when he was old and dying, blessed each of Josef's sons. He leaned his walking stick and worshipped God.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Josef, njam kaka témorwén krot, mwighé taikre norayai Israil ár tanéutorngai kétamé Ijifta. A fá témorwén féyotaro yénémamén ndené yémo tamndetau fá ménát sái társawét yáne sot yémofem njam fá kuwanongayénd.
bt: By big mind(faith) that Josef, when he was about to die, said confidently the Israil people take out of Ijif. And he was so sure of it that he commanded them to carry his bones when they leave.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye Moses yárefafem yátmafé yétneyayénd nambio ghérare yánjoyo mérnam njam fá náráftam. Yémofem yinjeyayénd fá témorwén mer ambum, a fá yau tarwén férfér warsagh ároene wafroghyammén.
bt: By big mind(faith) Moses's parents hid him three months by his family when he was born. They.d saw he was a good child, and they were not afraid of the ruling man(king)'s commandment.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye Moses, njam fá nanotam, nakenai sái tanmotawét Faraoene mérésoene ambum. 25 Yéné ménamén témorwén fá yau ménde témorwén waitoghét yaufi end a tayamnetau tekofnaye yaufiyam ménde. Wénde yaferyéng so tafnyangérwén Ngánjánene árfene wawérsoghan. 26 Fá kemnau ndené yémo tékrát téfandau Kerisoenemén, yéné yam yáinde mer ámnjoghét Ijifén yáne mer rokarafé. Ménamén fá kéretau mé kétán ndimbal fai árké Ngánjánam so taramangé yá. 27 Ndimbal mwighaye Mosesém noroi Ijif yau férfér témorwén warsagh ároene nékwmén. Fá ménjté mé téngmorwén ménamén yémo yinjoi fá efe yém fandaghofnar. 28 Ndimbal mwighaye ndené Mosesém tamndetau Israil ár ménát so tawafrotawét Waingégh téménd a sái táyátotawét férke sésafne sérasér ménát ndené kér ngi téfnár njafar si résagh árém yau so tásmétawét yéfene fronde sénko ambum.
bt: By big mind(faith) Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Farawa's daughter. 25 That is why he did not want to follow evil way and enjoyed the short time sinful preasure. But he chose to share the oppression of God's people. 26 He thought that he suffered for the sake of Keriso, this thing surpassed the good living in Ijif with good thing of it. Because he was looking ahead to the big reward which God would give him. 27 By big mind(faith) Moses left Ijift without being afraid of the ruling man(king)'s anger. He continue to go because he saw him who is without.seeing. 28 By big mind(faith) that Moses told the people of Israil to do the Passover and to sprinkle the blood on the doorposts so that the man carrying heavenly message of death would not kill the first-born sons.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye Israil ár tayotau yénamé Férkat Arakafamé ndenanit fá kar tamorwén ghérnat mbandan. Wénde njam Ijif árém tawaitotawét, fá semormén méinyotio kolitotawét.
bt: By big mind(faith) that the people of Israil went right through the Red Sea as if on dry land. But when the Ijift people followed, they were all drowned.

nama: Ndimbal mwighaye Israil árém sakwéle tarendawét Jeriko sefen efogh, a táf wím áráke nufngoyénd irorégh. 31 Ndimbal mwighaye ndené tukal mérés Raab yau yémormén kér yémafé méinyotio yéné árfé efe tamorwén yáne ndimbal tesenan, efe kakenawét áyárghét Ngánjánefaf. Ménamén mérsom eumyoi fútar ende ayafogh ár.
bt: By big mind(faith) Israil people marched around Jericho 7 days, and the stone fence began to fall down. 31 By big mind(faith) the prostitute girl Raab did not die with them all those people who were in her city, who refused to listen to God. Because she welcomed spies friendly way.

nama: Ndernáye wét njénambafé yénd so norayan? Yénayam so yérse tekfifi sái wéikor yéngwaftam yam ufroghmén ndimbal mwighamén yéfenemén, Gidon, Barak, Samson, Jefta, Deifid, Samuel, a méinyotio Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár. 33 Emofem ndimbal mwighaye tétumndangayénd warsagh árfene yu, a tawafrotawét wafroghyam mbarkándero, a tawanangayénd némé Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si norayai, emofem tamnangayénd ndimbal munjan fusafene sénfne, 34 tangéumbértotawét fés kánkékánké, a kunjarngayénd kérta kétamé witmafé sénté náifata. Yéfene muyafnar fifi stamorwén kawéngongayénd kétán muyat. Fá kénamndangayénd muyafé mékan a tákmangayénd yéfene mék téfnár wifarghét. 35 Amafam tawinjongayénd yéfenjo kér ár kanongayénd ghérsat wéikor, a ámb ár takmetawét tékrátamé, yau tinjongayénd wanjarégh ménát yémofem so tanangi merfifi anogh. 36 Ámb ár tamorwén tawafrotawét méndménd a yéfene sém tanfangayénd fene kufrotawét ménáyan fémoghan, a ámb ár taumbetawét seine a tanyawangayénd kwéfkwéf méngot. 37 Ámb ár kér tamorwén wíme wafyátoghan, a ámb ár tamorwén karéf taramangayénd. Ámb ár tamorwén krot tásmétawét witmafé sénté naifaye. Ámb ár tayotau tawawongérwén sif a gout ghéngan, siogh tausérmbotawét, tasmetawét a taikrataye tawafrotawét. 38 Mband maratam merfifi yau tafandau fá. Fá ngáronjawét fanfne yuwomé a mband tunakfamé, a féneyamé a mband fénemé.
bt: How much more do I need to say? This will take too long to count again the stories of the big mind(faith) of them, Gidon, Barak, Samson, Jefta, Deifid, Samuel, and all the God's people who make know(prophets). 33 Who by big mind(faith) conquered king's nations, and do straight things, and received what God said without.lying talk(promise), who closed the mouth of big wild cats(lions), 34 put off the flames of fire, and escaped death by the sword. Their without.power(weak) body were turned to strength. They became strong in battle and put whole armies to chase. 35 Women received their dead people raised to life again, and other people were put to pain, not accepting salvation so that they will receive better resurrection. 36 Some people were mocked and their backs were cut open with whips, and other people were tied in chains and put in the darkness house. 37 Some people died by stoning, and some people were split in half. Other people were killed by the sword. Some people went about wearing the skin of sheep and goat, hungry and oppressed and mistreated. 38 The world was not too good to them. They wandered in deserts and mountains, and in caves and holes in the ground.
